1Ôćĺ# Source Resolution Protocols
     2Ôćĺ
     3Ôćĺ## Remote Source Resolution (Forge/Deep only)
     4Ôćĺ
     5ÔćĺIf `source_repo` is a local path: proceed with the tier-appropriate strategy as normal.
     6Ôćĺ
     7ÔćĺIf `source_repo` is a remote URL (GitHub URL or owner/repo format) AND tier is Forge or Deep:
     8Ôćĺ
     9Ôćĺ1. **Check `git` availability:** Verify `git` is functional (`git --version`). If `git` is not available, skip to the fallback warning below.
    10Ôćĺ
    11Ôćĺ2. **Ephemeral shallow clone:** Clone the repository to a system temp path for AST access:
    12Ôćĺ
    13Ôćĺ   ```
    14Ôćĺ   temp_path = {system_temp}/skf-ephemeral-{skill-name}-{timestamp}/
    15Ôćĺ   git clone --depth 1 --branch {branch} --single-branch --filter=blob:none {source_repo} {temp_path}
    16Ôćĺ   ```
    17Ôćĺ
    18Ôćĺ   **If `include_patterns` are NOT specified:**
    19Ôćĺ
    20Ôćĺ   ```
    21Ôćĺ   git clone --depth 1 --branch {branch} --single-branch --filter=blob:none {source_repo} {temp_path}
    22Ôćĺ   ```
    23Ôćĺ
    24Ôćĺ   **If `include_patterns` ARE specified**, use sparse-checkout to limit the clone scope:
    25Ôćĺ
    26Ôćĺ   ```
    27Ôćĺ   git clone --depth 1 --branch {branch} --single-branch --filter=blob:none --sparse {source_repo} {temp_path}
    28Ôćĺ   ```
    29Ôćĺ
    30Ôćĺ   **Mode selection:** Choose sparse-checkout mode based on whether `exclude_patterns` exist:
    31Ôćĺ
    32Ôćĺ   - **No `exclude_patterns`:** Use default **cone mode** (faster). Convert `include_patterns` to directory roots.
    33Ôćĺ   - **`exclude_patterns` present:** Use **`--no-cone` mode** which supports gitignore-style negation patterns (`!` prefix). This applies both include and exclude at the git level, avoiding unnecessary blob downloads.
    34Ôćĺ
    35Ôćĺ   **Cone mode (no exclude patterns):**
    36Ôćĺ
    37Ôćĺ   **IMPORTANT:** `git sparse-checkout set` expects **directories**, not glob patterns. Convert `include_patterns` before passing them:
    38Ôćĺ
    39Ôćĺ   **Classification rule:** A pattern is an **individual file** if it contains no glob characters (`*`, `?`, `[`) AND does not end with `/`. Everything else is a glob ÔÇö strip it to its directory root (the path prefix before the first glob character or wildcard segment).
    40Ôćĺ
    41Ôćĺ   - Strip glob suffixes to directory roots (e.g., `src/core/**/*.py` Ôćĺ `src/core`, `src/api/*.ts` Ôćĺ `src/api`)
    42Ôćĺ   - Deduplicate the resulting directory list
    43Ôćĺ   - Individual files (e.g., `pyproject.toml`, `src/utils/helpers.py`) are kept as-is
    44Ôćĺ
    45Ôćĺ   **If only directory roots (no individual files):**
    46Ôćĺ
    47Ôćĺ   ```
    48Ôćĺ   git -C {temp_path} sparse-checkout set {converted_directory_roots}
    49Ôćĺ   ```
    50Ôćĺ
    51Ôćĺ   **If any individual files are present (or mixed):**
    52Ôćĺ
    53Ôćĺ   ```
    54Ôćĺ   git -C {temp_path} sparse-checkout set --skip-checks {converted_directory_roots} {individual_files}
    55Ôćĺ   ```
    56Ôćĺ
    57Ôćĺ   Example transformation:
    58Ôćĺ   ```
    59Ôćĺ   Brief include_patterns:        sparse-checkout args:
    60Ôćĺ   src/core/**/*.py          Ôćĺ    src/core          (directory root)
    61Ôćĺ   src/api/*.ts              Ôćĺ    src/api           (directory root)
    62Ôćĺ   examples/**/*.py          Ôćĺ    examples          (directory root)
    63Ôćĺ   pyproject.toml            Ôćĺ    pyproject.toml    (individual file, needs --skip-checks)
    64Ôćĺ   src/utils/helpers.py      Ôćĺ    src/utils/helpers.py (individual file, needs --skip-checks)
    65Ôćĺ   ```
    66Ôćĺ
    67Ôćĺ   **No-cone mode (exclude patterns present):**
    68Ôćĺ
    69Ôćĺ   When `exclude_patterns` exist, use `--no-cone` mode to pass both include and exclude patterns directly as gitignore-style rules:
    70Ôćĺ
    71Ôćĺ   1. Convert `include_patterns` to gitignore-style patterns. If a pattern contains no glob characters (`*`, `?`, `[`) and does not end with `/`, append `/**` to match directory contents recursively (e.g., `cognee` Ôćĺ `cognee/**`; `cognee/**` Ôćĺ kept as-is).
    72Ôćĺ   2. Convert `exclude_patterns` to negation patterns by prepending `!`. Apply the same anchoring rule (e.g., `cognee/tests` Ôćĺ `!cognee/tests/**`; `cognee/tests/**` Ôćĺ `!cognee/tests/**`; `**/test_*` Ôćĺ `!**/test_*`).
    73Ôćĺ   3. **CRITICAL:** List all include patterns BEFORE negated exclude patterns ÔÇö git processes patterns in order and a negation can only suppress a prior inclusion.
    74Ôćĺ   4. Pass to sparse-checkout ÔÇö include patterns first, then negated exclude patterns:
    75Ôćĺ
    76Ôćĺ   ```
    77Ôćĺ   git -C {temp_path} sparse-checkout set --no-cone {include_gitignore_patterns} {negated_exclude_patterns}
    78Ôćĺ   ```
    79Ôćĺ
    80Ôćĺ   Example transformation:
    81Ôćĺ   ```
    82Ôćĺ   Brief include_patterns:        Brief exclude_patterns:
    83Ôćĺ   cognee/**                      cognee/tests/**
    84Ôćĺ                                  cognee/alembic/**
    85Ôćĺ                                  **/test_*
    86Ôćĺ
    87Ôćĺ   sparse-checkout args (--no-cone):
    88Ôćĺ   'cognee/**' '!cognee/tests/**' '!cognee/alembic/**' '!**/test_*'
    89Ôćĺ   ```
    90Ôćĺ
    91Ôćĺ   **Note:** `--no-cone` mode is slower than cone mode for very large repositories but eliminates downloading excluded blobs entirely.
    92Ôćĺ
    93Ôćĺ   **Always-included root files:**
    94Ôćĺ
    95Ôćĺ   Regardless of `include_patterns`, always add these root-level version/manifest files to the sparse-checkout pattern list. These are needed for version reconciliation and must not require a fallback to `gh api`:
    96Ôćĺ
    97Ôćĺ   `pyproject.toml`, `package.json`, `Cargo.toml`, `go.mod`, `setup.py`, `setup.cfg`, `VERSION`
    98Ôćĺ
    99Ôćĺ   In cone mode, always use the `--skip-checks` command form when adding these files ÔÇö even if `include_patterns` resolved to only directory roots (which would normally use the form without `--skip-checks`). The command becomes: `git -C {temp_path} sparse-checkout set --skip-checks {directory_roots} pyproject.toml package.json Cargo.toml go.mod setup.py setup.cfg VERSION`. In no-cone mode, list them as explicit include patterns before any negation patterns. Do not flag them as extraneous inclusions during post-checkout filtering.
   100Ôćĺ
   101Ôćĺ   **Post-checkout filtering:**
   102Ôćĺ
   103Ôćĺ   After checkout, apply the original glob `include_patterns` as file-level filters when building the extraction file list ÔÇö sparse-checkout gets the right directories, glob filtering narrows to the exact files. When `--no-cone` mode was used, most exclude filtering is already done at the git level, but apply `exclude_patterns` as a final pass to catch any edge cases where gitignore pattern matching diverges from the brief's glob semantics. Always-included root files (see above) are exempt from post-checkout filtering.
   104Ôćĺ
   105Ôćĺ3. **If clone succeeds:** Update the working source path to `{temp_path}` for all subsequent AST operations in this step. Proceed with the **Forge/Deep Tier** extraction strategy below. Mark `ephemeral_clone_active = true` for cleanup.
   106Ôćĺ
   107Ôćĺ4. **If clone fails (network error, auth failure, timeout):**
   108Ôćĺ
   109Ôćĺ   ÔÜá´ŞĆ **Warn the user explicitly:**
   110Ôćĺ
   111Ôćĺ   "Ephemeral clone of `{source_repo}` failed: {error}. Degrading to source reading (T1-low) for this run. For T1 (AST-verified) confidence, clone the repository locally and update `source_repo` in your brief to the local path."
   112Ôćĺ
   113Ôćĺ   Proceed with Quick tier extraction strategy below. Note the degradation reason in context for the evidence report.
   114Ôćĺ
   115Ôćĺ**Ephemeral clone cleanup:** After extraction is complete for all files in scope (whether successful or partially failed), before presenting the Gate 2 summary (Section 6), if `ephemeral_clone_active`, delete the `{temp_path}` directory. Log: "Ephemeral source clone cleaned up." This ensures cleanup runs even if some extractions failed, as long as the step itself is still executing. **If any error halts the extraction step before Gate 2 is reached**, cleanup must still occur: attempt to delete `{temp_path}` before halting. Log the cleanup attempt regardless of success.
   116Ôćĺ
   117Ôćĺ---
   118Ôćĺ
   119Ôćĺ## Version Reconciliation (all tiers, source mode only)
   120Ôćĺ
   121Ôćĺ**If `source_type: "docs-only"`:** skip this section ÔÇö no source files exist to reconcile.
   122Ôćĺ
   123ÔćĺAfter the source path is accessible (local path from step-01, or ephemeral clone from above), check whether the source contains a version identifier and reconcile it with `brief.version`. Look for the first matching version file in the resolved source path:
   124Ôćĺ
   125Ôćĺ- Python: `pyproject.toml` (`[project] version`), `setup.py` (`version=`), `__version__` in `__init__.py`
   126Ôćĺ- JavaScript/TypeScript: `package.json` (`"version"`)
   127Ôćĺ- Rust: `Cargo.toml` (`[package] version`)
   128Ôćĺ- Go: `go.mod` (module version if tagged)
   129Ôćĺ
   130Ôćĺ**If a source version is found AND it differs from `brief.version`:**
   131Ôćĺ
   132ÔćĺÔÜá´ŞĆ Warn the user: "Brief version ({brief.version}) differs from source version ({source_version}). Using source version ({source_version})."
   133Ôćĺ
   134ÔćĺUpdate the working version in context to the source version. Record the mismatch in context for the evidence report (step-08).
   135Ôćĺ
   136Ôćĺ**If no version file is found or version cannot be extracted:** keep `brief.version` as-is. No warning needed.
   137Ôćĺ
   138Ôćĺ**If source is remote and accessed via Quick tier (gh_bridge, no local files):** attempt to read the version file via `gh_bridge.read_file(owner, repo, "{version_file}")` for the primary version file of the detected language. If the read fails, keep `brief.version`.
   139Ôćĺ

1Ôćĺ# Skill Template ÔÇö Quick Skill Output
     2Ôćĺ
     3Ôćĺ## SKILL.md Section Structure
     4Ôćĺ
     5ÔćĺThe following sections should be populated in the generated SKILL.md. Best-effort ÔÇö not all sections will have data for every skill.
     6Ôćĺ
     7Ôćĺ### Required Sections
     8Ôćĺ
     9Ôćĺ```markdown
    10Ôćĺ---
    11Ôćĺname: {skill_name}
    12Ôćĺdescription: >
    13Ôćĺ  {README-derived description, trigger-optimized for agent discovery.
    14Ôćĺ  Include what the package does and when to use it.
    15Ôćĺ  Mention what NOT to use it for if applicable.}
    16Ôćĺ---
    17Ôćĺ
    18Ôćĺ# {skill_name}
    19Ôćĺ
    20Ôćĺ## Overview
    21Ôćĺ- **Package:** {package_name}
    22Ôćĺ- **Repository:** {repo_url}
    23Ôćĺ- **Language:** {language}
    24Ôćĺ- **Source Authority:** community
    25Ôćĺ- **Generated:** {date}
    26Ôćĺ
    27Ôćĺ## Description
    28Ôćĺ{README-derived description of what the package does}
    29Ôćĺ
    30Ôćĺ## Key Exports
    31Ôćĺ{List of public exports with brief descriptions}
    32Ôćĺ
    33Ôćĺ## Usage Patterns
    34Ôćĺ{Common usage patterns extracted from README examples}
    35Ôćĺ```
    36Ôćĺ
    37Ôćĺ### Optional Sections (include when data available)
    38Ôćĺ
    39Ôćĺ```markdown
    40Ôćĺ## Configuration
    41Ôćĺ{Configuration options if found in source}
    42Ôćĺ
    43Ôćĺ## Dependencies
    44Ôćĺ{Key dependencies from manifest file}
    45Ôćĺ
    46Ôćĺ## Notes
    47Ôćĺ{Any caveats, limitations, or observations about the extraction}
    48Ôćĺ```
    49Ôćĺ
    50Ôćĺ## context-snippet.md Format (Vercel-Aligned)
    51Ôćĺ
    52ÔćĺIndexed format targeting ~80-120 tokens per skill:
    53Ôćĺ
    54Ôćĺ```markdown
    55Ôćĺ[{skill_name} v{version}]|root: skills/{skill_name}/
    56Ôćĺ|IMPORTANT: {skill_name} v{version} ÔÇö read SKILL.md before writing {skill_name} code. Do NOT rely on training data.
    57Ôćĺ|quick-start:{SKILL.md#quick-start}
    58Ôćĺ|api: {top-5 exports with () for functions}
    59Ôćĺ|key-types:{SKILL.md#key-types} ÔÇö {inline summary of most important type values}
    60Ôćĺ|gotchas: {2-3 most critical pitfalls or breaking changes, inline}
    61Ôćĺ```
    62Ôćĺ
    63Ôćĺ## metadata.json Format
    64Ôćĺ
    65Ôćĺ```json
    66Ôćĺ{
    67Ôćĺ  "name": "{skill_name}",
    68Ôćĺ  "version": "{source-version or 1.0.0}",
    69Ôćĺ  "description": "{brief description of the skill}",
    70Ôćĺ  "skill_type": "single",
    71Ôćĺ  "source_authority": "community",
    72Ôćĺ  "source_repo": "{repo_url}",
    73Ôćĺ  "source_root": "{resolved_source_path}",
    74Ôćĺ  "source_commit": "{commit_sha_if_available}",
    75Ôćĺ  "source_package": "{package_name}",
    76Ôćĺ  "language": "{language}",
    77Ôćĺ  "generated_by": "quick-skill",  // Optional provenance field ÔÇö indicates which workflow generated this skill
    78Ôćĺ  "generation_date": "{date}",
    79Ôćĺ  "confidence_tier": "Quick",
    80Ôćĺ  "spec_version": "1.3",
    81Ôćĺ  "exports": ["{export_1}", "{export_2}"],
    82Ôćĺ  "confidence_distribution": {
    83Ôćĺ    "t1": 0,
    84Ôćĺ    "t1_low": "{exports_count (integer, not string)}",
    85Ôćĺ    "t2": 0,
    86Ôćĺ    "t3": 0
    87Ôćĺ  },
    88Ôćĺ  "tool_versions": {
    89Ôćĺ    "ast_grep": null,
    90Ôćĺ    "qmd": null,
    91Ôćĺ    "skf": "{skf_version}"
    92Ôćĺ  },
    93Ôćĺ  "stats": {
    94Ôćĺ    "exports_documented": "{number}",
    95Ôćĺ    "exports_public_api": "{number}",
    96Ôćĺ    "exports_internal": 0,
    97Ôćĺ    "exports_total": "{number}",
    98Ôćĺ    "public_api_coverage": 1.0,
    99Ôćĺ    "total_coverage": 1.0,
   100Ôćĺ    "scripts_count": 0,
   101Ôćĺ    "assets_count": 0
   102Ôćĺ  },
   103Ôćĺ  "dependencies": [],
   104Ôćĺ  "compatibility": "{semver-range}"
   105Ôćĺ}
   106Ôćĺ```

   108Ôćĺ## references/{library}.md Structure

   127Ôćĺ## references/integrations/{pair}.md Structure

1Ôćĺ# Skill Brief Schema
     2Ôćĺ
     3Ôćĺ## Required Fields
     4Ôćĺ
     5Ôćĺ| Field | Type | Description | Validation |
     6Ôćĺ|-------|------|-------------|------------|
     7Ôćĺ| name | string | Kebab-case skill name | Must match `[a-z0-9-]+` |
     8Ôćĺ| version | string | Semantic version | Must match `X.Y.Z` pattern. Auto-detect from source (see Version Detection below), fall back to "1.0.0" |
     9Ôćĺ| source_repo | string | GitHub URL or local path | Must be accessible |
    10Ôćĺ| language | string | Primary programming language | Must be detected or user-specified |
    11Ôćĺ| scope | object | Inclusion/exclusion boundaries | Must have type + at least one pattern |
    12Ôćĺ| description | string | What this skill covers | 1-3 sentences |
    13Ôćĺ| forge_tier | string | Compilation tier | One of: Quick, Forge, Forge+, Deep (Title Case) |
    14Ôćĺ| created | string | Creation date | ISO date format |
    15Ôćĺ| created_by | string | User who created the brief | From config user_name |
    16Ôćĺ
    17Ôćĺ## Optional Fields
    18Ôćĺ
    19Ôćĺ| Field | Type | Description | Validation |
    20Ôćĺ|-------|------|-------------|------------|
    21Ôćĺ| source_type | string | Source mode: `source` or `docs-only` | Default `source`. When `docs-only`: `source_repo` optional, `doc_urls` required |
    22Ôćĺ| doc_urls | array | Documentation URLs for T3 content | Each entry: `{url, label}`. Required when `source_type: "docs-only"` |
    23Ôćĺ| `scripts_intent` | string | Describes whether scripts should be extracted. Values: `detect` (auto-detect from source ÔÇö default when absent), `none` (skip scripts), or a free-text description of expected scripts (e.g., "CLI validation tools in bin/"). |
    24Ôćĺ| `assets_intent` | string | Describes whether assets should be extracted. Values: `detect` (auto-detect from source ÔÇö default when absent), `none` (skip assets), or a free-text description of expected assets (e.g., "JSON schemas in schemas/"). |
    25Ôćĺ| `source_authority` | string | `official`, `community`, or `internal`. Default `community`. Set to `official` only when the skill creator is the library maintainer. Forced to `community` when `source_type: "docs-only"`. |
    26Ôćĺ
    27ÔćĺWhen `source_type: "docs-only"`:
    28Ôćĺ- `source_repo` becomes optional (set to doc site URL for reference)
    29Ôćĺ- `doc_urls` must have at least one entry
    30Ôćĺ- `source_authority` is forced to `community` (T3 external documentation cannot be `official`)
    31Ôćĺ- All extracted content gets `[EXT:{url}]` citations
    32Ôćĺ
    33Ôćĺ## Version Detection
    34Ôćĺ
    35ÔćĺDuring brief creation, attempt to auto-detect the source version before defaulting to `"1.0.0"`. Check the first matching file in the source:
    36Ôćĺ
    37Ôćĺ- **Python:** `pyproject.toml` `[project] version` (static) Ôćĺ if `dynamic = ["version"]`, check `__init__.py` for `__version__` Ôćĺ `_version.py` if exists Ôćĺ `setup.py` `version=` Ôćĺ `git describe --tags --abbrev=0`
    38Ôćĺ- **JavaScript/TypeScript:** `package.json` (`"version"`)
    39Ôćĺ- **Rust:** `Cargo.toml` `[package] version` (static) Ôćĺ if `version = { workspace = true }`, resolve from workspace root `Cargo.toml` Ôćĺ `git describe --tags --abbrev=0`
    40Ôćĺ- **Go:** version tag from `go.mod` or `git describe --tags --abbrev=0`
    41Ôćĺ
    42ÔćĺIf the source is a remote GitHub repo, use `gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/contents/{file}` to read the version file. If the source is local, read the file directly.
    43Ôćĺ
    44ÔćĺIf detection succeeds, use the detected version. If it fails or returns a non-semver value, fall back to `"1.0.0"`.
    45Ôćĺ
    46ÔćĺThe create-skill workflow (step-03-extract) also performs version reconciliation at extraction time ÔÇö if the source version has changed since the brief was created, the extraction step warns and uses the source version.
    47Ôćĺ
    48Ôćĺ## Scope Object Structure
    49Ôćĺ
    50Ôćĺ```yaml
    51Ôćĺscope:
    52Ôćĺ  type: full-library | specific-modules | public-api | docs-only
    53Ôćĺ  include:
    54Ôćĺ    - "src/**/*.ts"           # Glob patterns for included files/directories
    55Ôćĺ  exclude:
    56Ôćĺ    - "**/*.test.*"           # Glob patterns for excluded files
    57Ôćĺ    - "**/node_modules/**"
    58Ôćĺ  notes: "Optional notes about scope decisions"
    59Ôćĺ```
    60Ôćĺ
    61Ôćĺ## YAML Template
    62Ôćĺ
    63Ôćĺ```yaml
    64Ôćĺ---
    65Ôćĺname: "{skill-name}"
    66Ôćĺversion: "{detected-version or 1.0.0}"  # Auto-detect from source, fall back to 1.0.0
    67Ôćĺsource_type: "source"                    # "source" (default) or "docs-only"
    68Ôćĺsource_repo: "{github-url-or-local-path}"
    69Ôćĺlanguage: "{detected-language}"
    70Ôćĺdescription: "{brief-description}"
    71Ôćĺforge_tier: "{Quick|Forge|Forge+|Deep}"
    72Ôćĺcreated: "{date}"
    73Ôćĺcreated_by: "{user_name}"
    74Ôćĺscope:
    75Ôćĺ  type: "{full-library|specific-modules|public-api|docs-only}"
    76Ôćĺ  include:
    77Ôćĺ    - "{pattern}"
    78Ôćĺ  exclude:
    79Ôćĺ    - "{pattern}"
    80Ôćĺ  notes: "{optional-scope-notes}"
    81Ôćĺ# Optional: documentation URLs for T3 content (required when source_type: "docs-only")
    82Ôćĺ# doc_urls:
    83Ôćĺ#   - url: "https://docs.example.com/api"
    84Ôćĺ#     label: "API Reference"
    85Ôćĺ# scripts_intent: detect         # Optional: detect | none | description
    86Ôćĺ# assets_intent: detect          # Optional: detect | none | description
    87Ôćĺ# source_authority: community    # Optional: official | community | internal
    88Ôćĺ---
    89Ôćĺ```
    90Ôćĺ
    91Ôćĺ## Human-Readable Presentation Format
    92Ôćĺ
    93ÔćĺWhen presenting the brief for confirmation (brief-skill step 04 only ÔÇö not applicable to analyze-source batch generation), display as:
    94Ôćĺ
    95Ôćĺ```
    96ÔćĺSkill Brief: {name}
    97Ôćĺ====================
    98Ôćĺ
    99ÔćĺTarget:      {source_repo}
   100ÔćĺLanguage:    {language}
   101ÔćĺForge Tier:  {forge_tier}
   102ÔćĺDescription: {description}
   103Ôćĺ
   104ÔćĺScope: {scope.type}
   105Ôćĺ  Include: {scope.include patterns, one per line}
   106Ôćĺ  Exclude: {scope.exclude patterns, one per line}
   107Ôćĺ  Notes:   {scope.notes}
   108Ôćĺ
   109ÔćĺVersion:    {version}
   110ÔćĺCreated:    {created}
   111ÔćĺCreated by: {created_by}
   112Ôćĺ```
   113Ôćĺ
   114Ôćĺ## Validation Rules
   115Ôćĺ
   116Ôćĺ1. `name` must be unique within {forge_data_folder}
   117Ôćĺ2. `source_repo` must be accessible (gh api for GitHub, path exists for local)
   118Ôćĺ3. `language` must be a recognized programming language
   119Ôćĺ4. `scope.type` must be one of the four defined types
   120Ôćĺ5. `scope.include` must have at least one pattern (exception: `docs-only` scope, where include patterns are optional since no source code is available)
   121Ôćĺ6. `forge_tier` must be one of: Quick, Forge, Forge+, Deep (Title Case, must match the tier from forge-tier.yaml, or default to Quick)
   122Ôćĺ7. When `source_type: "docs-only"`: `doc_urls` must have >= 1 entry, `source_repo` becomes optional
   123Ôćĺ8. Each `doc_urls` entry must have a valid `url` field
   124Ôćĺ

1Ôćĺ# Confidence Tiers
     2Ôćĺ
     3Ôćĺ## Principle
     4Ôćĺ
     5ÔćĺEvery extracted claim carries a confidence tier label ÔÇö T1, T1-low, T2, or T3 ÔÇö that tells consuming agents how much to trust the information. The tier determines citation format, affects scoring weights, and governs how claims interact during updates and audits.
     6Ôćĺ
     7Ôćĺ## Rationale
     8Ôćĺ
     9ÔćĺNot all extracted information has equal reliability. An AST-parsed function signature is structurally verified; a type inferred from pattern matching might be wrong; a QMD-enriched usage note adds context but not structural proof; an external doc reference is only as current as its source.
    10Ôćĺ
    11ÔćĺWithout confidence tiers:
    12Ôćĺ- All claims appear equally trustworthy regardless of extraction method
    13Ôćĺ- Agents cannot distinguish verified signatures from inferred patterns
    14Ôćĺ- Audit and update workflows have no basis for prioritizing drift findings
    15Ôćĺ
    16ÔćĺWith confidence tiers:
    17Ôćĺ- Each claim transparently declares its evidence strength
    18Ôćĺ- Scoring weights adjust based on the distribution of tiers
    19Ôćĺ- Update workflows know which claims to re-verify first
    20Ôćĺ
    21Ôćĺ## Tier Definitions
    22Ôćĺ
    23Ôćĺ| Tier | Label | Source | Extraction Method | Available At |
    24Ôćĺ| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
    25Ôćĺ| T1 | AST-verified | Source code | ast-grep structural parsing | Forge, Forge+, Deep |
    26Ôćĺ| T1-low | Source-read | Source code | Pattern matching, file reading | Quick, Forge, Forge+, Deep |
    27Ôćĺ| T2 | QMD-enriched | Knowledge base | QMD semantic search + synthesis | Deep |
    28Ôćĺ| T3 | External reference | Docs, URLs | External documentation lookup | All tiers |
    29Ôćĺ
    30Ôćĺ## T2 Sub-Types
    31Ôćĺ
    32ÔćĺT2 enrichment content maps to temporal scopes that control where annotations surface:
    33Ôćĺ
    34Ôćĺ| Sub-type | Content | Surface Level |
    35Ôćĺ| --- | --- | --- |
    36Ôćĺ| T2-past | Closed issues, merged PRs, changelogs ÔÇö historical context explaining why the API looks the way it does | `references/` (full temporal context) |
    37Ôćĺ| T2-future | Open PRs, deprecation warnings, RFCs ÔÇö forward-looking context about upcoming changes | `SKILL.md` Section 4b (Migration & Deprecation Warnings) + `references/` |
    38Ôćĺ
    39ÔćĺThe T2-past/T2-future distinction is operationally significant: T2-future annotations trigger the presence of a Migration & Deprecation Warnings section in SKILL.md, while T2-past annotations live in progressive disclosure reference files only.
    40Ôćĺ
    41Ôćĺ## Citation Formats
    42Ôćĺ
    43ÔćĺEach tier has a defined citation format used consistently across all skill output:
    44Ôćĺ
    45Ôćĺ```
    46ÔćĺT1:     [AST:filepath:Lnn]          ÔÇö AST-verified, line-level precision
    47ÔćĺT1-low: [SRC:filepath:Lnn]          ÔÇö source-read, line-level but unverified structure
    48ÔćĺT2:     [QMD:collection:document]   ÔÇö QMD knowledge enrichment
    49ÔćĺT3:     [EXT:url-or-reference]      ÔÇö external documentation reference
    50Ôćĺ```
    51Ôćĺ
    52Ôćĺ## Pattern Examples
    53Ôćĺ
    54Ôćĺ### Example 1: T1 ÔÇö AST-Verified Export
    55Ôćĺ
    56Ôćĺ**Context:** ast-grep parses a TypeScript file and extracts a function export with full signature.
    57Ôćĺ
    58Ôćĺ**Implementation:**
    59Ôćĺ```markdown
    60Ôćĺ## `createServer(options: ServerOptions): Server`
    61Ôćĺ
    62ÔćĺCreates and configures an HTTP server instance.
    63Ôćĺ
    64Ôćĺ**Parameters:**
    65Ôćĺ| Name | Type | Required | Default |
    66Ôćĺ| --- | --- | --- | --- |
    67Ôćĺ| options | `ServerOptions` | yes | ÔÇö |
    68Ôćĺ
    69Ôćĺ[AST:src/server.ts:L23]
    70Ôćĺ```
    71Ôćĺ
    72Ôćĺ**Key Points:**
    73Ôćĺ- Signature extracted by AST ÔÇö structurally guaranteed to match source
    74Ôćĺ- Line number is exact, not approximate
    75Ôćĺ- Only available when ast-grep is present (Forge/Deep tier)
    76Ôćĺ
    77Ôćĺ### Example 2: T1-low ÔÇö Source-Read Extraction
    78Ôćĺ
    79Ôćĺ**Context:** Quick tier extraction reads source files without AST parsing.
    80Ôćĺ
    81Ôćĺ**Implementation:**
    82Ôćĺ```markdown
    83Ôćĺ## `createServer(options)`
    84Ôćĺ
    85ÔćĺCreates and configures an HTTP server instance.
    86Ôćĺ
    87Ôćĺ**Parameters:**
    88Ôćĺ| Name | Type | Required | Default |
    89Ôćĺ| --- | --- | --- | --- |
    90Ôćĺ| options | `object` | yes | ÔÇö |
    91Ôćĺ
    92Ôćĺ[SRC:src/server.ts:L23] ÔÇö type inferred from usage, not structurally verified
    93Ôćĺ```
    94Ôćĺ
    95Ôćĺ**Key Points:**
    96Ôćĺ- Same function, but parameter type is `object` not `ServerOptions` ÔÇö pattern matching missed the type alias
    97Ôćĺ- Citation explicitly notes the inference limitation
    98Ôćĺ- Still useful ÔÇö location is correct, signature is close
    99Ôćĺ
   100Ôćĺ### Example 3: T2 ÔÇö QMD Enrichment
   101Ôćĺ
   102Ôćĺ**Context:** Deep tier enriches an extracted API with usage patterns from QMD knowledge base.
   103Ôćĺ
   104Ôćĺ**Implementation:**
   105Ôćĺ```markdown
   106Ôćĺ### Usage Context
   107Ôćĺ
   108Ôćĺ`createServer` is typically used with `loadMiddleware()` in application bootstrap.
   109ÔćĺCommon pattern: create server first, then attach middleware chain before calling `.listen()`.
   110Ôćĺ
   111Ôćĺ[QMD:project-docs:architecture-overview]
   112Ôćĺ```
   113Ôćĺ
   114Ôćĺ**Key Points:**
   115Ôćĺ- Enrichment adds context, not structural claims
   116Ôćĺ- QMD citations reference collection and document, not line numbers
   117Ôćĺ- T2 content appears in enrichment sections, not in API signatures
   118Ôćĺ
   119Ôćĺ### Example 4: Tier Interaction During Updates
   120Ôćĺ
   121Ôćĺ**Context:** An update-skill workflow detects that a T1-low claim now contradicts fresh T1 extraction.
   122Ôćĺ
   123Ôćĺ**Implementation:** The merge algorithm applies tier precedence:
   124Ôćĺ1. T1 always overrides T1-low for the same export
   125Ôćĺ2. T2 enrichments are preserved unless the underlying T1/T1-low claim changed
   126Ôćĺ3. T3 references are flagged for manual review if the API they describe changed
   127Ôćĺ
   128Ôćĺ**Key Points:**
   129Ôćĺ- Higher confidence tiers take precedence in merge conflicts
   130Ôćĺ- T2 enrichments are additive ÔÇö they don't conflict with structural claims
   131Ôćĺ- T3 references may go stale and are flagged, not auto-removed
   132Ôćĺ
   133Ôćĺ## Confidence Distribution in Metadata
   134Ôćĺ
   135ÔćĺEvery generated skill includes a confidence distribution in `metadata.json`:
   136Ôćĺ
   137Ôćĺ```json
   138Ôćĺ{
   139Ôćĺ  "confidence_distribution": {
   140Ôćĺ    "t1": 42,
   141Ôćĺ    "t1_low": 5,
   142Ôćĺ    "t2": 12,
   143Ôćĺ    "t3": 3
   144Ôćĺ  },
   145Ôćĺ  "confidence_tier": "Deep"  // valid values: "Quick", "Forge", "Forge+", "Deep"
   146Ôćĺ}
   147Ôćĺ```
   148Ôćĺ
   149ÔćĺThis distribution feeds into test-skill scoring ÔÇö skills with higher T1 ratios score better on signature accuracy.
   150Ôćĺ
   151Ôćĺ## Anti-Patterns
   152Ôćĺ
   153Ôćĺ- Omitting the confidence tier from a citation ÔÇö every citation must declare its level
   154Ôćĺ- Using T1 format (`[AST:...]`) when ast-grep was not actually used for that extraction
   155Ôćĺ- Treating T1-low and T1 as equivalent during scoring ÔÇö they have different weight impacts
   156Ôćĺ- Adding T2 enrichment without a QMD citation ÔÇö enrichment without provenance is hallucination
   157Ôćĺ
   158Ôćĺ## Related Fragments
   159Ôćĺ
   160Ôćĺ- [zero-hallucination.md](zero-hallucination.md) ÔÇö the principle that motivates tiered confidence
   161Ôćĺ- [provenance-tracking.md](provenance-tracking.md) ÔÇö how citations are recorded in provenance-map.json
   162Ôćĺ- [progressive-capability.md](progressive-capability.md) ÔÇö how forge tier determines available confidence levels
   163Ôćĺ
   164Ôćĺ_Source: consolidated from extraction-patterns.md, create-skill steps 03/05, and scoring-rules.md_
   165Ôćĺ

1Ôćĺ# Skill Brief Schema
     2Ôćĺ
     3Ôćĺ## Purpose
     4Ôćĺ
     5ÔćĺDefines the output contract for skill-brief.yaml files generated by analyze-source. Each generated brief must conform to this schema to be consumable by brief-skill and create-skill downstream workflows.
     6Ôćĺ
     7Ôćĺ## Required Fields
     8Ôćĺ
     9Ôćĺ| Field | Type | Constraint | Description |
    10Ôćĺ|-------|------|------------|-------------|
    11Ôćĺ| name | string | kebab-case `[a-z0-9-]+` | Unique skill identifier |
    12Ôćĺ| version | string | Semantic `X.Y.Z` | Auto-detect from source (see Version Detection below), fall back to `1.0.0` |
    13Ôćĺ| source_repo | string | GitHub URL or local path | Repository or project root (optional when `source_type: "docs-only"`) |
    14Ôćĺ| language | string | Recognized language | Primary programming language |
    15Ôćĺ| scope | object | See Scope Object below | Boundary definition |
    16Ôćĺ| description | string | 1-3 sentences | What the skill covers |
    17Ôćĺ| forge_tier | string | `Quick` / `Forge` / `Forge+` / `Deep` | Inherited from forge-tier.yaml (Title Case) |
    18Ôćĺ| created | string | ISO date `YYYY-MM-DD` | Generation date |
    19Ôćĺ| created_by | string | user_name from config | Who generated the brief |
    20Ôćĺ
    21Ôćĺ## Optional Fields
    22Ôćĺ
    23Ôćĺ| Field | Type | Constraint | Description |
    24Ôćĺ|-------|------|------------|-------------|
    25Ôćĺ| source_type | string | `source` or `docs-only` | Default `source`. When `docs-only`: `source_repo` optional, `doc_urls` required |
    26Ôćĺ| doc_urls | array | `{url, label}` objects | Documentation URLs for T3 content. Required when `source_type: "docs-only"` |
    27Ôćĺ| `scripts_intent` | string | `detect` / `none` / free-text | Describes whether scripts should be extracted. Values: `detect` (auto-detect from source ÔÇö default when absent), `none` (skip scripts), or a free-text description of expected scripts (e.g., "CLI validation tools in bin/"). |
    28Ôćĺ| `assets_intent` | string | `detect` / `none` / free-text | Describes whether assets should be extracted. Values: `detect` (auto-detect from source ÔÇö default when absent), `none` (skip assets), or a free-text description of expected assets (e.g., "JSON schemas in schemas/"). |
    29Ôćĺ| `source_authority` | string | `official` / `community` / `internal` | Default `community`. Set to `official` only when the skill creator is the library maintainer. Forced to `community` when `source_type: "docs-only"`. |
    30Ôćĺ
    31ÔćĺWhen `source_type: "docs-only"`:
    32Ôćĺ- `source_repo` becomes optional (set to doc site URL for reference)
    33Ôćĺ- `doc_urls` must have at least one entry
    34Ôćĺ- `source_authority` is forced to `community` (T3 external documentation cannot be `official`)
    35Ôćĺ- All extracted content gets `[EXT:{url}]` citations
    36Ôćĺ
    37Ôćĺ## Version Detection
    38Ôćĺ
    39ÔćĺDuring brief generation, attempt to auto-detect the source version before defaulting to `"1.0.0"`. Check the first matching file in the source:
    40Ôćĺ
    41Ôćĺ- **Python:** `pyproject.toml` `[project] version` (static) Ôćĺ if `dynamic = ["version"]`, check `__init__.py` for `__version__` Ôćĺ `_version.py` if exists Ôćĺ `setup.py` `version=` Ôćĺ `git describe --tags --abbrev=0`
    42Ôćĺ- **JavaScript/TypeScript:** `package.json` (`"version"`)
    43Ôćĺ- **Rust:** `Cargo.toml` `[package] version` (static) Ôćĺ if `version = { workspace = true }`, resolve from workspace root `Cargo.toml` Ôćĺ `git describe --tags --abbrev=0`
    44Ôćĺ- **Go:** version tag from `go.mod` or `git describe --tags --abbrev=0`
    45Ôćĺ
    46ÔćĺIf the source is a remote GitHub repo, use `gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/contents/{file}` to read the version file. If the source is local, read the file directly.
    47Ôćĺ
    48ÔćĺIf detection succeeds, use the detected version. If it fails or returns a non-semver value, fall back to `"1.0.0"`.
    49Ôćĺ
    50ÔćĺThe create-skill workflow (step-03-extract) also performs version reconciliation at extraction time ÔÇö if the source version has changed since the brief was created, the extraction step warns and uses the source version.
    51Ôćĺ
    52Ôćĺ## Scope Object
    53Ôćĺ
    54Ôćĺ```yaml
    55Ôćĺscope:
    56Ôćĺ  type: full-library | specific-modules | public-api | docs-only
    57Ôćĺ  include:
    58Ôćĺ    - 'src/**/*.ts'          # At least one required
    59Ôćĺ  exclude:
    60Ôćĺ    - 'src/**/*.test.ts'     # Optional
    61Ôćĺ  notes: 'Optional rationale for scope decision'
    62Ôćĺ```
    63Ôćĺ
    64Ôćĺ### Scope Types
    65Ôćĺ
    66Ôćĺ| Type | Use When |
    67Ôćĺ|------|----------|
    68Ôćĺ| full-library | Entire codebase of a unit |
    69Ôćĺ| specific-modules | Selected components or packages |
    70Ôćĺ| public-api | Only exported interfaces |
    71Ôćĺ| docs-only | When source_type is docs-only ÔÇö no source code available, all content from doc_urls |
    72Ôćĺ
    73Ôćĺ## YAML Template
    74Ôćĺ
    75Ôćĺ```yaml
    76Ôćĺname: '{unit-name}'
    77Ôćĺversion: '{detected-version or 1.0.0}'
    78Ôćĺsource_type: 'source'
    79Ôćĺsource_repo: '{project_path}'
    80Ôćĺlanguage: '{detected_language}'
    81Ôćĺscope:
    82Ôćĺ  type: '{detected_scope_type}'
    83Ôćĺ  include:
    84Ôćĺ    - '{include_pattern}'
    85Ôćĺ  exclude:
    86Ôćĺ    - '{exclude_pattern}'
    87Ôćĺ  notes: '{scope_rationale}'
    88Ôćĺdescription: '{1-3 sentence description}'
    89Ôćĺforge_tier: '{Quick|Forge|Forge+|Deep}'
    90Ôćĺcreated: '{current_date}'
    91Ôćĺcreated_by: '{user_name}'
    92Ôćĺ```
    93Ôćĺ
    94Ôćĺ## Validation Rules
    95Ôćĺ
    96Ôćĺ1. **Name uniqueness**: No duplicate names within forge_data_folder
    97Ôćĺ2. **Source accessibility**: source_repo path must exist or be reachable
    98Ôćĺ3. **Language recognized**: Must be a known programming language
    99Ôćĺ4. **Scope type valid**: Must match one of the four defined types (full-library, specific-modules, public-api, docs-only)
   100Ôćĺ5. **Include patterns**: At least one include glob pattern required (exception: `docs-only` scope, where include patterns are optional since no source code is available)
   101Ôćĺ6. **Forge tier match**: Must match value in forge-tier.yaml
   102Ôćĺ7. **Docs-only mode**: When `source_type: "docs-only"`, `doc_urls` required (>= 1), `source_repo` optional
   103Ôćĺ8. Each `doc_urls` entry must have a valid `url` field
   104Ôćĺ
   105Ôćĺ## Output Location
   106Ôćĺ
   107ÔćĺEach skill-brief.yaml is written to:
   108Ôćĺ```
   109Ôćĺ{forge_data_folder}/{unit-name}/skill-brief.yaml
   110Ôćĺ```
   111Ôćĺ

1Ôćĺ# Skill Lifecycle
     2Ôćĺ
     3Ôćĺ## Principle
     4Ôćĺ
     5ÔćĺThe 12 SKF workflows form an end-to-end pipeline from source discovery through verified export, with a pre-code architecture verification path. Each workflow produces artifacts consumed by downstream workflows. Understanding the lifecycle enables Ferris to recommend the right workflow for a user's situation and to maintain artifact continuity across the pipeline.
     6Ôćĺ
     7Ôćĺ## Rationale
     8Ôćĺ
     9ÔćĺSkills are not created in a single step ÔÇö they move through discovery, design, compilation, verification, and distribution phases. Each phase has dedicated workflows with specific inputs and outputs. The lifecycle model prevents users from skipping critical steps and ensures artifacts flow cleanly between workflows.
    10Ôćĺ
    11ÔćĺWithout lifecycle awareness:
    12Ôćĺ- Users jump to create-skill without proper analysis or briefing
    13Ôćĺ- Generated skills lack provenance because upstream artifacts were skipped
    14Ôćĺ- Export packages reference stale or untested skills
    15Ôćĺ
    16ÔćĺWith lifecycle awareness:
    17Ôćĺ- Ferris recommends the appropriate entry point based on the user's context
    18Ôćĺ- Artifact dependencies are explicit ÔÇö each workflow declares its inputs
    19Ôćĺ- The full pipeline produces verified, export-ready skills with complete provenance
    20Ôćĺ
    21Ôćĺ## Pipeline Phases
    22Ôćĺ
    23Ôćĺ| Phase | Workflows | Purpose | Artifacts Produced |
    24Ôćĺ| --- | --- | --- | --- |
    25Ôćĺ| Setup | SF (Setup Forge) | Detect environment and set capability tier | `forge-tier.yaml` |
    26Ôćĺ| Discovery | AN (Analyze Source) | Scan project, identify skillable units | Analysis report, skill briefs |
    27Ôćĺ| Design | BS (Brief Skill) | Interactive scope definition for one skill | `skill-brief.yaml` |
    28Ôćĺ| Compilation | CS, QS, SS (Create/Quick/Stack) | Extract source and compile skill | `SKILL.md`, metadata, provenance, `scripts/`, `assets/` (when source contains them) |
    29Ôćĺ| Architecture Verification | VS, RA (Verify Stack, Refine Architecture) | Pre-code feasibility check and architecture refinement | Feasibility report, refined architecture doc |
    30Ôćĺ| Maintenance | US, AS (Update/Audit) | Detect drift and refresh skills | Updated `SKILL.md`, drift report |
    31Ôćĺ| Verification | TS (Test Skill) | Quality gate ÔÇö completeness scoring | Test report, pass/fail decision |
    32Ôćĺ| Distribution | EX (Export Skill) | Package and inject into agent context | agentskills.io bundle, snippets |
    33Ôćĺ
    34Ôćĺ## Typical Flows
    35Ôćĺ
    36Ôćĺ### First-Time Setup (Brownfield Project)
    37Ôćĺ
    38Ôćĺ```
    39ÔćĺSF Ôćĺ AN Ôćĺ CS (--batch) Ôćĺ TS Ôćĺ EX
    40Ôćĺ```
    41Ôćĺ
    42Ôćĺ1. **SF** detects tools, writes forge tier
    43Ôćĺ2. **AN** scans the project, identifies skillable units, generates skill briefs
    44Ôćĺ3. **CS** compiles skills from briefs (batch mode processes all briefs)
    45Ôćĺ4. **TS** runs completeness verification on each skill
    46Ôćĺ5. **EX** packages passing skills for distribution
    47Ôćĺ
    48Ôćĺ### Single Skill (Full Quality)
    49Ôćĺ
    50Ôćĺ```
    51ÔćĺSF Ôćĺ BS Ôćĺ CS Ôćĺ TS Ôćĺ EX
    52Ôćĺ```
    53Ôćĺ
    54Ôćĺ1. **SF** ensures environment is configured
    55Ôćĺ2. **BS** guides the user through scope definition, produces a validated brief
    56Ôćĺ3. **CS** compiles the skill with full provenance
    57Ôćĺ4. **TS** verifies completeness
    58Ôćĺ5. **EX** packages for distribution
    59Ôćĺ
    60Ôćĺ### Single Skill (Fast)
    61Ôćĺ
    62Ôćĺ```
    63ÔćĺSF Ôćĺ QS Ôćĺ EX
    64Ôćĺ```
    65Ôćĺ
    66Ôćĺ1. **SF** ensures environment is configured
    67Ôćĺ2. **QS** resolves a package name or URL to source and compiles directly ÔÇö no brief needed
    68Ôćĺ3. **EX** packages for distribution (optional TS between QS and EX for quality assurance)
    69Ôćĺ
    70Ôćĺ### Pre-Code Stack Verification
    71Ôćĺ
    72Ôćĺ```
    73ÔćĺSF Ôćĺ CS├ŚN (per library) Ôćĺ VS Ôćĺ RA Ôćĺ SS (compose) Ôćĺ TS Ôćĺ EX
    74Ôćĺ```
    75Ôćĺ
    76Ôćĺ1. **SF** ensures environment is configured
    77Ôćĺ2. **CS** compiles individual skills for each library in the stack
    78Ôćĺ3. **VS** cross-references skills against architecture and PRD ÔÇö produces a feasibility report with verdicts
    79Ôćĺ4. **RA** refines the architecture document using skill API evidence and VS findings
    80Ôćĺ5. **SS** (compose-mode) synthesizes the stack skill from individual skills + refined architecture
    81Ôćĺ6. **TS** verifies the composed stack skill
    82Ôćĺ7. **EX** packages for distribution
    83Ôćĺ
    84Ôćĺ### Maintenance
    85Ôćĺ
    86Ôćĺ```
    87ÔćĺAS Ôćĺ US Ôćĺ TS Ôćĺ EX
    88Ôćĺ```
    89Ôćĺ
    90Ôćĺ1. **AS** detects drift between skill and current source
    91Ôćĺ2. **US** re-extracts changed exports and merges, preserving \[MANUAL\] sections
    92Ôćĺ3. **TS** re-verifies the updated skill
    93Ôćĺ4. **EX** re-exports the updated package
    94Ôćĺ
    95Ôćĺ## Pattern Examples
    96Ôćĺ
    97Ôćĺ### Example 1: Workflow Selection Decision
    98Ôćĺ
    99Ôćĺ**Context:** A user asks Ferris to create a skill for a specific library.
   100Ôćĺ
   101Ôćĺ**Implementation:** Ferris evaluates the situation:
   102Ôćĺ
   103Ôćĺ| Condition | Recommended Flow |
   104Ôćĺ| --- | --- |
   105Ôćĺ| User knows exactly what to skill, has a brief | CS directly |
   106Ôćĺ| User knows the library but needs scope guidance | BS Ôćĺ CS |
   107Ôćĺ| User has a package name, wants fast results | QS |
   108Ôćĺ| User wants to skill their entire project | AN Ôćĺ CS (batch) |
   109Ôćĺ| User has an existing skill that may be outdated | AS Ôćĺ US |
   110Ôćĺ| User wants to verify tech stack before building | CS├ŚN Ôćĺ VS Ôćĺ RA Ôćĺ SS (compose) |
   111Ôćĺ
   112Ôćĺ**Key Points:**
   113Ôćĺ- SF is always prerequisite (but only needs to run once per project)
   114Ôćĺ- Quick skill (QS) skips briefing ÔÇö appropriate for well-known packages
   115Ôćĺ- Analyze source (AN) is the brownfield entry point for large projects
   116Ôćĺ
   117Ôćĺ### Example 2: Artifact Flow Between Workflows
   118Ôćĺ
   119Ôćĺ**Context:** Tracking how artifacts flow through the pipeline.
   120Ôćĺ
   121Ôćĺ**Implementation:**
   122Ôćĺ```
   123ÔćĺSF Ôćĺ forge-tier.yaml
   124Ôćĺ       Ôćô (read by all subsequent workflows)
   125ÔćĺAN Ôćĺ analysis-report.md + skill-brief.yaml[]
   126Ôćĺ       Ôćô (briefs consumed by CS)
   127ÔćĺCS Ôćĺ SKILL.md + metadata.json + provenance-map.json + scripts/ + assets/ (when present) + evidence-report.md
   128Ôćĺ       Ôćô (skill consumed by TS)
   129ÔćĺTS Ôćĺ test-report.md (pass/fail gate)
   130Ôćĺ       Ôćô (passing skill consumed by EX)
   131ÔćĺEX Ôćĺ agentskills.io bundle + context snippets
   132Ôćĺ
   133ÔćĺVS Ôćĺ feasibility-report-{project_name}.md (verdict + integration verdicts)
   134Ôćĺ       Ôćô (report consumed by RA)
   135ÔćĺRA Ôćĺ refined-architecture-{project_name}.md (gaps filled, issues flagged, improvements suggested)
   136Ôćĺ       Ôćô (refined doc consumed by SS compose-mode)
   137ÔćĺSS (compose) Ôćĺ SKILL.md (stack skill synthesized from individual skills + architecture)
   138Ôćĺ```
   139Ôćĺ
   140Ôćĺ**Key Points:**
   141Ôćĺ- `forge-tier.yaml` is the universal dependency ÔÇö all workflows read it
   142Ôćĺ- Skill briefs are the handoff between discovery/design and compilation
   143Ôćĺ- TS acts as a gate ÔÇö failing skills do not proceed to EX
   144Ôćĺ- VS/RA form a pre-code verification loop ÔÇö rerun after architecture changes
   145Ôćĺ
   146Ôćĺ### Example 3: Stack Skill vs. Individual Skills
   147Ôćĺ
   148Ôćĺ**Context:** A user wants to document how multiple libraries work together in their project.
   149Ôćĺ
   150Ôćĺ**Implementation:** Two approaches serve different needs:
   151Ôćĺ- **Individual skills** (via CS or QS): One skill per library, each with its own provenance and lifecycle. Best for reusable skills that apply across projects.
   152Ôćĺ- **Stack skill** (via SS): A single consolidated skill documenting how libraries connect. Detects co-import patterns and integration surfaces. Best for project-specific context.
   153Ôćĺ
   154Ôćĺ**Key Points:**
   155Ôćĺ- Stack skills and individual skills are complementary, not competing
   156Ôćĺ- SS detects integration patterns (e.g., "express + passport always used together for auth routes")
   157Ôćĺ- Individual skills track per-library provenance; stack skills track inter-library relationships
   158Ôćĺ
   159Ôćĺ## Integration Points
   160Ôćĺ
   161Ôćĺ- **Setup Forge** must run before any other workflow ÔÇö it establishes the tier
   162Ôćĺ- **Analyze Source** and **Brief Skill** are alternative entry points to compilation
   163Ôćĺ- **Test Skill** is optional but recommended ÔÇö the quality gate before export
   164Ôćĺ- **Audit Skill** and **Update Skill** form the maintenance loop
   165Ôćĺ- **Verify Stack** and **Refine Architecture** form the pre-code verification path
   166Ôćĺ- **Export Skill** is the terminal workflow ÔÇö it produces the distributable artifact
   167Ôćĺ
   168Ôćĺ## Related Fragments
   169Ôćĺ
   170Ôćĺ- [progressive-capability.md](progressive-capability.md) ÔÇö how the forge tier affects each pipeline phase
   171Ôćĺ- [agentskills-spec.md](agentskills-spec.md) ÔÇö the output format that export-skill packages
   172Ôćĺ- [provenance-tracking.md](provenance-tracking.md) ÔÇö how provenance flows through the pipeline
   173Ôćĺ
   174Ôćĺ_Source: synthesized from all 12 workflow.md files (including VS, RA) and module-help.csv_
   175Ôćĺ

1Ôćĺ# QMD Collection Registry
     2Ôćĺ
     3Ôćĺ## Principle
     4Ôćĺ
     5ÔćĺQMD collections in the Skill Forge follow a **progressive registry architecture**: workflow steps that produce artifacts index them into QMD collections and register them in `forge-tier.yaml`. Downstream workflow steps discover and query collections through the registry ÔÇö never by convention or assumption. Collections are created by producers, consumed by searchers, and maintained by a janitor.
     6Ôćĺ
     7Ôćĺ## Rationale
     8Ôćĺ
     9ÔćĺEarly SKF versions used a blind auto-index strategy: setup-forge would scan all project directories and index them into QMD collections. This created three problems:
    10Ôćĺ
    11Ôćĺ- On fresh workspaces, auto-indexing found nothing ÔÇö wasted work
    12Ôćĺ- Raw source code indexing produced low signal-to-noise search results
    13Ôćĺ- No connection between what was indexed and what workflows actually queried
    14Ôćĺ
    15ÔćĺThe progressive registry solves all three by indexing **curated workflow artifacts** (extraction snapshots, skill briefs) instead of raw source files, and only when a workflow produces them.
    16Ôćĺ
    17Ôćĺ## Architecture
    18Ôćĺ
    19Ôćĺ### Roles
    20Ôćĺ
    21Ôćĺ| Role | Workflow | Responsibility |
    22Ôćĺ| --- | --- | --- |
    23Ôćĺ| **Producer** | brief-skill, create-skill | Creates QMD collections from workflow artifacts and registers them in forge-tier.yaml |
    24Ôćĺ| **Consumer** | audit-skill, update-skill | Reads the registry, discovers collections by skill name and type, queries via qmd_bridge |
    25Ôćĺ| **Janitor** | setup-forge | Cross-references live QMD collections against the registry, cleans orphans and stale entries |
    26Ôćĺ
    27Ôćĺ### Registry Schema
    28Ôćĺ
    29ÔćĺThe `qmd_collections` array lives in `forge-tier.yaml` alongside tool availability and tier:
    30Ôćĺ
    31Ôćĺ```yaml
    32Ôćĺqmd_collections:
    33Ôćĺ  - name: "my-lib-extraction"
    34Ôćĺ    type: "extraction"
    35Ôćĺ    source_workflow: "create-skill"
    36Ôćĺ    skill_name: "my-lib"
    37Ôćĺ    created_at: "2026-03-14"
    38Ôćĺ  - name: "my-lib-brief"
    39Ôćĺ    type: "brief"
    40Ôćĺ    source_workflow: "brief-skill"
    41Ôćĺ    skill_name: "my-lib"
    42Ôćĺ    created_at: "2026-03-14"
    43Ôćĺ```
    44Ôćĺ
    45Ôćĺ### Collection Types
    46Ôćĺ
    47Ôćĺ| Type | Producer | Content | Primary Consumers |
    48Ôćĺ| --- | --- | --- | --- |
    49Ôćĺ| `extraction` | create-skill step-07 | Compiled SKILL.md, references, context-snippet ÔÇö structured, confidence-rated exports | audit-skill (drift detection), update-skill (T2 enrichment) |
    50Ôćĺ| `brief` | brief-skill step-05 | skill-brief.yaml ÔÇö intent, scope, target repository metadata | Portfolio-level search (cross-skill deduplication) |
    51Ôćĺ| `temporal` | create-skill step-03b | GitHub issues, PRs, releases, changelogs ÔÇö historical and planned context at T2 confidence | step-04 enrichment (temporal annotations per exported function) |
    52Ôćĺ| `docs` | create-skill step-03c | Fetched external documentation ÔÇö API references, guides, usage examples (T3 confidence) | step-04 enrichment (cross-reference doc context with source-extracted functions) |
    53Ôćĺ
    54Ôćĺ### Lifecycle
    55Ôćĺ
    56Ôćĺ```
    57Ôćĺbrief-skill writes brief Ôćĺ indexes {name}-brief Ôćĺ registers in forge-tier.yaml
    58Ôćĺ    Ôćô
    59Ôćĺcreate-skill fetches temporal context Ôćĺ indexes {name}-temporal Ôćĺ registers in forge-tier.yaml (Deep only)
    60Ôćĺ    Ôćô
    61Ôćĺcreate-skill fetches docs Ôćĺ indexes {name}-docs Ôćĺ registers in forge-tier.yaml (Deep only)
    62Ôćĺ    Ôćô
    63Ôćĺcreate-skill compiles skill Ôćĺ indexes {name}-extraction Ôćĺ registers in forge-tier.yaml
    64Ôćĺ    Ôćô
    65Ôćĺaudit-skill reads registry Ôćĺ queries {name}-extraction for drift baseline
    66Ôćĺupdate-skill reads registry Ôćĺ queries {name}-extraction for T2 enrichment
    67Ôćĺ    Ôćô
    68Ôćĺsetup-forge reads registry + QMD state Ôćĺ cleans orphans, removes stale entries
    69Ôćĺ```
    70Ôćĺ
    71Ôćĺ## Collection Gate
    72Ôćĺ
    73ÔćĺA new QMD collection type is justified ONLY when:
    74Ôćĺ
    75Ôćĺ1. A specific **workflow step** performs a **programmatic search** against it
    76Ôćĺ2. The data is not already accessible through existing artifacts (provenance map, file I/O)
    77Ôćĺ3. QMD search adds value beyond what direct file reading provides
    78Ôćĺ
    79ÔćĺHuman readability alone is not sufficient justification ÔÇö the file is already on disk.
    80Ôćĺ
    81Ôćĺ## Rejected Candidates
    82Ôćĺ
    83Ôćĺ### Source Clone Indexing
    84Ôćĺ
    85Ôćĺ**Proposal:** Clone the target repository and index all source files into QMD.
    86Ôćĺ
    87Ôćĺ**Rejection reason:** Freshness problem. A cloned repo is a snapshot pretending to be current. No clear owner for refresh lifecycle. The provenance map already records `file:line` for every export, and ast-grep provides structural queries against local source. QMD indexing of raw source adds noise, not signal.
    88Ôćĺ
    89Ôćĺ### Source-Map Indexing
    90Ôćĺ
    91Ôćĺ**Proposal:** Index the GitHub API tree response (directory structure + file metadata) as a lightweight `{name}-source-map` collection.
    92Ôćĺ
    93Ôćĺ**Rejection reason:** No workflow consumer. Audit-skill already has file paths from provenance-map.json. The source-map would only serve ad-hoc developer queries, which don't justify the collection overhead.
    94Ôćĺ
    95Ôćĺ### Analysis Report Indexing
    96Ôćĺ
    97Ôćĺ**Proposal:** Index the analyze-source decomposition report as a `{name}-analysis` collection.
    98Ôćĺ
    99Ôćĺ**Rejection reason:** Decision artifact, not a compilation input. The analyze-source report tells users which skills to create, but create-stack-skill works from individual skill artifacts, not the analysis report. The only consumer would be a human reading the file ÔÇö which they can do directly.
   100Ôćĺ
   101Ôćĺ## Relationship to CCC Index Registry
   102Ôćĺ
   103ÔćĺThe `ccc_index_registry` array in forge-tier.yaml is a **parallel but separate** registry from `qmd_collections`. They track different things:
   104Ôćĺ
   105Ôćĺ| Aspect | QMD Collections | CCC Index Registry |
   106Ôćĺ|--------|----------------|-------------------|
   107Ôćĺ| **What is indexed** | Curated workflow artifacts (SKILL.md, briefs, temporal data) | Source code (the actual codebase) |
   108Ôćĺ| **Index engine** | QMD (BM25 + optional vector search) | cocoindex-code (AST + vector embeddings) |
   109Ôćĺ| **Lifecycle** | Per-skill: created by create-skill, consumed by audit/update-skill | Per-project: created by setup-forge, verified by create-skill |
   110Ôćĺ| **Janitor** | setup-forge step-03 (orphan/stale QMD collection cleanup) | setup-forge step-03 section 5b (stale path cleanup) |
   111Ôćĺ| **Availability** | Deep tier only | Forge+ and Deep tiers |
   112Ôćĺ
   113ÔćĺThese registries are orthogonal ÔÇö they never reference each other, and their janitor sections operate independently.
   114Ôćĺ
   115Ôćĺ## Pattern Examples
   116Ôćĺ
   117Ôćĺ### Example 1: Producer Registration (create-skill)
   118Ôćĺ
   119Ôćĺ**Context:** After writing all skill artifacts to disk, create-skill registers them with QMD.
   120Ôćĺ
   121Ôćĺ**Implementation:** Deep tier only. Atomic replace if collection exists:
   122Ôćĺ
   123Ôćĺ```
   124Ôćĺqmd collection remove {name}-extraction   (if exists)
   125Ôćĺqmd collection add skills/{name} --name {name}-extraction --mask "**/*"
   126Ôćĺqmd embed
   127Ôćĺ```
   128Ôćĺ
   129ÔćĺThen append/replace the registry entry in forge-tier.yaml. Failures never block the workflow. The `qmd embed` call generates vector embeddings required for `vector_search` and `deep_search`.
   130Ôćĺ
   131Ôćĺ### Example 2: Consumer Discovery (audit-skill)
   132Ôćĺ
   133Ôćĺ**Context:** Audit-skill needs temporal context for drift detection.
   134Ôćĺ
   135Ôćĺ**Implementation:** Read `qmd_collections` from forge-tier.yaml. Find entry where `skill_name` matches AND `type` is `"extraction"`. If found, query via qmd_bridge. If not found, log and continue without T2 enrichment ÔÇö not an error.
   136Ôćĺ
   137Ôćĺ### Example 3: Janitor Hygiene (setup-forge)
   138Ôćĺ
   139Ôćĺ**Context:** Setup-forge verifies QMD health on every run.
   140Ôćĺ
   141Ôćĺ**Implementation:** Cross-reference live QMD collections against registry. Classify as healthy (in both), orphaned (in QMD only), or stale (in registry only). Prompt user before removing orphans. Silently clean stale registry entries.
   142Ôćĺ
   143Ôćĺ## Related Fragments
   144Ôćĺ
   145Ôćĺ- [progressive-capability.md](progressive-capability.md) ÔÇö tier model that gates QMD availability to Deep tier
   146Ôćĺ- [skill-lifecycle.md](skill-lifecycle.md) ÔÇö end-to-end pipeline showing where producers and consumers sit
   147Ôćĺ- [provenance-tracking.md](provenance-tracking.md) ÔÇö provenance map that provides file:line context without QMD
   148Ôćĺ
   149Ôćĺ_Source: progressive QMD registry architecture implemented across setup-forge, brief-skill, create-skill, audit-skill, and update-skill workflows_
   150Ôćĺ

1Ôćĺ# Source Resolution Protocols
     2Ôćĺ
     3Ôćĺ## Remote Source Resolution (Forge/Deep only)
     4Ôćĺ
     5ÔćĺIf `source_repo` is a local path: proceed with the tier-appropriate strategy as normal.
     6Ôćĺ
     7ÔćĺIf `source_repo` is a remote URL (GitHub URL or owner/repo format) AND tier is Forge or Deep:
     8Ôćĺ
     9Ôćĺ1. **Check `git` availability:** Verify `git` is functional (`git --version`). If `git` is not available, skip to the fallback warning below.
    10Ôćĺ
    11Ôćĺ2. **Ephemeral shallow clone:** Clone the repository to a system temp path for AST access:
    12Ôćĺ
    13Ôćĺ   ```
    14Ôćĺ   temp_path = {system_temp}/skf-ephemeral-{skill-name}-{timestamp}/
    15Ôćĺ   git clone --depth 1 --branch {branch} --single-branch --filter=blob:none {source_repo} {temp_path}
    16Ôćĺ   ```
    17Ôćĺ
    18Ôćĺ   **If `include_patterns` are NOT specified:**
    19Ôćĺ
    20Ôćĺ   ```
    21Ôćĺ   git clone --depth 1 --branch {branch} --single-branch --filter=blob:none {source_repo} {temp_path}
    22Ôćĺ   ```
    23Ôćĺ
    24Ôćĺ   **If `include_patterns` ARE specified**, use sparse-checkout to limit the clone scope:
    25Ôćĺ
    26Ôćĺ   ```
    27Ôćĺ   git clone --depth 1 --branch {branch} --single-branch --filter=blob:none --sparse {source_repo} {temp_path}
    28Ôćĺ   ```
    29Ôćĺ
    30Ôćĺ   **Mode selection:** Choose sparse-checkout mode based on whether `exclude_patterns` exist:
    31Ôćĺ
    32Ôćĺ   - **No `exclude_patterns`:** Use default **cone mode** (faster). Convert `include_patterns` to directory roots.
    33Ôćĺ   - **`exclude_patterns` present:** Use **`--no-cone` mode** which supports gitignore-style negation patterns (`!` prefix). This applies both include and exclude at the git level, avoiding unnecessary blob downloads.
    34Ôćĺ
    35Ôćĺ   **Cone mode (no exclude patterns):**
    36Ôćĺ
    37Ôćĺ   **IMPORTANT:** `git sparse-checkout set` expects **directories**, not glob patterns. Convert `include_patterns` before passing them:
    38Ôćĺ
    39Ôćĺ   **Classification rule:** A pattern is an **individual file** if it contains no glob characters (`*`, `?`, `[`) AND does not end with `/`. Everything else is a glob ÔÇö strip it to its directory root (the path prefix before the first glob character or wildcard segment).
    40Ôćĺ
    41Ôćĺ   - Strip glob suffixes to directory roots (e.g., `src/core/**/*.py` Ôćĺ `src/core`, `src/api/*.ts` Ôćĺ `src/api`)
    42Ôćĺ   - Deduplicate the resulting directory list
    43Ôćĺ   - Individual files (e.g., `pyproject.toml`, `src/utils/helpers.py`) are kept as-is
    44Ôćĺ
    45Ôćĺ   **If only directory roots (no individual files):**
    46Ôćĺ
    47Ôćĺ   ```
    48Ôćĺ   git -C {temp_path} sparse-checkout set {converted_directory_roots}
    49Ôćĺ   ```
    50Ôćĺ
    51Ôćĺ   **If any individual files are present (or mixed):**
    52Ôćĺ
    53Ôćĺ   ```
    54Ôćĺ   git -C {temp_path} sparse-checkout set --skip-checks {converted_directory_roots} {individual_files}
    55Ôćĺ   ```
    56Ôćĺ
    57Ôćĺ   Example transformation:
    58Ôćĺ   ```
    59Ôćĺ   Brief include_patterns:        sparse-checkout args:
    60Ôćĺ   src/core/**/*.py          Ôćĺ    src/core          (directory root)
    61Ôćĺ   src/api/*.ts              Ôćĺ    src/api           (directory root)
    62Ôćĺ   examples/**/*.py          Ôćĺ    examples          (directory root)
    63Ôćĺ   pyproject.toml            Ôćĺ    pyproject.toml    (individual file, needs --skip-checks)
    64Ôćĺ   src/utils/helpers.py      Ôćĺ    src/utils/helpers.py (individual file, needs --skip-checks)
    65Ôćĺ   ```
    66Ôćĺ
    67Ôćĺ   **No-cone mode (exclude patterns present):**
    68Ôćĺ
    69Ôćĺ   When `exclude_patterns` exist, use `--no-cone` mode to pass both include and exclude patterns directly as gitignore-style rules:
    70Ôćĺ
    71Ôćĺ   1. Convert `include_patterns` to gitignore-style patterns. If a pattern contains no glob characters (`*`, `?`, `[`) and does not end with `/`, append `/**` to match directory contents recursively (e.g., `cognee` Ôćĺ `cognee/**`; `cognee/**` Ôćĺ kept as-is).
    72Ôćĺ   2. Convert `exclude_patterns` to negation patterns by prepending `!`. Apply the same anchoring rule (e.g., `cognee/tests` Ôćĺ `!cognee/tests/**`; `cognee/tests/**` Ôćĺ `!cognee/tests/**`; `**/test_*` Ôćĺ `!**/test_*`).
    73Ôćĺ   3. **CRITICAL:** List all include patterns BEFORE negated exclude patterns ÔÇö git processes patterns in order and a negation can only suppress a prior inclusion.
    74Ôćĺ   4. Pass to sparse-checkout ÔÇö include patterns first, then negated exclude patterns:
    75Ôćĺ
    76Ôćĺ   ```
    77Ôćĺ   git -C {temp_path} sparse-checkout set --no-cone {include_gitignore_patterns} {negated_exclude_patterns}
    78Ôćĺ   ```
    79Ôćĺ
    80Ôćĺ   Example transformation:
    81Ôćĺ   ```
    82Ôćĺ   Brief include_patterns:        Brief exclude_patterns:
    83Ôćĺ   cognee/**                      cognee/tests/**
    84Ôćĺ                                  cognee/alembic/**
    85Ôćĺ                                  **/test_*
    86Ôćĺ
    87Ôćĺ   sparse-checkout args (--no-cone):
    88Ôćĺ   'cognee/**' '!cognee/tests/**' '!cognee/alembic/**' '!**/test_*'
    89Ôćĺ   ```
    90Ôćĺ
    91Ôćĺ   **Note:** `--no-cone` mode is slower than cone mode for very large repositories but eliminates downloading excluded blobs entirely.
    92Ôćĺ
    93Ôćĺ   **Always-included root files:**
    94Ôćĺ
    95Ôćĺ   Regardless of `include_patterns`, always add these root-level version/manifest files to the sparse-checkout pattern list. These are needed for version reconciliation and must not require a fallback to `gh api`:
    96Ôćĺ
    97Ôćĺ   `pyproject.toml`, `package.json`, `Cargo.toml`, `go.mod`, `setup.py`, `setup.cfg`, `VERSION`
    98Ôćĺ
    99Ôćĺ   In cone mode, always use the `--skip-checks` command form when adding these files ÔÇö even if `include_patterns` resolved to only directory roots (which would normally use the form without `--skip-checks`). The command becomes: `git -C {temp_path} sparse-checkout set --skip-checks {directory_roots} pyproject.toml package.json Cargo.toml go.mod setup.py setup.cfg VERSION`. In no-cone mode, list them as explicit include patterns before any negation patterns. Do not flag them as extraneous inclusions during post-checkout filtering.
   100Ôćĺ
   101Ôćĺ   **Post-checkout filtering:**
   102Ôćĺ
   103Ôćĺ   After checkout, apply the original glob `include_patterns` as file-level filters when building the extraction file list ÔÇö sparse-checkout gets the right directories, glob filtering narrows to the exact files. When `--no-cone` mode was used, most exclude filtering is already done at the git level, but apply `exclude_patterns` as a final pass to catch any edge cases where gitignore pattern matching diverges from the brief's glob semantics. Always-included root files (see above) are exempt from post-checkout filtering.
   104Ôćĺ
   105Ôćĺ3. **If clone succeeds:** Update the working source path to `{temp_path}` for all subsequent AST operations in this step. Proceed with the **Forge/Deep Tier** extraction strategy below. Mark `ephemeral_clone_active = true` for cleanup.
   106Ôćĺ
   107Ôćĺ4. **If clone fails (network error, auth failure, timeout):**
   108Ôćĺ
   109Ôćĺ   ÔÜá´ŞĆ **Warn the user explicitly:**
   110Ôćĺ
   111Ôćĺ   "Ephemeral clone of `{source_repo}` failed: {error}. Degrading to source reading (T1-low) for this run. For T1 (AST-verified) confidence, clone the repository locally and update `source_repo` in your brief to the local path."
   112Ôćĺ
   113Ôćĺ   Proceed with Quick tier extraction strategy below. Note the degradation reason in context for the evidence report.
   114Ôćĺ
   115Ôćĺ**Ephemeral clone cleanup:** After extraction is complete for all files in scope (whether successful or partially failed), before presenting the Gate 2 summary (Section 6), if `ephemeral_clone_active`, delete the `{temp_path}` directory. Log: "Ephemeral source clone cleaned up." This ensures cleanup runs even if some extractions failed, as long as the step itself is still executing. **If any error halts the extraction step before Gate 2 is reached**, cleanup must still occur: attempt to delete `{temp_path}` before halting. Log the cleanup attempt regardless of success.
   116Ôćĺ
   117Ôćĺ---
   118Ôćĺ
   119Ôćĺ## Version Reconciliation (all tiers, source mode only)
   120Ôćĺ
   121Ôćĺ**If `source_type: "docs-only"`:** skip this section ÔÇö no source files exist to reconcile.
   122Ôćĺ
   123ÔćĺAfter the source path is accessible (local path from step-01, or ephemeral clone from above), check whether the source contains a version identifier and reconcile it with `brief.version`. Look for the first matching version file in the resolved source path:
   124Ôćĺ
   125Ôćĺ- Python: `pyproject.toml` (`[project] version`), `setup.py` (`version=`), `__version__` in `__init__.py`
   126Ôćĺ- JavaScript/TypeScript: `package.json` (`"version"`)
   127Ôćĺ- Rust: `Cargo.toml` (`[package] version`)
   128Ôćĺ- Go: `go.mod` (module version if tagged)
   129Ôćĺ
   130Ôćĺ**If a source version is found AND it differs from `brief.version`:**
   131Ôćĺ
   132ÔćĺÔÜá´ŞĆ Warn the user: "Brief version ({brief.version}) differs from source version ({source_version}). Using source version ({source_version})."
   133Ôćĺ
   134ÔćĺUpdate the working version in context to the source version. Record the mismatch in context for the evidence report (step-08).
   135Ôćĺ
   136Ôćĺ**If no version file is found or version cannot be extracted:** keep `brief.version` as-is. No warning needed.
   137Ôćĺ
   138Ôćĺ**If source is remote and accessed via Quick tier (gh_bridge, no local files):** attempt to read the version file via `gh_bridge.read_file(owner, repo, "{version_file}")` for the primary version file of the detected language. If the read fails, keep `brief.version`.
   139Ôćĺ

     2Ôćĺname: 'step-04-report'

     5ÔćĺtierRulesData: '../data/tier-rules.md'
     6Ôćĺ---
     7Ôćĺ
     8Ôćĺ# Step 4: Forge Status Report
     9Ôćĺ
    10Ôćĺ## STEP GOAL:
    11Ôćĺ
    12ÔćĺDisplay the forge status report with positive capability framing, report tier changes on re-run, and optionally fetch the latest agentskills.io spec if flagged.
    13Ôćĺ
    14Ôćĺ## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES (READ FIRST):
    15Ôćĺ
    16Ôćĺ### Universal Rules:
    17Ôćĺ
    18Ôćĺ- ­čôľ CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action
    19Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Execute all operations autonomously ÔÇö display report and complete
    20Ôćĺ
    21Ôćĺ### Role Reinforcement:
    22Ôćĺ
    23Ôćĺ- Ôťů You are Ferris reporting forge readiness
    24Ôćĺ- Ôťů Frame ALL capabilities positively ÔÇö describe what the tier GIVES
    25Ôćĺ- Ôťů On re-run: explicitly report what changed (upgrade/downgrade/same)
    26Ôćĺ- Ôťů Brief forge metaphor appropriate here ÔÇö this is the completion moment
    27Ôćĺ
    28Ôćĺ### Step-Specific Rules:
    29Ôćĺ
    30Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Focus only on displaying the status report
    31Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to use negative framing ("missing", "lacking", "unavailable")
    32Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to list tools that are NOT available
    33Ôćĺ- ­čĺČ Use tier capability descriptions from tier-rules.md
    34Ôćĺ
    35Ôćĺ## EXECUTION PROTOCOLS:
    36Ôćĺ
    37Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Follow the MANDATORY SEQUENCE exactly
    38Ôćĺ- ­čĺż Load tier-rules.md for capability descriptions and re-run messages
    39Ôćĺ- ­čôľ Use context from step-01 (detected_tools, calculated_tier, previous_tier)
    40Ôćĺ- ­čÜź This is the final step ÔÇö no next step to load
    41Ôćĺ
    42Ôćĺ## CONTEXT BOUNDARIES:
    43Ôćĺ
    44Ôćĺ- Available: {detected_tools}, {calculated_tier}, {previous_tier}, {tier_override} from step-01
    45Ôćĺ- Available: tool version strings from step-01
    46Ôćĺ- Available: {hygiene_result}, {hygiene_healthy}, {hygiene_orphaned_removed}, {hygiene_orphaned_kept}, {hygiene_stale_cleaned}, {ccc_registry_stale_cleaned} from step-03
    47Ôćĺ- Available: {ccc_index_result} from step-01b (values: "fresh", "created", "failed", "none")
    48Ôćĺ- Focus: report display only ÔÇö no file modifications
    49Ôćĺ- Dependencies: steps 01-03 must have completed
    50Ôćĺ
    51Ôćĺ## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
    52Ôćĺ
    53Ôćĺ**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise.
    54Ôćĺ
    55Ôćĺ### 1. Load Capability Descriptions
    56Ôćĺ
    57ÔćĺLoad and read {tierRulesData} for the tier capability descriptions and re-run messages.
    58Ôćĺ
    59Ôćĺ### 2. Display Forge Status Report
    60Ôćĺ
    61Ôćĺ**Format the report as follows:**
    62Ôćĺ
    63Ôćĺ```
    64ÔćĺÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉ
    65Ôćĺ  FORGE STATUS
    66ÔćĺÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉ
    67Ôćĺ
    68Ôćĺ  Tier:  {calculated_tier}
    69Ôćĺ  {tier capability description from tier-rules.md}
    70Ôćĺ
    71Ôćĺ  Tools Detected:
    72Ôćĺ  {for each tool that is available, show: tool name ÔÇö version}
    73Ôćĺ
    74Ôćĺ  {if hygiene_result is "completed":}
    75Ôćĺ  QMD Registry:
    76Ôćĺ  {hygiene_healthy} collection(s) healthy
    77Ôćĺ  {if hygiene_orphaned_removed > 0: {hygiene_orphaned_removed} orphaned collection(s) removed}
    78Ôćĺ  {if hygiene_orphaned_kept > 0: {hygiene_orphaned_kept} orphaned collection(s) kept}
    79Ôćĺ  {if hygiene_stale_cleaned > 0: {hygiene_stale_cleaned} stale QMD registry entry/entries cleaned}
    80Ôćĺ  {end if}
    81Ôćĺ
    82Ôćĺ  {if ccc_registry_stale_cleaned > 0:}
    83Ôćĺ  CCC Registry: {ccc_registry_stale_cleaned} stale entry/entries cleaned
    84Ôćĺ  {end if}
    85Ôćĺ
    86Ôćĺ  {if hygiene_result is "completed" and hygiene_healthy is 0:}
    87Ôćĺ  QMD Registry: empty ÔÇö collections are created automatically when you run [CS] Create Skill.
    88Ôćĺ  {end if}
    89Ôćĺ
    90Ôćĺ  {if tools.ccc is true:}
    91Ôćĺ  CCC Index:
    92Ôćĺ  {if ccc_index_result is "fresh": up to date ÔÇö semantic discovery ready}
    93Ôćĺ  {if ccc_index_result is "created": indexed this run ÔÇö semantic discovery ready}
    94Ôćĺ  {if ccc_index_result is "failed": indexing failed ÔÇö semantic discovery unavailable this session}
    95Ôćĺ  {end if}
    96Ôćĺ
    97Ôćĺ{if tier_override is active:}
    98Ôćĺ  Note: Tier override active (set in preferences.yaml)
    99Ôćĺ
   100Ôćĺ{if re-run with tier change:}
   101Ôćĺ  {appropriate upgrade/downgrade message from tier-rules.md}
   102Ôćĺ
   103Ôćĺ{if re-run with same tier:}
   104Ôćĺ  {same-tier message from tier-rules.md}
   105Ôćĺ
   106ÔćĺÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉ
   107Ôćĺ  Forge ready. {calculated_tier} tier active.
   108ÔćĺÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉÔĽÉ
   109Ôćĺ```
   110Ôćĺ
   111Ôćĺ**Tool display rules:**
   112Ôćĺ- Only show tools that ARE available with their version strings
   113Ôćĺ- Do NOT list unavailable tools
   114Ôćĺ- Do NOT show a "missing" column or section
   115Ôćĺ
   116Ôćĺ### 3. Handle --update-spec Flag (Optional)
   117Ôćĺ
   118Ôćĺ**If the user included `--update-spec` in their workflow invocation (e.g., `@Ferris SF --update-spec`):**
   119Ôćĺ- Attempt to fetch the latest agentskills.io specification schema
   120Ôćĺ- Use `gh` or `curl` to retrieve the spec
   121Ôćĺ- Store in `{forge_data_folder}/agentskills-spec.json` (or appropriate format)
   122Ôćĺ- If fetch succeeds: display "agentskills.io spec updated."
   123Ôćĺ- If fetch fails: display "Could not fetch agentskills.io spec. Existing spec (if any) unchanged."
   124Ôćĺ- Do NOT fail the workflow over this ÔÇö it is purely optional
   125Ôćĺ
   126Ôćĺ**If `--update-spec` was NOT passed:**
   127Ôćĺ- Skip silently
   128Ôćĺ
   129Ôćĺ### 4. Workflow Complete
   130Ôćĺ
   131ÔćĺThis is the final step. The setup-forge workflow is now complete.
   132Ôćĺ
   133ÔćĺNo next step to load. The workflow ends here.
   134Ôćĺ
   135Ôćĺ---
   136Ôćĺ
   137Ôćĺ## ­čÜĘ SYSTEM SUCCESS/FAILURE METRICS
   138Ôćĺ
   139Ôćĺ### Ôťů SUCCESS:
   140Ôćĺ
   141Ôćĺ- Forge status report displayed with positive capability framing
   142Ôćĺ- Only available tools shown (no "missing" lists)
   143Ôćĺ- Tier capability description matches calculated tier
   144Ôćĺ- Re-run: tier change correctly reported (or same-tier confirmed)
   145Ôćĺ- --update-spec handled if flagged (success or graceful failure)
   146Ôćĺ- Workflow completes cleanly
   147Ôćĺ
   148Ôćĺ### ÔŁî SYSTEM FAILURE:
   149Ôćĺ
   150Ôćĺ- Negative framing in report ("missing", "unavailable", "lacking")
   151Ôćĺ- Listing tools that are NOT available
   152Ôćĺ- Not using tier capability descriptions from tier-rules.md
   153Ôćĺ- Not reporting tier change on re-run
   154Ôćĺ- Attempting to load a next step (this is the final step)
   155Ôćĺ
   156Ôćĺ**Master Rule:** The report must leave users feeling confident about their forge capabilities, not anxious about what they're missing. Positive framing only.
   157Ôćĺ

     8Ôćĺ# Step 2: Write Configuration
     9Ôćĺ
    10Ôćĺ## STEP GOAL:
    11Ôćĺ
    12ÔćĺWrite the detected tool availability and calculated tier to forge-tier.yaml, create preferences.yaml with defaults if it does not exist, and ensure the forge-data/ directory is present.
    13Ôćĺ
    14Ôćĺ## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES (READ FIRST):
    15Ôćĺ
    16Ôćĺ### Universal Rules:
    17Ôćĺ
    18Ôćĺ- ­čôľ CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action
    19Ôćĺ- ­čöä CRITICAL: When loading next step, ensure entire file is read
    20Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Execute all operations autonomously ÔÇö no user interaction
    21Ôćĺ
    22Ôćĺ### Role Reinforcement:
    23Ôćĺ
    24Ôćĺ- Ôťů You are a system executor writing configuration files
    25Ôćĺ- Ôťů File operations must be precise ÔÇö correct paths, correct YAML structure
    26Ôćĺ- Ôťů Preserve user data (preferences.yaml) ÔÇö never overwrite existing
    27Ôćĺ
    28Ôćĺ### Step-Specific Rules:
    29Ôćĺ
    30Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Focus only on writing configuration files and creating directories
    31Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to re-detect tools ÔÇö use results from step-01
    32Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to overwrite existing preferences.yaml
    33Ôćĺ- ­čĺČ File write failures ARE errors ÔÇö report clearly if they occur
    34Ôćĺ
    35Ôćĺ## EXECUTION PROTOCOLS:
    36Ôćĺ
    37Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Follow the MANDATORY SEQUENCE exactly
    38Ôćĺ- ­čĺż Write files to the forger-sidecar directory
    39Ôćĺ- ­čôľ Use context from step-01 (detected_tools, calculated_tier)
    40Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to proceed if forge-tier.yaml write fails
    41Ôćĺ
    42Ôćĺ## CONTEXT BOUNDARIES:
    43Ôćĺ
    44Ôćĺ- Available: {detected_tools}, {calculated_tier}, {previous_tier} from step-01; {ccc_index_result}, {ccc_indexed_path}, {ccc_last_indexed} from step-01b
    45Ôćĺ- Focus: file I/O operations only
    46Ôćĺ- Limits: do not modify preferences.yaml if it exists
    47Ôćĺ- Dependencies: step-01 and step-01b must have completed with tool detection and CCC index results
    48Ôćĺ
    49Ôćĺ## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
    50Ôćĺ
    51Ôćĺ**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise.
    52Ôćĺ
    53Ôćĺ### 1. Write forge-tier.yaml
    54Ôćĺ
    55ÔćĺWrite to `{project-root}/_bmad/_memory/forger-sidecar/forge-tier.yaml`:
    56Ôćĺ
    57Ôćĺ```yaml
    58Ôćĺ# Ferris Sidecar: Forge Tier State
    59Ôćĺ# Written by setup-forge workflow
    60Ôćĺ
    61Ôćĺ# Tool availability (detected during [SF] Setup Forge)
    62Ôćĺtools:
    63Ôćĺ  ast_grep: {true/false from detection}
    64Ôćĺ  gh_cli: {true/false from detection}
    65Ôćĺ  qmd: {true/false from detection}
    66Ôćĺ  ccc: {true/false from detection}
    67Ôćĺ  ccc_daemon: {ccc_daemon from step-01 if available: "healthy"|"stopped"|"error", or ~}
    68Ôćĺ  security_scan: {true/false ÔÇö true when SNYK_TOKEN is set}
    69Ôćĺ
    70Ôćĺ# Capability tier (derived from tool availability)
    71Ôćĺ# Quick = no tools | Forge = + ast-grep | Forge+ = + ast-grep + ccc | Deep = + ast-grep + gh + QMD
    72Ôćĺtier: {calculated_tier}
    73Ôćĺtier_detected_at: {current ISO timestamp}
    74Ôćĺ
    75Ôćĺ# CCC semantic index state (managed by setup-forge step-01b and extraction workflows)
    76Ôćĺccc_index:
    77Ôćĺ  indexed_path: {ccc_indexed_path from step-01b, or ~}
    78Ôćĺ  last_indexed: {ccc_last_indexed from step-01b, or ~}
    79Ôćĺ  status: {ccc_index_result from step-01b: "fresh"|"created"|"none"|"failed"}
    80Ôćĺ  staleness_threshold_hours: 24
    81Ôćĺ
    82Ôćĺ# CCC index registry (tracks which source paths have been indexed for skill workflows)
    83Ôćĺ# PRESERVE existing entries on re-runs ÔÇö see Note below
    84Ôćĺccc_index_registry: {preserved from existing forge-tier.yaml, or [] if first run}
    85Ôćĺ
    86Ôćĺ# QMD collection registry (populated by create-skill, consumed by audit/update-skill)
    87Ôćĺ# PRESERVE existing entries on re-runs ÔÇö see Note below
    88Ôćĺqmd_collections: {preserved from existing forge-tier.yaml, or [] if first run}
    89Ôćĺ```
    90Ôćĺ
    91Ôćĺ**Note on re-runs:** The `qmd_collections`, `ccc_index_registry` arrays, and `staleness_threshold_hours` value must be preserved across re-runs. Before overwriting forge-tier.yaml, read these existing values and re-inject them into the new write. These values are populated by create-skill workflows or customized by users and must not be reset.
    92Ôćĺ
    93Ôćĺ**This file is ALWAYS overwritten** on every run ÔÇö it reflects current tool state.
    94Ôćĺ
    95ÔćĺIf the write fails, report the error and halt the workflow.
    96Ôćĺ
    97Ôćĺ### 2. Handle preferences.yaml
    98Ôćĺ
    99ÔćĺCheck if `{project-root}/_bmad/_memory/forger-sidecar/preferences.yaml` exists:
   100Ôćĺ
   101Ôćĺ**If it does NOT exist (first run):** Create with defaults:
   102Ôćĺ
   103Ôćĺ```yaml
   104Ôćĺ# Ferris Sidecar: User Preferences
   105Ôćĺ# Created by setup-forge workflow on first run
   106Ôćĺ# Edit this file to customize Ferris behavior
   107Ôćĺ
   108Ôćĺ# Override detected tier (set to Quick, Forge, Forge+, or Deep to force a tier)
   109Ôćĺtier_override: ~
   110Ôćĺ
   111Ôćĺ# Passive context injection (set to false to skip snippet generation and CLAUDE.md updates during export)
   112Ôćĺpassive_context: true
   113Ôćĺ
   114Ôćĺ# Reserved for future use ÔÇö these fields are not yet consumed by any workflow step
   115Ôćĺ# output_language: ~
   116Ôćĺ# skill_format_version: ~
   117Ôćĺ# citation_style: ~
   118Ôćĺ# confidence_display: ~
   119Ôćĺ```
   120Ôćĺ
   121Ôćĺ**If it DOES exist:** Do not modify. Preserve entirely.
   122Ôćĺ
   123Ôćĺ### 3. Ensure forge-data/ Directory
   124Ôćĺ
   125ÔćĺCheck if `{forge_data_folder}` directory exists:
   126Ôćĺ
   127Ôćĺ- If missing: create it
   128Ôćĺ- If exists: skip silently
   129Ôćĺ
   130Ôćĺ### 4. Auto-Proceed
   131Ôćĺ
   132Ôćĺ"**Proceeding to QMD collection hygiene...**"
   133Ôćĺ
   134Ôćĺ#### Menu Handling Logic:
   135Ôćĺ
   136Ôćĺ- After all file operations complete successfully, immediately load, read entire file, then execute {nextStepFile}
   137Ôćĺ
   138Ôćĺ#### EXECUTION RULES:
   139Ôćĺ
   140Ôćĺ- This is an auto-proceed step with no user choices
   141Ôćĺ- Proceed directly to next step after configuration is written
   142Ôćĺ
   143Ôćĺ## CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE
   144Ôćĺ
   145ÔćĺONLY WHEN forge-tier.yaml has been written successfully and preferences.yaml exists (created or pre-existing) will you load and read fully `{nextStepFile}` to execute the auto-index step.

   149Ôćĺ## ­čÜĘ SYSTEM SUCCESS/FAILURE METRICS
   150Ôćĺ
   151Ôćĺ### Ôťů SUCCESS:
   152Ôćĺ
   153Ôćĺ- forge-tier.yaml written with accurate tool booleans (including ccc), tier, timestamp, ccc_index state, and preserved qmd_collections/ccc_index_registry arrays
   154Ôćĺ- preferences.yaml exists (created with defaults on first run, preserved on re-run)
