1Ôćĺ# Confidence Tiers
     2Ôćĺ
     3Ôćĺ## Principle
     4Ôćĺ
     5ÔćĺEvery extracted claim carries a confidence tier label ÔÇö T1, T1-low, T2, or T3 ÔÇö that tells consuming agents how much to trust the information. The tier determines citation format, affects scoring weights, and governs how claims interact during updates and audits.
     6Ôćĺ
     7Ôćĺ## Rationale
     8Ôćĺ
     9ÔćĺNot all extracted information has equal reliability. An AST-parsed function signature is structurally verified; a type inferred from pattern matching might be wrong; a QMD-enriched usage note adds context but not structural proof; an external doc reference is only as current as its source.
    10Ôćĺ
    11ÔćĺWithout confidence tiers:
    12Ôćĺ- All claims appear equally trustworthy regardless of extraction method
    13Ôćĺ- Agents cannot distinguish verified signatures from inferred patterns
    14Ôćĺ- Audit and update workflows have no basis for prioritizing drift findings
    15Ôćĺ
    16ÔćĺWith confidence tiers:
    17Ôćĺ- Each claim transparently declares its evidence strength
    18Ôćĺ- Scoring weights adjust based on the distribution of tiers
    19Ôćĺ- Update workflows know which claims to re-verify first
    20Ôćĺ
    21Ôćĺ## Tier Definitions
    22Ôćĺ
    23Ôćĺ| Tier | Label | Source | Extraction Method | Available At |
    24Ôćĺ| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
    25Ôćĺ| T1 | AST-verified | Source code | ast-grep structural parsing | Forge, Forge+, Deep |
    26Ôćĺ| T1-low | Source-read | Source code | Pattern matching, file reading | Quick, Forge, Forge+, Deep |
    27Ôćĺ| T2 | QMD-enriched | Knowledge base | QMD semantic search + synthesis | Deep |
    28Ôćĺ| T3 | External reference | Docs, URLs | External documentation lookup | All tiers |
    29Ôćĺ
    30Ôćĺ## T2 Sub-Types
    31Ôćĺ
    32ÔćĺT2 enrichment content maps to temporal scopes that control where annotations surface:
    33Ôćĺ
    34Ôćĺ| Sub-type | Content | Surface Level |
    35Ôćĺ| --- | --- | --- |
    36Ôćĺ| T2-past | Closed issues, merged PRs, changelogs ÔÇö historical context explaining why the API looks the way it does | `references/` (full temporal context) |
    37Ôćĺ| T2-future | Open PRs, deprecation warnings, RFCs ÔÇö forward-looking context about upcoming changes | `SKILL.md` Section 4b (Migration & Deprecation Warnings) + `references/` |
    38Ôćĺ
    39ÔćĺThe T2-past/T2-future distinction is operationally significant: T2-future annotations trigger the presence of a Migration & Deprecation Warnings section in SKILL.md, while T2-past annotations live in progressive disclosure reference files only.
    40Ôćĺ
    41Ôćĺ## Citation Formats
    42Ôćĺ
    43ÔćĺEach tier has a defined citation format used consistently across all skill output:
    44Ôćĺ
    45Ôćĺ```
    46ÔćĺT1:     [AST:filepath:Lnn]          ÔÇö AST-verified, line-level precision
    47ÔćĺT1-low: [SRC:filepath:Lnn]          ÔÇö source-read, line-level but unverified structure
    48ÔćĺT2:     [QMD:collection:document]   ÔÇö QMD knowledge enrichment
    49ÔćĺT3:     [EXT:url-or-reference]      ÔÇö external documentation reference
    50Ôćĺ```
    51Ôćĺ
    52Ôćĺ## Pattern Examples
    53Ôćĺ
    54Ôćĺ### Example 1: T1 ÔÇö AST-Verified Export
    55Ôćĺ
    56Ôćĺ**Context:** ast-grep parses a TypeScript file and extracts a function export with full signature.
    57Ôćĺ
    58Ôćĺ**Implementation:**
    59Ôćĺ```markdown
    60Ôćĺ## `createServer(options: ServerOptions): Server`
    61Ôćĺ
    62ÔćĺCreates and configures an HTTP server instance.
    63Ôćĺ
    64Ôćĺ**Parameters:**
    65Ôćĺ| Name | Type | Required | Default |
    66Ôćĺ| --- | --- | --- | --- |
    67Ôćĺ| options | `ServerOptions` | yes | ÔÇö |
    68Ôćĺ
    69Ôćĺ[AST:src/server.ts:L23]
    70Ôćĺ```
    71Ôćĺ
    72Ôćĺ**Key Points:**
    73Ôćĺ- Signature extracted by AST ÔÇö structurally guaranteed to match source
    74Ôćĺ- Line number is exact, not approximate
    75Ôćĺ- Only available when ast-grep is present (Forge/Deep tier)
    76Ôćĺ
    77Ôćĺ### Example 2: T1-low ÔÇö Source-Read Extraction
    78Ôćĺ
    79Ôćĺ**Context:** Quick tier extraction reads source files without AST parsing.
    80Ôćĺ
    81Ôćĺ**Implementation:**
    82Ôćĺ```markdown
    83Ôćĺ## `createServer(options)`
    84Ôćĺ
    85ÔćĺCreates and configures an HTTP server instance.
    86Ôćĺ
    87Ôćĺ**Parameters:**
    88Ôćĺ| Name | Type | Required | Default |
    89Ôćĺ| --- | --- | --- | --- |
    90Ôćĺ| options | `object` | yes | ÔÇö |
    91Ôćĺ
    92Ôćĺ[SRC:src/server.ts:L23] ÔÇö type inferred from usage, not structurally verified
    93Ôćĺ```
    94Ôćĺ
    95Ôćĺ**Key Points:**
    96Ôćĺ- Same function, but parameter type is `object` not `ServerOptions` ÔÇö pattern matching missed the type alias
    97Ôćĺ- Citation explicitly notes the inference limitation
    98Ôćĺ- Still useful ÔÇö location is correct, signature is close
    99Ôćĺ
   100Ôćĺ### Example 3: T2 ÔÇö QMD Enrichment
   101Ôćĺ
   102Ôćĺ**Context:** Deep tier enriches an extracted API with usage patterns from QMD knowledge base.
   103Ôćĺ
   104Ôćĺ**Implementation:**
   105Ôćĺ```markdown
   106Ôćĺ### Usage Context
   107Ôćĺ
   108Ôćĺ`createServer` is typically used with `loadMiddleware()` in application bootstrap.
   109ÔćĺCommon pattern: create server first, then attach middleware chain before calling `.listen()`.
   110Ôćĺ
   111Ôćĺ[QMD:project-docs:architecture-overview]
   112Ôćĺ```
   113Ôćĺ
   114Ôćĺ**Key Points:**
   115Ôćĺ- Enrichment adds context, not structural claims
   116Ôćĺ- QMD citations reference collection and document, not line numbers
   117Ôćĺ- T2 content appears in enrichment sections, not in API signatures
   118Ôćĺ
   119Ôćĺ### Example 4: Tier Interaction During Updates
   120Ôćĺ
   121Ôćĺ**Context:** An update-skill workflow detects that a T1-low claim now contradicts fresh T1 extraction.
   122Ôćĺ
   123Ôćĺ**Implementation:** The merge algorithm applies tier precedence:
   124Ôćĺ1. T1 always overrides T1-low for the same export
   125Ôćĺ2. T2 enrichments are preserved unless the underlying T1/T1-low claim changed
   126Ôćĺ3. T3 references are flagged for manual review if the API they describe changed
   127Ôćĺ
   128Ôćĺ**Key Points:**
   129Ôćĺ- Higher confidence tiers take precedence in merge conflicts
   130Ôćĺ- T2 enrichments are additive ÔÇö they don't conflict with structural claims
   131Ôćĺ- T3 references may go stale and are flagged, not auto-removed
   132Ôćĺ
   133Ôćĺ## Confidence Distribution in Metadata
   134Ôćĺ
   135ÔćĺEvery generated skill includes a confidence distribution in `metadata.json`:
   136Ôćĺ
   137Ôćĺ```json
   138Ôćĺ{
   139Ôćĺ  "confidence_distribution": {
   140Ôćĺ    "t1": 42,
   141Ôćĺ    "t1_low": 5,
   142Ôćĺ    "t2": 12,
   143Ôćĺ    "t3": 3
   144Ôćĺ  },
   145Ôćĺ  "confidence_tier": "Deep"  // valid values: "Quick", "Forge", "Forge+", "Deep"
   146Ôćĺ}
   147Ôćĺ```
   148Ôćĺ
   149ÔćĺThis distribution feeds into test-skill scoring ÔÇö skills with higher T1 ratios score better on signature accuracy.
   150Ôćĺ
   151Ôćĺ## Anti-Patterns
   152Ôćĺ
   153Ôćĺ- Omitting the confidence tier from a citation ÔÇö every citation must declare its level
   154Ôćĺ- Using T1 format (`[AST:...]`) when ast-grep was not actually used for that extraction
   155Ôćĺ- Treating T1-low and T1 as equivalent during scoring ÔÇö they have different weight impacts
   156Ôćĺ- Adding T2 enrichment without a QMD citation ÔÇö enrichment without provenance is hallucination
   157Ôćĺ
   158Ôćĺ## Related Fragments
   159Ôćĺ
   160Ôćĺ- [zero-hallucination.md](zero-hallucination.md) ÔÇö the principle that motivates tiered confidence
   161Ôćĺ- [provenance-tracking.md](provenance-tracking.md) ÔÇö how citations are recorded in provenance-map.json
   162Ôćĺ- [progressive-capability.md](progressive-capability.md) ÔÇö how forge tier determines available confidence levels
   163Ôćĺ
   164Ôćĺ_Source: consolidated from extraction-patterns.md, create-skill steps 03/05, and scoring-rules.md_
   165Ôćĺ

1Ôćĺ# Tier Calculation Rules
     2Ôćĺ
     3Ôćĺ## Tool Detection Commands
     4Ôćĺ
     5Ôćĺ| Tool | Verification Command | What Confirms Availability |
     6Ôćĺ|------|---------------------|---------------------------|
     7Ôćĺ| ast-grep | `ast-grep --version` | Returns version string without error |
     8Ôćĺ| gh | `gh --version` | Returns version string without error |
     9Ôćĺ| qmd | `qmd status` | Returns status indicating initialized and operational |
    10Ôćĺ| ccc | Step A: `ccc --help` Step B: `ccc doctor` | Step A: exits 0 (binary exists). Step B: daemon running, version string, model check OK |
    11Ôćĺ
    12Ôćĺ**Important:** Use verification commands, not existence checks (`which`, `command -v`). A tool must be functional, not just present on PATH. For daemon-based tools (ccc), verify both binary existence and daemon health.
    13Ôćĺ
    14Ôćĺ## Tier Calculation
    15Ôćĺ
    16Ôćĺ| Priority | Tier | Required Tools | Rule |
    17Ôćĺ|----------|------|---------------|------|
    18Ôćĺ| 1 (highest) | Deep | ast-grep + gh + qmd | All three core tools available and functional |
    19Ôćĺ| 2 | Forge+ | ast-grep + ccc | ast-grep AND ccc available, regardless of gh/qmd |
    20Ôćĺ| 3 | Forge | ast-grep | ast-grep available, without ccc/gh/qmd |
    21Ôćĺ| 4 (default) | Quick | None | Default when no tools are available |
    22Ôćĺ
    23Ôćĺ**Evaluation order:** Check Deep first, then Forge+, then Forge. The first match wins. This ensures Deep always takes priority when all tools are present.
    24Ôćĺ
    25Ôćĺ**Override:** If `tier_override` is set in preferences.yaml, use that value instead of calculated tier.
    26Ôćĺ
    27Ôćĺ**Edge cases:**
    28Ôćĺ- gh available but no ast-grep Ôćĺ Quick (gh alone doesn't unlock Forge)
    29Ôćĺ- ast-grep + gh but no qmd Ôćĺ Forge (qmd required for Deep)
    30Ôćĺ- ast-grep + qmd but no gh Ôćĺ Forge (gh required for Deep)
    31Ôćĺ- ccc available but no ast-grep Ôćĺ Quick (ccc alone doesn't unlock Forge+)
    32Ôćĺ- ast-grep + ccc + gh but no qmd Ôćĺ Forge+ (qmd required for Deep)
    33Ôćĺ- ast-grep + gh + qmd + ccc Ôćĺ Deep (Deep subsumes Forge+; ccc enhances transparently)
    34Ôćĺ
    35Ôćĺ## Tier Capability Descriptions
    36Ôćĺ
    37ÔćĺUse these for positive-framing in the report step. Describe what the tier GIVES, never what it lacks.
    38Ôćĺ
    39Ôćĺ### Quick Tier
    40Ôćĺ"Quick tier active. You have fast, template-driven skill generation with package-name resolution. Perfect for getting started quickly."
    41Ôćĺ
    42Ôćĺ### Forge Tier
    43Ôćĺ"Forge tier active. You have AST-backed structural code analysis with line-level citations, plus template-driven generation. Every skill instruction traces to verified source code."
    44Ôćĺ
    45Ôćĺ### Forge+ Tier
    46Ôćĺ"Forge+ tier active. Semantic-guided precision compilation ÔÇö cocoindex-code maps the codebase semantically before AST extraction runs. Every skill begins with a ranked discovery pass that surfaces the most relevant source regions, then AST-backed verification gives each export its line-level citation."
    47Ôćĺ
    48Ôćĺ### Deep Tier
    49Ôćĺ"Deep tier active. Full capability unlocked ÔÇö AST-backed code analysis, GitHub repository exploration, and QMD knowledge search with cross-repository synthesis. Maximum provenance and intelligence."
    50Ôćĺ
    51Ôćĺ## Re-run Tier Change Messages
    52Ôćĺ
    53Ôćĺ### Upgrade
    54Ôćĺ"Tier upgraded from {previous} to {current}. {newly available tool(s)} now detected ÔÇö expanded capabilities unlocked."
    55Ôćĺ
    56Ôćĺ### Downgrade
    57Ôćĺ"Tier changed from {previous} to {current}. {tool} no longer detected. Run the tool's installation to restore capabilities."
    58Ôćĺ
    59Ôćĺ### Same
    60Ôćĺ"Tier unchanged: {current}. All previously detected tools confirmed."
    61Ôćĺ

1Ôćĺ# Extraction Patterns: Tracing and File-Level Extraction
     2Ôćĺ
     3ÔćĺThis file covers re-export tracing protocols and script/asset file-level extraction patterns. For core tier strategies and AST extraction protocol, see `extraction-patterns.md`.
     4Ôćĺ
     5Ôćĺ---
     6Ôćĺ
     7Ôćĺ## Re-Export Tracing
     8Ôćĺ
     9ÔćĺAfter initial AST extraction, some top-level exports may resolve to **module imports** rather than direct function definitions. This is common in Python libraries that use `__init__.py` re-exports for a clean public API.
    10Ôćĺ
    11Ôćĺ**Detection heuristic:** For each top-level export from `__init__.py` (or equivalent entry point), check if the import path resolves to a directory (contains `__init__.py`) rather than a `.py` file with a matching `def` or `class`. If the initial AST scan found no function/class definition for a known public export, it is likely a module re-export.
    12Ôćĺ
    13Ôćĺ**Tracing protocol:**
    14Ôćĺ
    15Ôćĺ1. Read the entry point file (e.g., `{package}/__init__.py`) and extract all `from .X import Y` statements
    16Ôćĺ2. For each import where Y was NOT found by the initial AST scan:
    17Ôćĺ   - Check if the import path resolves to a directory (e.g., `{package}/api/v1/delete/` exists with `__init__.py`)
    18Ôćĺ   - If directory: read its `__init__.py` to find the actual re-exported symbol
    19Ôćĺ   - **Handle aliases:** Check for `from .module import A as B` patterns in the intermediate `__init__.py`. If the parent imports `B`, trace through to `A` in `.module`. If the parent imports `A` but the `__init__.py` only exports it as `B` (via `from .module import A as B`), match by original name `A` and note the alias
    20Ôćĺ   - Trace the symbol to its definition file and run AST extraction on that file
    21Ôćĺ3. Cite the actual definition location: `[AST:{definition_file}:L{line}]`
    22Ôćĺ
    23Ôćĺ**Examples:**
    24Ôćĺ
    25Ôćĺ```python
    26Ôćĺ# Module re-export ÔÇö follow required
    27Ôćĺfrom .api.v1.delete import delete    # delete/ is a directory Ôćĺ read delete/__init__.py
    28Ôćĺ
    29Ôćĺ# Direct function import ÔÇö no follow needed
    30Ôćĺfrom .api.v1.add.add import add      # add.py exists with def add()
    31Ôćĺ
    32Ôćĺ# Aliased re-export ÔÇö follow through alias
    33Ôćĺ# In cognee/api/v1/visualize/__init__.py:
    34Ôćĺ#   from .start_visualization_server import visualization_server
    35Ôćĺ# In cognee/__init__.py:
    36Ôćĺ#   from .api.v1.visualize import start_visualization_server
    37Ôćĺ# Ôćĺ Match start_visualization_server against both definition names AND alias names
    38Ôćĺ#   in the intermediate __init__.py to resolve the chain
    39Ôćĺ```
    40Ôćĺ
    41Ôćĺ**Unresolvable imports:** If the import statement is a star-import (`from .X import *`) or a conditional import (`try`/`except`), the symbol cannot be reliably traced via this protocol. Record it with `[SRC:{package}/__init__.py:L{line}]` (T1-low) and a note: "star/conditional import ÔÇö manual trace required."
    42Ôćĺ
    43Ôćĺ**Scope limit:** Only trace re-exports for symbols listed in the top-level entry point's public API. Do not recursively trace beyond one level of `__init__.py` indirection. If a re-export cannot be resolved after one level, record it with a `[SRC:{package}/__init__.py:L{line}]` citation (T1-low) from the import statement itself.
    44Ôćĺ
    45Ôćĺ**Other languages:** JS/TS barrel files (`index.ts` with `export { X } from './module'`) follow the same principle ÔÇö trace the re-export to the definition file. Rust `pub use` and Go package-level re-exports are less common but follow the same heuristic when encountered.
    46Ôćĺ
    47Ôćĺ---
    48Ôćĺ
    49Ôćĺ## Script/Asset Extraction Patterns
    50Ôćĺ
    51ÔćĺScripts and assets are file-level artifacts, not code exports. They follow the **file-copy extraction method** ÔÇö detected in source, copied with provenance citations.
    52Ôćĺ
    53Ôćĺ### Detection Heuristics
    54Ôćĺ
    55Ôćĺ**Script directories:** `scripts/`, `bin/`, `tools/`, `cli/`
    56Ôćĺ**Asset directories:** `assets/`, `templates/`, `schemas/`, `configs/`, `examples/`
    57Ôćĺ
    58Ôćĺ**Script file signals:**
    59Ôćĺ
    60Ôćĺ| Signal | Strength | Pattern |
    61Ôćĺ|--------|----------|---------|
    62Ôćĺ| Entry point declaration | Strong | `package.json` `bin` field, Cargo.toml `[[bin]]`, pyproject.toml `[project.scripts]` |
    63Ôćĺ| Shebang + executable | Strong | `#!/bin/bash`, `#!/usr/bin/env python`, `#!/usr/bin/env node` |
    64Ôćĺ| CLI argument parser | Moderate | `argparse`, `yargs`, `commander`, `cobra`, `clap` imports in file |
    65Ôćĺ| Directory convention | Moderate | File in `scripts/`, `bin/`, `tools/` directory |
    66Ôćĺ| CI/CD reference | Moderate | Script referenced in `.github/workflows/*.yml` |
    67Ôćĺ
    68Ôćĺ**Asset file signals:**
    69Ôćĺ
    70Ôćĺ| Signal | Strength | Pattern |
    71Ôćĺ|--------|----------|---------|
    72Ôćĺ| JSON Schema | Strong | `*.schema.json`, file contains `"$schema"` key |
    73Ôćĺ| Config template | Strong | `*.example`, `*.template.*`, `*.sample` extension |
    74Ôćĺ| Official example | Moderate | File in `examples/` directory, referenced in README |
    75Ôćĺ| OpenAPI/GraphQL | Moderate | `openapi.json`, `*.graphql`, `swagger.yaml` |
    76Ôćĺ| Design tokens | Weak | `tokens.json`, `theme.json` in `assets/` |
    77Ôćĺ
    78Ôćĺ### Inclusion Rules
    79Ôćĺ
    80Ôćĺ- Only include files within brief's `scope.include` patterns (or auto-detected directories)
    81Ôćĺ- Exclude binary files (check extension: `.so`, `.dll`, `.jar`, `.wasm`, `.exe`)
    82Ôćĺ- Exclude generated files (`dist/`, `build/`, `.webpack/` output)
    83Ôćĺ- Exclude vendored/third-party files
    84Ôćĺ- Flag files >500 lines for user confirmation (may be too large for skill package)
    85Ôćĺ- If `scripts_intent: "none"` or `assets_intent: "none"` in brief, skip that category
    86Ôćĺ
    87Ôćĺ### Provenance and Hashing
    88Ôćĺ
    89ÔćĺEach extracted file receives:
    90Ôćĺ- Citation: `[SRC:{source_path}:L1]` (T1-low ÔÇö file verified to exist but content not AST-analyzed)
    91Ôćĺ- Content hash: SHA-256 of file content (for drift detection in audit-skill)
    92Ôćĺ- Extraction method: `"file-copy"` (distinct from `"ast_bridge"` for code exports)
    93Ôćĺ
    94Ôćĺ### Inventory Structure
    95Ôćĺ
    96Ôćĺ**Script inventory entry:**
    97Ôćĺ- `name`: filename (e.g., `validate-config.sh`)
    98Ôćĺ- `source_file`: path relative to source root
    99Ôćĺ- `purpose`: extracted from file header comments or README reference (if none found, use filename)
   100Ôćĺ- `language`: detected from extension or shebang
   101Ôćĺ- `content_hash`: SHA-256
   102Ôćĺ- `confidence`: T1-low
   103Ôćĺ
   104Ôćĺ**Asset inventory entry:**
   105Ôćĺ- `name`: filename (e.g., `config-schema.json`)
   106Ôćĺ- `source_file`: path relative to source root
   107Ôćĺ- `purpose`: extracted from file header or schema `title`/`description` field
   108Ôćĺ- `type`: `template`, `schema`, `config`, `example`
   109Ôćĺ- `content_hash`: SHA-256
   110Ôćĺ- `confidence`: T1-low
   111Ôćĺ

1Ôćĺ# SKILL.md Compilation Assembly Rules
     2Ôćĺ
     3Ôćĺ## Frontmatter (REQUIRED ÔÇö agentskills.io compliance)
     4Ôćĺ
     5Ôćĺ```yaml
     6Ôćĺ---
     7Ôćĺname: {brief.name}
     8Ôćĺdescription: >
     9Ôćĺ  {Trigger-optimized description from brief and extraction data.
    10Ôćĺ  Include what it does, when to use it, and what NOT to use it for.
    11Ôćĺ  1-1024 chars, optimized for agent discovery.}
    12Ôćĺ---
    13Ôćĺ```
    14Ôćĺ
    15Ôćĺ**Frontmatter rules:**
    16Ôćĺ
    17Ôćĺ- `name`: lowercase alphanumeric + hyphens only, must match the skill output directory name. Prefer gerund form (`processing-pdfs`, `analyzing-spreadsheets`) for clarity.
    18Ôćĺ- `description`: non-empty, max 1024 chars, optimized for agent discovery. **MUST use third-person voice** ("Processes Excel files..." not "I can help you..." or "You can use this to..."). Inconsistent point-of-view causes discovery problems since the description is injected into the system prompt.
    19Ôćĺ- Only `name` and `description` in frontmatter ÔÇö `version` and `author` go in metadata.json
    20Ôćĺ- No other frontmatter fields for standard skills (only `name`, `description`, `license`, `compatibility`, `metadata`, `allowed-tools` are permitted by spec)
    21Ôćĺ
    22Ôćĺ## Two-Tier Assembly
    23Ôćĺ
    24Ôćĺ**CRITICAL: Two-tier assembly.** SKILL.md must retain actionable inline content that survives `split-body` extraction. Assemble Tier 1 sections first (always inline), then Tier 2 sections (reference-eligible, may be extracted by split-body).
    25Ôćĺ
    26Ôćĺ### Tier 1 ÔÇö Always Inline (must survive split-body)
    27Ôćĺ
    28ÔćĺThese sections form the essential standalone body. Target: **under 300 lines total** for Tier 1. An agent loading only SKILL.md (without references) must get enough to act.
    29Ôćĺ
    30Ôćĺ**Section 1 ÔÇö Overview (~10 lines):**
    31Ôćĺ- 1-line summary of what the library does
    32Ôćĺ- Source repo, version, branch
    33Ôćĺ- Forge tier used for compilation
    34Ôćĺ- Export count and confidence summary
    35Ôćĺ
    36Ôćĺ**Section 2 ÔÇö Quick Start (~30 lines):**
    37Ôćĺ- Select the 3-5 most commonly used functions (by import frequency or documentation prominence)
    38Ôćĺ- One runnable code example showing a typical end-to-end flow (e.g., `add Ôćĺ process Ôćĺ search`)
    39Ôćĺ- Minimal usage examples ÔÇö ONLY from source tests or official docs
    40Ôćĺ- If no examples exist in source, show signature-only quick start
    41Ôćĺ- Provenance citation for each function
    42Ôćĺ
    43Ôćĺ**Section 3 ÔÇö Common Workflows (~30 lines):**
    44Ôćĺ- 4-5 patterns showing typical function call sequences
    45Ôćĺ- Each pattern: 1-line bold description + function call chain with key params
    46Ôćĺ- Focus on the most common developer tasks, not exhaustive coverage
    47Ôćĺ- Format example:
    48Ôćĺ  ```
    49Ôćĺ  **Add and process data:**
    50Ôćĺ  `await cognee.add(data) Ôćĺ await cognee.cognify() Ôćĺ await cognee.search(query)`
    51Ôćĺ  ```
    52Ôćĺ
    53Ôćĺ**Section 4 ÔÇö Key API Summary (~20 lines):**
    54Ôćĺ- Table of top 10-15 functions: name, purpose, key parameters
    55Ôćĺ- One row per function ÔÇö no full signatures, just enough for discovery
    56Ôćĺ- Provenance citation per function
    57Ôćĺ
    58Ôćĺ**Section 4b ÔÇö Migration & Deprecation Warnings (~10 lines, Deep tier only):**
    59Ôćĺ- Only populated when step-04 enrichment produced **T2-future** annotations (deprecation warnings, breaking changes, planned renames)
    60Ôćĺ- List each warning as a single-line bullet: function name, what changed or will change, source citation
    61Ôćĺ- Max 10 lines ÔÇö just the actionable warnings, not full context
    62Ôćĺ- Link to Tier 2 Full API Reference for details: "See Full API Reference for migration details."
    63Ôćĺ- **Skip entirely** for Quick/Forge tiers or when no T2-future annotations exist ÔÇö do not emit an empty section
    64Ôćĺ- This section survives split-body, ensuring agents always see critical migration context
    65Ôćĺ
    66Ôćĺ**Section 5 ÔÇö Key Types (~20 lines):**
    67Ôćĺ- Most important enum/type definitions inline (e.g., SearchType values, config options)
    68Ôćĺ- Only types that appear in Quick Start or Common Workflows
    69Ôćĺ- Full type details go in Tier 2
    70Ôćĺ
    71Ôćĺ**Section 6 ÔÇö Architecture at a Glance (~10 lines):**
    72Ôćĺ- Bullet list of major subsystem categories (e.g., "Graph DBs: Neo4j, Kuzu, Neptune")
    73Ôćĺ- Adapter/driver overview ÔÇö what's available, not how it works
    74Ôćĺ- Skip for Quick tier or small libraries with < 5 modules
    75Ôćĺ
    76Ôćĺ**Section 7 ÔÇö CLI (~10 lines, if applicable):**
    77Ôćĺ- Basic CLI commands if the library has a CLI interface
    78Ôćĺ- Skip if no CLI exists
    79Ôćĺ
    80Ôćĺ**Section 7b ÔÇö Scripts & Assets (~10 lines, if applicable):**
    81Ôćĺ- Manifest table of included scripts with filename, one-line purpose, and provenance citation
    82Ôćĺ- Manifest table of included assets with filename, one-line purpose, and provenance citation
    83Ôćĺ- Each entry: `scripts/{filename}` or `assets/{filename}`, purpose, `[SRC:{source_path}:L1]`
    84Ôćĺ- Include a note: "Load scripts from `scripts/` and assets from `assets/` when directed by the instructions above."
    85Ôćĺ- **Skip entirely** when no scripts or assets detected in extraction inventory ÔÇö do not emit an empty section
    86Ôćĺ- Like Sections 4b and 7, parsers must treat this section as optional
    87Ôćĺ
    88Ôćĺ**Section 8 ÔÇö Manual Sections:**
    89Ôćĺ- Seed empty `<!-- [MANUAL] -->` markers:
    90Ôćĺ```markdown
    91Ôćĺ<!-- [MANUAL:additional-notes] -->
    92Ôćĺ<!-- Add custom notes here. This section is preserved during skill updates. -->
    93Ôćĺ<!-- [/MANUAL:additional-notes] -->
    94Ôćĺ```
    95Ôćĺ- Place after Quick Start and after Key API Summary sections
    96Ôćĺ
    97Ôćĺ### Tier 2 ÔÇö Reference-Eligible (can be extracted by split-body)
    98Ôćĺ
    99ÔćĺAssemble Sections 9-11 (Full API Reference, Full Type Definitions, Full Integration Patterns) as defined in the skill-sections data file. These contain full detail and are split into `references/` when the body exceeds 500 lines. Include T2 annotations from enrichment in the Full API Reference (Deep tier only).
   100Ôćĺ
   101Ôćĺ**CRITICAL ÔÇö Tier 2 differentiation from Tier 1:** Tier 2 Full API Reference must contain content that is NOT present in Tier 1's Key API Summary. Specifically:
   102Ôćĺ
   103Ôćĺ- **Full parameter tables** with types, defaults, and required/optional flags (Tier 1 only lists key params)
   104Ôćĺ- **Return value details** including structure, types, and error conditions
   105Ôćĺ- **T2 temporal annotations** ÔÇö migration notes, deprecation details, breaking change context (Deep tier)
   106Ôćĺ- **Usage examples** from source tests or documentation (Tier 1 has signature-only references)
   107Ôćĺ- **Edge cases and constraints** ÔÇö parameter validation rules, size limits, behavioral notes
   108Ôćĺ
   109ÔćĺDo NOT repeat Tier 1's name/purpose/key-params table format in Tier 2. Tier 2 is a deep reference, not a reformatted summary. This distinction prevents conciseness scorers from flagging the two-tier design as redundancy.
   110Ôćĺ
   111Ôćĺ### Assembly Rules
   112Ôćĺ
   113Ôćĺ1. Assemble all Tier 1 sections first ÔÇö these form the essential standalone body
   114Ôćĺ2. Assemble all Tier 2 sections after ÔÇö these are progressive disclosure detail
   115Ôćĺ3. Tier 1 content MUST be under 300 lines (excluding frontmatter)
   116Ôćĺ4. If Tier 1 alone exceeds 300 lines, reduce Key API Summary and Architecture at a Glance
   117Ôćĺ5. Tier 1 sections are kept short enough that `split-body` targets the larger Tier 2 sections (`## Full ...` headings) instead
   118Ôćĺ6. After split-body, SKILL.md must still contain all Tier 1 sections with actionable content
   119Ôćĺ
   120Ôćĺ### Reference File Rules
   121Ôćĺ
   122Ôćĺ- **Table of contents required** for any reference file exceeding 100 lines ÔÇö include a `## Contents` section at the top listing all sub-sections. This ensures agents can see the full scope even when previewing with partial reads.
   123Ôćĺ- One file per major function group or type ÔÇö group by module, file, or functional area
   124Ôćĺ- Name files descriptively: `form_validation_rules.md`, not `doc2.md`
   125Ôćĺ
   126Ôćĺ### Content Quality Rules
   127Ôćĺ
   128ÔćĺThese rules apply to all content assembled in SKILL.md and reference files.
   129Ôćĺ
   130Ôćĺ**Degrees of freedom:** Match instruction specificity to the task's fragility and variability:
   131Ôćĺ- **High freedom** (text guidance): When multiple approaches are valid and context determines the best one. Example: code review patterns, architecture suggestions.
   132Ôćĺ- **Medium freedom** (pseudocode/parameterized scripts): When a preferred pattern exists but variation is acceptable. Example: configuration templates, report generation.
   133Ôćĺ- **Low freedom** (exact scripts, no parameters): When operations are fragile and consistency is critical. Example: database migrations, deployment sequences. Use "Run exactly this" language.
   134Ôćĺ
   135Ôćĺ**Consistent terminology:** Choose one term per concept and use it throughout the skill. Do not mix synonyms (e.g., "API endpoint" vs "URL" vs "route", or "field" vs "box" vs "element"). Consistency helps agents understand and follow instructions deterministically.
   136Ôćĺ
   137Ôćĺ**Avoid time-sensitive information:** Do not include date-conditional instructions ("If before August 2025, use the old API"). Instead, document the current method and place deprecated patterns in a collapsible "Old patterns" section with the deprecation date.
   138Ôćĺ
   139Ôćĺ**Template and examples patterns:**
   140Ôćĺ- **For strict requirements** (API responses, data formats): Provide an exact template with "ALWAYS use this exact structure" language.
   141Ôćĺ- **For flexible guidance** (reports, analysis): Provide a sensible default template with "Adjust sections as needed" language.
   142Ôćĺ- **Input/output examples:** When output quality depends on seeing examples and concrete pairs exist in source tests or official docs, include 2-3 input/output pairs sourced from those tests or docs. Examples help agents understand desired style and detail more clearly than descriptions alone. If no examples exist in source, note the gap rather than fabricating pairs ÔÇö zero hallucination applies.
   143Ôćĺ
   144Ôćĺ**Workflow checklist pattern:** When a skill includes multi-step workflows, provide a copy-paste checklist that agents can track progress against:
   145Ôćĺ```markdown
   146ÔćĺCopy this checklist and track your progress:
   147Ôćĺ- [ ] Step 1: {action}
   148Ôćĺ- [ ] Step 2: {action}
   149Ôćĺ```
   150Ôćĺ
   151Ôćĺ**Verifiable intermediate outputs:** For skills involving batch operations, destructive changes, or complex validation, recommend the plan-validate-execute pattern: create a structured plan file (e.g., `changes.json`), validate it with a script, then execute. This catches errors before changes are applied.
   152Ôćĺ

1Ôćĺ---
     2Ôćĺname: 'step-01-detect-and-tier'
     3Ôćĺdescription: 'Detect available tools and determine forge capability tier'
     4Ôćĺ
     5ÔćĺnextStepFile: './step-01b-ccc-index.md'
     6ÔćĺtierRulesData: '../data/tier-rules.md'
     7Ôćĺ---
     8Ôćĺ
     9Ôćĺ# Step 1: Detect Tools and Determine Tier
    10Ôćĺ
    11Ôćĺ## STEP GOAL:
    12Ôćĺ
    13ÔćĺVerify availability of the four forge tools (ast-grep, gh, qmd, ccc), read any existing configuration for re-run comparison, check for tier override, and calculate the capability tier.
    14Ôćĺ
    15Ôćĺ## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES (READ FIRST):
    16Ôćĺ
    17Ôćĺ### Universal Rules:
    18Ôćĺ
    19Ôćĺ- ­čôľ CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action
    20Ôćĺ- ­čöä CRITICAL: When loading next step, ensure entire file is read
    21Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Execute all operations autonomously ÔÇö no user interaction
    22Ôćĺ
    23Ôćĺ### Role Reinforcement:
    24Ôćĺ
    25Ôćĺ- Ôťů You are a system executor performing environment resolution
    26Ôćĺ- Ôťů Tool detection must be empirical ÔÇö run actual commands, never assume
    27Ôćĺ- Ôťů Record exact results (version strings, error messages)
    28Ôćĺ
    29Ôćĺ### Step-Specific Rules:
    30Ôćĺ
    31Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Focus only on tool detection and tier calculation
    32Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to write any files ÔÇö that is step-02's job
    33Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to skip any tool check ÔÇö all 4 must be verified
    34Ôćĺ- ­čĺČ Tool command failures are NOT errors ÔÇö they indicate unavailability
    35Ôćĺ
    36Ôćĺ## EXECUTION PROTOCOLS:
    37Ôćĺ
    38Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Follow the MANDATORY SEQUENCE exactly
    39Ôćĺ- ­čĺż Store all results in memory for step-02
    40Ôćĺ- ­čôľ Load tier-rules.md for calculation reference
    41Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to proceed without checking all 4 tools
    42Ôćĺ
    43Ôćĺ## CONTEXT BOUNDARIES:
    44Ôćĺ
    45Ôćĺ- This is the first step ÔÇö no prior context exists
    46Ôćĺ- Available: forge-tier.yaml and preferences.yaml may exist from prior runs
    47Ôćĺ- Focus: tool verification and tier calculation only
    48Ôćĺ- Produces: `{detected_tools}`, `{calculated_tier}`, `{previous_tier}`, `{tier_override}` for downstream steps
    49Ôćĺ- Dependencies: none ÔÇö this step bootstraps everything
    50Ôćĺ
    51Ôćĺ## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
    52Ôćĺ
    53Ôćĺ**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise.
    54Ôćĺ
    55Ôćĺ### 1. Load Tier Rules
    56Ôćĺ
    57ÔćĺLoad and read {tierRulesData} for the tool detection commands and tier calculation logic.
    58Ôćĺ
    59Ôćĺ### 2. Check for Existing Configuration (Re-run Detection)
    60Ôćĺ
    61Ôćĺ**Read existing forge-tier.yaml** at `{project-root}/_bmad/_memory/forger-sidecar/forge-tier.yaml`:
    62Ôćĺ- If exists: store the current `tier` value as `{previous_tier}` and `tier_detected_at` as `{previous_detection_date}`
    63Ôćĺ- If not found: set `{previous_tier}` to null (first run)
    64Ôćĺ
    65Ôćĺ**Read existing preferences.yaml** at `{project-root}/_bmad/_memory/forger-sidecar/preferences.yaml`:
    66Ôćĺ- If exists: check for `tier_override` value
    67Ôćĺ- If not found: set `{tier_override}` to null
    68Ôćĺ
    69Ôćĺ### 3. Verify Tool: ast-grep
    70Ôćĺ
    71ÔćĺRun: `ast-grep --version`
    72Ôćĺ
    73Ôćĺ- If succeeds: record `{ast_grep: true}` and store version string
    74Ôćĺ- If fails (command not found or error): record `{ast_grep: false}`
    75Ôćĺ
    76Ôćĺ### 4. Verify Tool: gh
    77Ôćĺ
    78ÔćĺRun: `gh --version`
    79Ôćĺ
    80Ôćĺ- If succeeds: record `{gh_cli: true}` and store version string
    81Ôćĺ- If fails: record `{gh_cli: false}`
    82Ôćĺ
    83Ôćĺ### 5. Verify Tool: qmd
    84Ôćĺ
    85ÔćĺRun: `qmd status`
    86Ôćĺ
    87Ôćĺ- If succeeds and indicates operational: record `{qmd: true}`
    88Ôćĺ- If fails or indicates not initialized: record `{qmd: false}`
    89Ôćĺ
    90Ôćĺ### 6. Check Optional: Security Scan (SNYK_TOKEN)
    91Ôćĺ
    92ÔćĺCheck if the `SNYK_TOKEN` environment variable is set:
    93Ôćĺ
    94Ôćĺ- If `SNYK_TOKEN` is non-empty: record `{security_scan: true}`
    95Ôćĺ- If `SNYK_TOKEN` is empty or unset: record `{security_scan: false}`
    96Ôćĺ
    97ÔćĺThis is informational only ÔÇö security scan availability does NOT affect the tier level. It is recorded in forge-tier.yaml so that create-skill's validation step can report actionable guidance when security scanning is unavailable.
    98Ôćĺ
    99Ôćĺ### 7. Verify Tool: ccc (cocoindex-code)
   100Ôćĺ
   101Ôćĺ**Step A ÔÇö Binary existence:** Run `ccc --help`
   102Ôćĺ
   103Ôćĺ- If exits 0: binary confirmed. Continue to Step B.
   104Ôćĺ- If fails (command not found or error): record `{ccc: false}`. Skip Step B.
   105Ôćĺ
   106Ôćĺ**Step B ÔÇö Daemon health:** Run `ccc doctor`
   107Ôćĺ
   108Ôćĺ- If daemon is running and model check OK: record `{ccc: true, ccc_daemon: "healthy"}` and store version string from output
   109Ôćĺ- If daemon is not running: record `{ccc: true, ccc_daemon: "stopped"}` ÔÇö binary available, daemon needs starting. Step-01b will handle this.
   110Ôćĺ- If error or timeout: record `{ccc: true, ccc_daemon: "error"}` ÔÇö binary works but daemon has issues.
   111Ôćĺ
   112Ôćĺccc availability gates the Forge+ tier and enhances Deep tier when present.
   113Ôćĺ
   114Ôćĺ### 8. Calculate Tier
   115Ôćĺ
   116Ôćĺ**If `{tier_override}` is set and valid (Quick, Forge, Forge+, or Deep):**
   117Ôćĺ- Use `{tier_override}` as `{calculated_tier}`
   118Ôćĺ- Note that override is active for the report step
   119Ôćĺ
   120Ôćĺ**If no override, apply tier rules from {tierRulesData} in order ÔÇö the first matching rule wins. Do not continue checking once a match is found:**
   121Ôćĺ- `{ast_grep}` AND `{gh_cli}` AND `{qmd}` all true Ôćĺ **Deep**
   122Ôćĺ- `{ast_grep}` AND `{ccc}` both true (regardless of gh/qmd) Ôćĺ **Forge+**
   123Ôćĺ- `{ast_grep}` true (regardless of ccc/gh/qmd) Ôćĺ **Forge**
   124Ôćĺ- Otherwise Ôćĺ **Quick**
   125Ôćĺ
   126Ôćĺ**If `{tier_override}` is set but invalid:** ignore it, use detected tier, flag for warning in report.
   127Ôćĺ
   128Ôćĺ### 9. Auto-Proceed
   129Ôćĺ
   130Ôćĺ"**Proceeding to CCC index check...**"
   131Ôćĺ
   132Ôćĺ#### Menu Handling Logic:
   133Ôćĺ
   134Ôćĺ- After tier calculation is complete, immediately load, read entire file, then execute {nextStepFile}
   135Ôćĺ
   136Ôćĺ#### EXECUTION RULES:
   137Ôćĺ
   138Ôćĺ- This is an auto-proceed step with no user choices
   139Ôćĺ- Proceed directly to next step after detection and calculation
   140Ôćĺ
   141Ôćĺ## CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE
   142Ôćĺ
   143ÔćĺONLY WHEN all 4 core tools have been verified, optional security scan checked, and the tier calculated will you load and read fully `{nextStepFile}` to execute the CCC index check step.
   144Ôćĺ
   145Ôćĺ---
   146Ôćĺ
   147Ôćĺ## ­čÜĘ SYSTEM SUCCESS/FAILURE METRICS
   148Ôćĺ
   149Ôćĺ### Ôťů SUCCESS:
   150Ôćĺ
   151Ôćĺ- All 4 tools checked via verification commands (not existence checks)
   152Ôćĺ- Existing forge-tier.yaml read for re-run comparison (if present)
   153Ôćĺ- Existing preferences.yaml read for tier_override (if present)
   154Ôćĺ- Tier correctly calculated from tool results or override applied
   155Ôćĺ- All results stored in context for step-01b and step-02
   156Ôćĺ- Auto-proceeded to step-01b
   157Ôćĺ
   158Ôćĺ### ÔŁî SYSTEM FAILURE:
   159Ôćĺ
   160Ôćĺ- Skipping any tool verification (ast-grep, gh, qmd, ccc ÔÇö all 4 must be checked)
   161Ôćĺ- Using `which` or `command -v` instead of verification commands
   162Ôćĺ- Assuming tool availability without running the command
   163Ôćĺ- Writing files in this step (that is step-02)
   164Ôćĺ- Not checking for existing configuration on re-runs
   165Ôćĺ- Not checking ccc availability (both binary and daemon health)
   166Ôćĺ
   167Ôćĺ**Master Rule:** Every tool MUST be verified empirically. No assumptions, no shortcuts.
   168Ôćĺ

1Ôćĺ# Tier Degradation Rules
     2Ôćĺ
     3Ôćĺ## Remote Source at Forge/Deep Tier
     4Ôćĺ
     5ÔćĺWhen `source_repo` is a remote URL (GitHub URL or owner/repo format) and the tier is Forge or Deep:
     6Ôćĺ
     7Ôćĺ- **ast-grep requires local files** ÔÇö it cannot operate on remote URLs
     8Ôćĺ
     9Ôćĺ**Ephemeral clone strategy (preferred):**
    10Ôćĺ
    11Ôćĺ1. Check `git` availability (`git --version`). `git` is effectively guaranteed at Deep tier (via `gh` dependency) but NOT guaranteed at Forge tier.
    12Ôćĺ2. If `git` is available: perform an ephemeral shallow clone to a system temp path (`{system_temp}/skf-ephemeral-{skill-name}-{timestamp}/`).
    13Ôćĺ3. For create-skill: use `--depth 1 --single-branch --filter=blob:none`; if `include_patterns` are specified, apply mode selection: if `exclude_patterns` are absent, use cone mode (convert include_patterns to directory roots; use `--skip-checks` for individual file paths); if `exclude_patterns` are present, use `--no-cone` mode (pass gitignore-style patterns with `!`-prefixed excludes ÔÇö includes first, then negations). See source-resolution-protocols.md for the full conversion rules.
    14Ôćĺ4. For update-skill: use sparse-checkout with `--skip-checks` scoped to the changed files from the change manifest only (file paths require `--skip-checks`). No `--branch` flag ÔÇö uses the remote default branch (must match the branch used during original create-skill run).
    15Ôćĺ5. If clone succeeds: use the local clone path for AST extraction. All results are T1 with `[AST:...]` citations.
    16Ôćĺ6. Cleanup: delete the temp directory after extraction inventory is built and all data is in context. The clone never persists beyond the extraction step.
    17Ôćĺ
    18Ôćĺ**Fallback (clone fails or `git` unavailable):**
    19Ôćĺ
    20Ôćĺ- The extraction step MUST warn the user explicitly before degrading
    21Ôćĺ- **create-skill:** Warning must include actionable guidance ÔÇö clone locally and update `source_repo` in the brief to the local path
    22Ôćĺ- **update-skill:** Warning must include actionable guidance ÔÇö clone locally, re-run [CS] Create Skill with the local path to regenerate provenance data, then re-run the update
    23Ôćĺ- Extraction proceeds using Quick tier strategy (source reading via gh_bridge)
    24Ôćĺ- All results labeled T1-low with `[SRC:...]` citations
    25Ôćĺ- The degradation reason is recorded in the evidence report
    26Ôćĺ
    27ÔćĺSilent degradation is **forbidden**. The user must always know when AST extraction was skipped and why.
    28Ôćĺ
    29Ôćĺ## AST Tool Unavailable at Forge/Deep Tier
    30Ôćĺ
    31ÔćĺWhen the tier is Forge or Deep but ast-grep is not functional:
    32Ôćĺ
    33Ôćĺ- The extraction step MUST warn the user explicitly before degrading
    34Ôćĺ- Warning must include actionable guidance: run [SF] Setup Forge to detect tools
    35Ôćĺ- Extraction proceeds using Quick tier strategy
    36Ôćĺ- All results labeled T1-low
    37Ôćĺ- The degradation reason is recorded in the evidence report
    38Ôćĺ
    39Ôćĺ## Per-File AST Failure
    40Ôćĺ
    41ÔćĺWhen ast-grep fails on an individual file (parse error, unsupported syntax):
    42Ôćĺ
    43Ôćĺ- Fall back to source reading for **that file only**
    44Ôćĺ- Other files continue with AST extraction
    45Ôćĺ- The affected file's results are labeled T1-low; unaffected files retain T1
    46Ôćĺ- Log a warning noting which file degraded and why
    47Ôćĺ

1Ôćĺ---
     2Ôćĺname: setup-forge
     3Ôćĺdescription: Initialize forge environment, detect tools, set tier, verify QMD collection hygiene
     4Ôćĺweb_bundle: true
     5Ôćĺinstalled_path: '{project-root}/_bmad/skf/workflows/skillforge/setup-forge'
     6Ôćĺ---
     7Ôćĺ
     8Ôćĺ# Setup Forge
     9Ôćĺ
    10Ôćĺ**Goal:** Initialize the forge environment by detecting available tools, determining the capability tier (Quick/Forge/Forge+/Deep), writing persistent configuration, and optionally indexing the project for deep search.
    11Ôćĺ
    12Ôćĺ**Your Role:** You are a system executor performing environment resolution. This is a fully autonomous workflow ÔÇö no user interaction is required during execution. Run each step in sequence, write configuration files, and report results at completion.
    13Ôćĺ
    14Ôćĺ## WORKFLOW ARCHITECTURE
    15Ôćĺ
    16Ôćĺ### Core Principles
    17Ôćĺ
    18Ôćĺ- **Micro-file Design**: Each step of the overall goal is a self contained instruction file that you will adhere to 1 file as directed at a time
    19Ôćĺ- **Just-In-Time Loading**: Only 1 current step file will be loaded, read, and executed to completion - never load future step files until told to do so
    20Ôćĺ- **Sequential Enforcement**: Sequence within the step files must be completed in order, no skipping or optimization allowed
    21Ôćĺ- **Fully Autonomous**: All steps auto-proceed with no user interaction until the final report
    22Ôćĺ
    23Ôćĺ### Step Processing Rules
    24Ôćĺ
    25Ôćĺ1. **READ COMPLETELY**: Always read the entire step file before taking any action
    26Ôćĺ2. **FOLLOW SEQUENCE**: Execute all numbered sections in order, never deviate
    27Ôćĺ3. **AUTO-PROCEED**: Each step proceeds to the next automatically after completion
    28Ôćĺ4. **LOAD NEXT**: When directed, load, read entire file, then execute the next step file
    29Ôćĺ
    30Ôćĺ### Critical Rules (NO EXCEPTIONS)
    31Ôćĺ
    32Ôćĺ- ­čŤĹ **NEVER** load multiple step files simultaneously
    33Ôćĺ- ­čôľ **ALWAYS** read entire step file before execution
    34Ôćĺ- ­čÜź **NEVER** skip steps or optimize the sequence
    35Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» **ALWAYS** follow the exact instructions in the step file
    36Ôćĺ- ­čôő **NEVER** create mental todo lists from future steps
    37Ôćĺ
    38Ôćĺ---
    39Ôćĺ
    40Ôćĺ## INITIALIZATION SEQUENCE
    41Ôćĺ
    42Ôćĺ### 1. Module Configuration Loading
    43Ôćĺ
    44ÔćĺLoad and read full config from {project-root}/_bmad/skf/config.yaml and resolve:
    45Ôćĺ
    46Ôćĺ- `project_name`, `output_folder`, `user_name`, `communication_language`, `document_output_language`
    47Ôćĺ- `skills_output_folder`, `forge_data_folder`, `sidecar_path`
    48Ôćĺ
    49Ôćĺ### 2. First Step Execution
    50Ôćĺ
    51ÔćĺLoad, read the full file and then execute ./steps-c/step-01-detect-and-tier.md to begin the workflow.
    52Ôćĺ

1Ôćĺ# Extraction Patterns by Tier
     2Ôćĺ
     3Ôćĺ## Quick Tier (No AST)
     4Ôćĺ
     5ÔćĺSource reading via gh_bridge ÔÇö infer exports from file structure and content.
     6Ôćĺ
     7Ôćĺ### Strategy
     8Ôćĺ1. `gh_bridge.list_tree(owner, repo, branch)` ÔÇö map source structure
     9Ôćĺ2. Identify entry points: index files, main exports, public modules
    10Ôćĺ3. `gh_bridge.read_file(owner, repo, path)` ÔÇö read each entry point
    11Ôćĺ4. Extract: exported function names, parameter lists, return types (from signatures)
    12Ôćĺ5. Infer types from JSDoc, docstrings, type annotations in source
    13Ôćĺ
    14Ôćĺ### Confidence
    15Ôćĺ- All results: T1-low (source reading without structural verification)
    16Ôćĺ- No co-import detection available
    17Ôćĺ- No AST-backed line numbers
    18Ôćĺ
    19Ôćĺ### Supported Patterns
    20Ôćĺ- `export function name(...)` / `export const name = ...` (JS/TS)
    21Ôćĺ- `pub fn name(...)` (Rust)
    22Ôćĺ- `def name(...)` with `__all__` (Python)
    23Ôćĺ- `func Name(...)` (Go, capitalized = exported)
    24Ôćĺ
    25Ôćĺ---
    26Ôćĺ
    27Ôćĺ## Forge Tier (AST Available)
    28Ôćĺ
    29ÔćĺStructural extraction via ast-grep ÔÇö verified exports with line-level citations.
    30Ôćĺ
    31Ôćĺ> **Note:** `ast_bridge.*`, `qmd_bridge.*`, and `ccc_bridge.*` references below are **conceptual interfaces**, not callable functions. They describe the operation to perform. Use ast-grep (MCP tool or CLI) for `ast_bridge.*` operations, QMD (MCP tool or CLI) for `qmd_bridge.*` operations, and ccc CLI or MCP for `ccc_bridge.*` operations. See the AST Extraction Protocol section below and the TOOL/SUBPROCESS FALLBACK rule for dispatch details.
    32Ôćĺ
    33Ôćĺ### Strategy
    34Ôćĺ
    35Ôćĺ1. Detect language from brief or file extensions
    36Ôćĺ2. Use ast-grep to extract all exports from `path` for the given `language` (scan definitions)
    37Ôćĺ3. For each export: function name, full signature, parameter types, return type, line number
    38Ôćĺ4. Use ast-grep to detect co-imported symbols in `path` for the given `libraries[]`
    39Ôćĺ5. Build extraction rules YAML for reproducibility
    40Ôćĺ
    41Ôćĺ### Confidence
    42Ôćĺ- Exported functions with full signatures: T1 (AST-verified)
    43Ôćĺ- Type definitions and interfaces: T1
    44Ôćĺ- Co-import patterns: T1
    45Ôćĺ- Internal/private functions: excluded (not part of public API)
    46Ôćĺ
    47Ôćĺ### ast-grep Patterns
    48Ôćĺ- JS/TS: `export function $NAME($$$PARAMS): $RET` / `export const $NAME`
    49Ôćĺ- Rust: `pub fn $NAME($$$PARAMS) -> $RET`
    50Ôćĺ- Python: function definitions within `__all__` list
    51Ôćĺ- Go: capitalized function definitions
    52Ôćĺ
    53Ôćĺ---
    54Ôćĺ
    55Ôćĺ## Forge+ Tier (AST + CCC)
    56Ôćĺ
    57ÔćĺIdentical extraction to Forge tier. CCC adds an upstream semantic discovery step that pre-ranks the file extraction queue.
    58Ôćĺ
    59Ôćĺ### When CCC Pre-Discovery Applies
    60Ôćĺ
    61ÔćĺCCC pre-discovery runs in step-02b-ccc-discover (before this extraction step) when ALL of the following are true:
    62Ôćĺ- Tier is Forge+ or Deep
    63Ôćĺ- `tools.ccc: true` in forge-tier.yaml
    64Ôćĺ- `ccc_index.status` is `"fresh"`, `"stale"`, `"created"`, or `"none"`/`"failed"` (step-02b attempts lazy indexing for the latter two)
    65Ôćĺ
    66ÔćĺThe discovery step stores `{ccc_discovery: [{file, score, snippet}]}` in context. This extraction step consumes those results to pre-rank the file list.
    67Ôćĺ
    68Ôćĺ### CCC Pre-Ranking Strategy
    69Ôćĺ
    70ÔćĺWhen `{ccc_discovery}` is present and non-empty:
    71Ôćĺ
    72Ôćĺ1. Files appearing in `{ccc_discovery}` results move to the front of the extraction queue, sorted by relevance score descending
    73Ôćĺ2. Files NOT in CCC results remain in the queue ÔÇö they are not excluded, only deprioritized
    74Ôćĺ3. If the CCC intersection with scoped files produces <10 files: include all scoped files (CCC results too narrow)
    75Ôćĺ4. Proceed with the AST Extraction Protocol on the pre-ranked list
    76Ôćĺ
    77Ôćĺ### ast-grep Patterns
    78Ôćĺ
    79ÔćĺSame patterns as Forge tier ÔÇö see Forge tier section above. CCC pre-ranking does not change which AST patterns are used, only which files are processed first.
    80Ôćĺ
    81Ôćĺ### Confidence
    82Ôćĺ
    83ÔćĺAll results: T1 (AST-verified) ÔÇö identical to Forge tier. CCC is upstream discovery only and is invisible in the output artifact.
    84Ôćĺ
    85Ôćĺ### Important
    86Ôćĺ
    87ÔćĺCCC pre-discovery failures (ccc unavailable, command error, empty results) always result in standard Forge extraction behavior. This is not reported to the user as a problem ÔÇö it is normal behavior when ccc has no relevant results for the skill's scope.
    88Ôćĺ
    89Ôćĺ---
    90Ôćĺ
    91Ôćĺ## Deep Tier (AST + QMD)
    92Ôćĺ
    93ÔćĺSame extraction as Forge tier. Deep tier adds enrichment in step-04, not extraction.
    94Ôćĺ
    95Ôćĺ### Strategy
    96Ôćĺ- Identical to Forge tier extraction
    97Ôćĺ- QMD enrichment happens in the next step (step-04-enrich)
    98Ôćĺ- Extraction results carry forward unchanged
    99Ôćĺ
   100Ôćĺ### Confidence
   101Ôćĺ- Extraction: same as Forge (T1)
   102Ôćĺ- Enrichment annotations added in step-04: T2
   103Ôćĺ
   104Ôćĺ---
   105Ôćĺ
   106Ôćĺ## AST Extraction Protocol
   107Ôćĺ
   108ÔćĺWhen AST tools are available (Forge/Deep tier), follow this deterministic protocol to prevent output overflow on large codebases.
   109Ôćĺ
   110Ôćĺ**"Files in scope"** = files remaining after applying `include_patterns` and `exclude_patterns` from the brief, filtered by the target language extension. This is NOT the total repository file count from step-01's tree listing. Use the filtered count from step-03 section 2 as the decision tree input.
   111Ôćĺ
   112Ôćĺ### Decision Tree
   113Ôćĺ
   114ÔćĺApply the first matching condition:
   115Ôćĺ
   116Ôćĺ```
   117ÔćĺFiles in scope ÔëĄ 100
   118Ôćĺ  Ôćĺ Use ast-grep MCP tool: find_code(pattern, max_results=100, output_format="text")
   119Ôćĺ  Ôćĺ Parse compact text output directly into extraction inventory
   120Ôćĺ
   121ÔćĺFiles in scope 101ÔÇô500
   122Ôćĺ  Ôćĺ Use ast-grep MCP tool: find_code_by_rule(yaml, max_results=150, output_format="text")
   123Ôćĺ  Ôćĺ Use scoped YAML rules (see recipes below) to filter at the AST level
   124Ôćĺ  Ôćĺ Parse compact text output into extraction inventory
   125Ôćĺ
   126ÔćĺFiles in scope > 500
   127Ôćĺ  Ôćĺ CLI streaming fallback: ast-grep --json=stream + line-by-line Python processing
   128Ôćĺ  Ôćĺ Process in directory batches, cap per-batch output
   129Ôćĺ  Ôćĺ Merge batch results into extraction inventory
   130Ôćĺ```
   131Ôćĺ
   132Ôćĺ### Safety Valve
   133Ôćĺ
   134ÔćĺIf any ast-grep operation (MCP or CLI) visibly causes a timeout, returns an error related to output size, or produces unexpectedly large output: immediately switch to the CLI streaming fallback with `--json=stream`. Do not retry the same approach. When falling back to the CLI streaming template, inject the brief's `scope.exclude` patterns into the `EXCLUDES` list (use `[]` if absent) ÔÇö this applies regardless of which path triggered the fallback. Note: `max_results` in the MCP tool and `| head -N` in the CLI path provide hard caps, but this safety valve covers cases where the upstream tool itself fails before returning results (e.g., OOM during JSON serialization).
   135Ôćĺ
   136Ôćĺ### MCP Tool Usage (Preferred)
   137Ôćĺ
   138Ôćĺ**Simple pattern search:**
   139Ôćĺ
   140Ôćĺ```
   141Ôćĺfind_code(
   142Ôćĺ  project_folder="{source_path}",
   143Ôćĺ  pattern="async def $NAME($$$PARAMS)",
   144Ôćĺ  language="python",
   145Ôćĺ  max_results=100,
   146Ôćĺ  output_format="text"
   147Ôćĺ)
   148Ôćĺ```
   149Ôćĺ
   150Ôćĺ**Scoped YAML rule search (for larger repos):**
   151Ôćĺ
   152Ôćĺ```
   153Ôćĺfind_code_by_rule(
   154Ôćĺ  project_folder="{source_path}",
   155Ôćĺ  yaml="id: public-api\nlanguage: python\nrule:\n  pattern: 'def $NAME($$$PARAMS)'\n  inside:\n    kind: module\n    stopBy: end\nconstraints:\n  NAME:\n    regex: '^[^_]'",
   156Ôćĺ  max_results=150,
   157Ôćĺ  output_format="text"
   158Ôćĺ)
   159Ôćĺ```
   160Ôćĺ
   161Ôćĺ### CLI Streaming Fallback
   162Ôćĺ
   163ÔćĺWhen MCP tools are unavailable or the repo exceeds 500 files in scope, use `--json=stream` (NEVER `--json` or `--json=pretty`) with line-by-line Python processing:
   164Ôćĺ
   165Ôćĺ```bash
   166Ôćĺ# Note: use $$$ for variadic params in ast-grep patterns (e.g., 'def $NAME($$$PARAMS)')
   167Ôćĺ# {exclude_patterns} = Python list from brief's scope.exclude, e.g. ['tests/**', '**/test_*']
   168Ôćĺ# If scope.exclude is absent or empty in the brief, inject [] as the default.
   169Ôćĺ# Patterns are matched against the full file path as emitted by ast-grep.
   170Ôćĺ# Ensure paths are relative to the same root as the patterns (strip ./ prefix if needed).
   171Ôćĺast-grep -p '{pattern}' -l {language} --json=stream {path} | python3 -c "
   172Ôćĺimport sys, json, fnmatch, signal
   173Ôćĺsignal.signal(signal.SIGPIPE, signal.SIG_DFL)
   174Ôćĺ
   175ÔćĺEXCLUDES = {exclude_patterns}
   176Ôćĺ
   177Ôćĺfor line in sys.stdin:
   178Ôćĺ    try:
   179Ôćĺ        m = json.loads(line)
   180Ôćĺ        f = m.get('file','')
   181Ôćĺ        if EXCLUDES and any(fnmatch.fnmatch(f, pat) for pat in EXCLUDES):
   182Ôćĺ            continue
   183Ôćĺ        v = m.get('metaVariables',{})
   184Ôćĺ        name = v.get('single',{}).get('NAME',{}).get('text','')
   185Ôćĺ        if name and not name.startswith('_'):
   186Ôćĺ            ln = m.get('range',{}).get('start',{}).get('line',0)+1
   187Ôćĺ            sig = m.get('text','').split(chr(10))[0].strip()
   188Ôćĺ            print(f'[AST:{f}:L{ln}] {sig}')
   189Ôćĺ    except: pass
   190Ôćĺ" | head -200
   191Ôćĺ```
   192Ôćĺ
   193Ôćĺ**Critical constraints:**
   194Ôćĺ
   195Ôćĺ- ALWAYS use `--json=stream` ÔÇö never `--json` (loads entire array into memory)
   196Ôćĺ- ALWAYS process line-by-line (`for line in sys.stdin`) ÔÇö never `json.load(sys.stdin)`
   197Ôćĺ- ALWAYS cap output with `| head -N` as a safety valve
   198Ôćĺ- For repos > 500 files, process in directory batches of 20-50 files each
   199Ôćĺ
   200Ôćĺ### YAML Rule Recipes by Language
   201Ôćĺ
   202Ôćĺ**Python ÔÇö public functions:**
   203Ôćĺ
   204Ôćĺ```yaml
   205Ôćĺid: python-public-functions
   206Ôćĺlanguage: python
   207Ôćĺrule:
   208Ôćĺ  pattern: 'def $NAME($$$PARAMS)'
   209Ôćĺ  inside:
   210Ôćĺ    kind: module
   211Ôćĺ    stopBy: end
   212Ôćĺconstraints:
   213Ôćĺ  NAME:
   214Ôćĺ    regex: '^[^_]'
   215Ôćĺ```
   216Ôćĺ
   217Ôćĺ**Python ÔÇö public classes:**
   218Ôćĺ
   219Ôćĺ```yaml
   220Ôćĺid: python-public-classes
   221Ôćĺlanguage: python
   222Ôćĺrule:
   223Ôćĺ  pattern: 'class $NAME($$$BASES)'
   224Ôćĺ  inside:
   225Ôćĺ    kind: module
   226Ôćĺ    stopBy: end
   227Ôćĺconstraints:
   228Ôćĺ  NAME:
   229Ôćĺ    regex: '^[^_]'
   230Ôćĺ```
   231Ôćĺ
   232Ôćĺ**JavaScript/TypeScript ÔÇö exported functions:**
   233Ôćĺ
   234Ôćĺ```yaml
   235Ôćĺid: js-exported-functions
   236Ôćĺlanguage: typescript
   237Ôćĺrule:
   238Ôćĺ  pattern: 'export function $NAME($$$PARAMS)'
   239Ôćĺ```
   240Ôćĺ
   241Ôćĺ**JavaScript/TypeScript ÔÇö exported constants:**
   242Ôćĺ
   243Ôćĺ```yaml
   244Ôćĺid: js-exported-constants
   245Ôćĺlanguage: typescript
   246Ôćĺrule:
   247Ôćĺ  pattern: 'export const $NAME = $VALUE'
   248Ôćĺ```
   249Ôćĺ
   250Ôćĺ**Rust ÔÇö public functions:**
   251Ôćĺ
   252Ôćĺ```yaml
   253Ôćĺid: rust-public-functions
   254Ôćĺlanguage: rust
   255Ôćĺrule:
   256Ôćĺ  any:
   257Ôćĺ    - pattern: 'pub fn $NAME($$$PARAMS) -> $RET'
   258Ôćĺ    - pattern: 'pub fn $NAME($$$PARAMS)'
   259Ôćĺ```
   260Ôćĺ
   261Ôćĺ**Go ÔÇö exported functions (capitalized):**
   262Ôćĺ
   263Ôćĺ```yaml
   264Ôćĺid: go-exported-functions
   265Ôćĺlanguage: go
   266Ôćĺrule:
   267Ôćĺ  pattern: 'func $NAME($$$PARAMS) $RET'
   268Ôćĺconstraints:
   269Ôćĺ  NAME:
   270Ôćĺ    regex: '^[A-Z]'
   271Ôćĺ```
   272Ôćĺ
   273Ôćĺ### Re-Export Tracing and Script/Asset Extraction
   274Ôćĺ
   275ÔćĺSee `extraction-patterns-tracing.md` for:
   276Ôćĺ- **Re-export tracing protocol** ÔÇö resolving module imports through `__init__.py`, barrel files, `pub use`
   277Ôćĺ- **Script/asset extraction patterns** ÔÇö detection heuristics, inclusion rules, provenance, inventory structure
   278Ôćĺ
   279Ôćĺ

     8Ôćĺ# Step 1b: CCC Index Verification
     9Ôćĺ
    10Ôćĺ## STEP GOAL:
    11Ôćĺ
    12ÔćĺIf ccc is available (`{ccc: true}` from step-01), verify that the ccc index exists for the project root. If no index exists, create it. Store index state in context for step-02 to write into forge-tier.yaml.
    13Ôćĺ
    14ÔćĺFor Quick and Forge tiers, or when ccc is unavailable, skip silently and proceed.
    15Ôćĺ
    16Ôćĺ## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES (READ FIRST):
    17Ôćĺ
    18Ôćĺ### Universal Rules:
    19Ôćĺ
    20Ôćĺ- ­čôľ CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action
    21Ôćĺ- ­čöä CRITICAL: When loading next step, ensure entire file is read
    22Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Execute all operations autonomously ÔÇö no user interaction
    23Ôćĺ
    24Ôćĺ### Role Reinforcement:
    25Ôćĺ
    26Ôćĺ- Ôťů You are a system executor verifying the ccc index state
    27Ôćĺ- Ôťů ccc indexing failure is never a workflow error ÔÇö degrade gracefully
    28Ôćĺ- Ôťů No negative messaging for tiers that skip this step
    29Ôćĺ
    30Ôćĺ### Step-Specific Rules:
    31Ôćĺ
    32Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Focus only on ccc index verification and creation
    33Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to display skip messages for Quick/Forge tiers
    34Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to fail the workflow if ccc indexing fails
    35Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to re-index if ccc index already exists and is fresh (< staleness threshold)
    36Ôćĺ
    37Ôćĺ## EXECUTION PROTOCOLS:
    38Ôćĺ
    39Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Follow the MANDATORY SEQUENCE exactly
    40Ôćĺ- ­čĺż Store ccc index state in context for step-02
    41Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to write forge-tier.yaml ÔÇö that is step-02's job
    42Ôćĺ
    43Ôćĺ## CONTEXT BOUNDARIES:
    44Ôćĺ
    45Ôćĺ- Available: {ccc} boolean and {ccc_daemon} status from step-01
    46Ôćĺ- Available: {calculated_tier} from step-01
    47Ôćĺ- Available: existing forge-tier.yaml may contain prior `ccc_index` state
    48Ôćĺ- Focus: ccc index state verification and creation only
    49Ôćĺ- Dependencies: step-01 must have completed with tool detection results
    50Ôćĺ
    51Ôćĺ## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
    52Ôćĺ
    53Ôćĺ**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise.
    54Ôćĺ
    55Ôćĺ### 1. Check Eligibility
    56Ôćĺ
    57ÔćĺRead `{ccc}` from step-01 context.
    58Ôćĺ
    59Ôćĺ**If `{ccc}` is false:** Set `{ccc_index_result: "none", ccc_indexed_path: null, ccc_last_indexed: null}`. Proceed directly to section 4 (Auto-Proceed) ÔÇö no output, no messaging.
    60Ôćĺ
    61Ôćĺ**If `{ccc}` is true:** Continue to section 2.
    62Ôćĺ
    63Ôćĺ### 2. Check Existing Index State
    64Ôćĺ
    65ÔćĺRead existing forge-tier.yaml at `{project-root}/_bmad/_memory/forger-sidecar/forge-tier.yaml` (if it exists from a previous run).
    66Ôćĺ
    67ÔćĺCheck the `ccc_index` section:
    68Ôćĺ- If `ccc_index.indexed_path` matches `{project-root}` AND `ccc_index.status` is `"fresh"`:
    69Ôćĺ  - Check freshness: if `ccc_index.last_indexed` is within `staleness_threshold_hours` (default 24h) of now Ôćĺ index is fresh
    70Ôćĺ  - Store `{ccc_index_result: "fresh", ccc_indexed_path: {project-root}, ccc_last_indexed: {existing timestamp}}`
    71Ôćĺ  - Proceed to section 4
    72Ôćĺ
    73Ôćĺ- If `ccc_index.indexed_path` matches `{project-root}` but timestamp is older than threshold:
    74Ôćĺ  - Note index is stale ÔÇö proceed to section 3 for re-index (section 3 will overwrite `ccc_index_result` to `"created"` or `"failed"`)
    75Ôćĺ
    76Ôćĺ- If `ccc_index` is missing, has null values, or path doesn't match:
    77Ôćĺ  - Proceed to section 3 for initial index
    78Ôćĺ
    79Ôćĺ### 3. Create or Refresh CCC Index
    80Ôćĺ
    81Ôćĺ**If `{ccc_daemon}` is `"stopped"` or undefined (healthy daemon where no explicit state was recorded):**
    82Ôćĺ
    83ÔćĺThe `ccc index` command auto-starts the daemon when needed. Proceed with indexing below.
    84Ôćĺ
    85Ôćĺ**If `{ccc_daemon}` is `"error"`:**
    86Ôćĺ
    87ÔćĺAttempt indexing anyway ÔÇö errors will be caught below.
    88Ôćĺ
    89ÔćĺRun:
    90Ôćĺ```bash
    91Ôćĺccc init {project-root}
    92Ôćĺ```
    93Ôćĺ
    94Ôćĺ**If init fails** (project may already be initialized): continue ÔÇö this is not an error.
    95Ôćĺ
    96ÔćĺThen run:
    97Ôćĺ```bash
    98Ôćĺccc index
    99Ôćĺ```
   100Ôćĺ
   101Ôćĺ**If succeeds:**
   102Ôćĺ- Run `ccc status` to get file count
   103Ôćĺ- Store `{ccc_index_result: "created", ccc_indexed_path: {project-root}, ccc_last_indexed: {current ISO timestamp}, ccc_file_count: {count from status}}`
   104Ôćĺ- Display: "**CCC index created.** {file_count} files indexed for semantic discovery."
   105Ôćĺ
   106Ôćĺ**If fails:**
   107Ôćĺ- Store `{ccc_index_result: "failed", ccc_indexed_path: null, ccc_last_indexed: null}`
   108Ôćĺ- Display: "CCC indexing failed: {error}. Extraction will use direct AST scanning ÔÇö semantic pre-ranking unavailable this session."
   109Ôćĺ- Continue ÔÇö this is NOT a workflow error
   110Ôćĺ
   111Ôćĺ### 4. Auto-Proceed
   112Ôćĺ
   113Ôćĺ"**Proceeding to write configuration...**"
   114Ôćĺ
   115Ôćĺ#### Menu Handling Logic:
   116Ôćĺ
   117Ôćĺ- After ccc index check completes (or is skipped), immediately load, read entire file, then execute {nextStepFile}
   118Ôćĺ
   119Ôćĺ#### EXECUTION RULES:
   120Ôćĺ
   121Ôćĺ- This is an auto-proceed step with no user choices
   122Ôćĺ- Proceed directly to next step after ccc index verification
   123Ôćĺ
   124Ôćĺ## CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE
   125Ôćĺ
   126ÔćĺONLY WHEN ccc index verification is complete (or step is skipped for ccc unavailable) will you load and read fully `{nextStepFile}` to execute the configuration write step.
   127Ôćĺ
   128Ôćĺ---
   129Ôćĺ
   130Ôćĺ## ­čÜĘ SYSTEM SUCCESS/FAILURE METRICS
   131Ôćĺ
   132Ôćĺ### Ôťů SUCCESS:
   133Ôćĺ
   134Ôćĺ- ccc unavailable: skipped silently with no output, context variables set to null/none
   135Ôćĺ- ccc available with fresh index: verified freshness, skipped re-index, context variables set
   136Ôćĺ- ccc available with stale/missing index: index created, context variables set with fresh timestamp
   137Ôćĺ- ccc indexing fails: logged gracefully, workflow continues, context variables set to failed/null
   138Ôćĺ- Auto-proceeded to step-02
   139Ôćĺ
   140Ôćĺ### ÔŁî SYSTEM FAILURE:
   141Ôćĺ
   142Ôćĺ- Displaying skip messages when ccc is unavailable
   143Ôćĺ- Halting the workflow on ccc index failure
   144Ôćĺ- Re-indexing when index is already fresh and path matches
   145Ôćĺ- Writing forge-tier.yaml (that is step-02's responsibility)
   146Ôćĺ- Not storing ccc index context variables for step-02
   147Ôćĺ
   148Ôćĺ**Master Rule:** CCC indexing is always best-effort. Failures degrade gracefully. The workflow never halts over ccc issues.

     8Ôćĺ# Step 3: QMD Collection Hygiene
     9Ôćĺ
    10Ôćĺ## STEP GOAL:
    11Ôćĺ
    12ÔćĺIf the detected tier is Deep, verify the health of existing QMD collections by cross-referencing them against the `qmd_collections` registry in `forge-tier.yaml`. Identify orphaned collections (in QMD but not in registry) and stale registry entries (in registry but collection missing from QMD). Prompt the user before removing orphaned collections.
    13Ôćĺ
    14ÔćĺFor Quick and Forge tiers (without ccc), skip silently and proceed. For Forge+ tier, skip QMD hygiene but the step routes correctly to the next step.
    15Ôćĺ
    16Ôćĺ## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES (READ FIRST):
    17Ôćĺ
    18Ôćĺ### Universal Rules:
    19Ôćĺ
    20Ôćĺ- ­čôľ CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action
    21Ôćĺ- ­čöä CRITICAL: When loading next step, ensure entire file is read
    22Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Execute all operations autonomously ÔÇö minimal user interaction (only prompt before deletion)
    23Ôćĺ
    24Ôćĺ### Role Reinforcement:
    25Ôćĺ
    26Ôćĺ- Ôťů You are a system executor performing QMD collection maintenance
    27Ôćĺ- Ôťů Graceful degradation is paramount ÔÇö never fail the workflow over hygiene checks
    28Ôćĺ- Ôťů No negative messaging ÔÇö do not mention what non-Deep tiers are missing
    29Ôćĺ
    30Ôćĺ### Step-Specific Rules:
    31Ôćĺ
    32Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Focus only on verifying and cleaning QMD collections (Deep tier) or graceful skip (other tiers)
    33Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to display "missing" or "skipped" messages for non-Deep tiers
    34Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to fail the workflow if QMD hygiene encounters errors
    35Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to create new QMD collections ÔÇö that responsibility belongs to create-skill
    36Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to silently delete collections ÔÇö always prompt user before removal
    37Ôćĺ- ­čĺČ If hygiene fails: log the issue, note that it can be retried, continue
    38Ôćĺ
    39Ôćĺ## EXECUTION PROTOCOLS:
    40Ôćĺ
    41Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Follow the MANDATORY SEQUENCE exactly
    42Ôćĺ- ­čĺż QMD hygiene only verifies and cleans ÔÇö it does NOT index new content
    43Ôćĺ- ­čôľ Use {calculated_tier} from step-01 context
    44Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to attempt QMD hygiene for Quick, Forge, or Forge+ tiers (Forge+ has no qmd)
    45Ôćĺ
    46Ôćĺ## CONTEXT BOUNDARIES:
    47Ôćĺ
    48Ôćĺ- Available: {calculated_tier} from step-01, forge-tier.yaml written in step-02
    49Ôćĺ- Available: {project_name} from workflow config
    50Ôćĺ- Focus: QMD collection verification and cleanup only
    51Ôćĺ- Limits: only run if Deep tier
    52Ôćĺ- Dependencies: step-02 must have completed (forge-tier.yaml exists with qmd_collections registry)
    53Ôćĺ
    54Ôćĺ## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
    55Ôćĺ
    56Ôćĺ**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise.
    57Ôćĺ
    58Ôćĺ### 1. Check Tier
    59Ôćĺ
    60ÔćĺRead `{calculated_tier}` from context.
    61Ôćĺ
    62Ôćĺ**If tier is Quick or Forge:** Set `{ccc_registry_stale_cleaned: 0}`. Proceed directly to section 6 (Auto-Proceed) ÔÇö no output, no messaging.
    63Ôćĺ
    64Ôćĺ**If tier is Forge+:** Skip QMD hygiene (qmd is not available at Forge+). Proceed directly to section 5b (CCC Index Registry Hygiene) ÔÇö no QMD output, no messaging.
    65Ôćĺ
    66Ôćĺ**If tier IS Deep:** Continue to section 2.
    67Ôćĺ
    68Ôćĺ### 2. Load Registry and QMD State
    69Ôćĺ
    70ÔćĺRead the `qmd_collections` array from `{project-root}/_bmad/_memory/forger-sidecar/forge-tier.yaml`.
    71Ôćĺ
    72ÔćĺList live QMD collections:
    73Ôćĺ```bash
    74Ôćĺqmd collection list
    75Ôćĺ```
    76Ôćĺ
    77Ôćĺ**Store both lists for cross-reference:**
    78Ôćĺ- `{registry_collections}` ÔÇö entries from forge-tier.yaml qmd_collections
    79Ôćĺ- `{live_collections}` ÔÇö collections currently in QMD
    80Ôćĺ
    81Ôćĺ**Error handling:** If `qmd collection list` fails, log the error, store `{hygiene_result: "qmd_unavailable"}`, and proceed to section 6.
    82Ôćĺ
    83Ôćĺ### 3. Cross-Reference and Classify
    84Ôćĺ
    85ÔćĺCompare the two lists:
    86Ôćĺ
    87Ôćĺ**Healthy** ÔÇö collection exists in both registry AND QMD:
    88Ôćĺ- Mark as verified
    89Ôćĺ- No action needed
    90Ôćĺ
    91Ôćĺ**Orphaned** ÔÇö collection exists in QMD but NOT in registry:
    92Ôćĺ- These may be leftover from prior auto-indexing or manual indexing
    93Ôćĺ- Flag for user-prompted removal
    94Ôćĺ
    95Ôćĺ**Stale** ÔÇö entry exists in registry but collection is missing from QMD:
    96Ôćĺ- The QMD collection was deleted or lost
    97Ôćĺ- Remove the stale entry from the registry
    98Ôćĺ
    99Ôćĺ### 4. Handle Orphaned Collections
   100Ôćĺ
   101Ôćĺ**If orphaned collections found:**
   102Ôćĺ
   103ÔćĺDisplay to user:
   104Ôćĺ"**QMD Hygiene: Found {count} orphaned collection(s) not tracked in the forge registry:**
   105Ôćĺ
   106Ôćĺ{list orphaned collection names}
   107Ôćĺ
   108ÔćĺThese collections exist in QMD but are not managed by any skill workflow. They may be from a previous auto-index run or manual creation.
   109Ôćĺ
   110Ôćĺ**[R]emove** orphaned collections ÔÇö clean up QMD
   111Ôćĺ**[K]eep** orphaned collections ÔÇö leave them as-is"
   112Ôćĺ
   113Ôćĺ**If user selects R (Remove):**
   114ÔćĺFor each orphaned collection:
   115Ôćĺ```bash
   116Ôćĺqmd collection remove {collection_name}
   117Ôćĺ```
   118ÔćĺLog each removal.
   119Ôćĺ
   120Ôćĺ**If user selects K (Keep):**
   121ÔćĺSkip removal. Log that orphaned collections were kept.
   122Ôćĺ
   123Ôćĺ**If no orphaned collections:** Skip this section silently.
   124Ôćĺ
   125Ôćĺ### 5. Handle Stale Registry Entries
   126Ôćĺ
   127Ôćĺ**If stale registry entries found:**
   128Ôćĺ
   129ÔćĺRemove stale entries from the `qmd_collections` array in forge-tier.yaml.
   130Ôćĺ
   131ÔćĺDisplay: "**Cleaned {count} stale registry entry/entries** (collection no longer exists in QMD)."
   132Ôćĺ
   133ÔćĺUpdate forge-tier.yaml with the cleaned registry.
   134Ôćĺ
   135Ôćĺ**If no stale entries:** Skip this section silently.
   136Ôćĺ
   137Ôćĺ### 5b. CCC Index Registry Hygiene (Forge+ and Deep with ccc)
   138Ôćĺ
   139Ôćĺ**IF `tools.ccc` is true in forge-tier.yaml (regardless of whether QMD hygiene ran):**
   140Ôćĺ
   141ÔćĺRead the `ccc_index_registry` array from forge-tier.yaml.
   142Ôćĺ
   143ÔćĺFor each entry, verify the indexed path still exists on disk:
   144Ôćĺ
   145Ôćĺ**Healthy** ÔÇö path exists: no action needed.
   146Ôćĺ
   147Ôćĺ**Stale** ÔÇö path does not exist (source directory removed or moved):
   148Ôćĺ- Remove the stale entry from `ccc_index_registry`
   149Ôćĺ- Log: "Removed stale CCC index registry entry: {path} (path no longer exists)"
   150Ôćĺ
   151ÔćĺUpdate forge-tier.yaml with the cleaned registry.
   152Ôćĺ
   153ÔćĺStore `{ccc_registry_stale_cleaned: count}` in context for step-04 reporting.
   154Ôćĺ
   155Ôćĺ**IF `tools.ccc` is false:** Skip this section silently.
   156Ôćĺ
   157Ôćĺ### 6. Store Hygiene Results and Auto-Proceed
   158Ôćĺ
   159ÔćĺStore in context for step-04 reporting:
   160Ôćĺ```
   161Ôćĺ{hygiene_result: "completed"|"skipped"|"qmd_unavailable"}
   162Ôćĺ{hygiene_healthy: count}
   163Ôćĺ{hygiene_orphaned_removed: count}
   164Ôćĺ{hygiene_orphaned_kept: count}
   165Ôćĺ{hygiene_stale_cleaned: count}
   166Ôćĺ{ccc_registry_stale_cleaned: count}
   167Ôćĺ```
   168Ôćĺ
   169Ôćĺ"**Proceeding to forge status report...**"
   170Ôćĺ
   171Ôćĺ#### Menu Handling Logic:
   172Ôćĺ
   173Ôćĺ- After hygiene completes (or is skipped), immediately load, read entire file, then execute {nextStepFile}
   174Ôćĺ
   175Ôćĺ#### EXECUTION RULES:
   176Ôćĺ
   177Ôćĺ- This step has one optional user interaction (orphan removal prompt)
   178Ôćĺ- If no orphans found, this is an auto-proceed step
   179Ôćĺ- Proceed directly to next step after hygiene or skip
   180Ôćĺ
   181Ôćĺ## CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE
   182Ôćĺ
   183ÔćĺONLY WHEN the hygiene check has been performed (or skipped for non-Deep tiers) will you load and read fully `{nextStepFile}` to execute the report step.
   184Ôćĺ
   185Ôćĺ---
   186Ôćĺ
   187Ôćĺ## ­čÜĘ SYSTEM SUCCESS/FAILURE METRICS
   188Ôćĺ
   189Ôćĺ### Ôťů SUCCESS:
   190Ôćĺ
   191Ôćĺ- Deep tier: forge-tier.yaml registry cross-referenced with live QMD collections
   192Ôćĺ- Healthy collections identified and verified
   193Ôćĺ- Orphaned collections flagged and user prompted before removal
   194Ôćĺ- Stale registry entries cleaned from forge-tier.yaml
   195Ôćĺ- Hygiene results stored for step-04 reporting
   196Ôćĺ- Quick/Forge/Forge+ tier: skipped silently with no negative messaging
   197Ôćĺ- Workflow continues regardless of hygiene outcome
   198Ôćĺ- Auto-proceeded to step-04
   199Ôćĺ
   200Ôćĺ### ÔŁî SYSTEM FAILURE:
   201Ôćĺ
   202Ôćĺ- Creating new QMD collections (that's create-skill's responsibility)
   203Ôćĺ- Silently deleting collections without user prompt
   204Ôćĺ- Attempting QMD hygiene for Quick, Forge, or Forge+ tiers
   205Ôćĺ- Displaying "skipped" or "missing" messages for non-Deep tiers
   206Ôćĺ- Halting the workflow due to QMD hygiene failure
   207Ôćĺ- Indexing project directories (old auto-index behavior ÔÇö removed)
   208Ôćĺ- Not proceeding to step-04 after this step
   209Ôćĺ
   210Ôćĺ**Master Rule:** This step ONLY verifies and cleans. It never indexes new content. QMD collection creation is the responsibility of create-skill. Non-Deep tiers skip silently. Always prompt before removing orphaned collections.
   211Ôćĺ

1Ôćĺ# Tier Calculation Rules
     2Ôćĺ
     3Ôćĺ## Tool Detection Commands
     4Ôćĺ
     5Ôćĺ| Tool | Verification Command | What Confirms Availability |
     6Ôćĺ|------|---------------------|---------------------------|
     7Ôćĺ| ast-grep | `ast-grep --version` | Returns version string without error |
     8Ôćĺ| gh | `gh --version` | Returns version string without error |
     9Ôćĺ| qmd | `qmd status` | Returns status indicating initialized and operational |
    10Ôćĺ| ccc | Step A: `ccc --help` Step B: `ccc doctor` | Step A: exits 0 (binary exists). Step B: daemon running, version string, model check OK |
    11Ôćĺ
    12Ôćĺ**Important:** Use verification commands, not existence checks (`which`, `command -v`). A tool must be functional, not just present on PATH. For daemon-based tools (ccc), verify both binary existence and daemon health.
    13Ôćĺ
    14Ôćĺ## Tier Calculation
    15Ôćĺ
    16Ôćĺ| Priority | Tier | Required Tools | Rule |
    17Ôćĺ|----------|------|---------------|------|
    18Ôćĺ| 1 (highest) | Deep | ast-grep + gh + qmd | All three core tools available and functional |
    19Ôćĺ| 2 | Forge+ | ast-grep + ccc | ast-grep AND ccc available, regardless of gh/qmd |
    20Ôćĺ| 3 | Forge | ast-grep | ast-grep available, without ccc/gh/qmd |
    21Ôćĺ| 4 (default) | Quick | None | Default when no tools are available |
    22Ôćĺ
    23Ôćĺ**Evaluation order:** Check Deep first, then Forge+, then Forge. The first match wins. This ensures Deep always takes priority when all tools are present.
    24Ôćĺ
    25Ôćĺ**Override:** If `tier_override` is set in preferences.yaml, use that value instead of calculated tier.
    26Ôćĺ
    27Ôćĺ**Edge cases:**
    28Ôćĺ- gh available but no ast-grep Ôćĺ Quick (gh alone doesn't unlock Forge)
    29Ôćĺ- ast-grep + gh but no qmd Ôćĺ Forge (qmd required for Deep)
    30Ôćĺ- ast-grep + qmd but no gh Ôćĺ Forge (gh required for Deep)
    31Ôćĺ- ccc available but no ast-grep Ôćĺ Quick (ccc alone doesn't unlock Forge+)
    32Ôćĺ- ast-grep + ccc + gh but no qmd Ôćĺ Forge+ (qmd required for Deep)
    33Ôćĺ- ast-grep + gh + qmd + ccc Ôćĺ Deep (Deep subsumes Forge+; ccc enhances transparently)
    34Ôćĺ
    35Ôćĺ## Tier Capability Descriptions
    36Ôćĺ
    37ÔćĺUse these for positive-framing in the report step. Describe what the tier GIVES, never what it lacks.
    38Ôćĺ
    39Ôćĺ### Quick Tier
    40Ôćĺ"Quick tier active. You have fast, template-driven skill generation with package-name resolution. Perfect for getting started quickly."

     8Ôćĺ# Create Skill
     9Ôćĺ
    10Ôćĺ**Goal:** Compile a verified agent skill from a skill-brief.yaml and source code, producing an agentskills.io-compliant SKILL.md with provenance map, evidence report, and progressive disclosure references.
    11Ôćĺ
    12Ôćĺ**Your Role:** You are operating in **Ferris Architect mode** ÔÇö a skill compilation engine performing structural extraction and assembly. This workflow is mostly autonomous with three interaction points ÔÇö one after ecosystem check (if a match is found), one after source extraction (to confirm findings before compilation), and one after content quality review (when tessl produces suggestions). All other steps auto-proceed. Apply zero hallucination tolerance: every instruction in the output must trace to source code with a confidence tier citation.
    13Ôćĺ
    14Ôćĺ## WORKFLOW ARCHITECTURE
    15Ôćĺ
    16Ôćĺ### Core Principles
    17Ôćĺ
    18Ôćĺ- **Micro-file Design**: Each step of the overall goal is a self contained instruction file that you will adhere to 1 file as directed at a time
    19Ôćĺ- **Just-In-Time Loading**: Only 1 current step file will be loaded, read, and executed to completion - never load future step files until told to do so
    20Ôćĺ- **Sequential Enforcement**: Sequence within the step files must be completed in order, no skipping or optimization allowed
    21Ôćĺ- **Tier-Aware Execution**: Steps adapt behavior based on forge tier (Quick/Forge/Forge+/Deep) loaded from sidecar at init
    22Ôćĺ- **Zero Hallucination**: Every claim requires a provenance citation ÔÇö uncitable content is excluded, not guessed
    23Ôćĺ
    24Ôćĺ### Step Processing Rules
    25Ôćĺ
    26Ôćĺ1. **READ COMPLETELY**: Always read the entire step file before taking any action
    27Ôćĺ2. **FOLLOW SEQUENCE**: Execute all numbered sections in order, never deviate
    28Ôćĺ3. **WAIT FOR INPUT**: At confirmation gates (steps 02 and 03), halt and wait for user selection
    29Ôćĺ4. **AUTO-PROCEED**: All other steps proceed to the next automatically after completion
    30Ôćĺ5. **LOAD NEXT**: When directed, load, read entire file, then execute the next step file
    31Ôćĺ
    32Ôćĺ### Critical Rules (NO EXCEPTIONS)
    33Ôćĺ
    34Ôćĺ- ­čŤĹ **NEVER** load multiple step files simultaneously
    35Ôćĺ- ­čôľ **ALWAYS** read entire step file before execution
    36Ôćĺ- ­čÜź **NEVER** skip steps or optimize the sequence
    37Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» **ALWAYS** follow the exact instructions in the step file
    38Ôćĺ- ­čôő **NEVER** create mental todo lists from future steps
    39Ôćĺ- ÔÜĺ´ŞĆ **NEVER** include content in SKILL.md that cannot be cited to source code
    40Ôćĺ
    41Ôćĺ---
    42Ôćĺ
    43Ôćĺ## INITIALIZATION SEQUENCE
    44Ôćĺ
    45Ôćĺ### 1. Module Configuration Loading
    46Ôćĺ
    47ÔćĺLoad and read full config from {project-root}/_bmad/skf/config.yaml and resolve:
