   173Ôćĺ
   174Ôćĺ**Update-skill workflow complete for `{skill_name}`.**"
   175Ôćĺ
   176ÔćĺÔćĺ **No next step.** Workflow ends here.
   177Ôćĺ
   178Ôćĺ---
   179Ôćĺ
   180Ôćĺ## ­čÜĘ SYSTEM SUCCESS/FAILURE METRICS
   181Ôćĺ
   182Ôćĺ### Ôťů SUCCESS:
   183Ôćĺ
   184Ôćĺ- Comprehensive summary displayed with all metrics from the update operation
   185Ôćĺ- Change categories broken down clearly
   186Ôćĺ- Confidence tier breakdown reported
   187Ôćĺ- [MANUAL] preservation status confirmed
   188Ôćĺ- Validation findings presented if any
   189Ôćĺ- Files written listed with paths
   190Ôćĺ- Workflow chaining recommendations provided
   191Ôćĺ- No-change shortcut handled correctly
   192Ôćĺ
   193Ôćĺ### ÔŁî SYSTEM FAILURE:
   194Ôćĺ
   195Ôćĺ- Reporting metrics not backed by actual operations
   196Ôćĺ- Not reporting [MANUAL] preservation status
   197Ôćĺ- Not providing workflow chaining recommendations
   198Ôćĺ- Modifying any files during reporting
   199Ôćĺ- Not handling the no-change shortcut from step 02
   200Ôćĺ
   201Ôćĺ**Master Rule:** Skipping steps, optimizing sequences, or not following exact instructions is FORBIDDEN and constitutes SYSTEM FAILURE.
   202Ôćĺ

1Ôćĺ# Registry Resolution Patterns
     2Ôćĺ
     3Ôćĺ## Package-to-Repo Resolution
     4Ôćĺ
     5ÔćĺWhen the user provides a package name instead of a GitHub URL, use this fallback chain to resolve the source repository.
     6Ôćĺ
     7Ôćĺ### Detection: URL vs Package Name
     8Ôćĺ
     9Ôćĺ- **GitHub URL:** Starts with `https://github.com/` or `github.com/` ÔÇö extract org/repo directly
    10Ôćĺ- **Package name:** Everything else ÔÇö enter resolution chain below
    11Ôćĺ
    12Ôćĺ### Resolution Fallback Chain
    13Ôćĺ
    14ÔćĺTry each registry in order. Stop at first success.
    15Ôćĺ
    16Ôćĺ#### 1. npm Registry (JavaScript/TypeScript)
    17Ôćĺ
    18Ôćĺ```
    19ÔćĺURL: https://registry.npmjs.org/{package_name}
    20ÔćĺField: repository.url
    21ÔćĺFallback field: homepage
    22Ôćĺ```
    23Ôćĺ
    24ÔćĺExtract `repository.url`, strip `git+` prefix and `.git` suffix if present.
    25Ôćĺ
    26Ôćĺ#### 2. PyPI Registry (Python)
    27Ôćĺ
    28Ôćĺ```
    29ÔćĺURL: https://pypi.org/pypi/{package_name}/json
    30ÔćĺField: info.project_urls.Source OR info.project_urls.Repository OR info.home_page
    31Ôćĺ```
    32Ôćĺ
    33ÔćĺCheck `project_urls` keys in order: Source, Repository, Homepage. Filter for GitHub URLs.
    34Ôćĺ
    35Ôćĺ#### 3. crates.io Registry (Rust)
    36Ôćĺ
    37Ôćĺ```
    38ÔćĺURL: https://crates.io/api/v1/crates/{package_name}
    39ÔćĺField: crate.repository
    40Ôćĺ```
    41Ôćĺ
    42ÔćĺDirect `repository` field usually points to GitHub.
    43Ôćĺ
    44Ôćĺ#### 4. Web Search Fallback
    45Ôćĺ
    46ÔćĺIf all registry lookups fail:
    47Ôćĺ
    48Ôćĺ```
    49ÔćĺSearch: "{package_name} github repository"
    50Ôćĺ```
    51Ôćĺ
    52ÔćĺLook for GitHub URL in top results. Verify it matches the package name.
    53Ôćĺ
    54Ôćĺ#### 5. Resolution Failure
    55Ôćĺ
    56ÔćĺIf all methods fail:
    57Ôćĺ
    58Ôćĺ```
    59Ôćĺ"Could not resolve '{package_name}' to a GitHub repository.
    60Ôćĺ
    61ÔćĺPlease provide the GitHub URL directly, or check:
    62Ôćĺ- Is the package name spelled correctly?
    63Ôćĺ- Is it a private package?
    64Ôćĺ- Is the source hosted on a non-GitHub platform?"
    65Ôćĺ```
    66Ôćĺ
    67Ôćĺ**Hard halt** ÔÇö cannot proceed without a resolved source.
    68Ôćĺ
    69Ôćĺ## Language Detection
    70Ôćĺ
    71ÔćĺAfter resolving to a GitHub repo, detect primary language from:
    72Ôćĺ
    73Ôćĺ1. Manifest file presence:
    74Ôćĺ   - `package.json` Ôćĺ JavaScript/TypeScript
    75Ôćĺ   - `pyproject.toml` or `setup.py` Ôćĺ Python
    76Ôćĺ   - `Cargo.toml` Ôćĺ Rust
    77Ôćĺ   - `go.mod` Ôćĺ Go
    78Ôćĺ   - `pom.xml` or `build.gradle` Ôćĺ Java
    79Ôćĺ2. User-provided language hint (overrides detection)
    80Ôćĺ3. GitHub API language field (if accessible)
    81Ôćĺ
    82Ôćĺ## Manifest File Priority
    83Ôćĺ
    84ÔćĺFor each language, the primary manifest to read:
    85Ôćĺ
    86Ôćĺ| Language | Manifest | Key Fields |
    87Ôćĺ|----------|----------|------------|
    88Ôćĺ| JS/TS | package.json | name, version, description, main, exports, dependencies |
    89Ôćĺ| Python | pyproject.toml | project.name, project.version, project.description, project.dependencies |
    90Ôćĺ| Rust | Cargo.toml | package.name, package.version, package.description, dependencies |
    91Ôćĺ| Go | go.mod | module path, require list |
    92Ôćĺ

1Ôćĺ# Unit Detection Heuristics
     2Ôćĺ
     3Ôćĺ## Purpose
     4Ôćĺ
     5ÔćĺRules for identifying discrete skillable units within a project. A "skillable unit" is a self-contained component with clear boundaries that can be documented as an independent skill.
     6Ôćĺ
     7Ôćĺ## Detection Signals
     8Ôćĺ
     9Ôćĺ### Strong Signals (High Confidence)
    10Ôćĺ
    11Ôćĺ| Signal | Description | Example |
    12Ôćĺ|--------|-------------|---------|
    13Ôćĺ| Independent package.json / Cargo.toml / go.mod | Unit has its own dependency manifest | `packages/auth/package.json` |
    14Ôćĺ| Separate entry point | Unit has a main/index file | `services/api/src/index.ts` |
    15Ôćĺ| Docker/service definition | Unit runs as an independent service | `docker-compose.yml` service entry |
    16Ôćĺ| Distinct export surface | Unit exports a public API consumed by others | `src/lib/index.ts` with re-exports |
    17Ôćĺ| Workspace member | Listed in root workspace configuration | `pnpm-workspace.yaml` packages |
    18Ôćĺ
    19Ôćĺ### Moderate Signals (Medium Confidence)
    20Ôćĺ
    21Ôćĺ| Signal | Description | Example |
    22Ôćĺ|--------|-------------|---------|
    23Ôćĺ| Directory depth boundary | Top-level directory with self-contained structure | `src/modules/payments/` |
    24Ôćĺ| Naming convention | Follows organizational naming pattern | `@org/package-name` |
    25Ôćĺ| Separate test suite | Has its own test directory or config | `packages/auth/__tests__/` |
    26Ôćĺ| README.md presence | Has documentation at directory level | `libs/utils/README.md` |
    27Ôćĺ| CI/CD pipeline reference | Referenced in build/deploy configuration | `.github/workflows/deploy-auth.yml` |
    28Ôćĺ
    29Ôćĺ### Weak Signals (Low Confidence ÔÇö Require Corroboration)
    30Ôćĺ
    31Ôćĺ| Signal | Description | Example |
    32Ôćĺ|--------|-------------|---------|
    33Ôćĺ| Large directory | Many files in a subtree | 50+ files under one directory |
    34Ôćĺ| Comment boundaries | Code comments marking sections | `// --- Auth Module ---` |
    35Ôćĺ| Import clustering | Files that import primarily from each other | Tight import graph cluster |
    36Ôćĺ
    37Ôćĺ## Boundary Classification
    38Ôćĺ
    39Ôćĺ### Service Boundary
    40Ôćĺ- Independent deployable unit
    41Ôćĺ- Own process, port, or container
    42Ôćĺ- Clear network interface (REST, gRPC, message queue)
    43Ôćĺ- Scope type: `full-library`
    44Ôćĺ
    45Ôćĺ### Package Boundary
    46Ôćĺ- Workspace member or independently versioned package
    47Ôćĺ- Own dependency manifest
    48Ôćĺ- Exports consumed by other packages
    49Ôćĺ- Scope type: `full-library` or `specific-modules`
    50Ôćĺ
    51Ôćĺ### Module Boundary
    52Ôćĺ- Logical grouping within a single package
    53Ôćĺ- Shared namespace or directory structure
    54Ôćĺ- Internal cohesion, external coupling through defined interface
    55Ôćĺ- Scope type: `specific-modules` or `public-api`
    56Ôćĺ
    57Ôćĺ### Library Boundary
    58Ôćĺ- Third-party dependency with significant project-specific usage patterns
    59Ôćĺ- Custom wrappers, configurations, or integration code
    60Ôćĺ- Scope type: `public-api`
    61Ôćĺ
    62Ôćĺ## Disqualification Rules
    63Ôćĺ
    64ÔćĺDo NOT recommend as a skillable unit if:
    65Ôćĺ
    66Ôćĺ1. **Too small**: Fewer than 3 source files or 100 lines of code
    67Ôćĺ2. **Generated code**: Auto-generated files (protobuf, GraphQL codegen, etc.)
    68Ôćĺ3. **Pure configuration**: Only config files with no logic
    69Ôćĺ4. **Test-only**: Test utilities with no production code
    70Ôćĺ5. **Vendor/dependency**: Third-party code copied into project
    71Ôćĺ6. **Already skilled**: Existing skill found in forge_data_folder (recommend update-skill instead)
    72Ôćĺ
    73Ôćĺ## Script/Asset Detection Signals
    74Ôćĺ
    75ÔćĺDuring per-unit analysis, check for scripts and assets alongside code exports.
    76Ôćĺ
    77Ôćĺ**Script signals:**
    78Ôćĺ
    79Ôćĺ| Strength | Signal | Example |
    80Ôćĺ|----------|--------|---------|
    81Ôćĺ| Strong | Entry point in `package.json` `bin`, Cargo.toml `[[bin]]`, pyproject.toml `[project.scripts]` | `"bin": { "migrate": "scripts/migrate.js" }` |
    82Ôćĺ| Strong | Shebang + executable file | `#!/usr/bin/env python` in `scripts/setup.py` |
    83Ôćĺ| Moderate | File in `scripts/`, `bin/`, `tools/`, `cli/` directory | `scripts/validate.sh` |
    84Ôćĺ| Moderate | CI/CD reference to script | `.github/workflows/test.yml` runs `scripts/test.sh` |
    85Ôćĺ
    86Ôćĺ**Asset signals:**
    87Ôćĺ
    88Ôćĺ| Strength | Signal | Example |
    89Ôćĺ|----------|--------|---------|
    90Ôćĺ| Strong | JSON Schema file with `$schema` key | `schemas/config.schema.json` |
    91Ôćĺ| Strong | Config template with `.example` or `.template` extension | `config.yaml.example` |
    92Ôćĺ| Moderate | File in `assets/`, `templates/`, `schemas/`, `configs/`, `examples/` directory | `templates/report.hbs` |
    93Ôćĺ| Moderate | OpenAPI/GraphQL definition | `openapi.json`, `schema.graphql` |
    94Ôćĺ
    95Ôćĺ**Per-unit output:** Record `has_scripts: boolean`, `has_assets: boolean`, `script_files: string[]`, `asset_files: string[]`.
    96Ôćĺ
    97Ôćĺ**Disqualify:** Generated files (dist/, build/), vendored dependencies, IDE configs (.vscode/, .idea/), binary files (.so, .dll, .jar).
    98Ôćĺ
    99Ôćĺ## Stack Skill Candidate Detection
   100Ôćĺ
   101ÔćĺFlag units as stack skill candidates when:
   102Ôćĺ
   103Ôćĺ1. **Co-import frequency**: Two or more units are imported together in 3+ files
   104Ôćĺ2. **Integration adapter**: A unit exists primarily to bridge two other units
   105Ôćĺ3. **Shared state**: Multiple units read/write to the same data store
   106Ôćĺ4. **Orchestration layer**: A unit coordinates calls across multiple other units
   107Ôćĺ
   108Ôćĺ## Tier-Aware Scanning Depth
   109Ôćĺ
   110Ôćĺ| Forge Tier | Scanning Approach |
   111Ôćĺ|------------|-------------------|
   112Ôćĺ| Quick | File structure analysis: directory trees, manifest files, entry points, naming conventions |
   113Ôćĺ| Forge | AST analysis: export surfaces, import graphs, dependency trees, type hierarchies |
   114Ôćĺ| Forge+ | AST + CCC: semantic file pre-ranking before structural analysis, CCC signals for relevance scoring |
   115Ôćĺ| Deep | AST + QMD: temporal evolution, refactoring patterns, semantic relationships, architectural drift |
   116Ôćĺ

1Ôćĺ# Context Snippet Format (Vercel-Aligned Indexed Format)
     2Ôćĺ
     3Ôćĺ## Format Rules
     4Ôćĺ
     5Ôćĺ- Indexed pipe-delimited format per skill ÔÇö retrieval instruction + file map + inline gotchas
     6Ôćĺ- ~80-120 tokens per skill target (up from ~50-80, justified by Vercel research)
     7Ôćĺ- T1-now content only (AST-current, no annotations)
     8Ôćĺ- Line 2 (IMPORTANT) is the RETRIEVAL INSTRUCTION ÔÇö always present, tells agent to read SKILL.md
     9Ôćĺ- Section anchors (`#quick-start`, `#key-types`) must match actual SKILL.md heading slugs
    10Ôćĺ- gotchas line prevents common mistakes without requiring a file read
    11Ôćĺ- Version comes from source detection, not brief default
    12Ôćĺ
    13Ôćĺ## Single Skill Snippet Template
    14Ôćĺ
    15Ôćĺ```markdown
    16Ôćĺ[{skill-name} v{version}]|root: skills/{skill-name}/
    17Ôćĺ|IMPORTANT: {skill-name} v{version} ÔÇö read SKILL.md before writing {skill-name} code. Do NOT rely on training data.
    18Ôćĺ|quick-start:{SKILL.md#quick-start}
    19Ôćĺ|api: {top exports with () for functions, comma-separated}
    20Ôćĺ|key-types:{SKILL.md#key-types} ÔÇö {inline summary of most important type values}
    21Ôćĺ|gotchas: {2-3 most critical pitfalls or breaking changes, inline}
    22Ôćĺ```
    23Ôćĺ
    24Ôćĺ- **Line 1:** Skill name + version + root path ÔÇö version signals training data staleness
    25Ôćĺ- **Line 2 (IMPORTANT):** Retrieval instruction ÔÇö always present
    26Ôćĺ- **Line 3 (quick-start):** Anchor pointer to Quick Start section
    27Ôćĺ- **Line 4 (api):** Top exports from metadata.json `exports` array (up to 10, with `()` for functions)
    28Ôćĺ- **Line 5 (key-types):** Anchor pointer + inline summary of most important type/enum values
    29Ôćĺ- **Line 6 (gotchas):** 2-3 most critical pitfalls ÔÇö derived from T2-future annotations, async requirements, breaking changes
    30Ôćĺ
    31Ôćĺ## Stack Skill Snippet Template
    32Ôćĺ
    33Ôćĺ```markdown
    34Ôćĺ[{project}-stack v{version}]|root: skills/{project}-stack/
    35Ôćĺ|IMPORTANT: {project}-stack ÔÇö read SKILL.md before writing integration code. Do NOT rely on training data.
    36Ôćĺ|stack: {dep-1}@{v1}, {dep-2}@{v2}, {dep-3}@{v3}
    37Ôćĺ|integrations: {pattern-1}, {pattern-2}
    38Ôćĺ|gotchas: {2-3 most critical integration pitfalls, inline}
    39Ôćĺ```
    40Ôćĺ
    41Ôćĺ## Rules
    42Ôćĺ
    43Ôćĺ- **api line**: Top exports from metadata.json `exports` array (up to 10 for Deep tier, up to 5 for all other tiers). Append `()` to function names. Comma-separated.
    44Ôćĺ- **key-types line**: Anchor to `#key-types` section + inline summary (~10 words) of the most important type values
    45Ôćĺ- **gotchas line**: Derived from: T2-future annotations (breaking changes), async requirements, version-specific behavior. If no gotchas available, omit the line.
    46Ôćĺ- **stack line**: Component versions from metadata.json `components` for stack skills
    47Ôćĺ- **integrations line**: Co-import patterns from metadata.json `integrations` for stack skills
    48Ôćĺ- If fewer exports than the limit, list all available
    49Ôćĺ- If no exports data available, omit the api line
    50Ôćĺ- Section anchors must be verified against actual SKILL.md headings during generation. For split-body skills (where `references/` exists and `## Full` headings are stubs), if a heading is missing from SKILL.md, rewrite the anchor to point to the reference file path (preferred). Omit the anchor line only if the heading cannot be found in either SKILL.md or `references/*.md`
    51Ôćĺ- Skill path is relative to project root
    52Ôćĺ

1Ôćĺ# [MANUAL] Section Rules
     2Ôćĺ
     3Ôćĺ## Detection Pattern
     4Ôćĺ
     5Ôćĺ[MANUAL] sections are developer-authored content blocks within generated SKILL.md files that must survive regeneration.
     6Ôćĺ
     7Ôćĺ### Identification
     8Ôćĺ
     9ÔćĺA [MANUAL] section is delimited by markers in the SKILL.md:
    10Ôćĺ
    11Ôćĺ```markdown
    12Ôćĺ<!-- [MANUAL:section-name] -->
    13ÔćĺDeveloper-authored content here.
    14ÔćĺThis content was added by the developer after skill generation.
    15ÔćĺIt must be preserved during any update operation.
    16Ôćĺ<!-- [/MANUAL:section-name] -->
    17Ôćĺ```
    18Ôćĺ
    19Ôćĺ### Rules
    20Ôćĺ
    21Ôćĺ1. **Never modify content between [MANUAL] markers** ÔÇö treat as immutable
    22Ôćĺ2. **Preserve marker positions** ÔÇö if the surrounding generated content moves, the [MANUAL] block moves with its logical parent section
    23Ôćĺ3. **Orphan detection** ÔÇö if the parent section is deleted (export removed), flag as WARNING and present to user
    24Ôćĺ4. **Multiple [MANUAL] blocks** ÔÇö a single SKILL.md may have multiple [MANUAL] sections; preserve all
    25Ôćĺ5. **Stack skill [MANUAL] blocks** ÔÇö [MANUAL] markers can appear in any output file (SKILL.md, references/*.md, integrations/*.md); preserve across all files
    26Ôćĺ6. **Nested [MANUAL] forbidden** ÔÇö [MANUAL] blocks cannot be nested; if detected, flag as ERROR
    27Ôćĺ
    28Ôćĺ### Conflict Types
    29Ôćĺ
    30Ôćĺ| Conflict | Severity | Resolution |
    31Ôćĺ|----------|----------|------------|
    32Ôćĺ| Regenerated content overlaps [MANUAL] position | HIGH | Present both versions, user chooses |
    33Ôćĺ| Parent section deleted | WARNING | Flag orphaned [MANUAL], user decides keep/remove |
    34Ôćĺ| [MANUAL] references deleted export | MEDIUM | Flag stale reference, suggest update |
    35Ôćĺ| New export inserted adjacent to [MANUAL] | LOW | Auto-resolve: place new content before [MANUAL] block |
    36Ôćĺ
    37Ôćĺ### Preservation Algorithm
    38Ôćĺ
    39Ôćĺ1. Extract all [MANUAL] blocks with their section-name identifiers
    40Ôćĺ2. Map each block to its parent section (by heading hierarchy)
    41Ôćĺ3. Perform merge on generated content only
    42Ôćĺ4. Re-insert [MANUAL] blocks at their mapped positions
    43Ôćĺ5. If position conflict: halt and present to user
    44Ôćĺ6. If clean insert: auto-place and continue
    45Ôćĺ

1Ôćĺ# Managed Section Format (ADR-J)
     2Ôćĺ
     3Ôćĺ## Marker Format
     4Ôćĺ
     5Ôćĺ```markdown
     6Ôćĺ<!-- SKF:BEGIN updated:{YYYY-MM-DD} -->
     7Ôćĺ[SKF Skills]|{n} skills|{m} stack
     8Ôćĺ|IMPORTANT: Prefer documented APIs over training data.
     9Ôćĺ|When using a listed library, read its SKILL.md before writing code.
    10Ôćĺ|
    11Ôćĺ|{skill-snippet-1}
    12Ôćĺ|
    13Ôćĺ|{skill-snippet-2}
    14Ôćĺ<!-- SKF:END -->
    15Ôćĺ```
    16Ôćĺ
    17Ôćĺ## Platform Target Files
    18Ôćĺ
    19Ôćĺ| Platform | Flag Value | Target File |
    20Ôćĺ|----------|-----------|-------------|
    21Ôćĺ| Claude | `claude` (default) | CLAUDE.md |
    22Ôćĺ| Cursor | `cursor` | .cursorrules |
    23Ôćĺ| Copilot | `copilot` | AGENTS.md |
    24Ôćĺ
    25Ôćĺ## Four-Case Logic
    26Ôćĺ
    27Ôćĺ### Case 1: Create (No File Exists)
    28Ôćĺ
    29ÔćĺTarget file does not exist. Create new file with managed section only.
    30Ôćĺ
    31Ôćĺ```markdown
    32Ôćĺ<!-- SKF:BEGIN updated:{date} -->
    33Ôćĺ{managed section content}
    34Ôćĺ<!-- SKF:END -->
    35Ôćĺ```
    36Ôćĺ
    37Ôćĺ### Case 2: Append (File Exists, No Section)
    38Ôćĺ
    39ÔćĺTarget file exists but contains no `<!-- SKF:BEGIN` marker. Append managed section at end of file.
    40Ôćĺ
    41Ôćĺ1. Read existing file content
    42Ôćĺ2. Append two blank lines
    43Ôćĺ3. Append managed section with markers
    44Ôćĺ4. Write file
    45Ôćĺ
    46Ôćĺ### Case 3: Regenerate (Existing Section)
    47Ôćĺ
    48ÔćĺTarget file contains `<!-- SKF:BEGIN` and `<!-- SKF:END -->` markers. Replace content between markers.
    49Ôćĺ
    50Ôćĺ1. Read existing file content
    51Ôćĺ2. Find `<!-- SKF:BEGIN` line (preserve everything before it)
    52Ôćĺ3. Find `<!-- SKF:END -->` line (preserve everything after it)
    53Ôćĺ4. Replace everything between markers (inclusive) with new managed section
    54Ôćĺ5. Write file
    55Ôćĺ
    56Ôćĺ### Case 4: Malformed Markers (Halt)
    57Ôćĺ
    58ÔćĺTarget file contains `<!-- SKF:BEGIN` but no matching `<!-- SKF:END -->` marker. This indicates a corrupted managed section.
    59Ôćĺ
    60Ôćĺ1. Halt workflow with error: "Malformed managed section ÔÇö `<!-- SKF:BEGIN` found but no `<!-- SKF:END -->`. Fix the markers manually before re-running export."
    61Ôćĺ2. Do not modify the file
    62Ôćĺ
    63Ôćĺ## Regeneration: Full Index Rebuild
    64Ôćĺ
    65ÔćĺWhen regenerating (Case 3), rebuild the COMPLETE skill index:
    66Ôćĺ
    67Ôćĺ1. Scan `{skills_output_folder}/*/context-snippet.md` for all exported skills
    68Ôćĺ2. Count total skills and stack skills
    69Ôćĺ3. Assemble all snippets into managed section
    70Ôćĺ4. Sort alphabetically by skill name
    71Ôćĺ5. Update the header line with correct counts
    72Ôćĺ
    73Ôćĺ## Safety Rules
    74Ôćĺ
    75Ôćĺ- NEVER modify content outside the `<!-- SKF:BEGIN/END -->` markers
    76Ôćĺ- ALWAYS preserve existing file content above and below markers
    77Ôćĺ- ALWAYS verify file was written correctly after write
    78Ôćĺ- If write fails, report error ÔÇö do not attempt partial writes
    79Ôćĺ

1Ôćĺ# Merge Conflict Rules
     2Ôćĺ
     3Ôćĺ## Change Categories
     4Ôćĺ
     5Ôćĺ### Category 1: Modified Exports (signature/type change)
     6Ôćĺ
     7Ôćĺ- **Action:** Re-extract affected export with fresh AST analysis
     8Ôćĺ- **Merge:** Replace generated content for that export; preserve adjacent [MANUAL] blocks
     9Ôćĺ- **Confidence:** Same tier as original (T1/T1-low/T2)
    10Ôćĺ
    11Ôćĺ### Category 2: New Exports (added since last generation)
    12Ôćĺ
    13Ôćĺ- **Action:** Extract new export with full AST analysis
    14Ôćĺ- **Merge:** Append to appropriate section based on export type (function, type, class, constant)
    15Ôćĺ- **Confidence:** Label with extraction tier confidence
    16Ôćĺ- **[MANUAL] impact:** None ÔÇö new content, no existing [MANUAL] blocks
    17Ôćĺ
    18Ôćĺ### Category 3: Deleted Exports (removed from source)
    19Ôćĺ
    20Ôćĺ- **Action:** Remove generated content for that export
    21Ôćĺ- **Merge:** Check for attached [MANUAL] blocks before deletion
    22Ôćĺ- **If [MANUAL] attached:** Flag as orphan, present to user for decision
    23Ôćĺ- **If no [MANUAL]:** Auto-remove generated content
    24Ôćĺ
    25Ôćĺ### Category 4: Moved Exports (file relocated)
    26Ôćĺ
    27Ôćĺ- **Action:** Update provenance map file references
    28Ôćĺ- **Merge:** Update file:line citations in generated content
    29Ôćĺ- **Confidence:** Retain original tier if AST structure unchanged; downgrade to T1-low if structure changed
    30Ôćĺ- **[MANUAL] impact:** Preserve ÔÇö content unchanged, only provenance metadata updates
    31Ôćĺ
    32Ôćĺ### Category 5: Renamed Exports (identifier changed)
    33Ôćĺ
    34Ôćĺ- **Action:** Re-extract with new identifier
    35Ôćĺ- **Merge:** Replace old identifier references in generated content
    36Ôćĺ- **[MANUAL] impact:** Flag if [MANUAL] blocks reference old identifier name
    37Ôćĺ
    38Ôćĺ## Merge Priority Order
    39Ôćĺ
    40Ôćĺ1. Process deleted exports first (remove generated content, flag [MANUAL] orphans)
    41Ôćĺ2. Process moved exports second (update references only)
    42Ôćĺ3. Process renamed exports third (update identifiers)
    43Ôćĺ4. Process modified exports fourth (re-extract and replace)
    44Ôćĺ5. Process new exports last (append to sections)
    45Ôćĺ
    46Ôćĺ## Stack Skill Merge Rules
    47Ôćĺ
    48ÔćĺFor stack skills with multi-file outputs:
    49Ôćĺ
    50Ôćĺ1. **SKILL.md:** Apply standard merge rules above
    51Ôćĺ2. **references/{library}.md:** Merge per-library, preserving [MANUAL] blocks within each
    52Ôćĺ3. **references/integrations/{pair}.md:** Merge per-integration-pair
    53Ôćĺ4. **metadata.json:** Regenerate completely (no [MANUAL] support in JSON)
    54Ôćĺ5. **context-snippet.md:** Regenerate completely (no [MANUAL] support ÔÇö too concise)
    55Ôćĺ
    56Ôćĺ## Conflict Resolution Strategies
    57Ôćĺ
    58Ôćĺ| Strategy | When | Action |
    59Ôćĺ|----------|------|--------|
    60Ôćĺ| Auto-resolve | No [MANUAL] conflicts, clean merge | Proceed without user input |
    61Ôćĺ| User-resolve | [MANUAL] conflicts detected | Halt, present conflicts, require decision |
    62Ôćĺ| Abort | Critical structural incompatibility | Stop workflow, recommend full re-creation |
    63Ôćĺ

1Ôćĺ# Remote Source Resolution (Forge/Deep Tier)
     2Ôćĺ
     3ÔćĺIf `source_root` is a local path: proceed with the tier-appropriate strategy as normal.
     4Ôćĺ
     5ÔćĺIf `source_root` (from metadata.json) is a remote URL (GitHub URL or owner/repo format) AND tier is Forge or Deep:
     6Ôćĺ
     7Ôćĺ1. **Check `git` availability:** Verify `git` is functional (`git --version`). If `git` is not available, skip to the fallback warning below.
     8Ôćĺ
     9Ôćĺ2. **Ephemeral sparse clone:** Clone only the changed files from the change manifest to a system temp path. Note: at this point in the flow, `{source_root}` is known to be a remote URL (the local-path case was already handled above).
    10Ôćĺ
    11Ôćĺ   ```
    12Ôćĺ   temp_path = {system_temp}/skf-ephemeral-{skill-name}-{timestamp}/
    13Ôćĺ   git clone --depth 1 --single-branch --filter=blob:none --sparse {source_root} {temp_path}
    14Ôćĺ   git -C {temp_path} sparse-checkout set --skip-checks {changed_files_from_manifest}
    15Ôćĺ   ```
    16Ôćĺ
    17Ôćĺ   **Note:** `--skip-checks` is required because `changed_files_from_manifest` contains individual file paths (e.g., `src/core/parser.py`), not directories. Without this flag, `git sparse-checkout set` rejects non-directory entries.
    18Ôćĺ
    19Ôćĺ   No `--branch` flag is used ÔÇö the clone targets the remote's default branch, which must match the branch used during the original [CS] Create Skill run. This scopes the clone to only the files identified in step-02's change manifest, avoiding a full repository download.
    20Ôćĺ
    21Ôćĺ3. **If clone succeeds:** Update the working source path to `{temp_path}` for all subsequent AST operations in this step. Proceed with the **Forge tier** extraction strategy below. Mark `ephemeral_clone_active = true` for cleanup.
    22Ôćĺ
    23Ôćĺ4. **If clone fails (network error, auth failure, timeout):**
    24Ôćĺ
    25Ôćĺ   Warning message: "Ephemeral clone of `{source_root}` failed: {error}. Degrading to source reading (T1-low) for this run. For T1 (AST-verified) confidence, clone the repository locally and re-run [CS] Create Skill with the local path, then re-run this update."
    26Ôćĺ
    27Ôćĺ   Override the extraction strategy to Quick tier for this run. Note the degradation reason in context for the evidence report.
    28Ôćĺ
    29Ôćĺ## Ephemeral Clone Cleanup
    30Ôćĺ
    31ÔćĺAfter extraction is complete for all files in scope (whether successful or partially failed), before presenting the extraction summary, if `ephemeral_clone_active`, delete the `{temp_path}` directory. Log: "Ephemeral source clone cleaned up." This ensures cleanup runs even if some extractions failed, as long as the step itself is still executing.
    32Ôćĺ
    33Ôćĺ## Version Reconciliation
    34Ôćĺ
    35ÔćĺAfter the source path is accessible, check whether the source version has changed since the original skill was created. Look for the version file matching the detected language (e.g., `pyproject.toml`, `package.json`, `Cargo.toml`). If the source version differs from the current `metadata.json` version, record `source_version_detected` in context for step-06 to use when updating `metadata.json`. No warning needed here ÔÇö step-06 handles the version update.
    36Ôćĺ
    37Ôćĺ## AST Tool Unavailability (Local Source)
    38Ôćĺ
    39ÔćĺIf AST tool is unavailable at Forge/Deep tier with local source:
    40Ôćĺ
    41ÔćĺWarning message: "AST tools are unavailable ÔÇö extraction will use source reading (T1-low). Run [SF] Setup Forge to detect and configure AST tools for T1 confidence."
    42Ôćĺ
    43ÔćĺDegrade to Quick tier extraction. Note the degradation reason in context for the evidence report.
    44Ôćĺ

1Ôćĺ---
     2Ôćĺname: 'step-01-detect-and-tier'
     3Ôćĺdescription: 'Detect available tools and determine forge capability tier'
     4Ôćĺ
     5ÔćĺnextStepFile: './step-01b-ccc-index.md'
     6ÔćĺtierRulesData: '../data/tier-rules.md'
     7Ôćĺ---
     8Ôćĺ
     9Ôćĺ# Step 1: Detect Tools and Determine Tier
    10Ôćĺ
    11Ôćĺ## STEP GOAL:
    12Ôćĺ
    13ÔćĺVerify availability of the four forge tools (ast-grep, gh, qmd, ccc), read any existing configuration for re-run comparison, check for tier override, and calculate the capability tier.
    14Ôćĺ
    15Ôćĺ## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES (READ FIRST):
    16Ôćĺ
    17Ôćĺ### Universal Rules:
    18Ôćĺ
    19Ôćĺ- ­čôľ CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action
    20Ôćĺ- ­čöä CRITICAL: When loading next step, ensure entire file is read
    21Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Execute all operations autonomously ÔÇö no user interaction
    22Ôćĺ
    23Ôćĺ### Role Reinforcement:
    24Ôćĺ
    25Ôćĺ- Ôťů You are a system executor performing environment resolution
    26Ôćĺ- Ôťů Tool detection must be empirical ÔÇö run actual commands, never assume
    27Ôćĺ- Ôťů Record exact results (version strings, error messages)
    28Ôćĺ
    29Ôćĺ### Step-Specific Rules:
    30Ôćĺ
    31Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Focus only on tool detection and tier calculation
    32Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to write any files ÔÇö that is step-02's job
    33Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to skip any tool check ÔÇö all 4 must be verified
    34Ôćĺ- ­čĺČ Tool command failures are NOT errors ÔÇö they indicate unavailability
    35Ôćĺ
    36Ôćĺ## EXECUTION PROTOCOLS:
    37Ôćĺ
    38Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Follow the MANDATORY SEQUENCE exactly
    39Ôćĺ- ­čĺż Store all results in memory for step-02
    40Ôćĺ- ­čôľ Load tier-rules.md for calculation reference
    41Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to proceed without checking all 4 tools
    42Ôćĺ
    43Ôćĺ## CONTEXT BOUNDARIES:
    44Ôćĺ
    45Ôćĺ- This is the first step ÔÇö no prior context exists
    46Ôćĺ- Available: forge-tier.yaml and preferences.yaml may exist from prior runs
    47Ôćĺ- Focus: tool verification and tier calculation only
    48Ôćĺ- Produces: `{detected_tools}`, `{calculated_tier}`, `{previous_tier}`, `{tier_override}` for downstream steps
    49Ôćĺ- Dependencies: none ÔÇö this step bootstraps everything
    50Ôćĺ
    51Ôćĺ## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
    52Ôćĺ
    53Ôćĺ**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise.
    54Ôćĺ
    55Ôćĺ### 1. Load Tier Rules
    56Ôćĺ
    57ÔćĺLoad and read {tierRulesData} for the tool detection commands and tier calculation logic.
    58Ôćĺ
    59Ôćĺ### 2. Check for Existing Configuration (Re-run Detection)
    60Ôćĺ
    61Ôćĺ**Read existing forge-tier.yaml** at `{project-root}/_bmad/_memory/forger-sidecar/forge-tier.yaml`:
    62Ôćĺ- If exists: store the current `tier` value as `{previous_tier}` and `tier_detected_at` as `{previous_detection_date}`
    63Ôćĺ- If not found: set `{previous_tier}` to null (first run)
    64Ôćĺ
    65Ôćĺ**Read existing preferences.yaml** at `{project-root}/_bmad/_memory/forger-sidecar/preferences.yaml`:
    66Ôćĺ- If exists: check for `tier_override` value
    67Ôćĺ- If not found: set `{tier_override}` to null
    68Ôćĺ
    69Ôćĺ### 3. Verify Tool: ast-grep
    70Ôćĺ
    71ÔćĺRun: `ast-grep --version`
    72Ôćĺ
    73Ôćĺ- If succeeds: record `{ast_grep: true}` and store version string
    74Ôćĺ- If fails (command not found or error): record `{ast_grep: false}`
    75Ôćĺ
    76Ôćĺ### 4. Verify Tool: gh
    77Ôćĺ
    78ÔćĺRun: `gh --version`
    79Ôćĺ
    80Ôćĺ- If succeeds: record `{gh_cli: true}` and store version string
    81Ôćĺ- If fails: record `{gh_cli: false}`
    82Ôćĺ
    83Ôćĺ### 5. Verify Tool: qmd
    84Ôćĺ
    85ÔćĺRun: `qmd status`
    86Ôćĺ
    87Ôćĺ- If succeeds and indicates operational: record `{qmd: true}`
    88Ôćĺ- If fails or indicates not initialized: record `{qmd: false}`
    89Ôćĺ
    90Ôćĺ### 6. Check Optional: Security Scan (SNYK_TOKEN)
    91Ôćĺ
    92ÔćĺCheck if the `SNYK_TOKEN` environment variable is set:
    93Ôćĺ
    94Ôćĺ- If `SNYK_TOKEN` is non-empty: record `{security_scan: true}`
    95Ôćĺ- If `SNYK_TOKEN` is empty or unset: record `{security_scan: false}`
    96Ôćĺ
    97ÔćĺThis is informational only ÔÇö security scan availability does NOT affect the tier level. It is recorded in forge-tier.yaml so that create-skill's validation step can report actionable guidance when security scanning is unavailable.
    98Ôćĺ
    99Ôćĺ### 7. Verify Tool: ccc (cocoindex-code)
   100Ôćĺ
   101Ôćĺ**Step A ÔÇö Binary existence:** Run `ccc --help`
   102Ôćĺ
   103Ôćĺ- If exits 0: binary confirmed. Continue to Step B.
   104Ôćĺ- If fails (command not found or error): record `{ccc: false}`. Skip Step B.
   105Ôćĺ
   106Ôćĺ**Step B ÔÇö Daemon health:** Run `ccc doctor`
   107Ôćĺ
   108Ôćĺ- If daemon is running and model check OK: record `{ccc: true, ccc_daemon: "healthy"}` and store version string from output
   109Ôćĺ- If daemon is not running: record `{ccc: true, ccc_daemon: "stopped"}` ÔÇö binary available, daemon needs starting. Step-01b will handle this.
   110Ôćĺ- If error or timeout: record `{ccc: true, ccc_daemon: "error"}` ÔÇö binary works but daemon has issues.
   111Ôćĺ
   112Ôćĺccc availability gates the Forge+ tier and enhances Deep tier when present.
   113Ôćĺ
   114Ôćĺ### 8. Calculate Tier
   115Ôćĺ
   116Ôćĺ**If `{tier_override}` is set and valid (Quick, Forge, Forge+, or Deep):**
   117Ôćĺ- Use `{tier_override}` as `{calculated_tier}`
   118Ôćĺ- Note that override is active for the report step
   119Ôćĺ
   120Ôćĺ**If no override, apply tier rules from {tierRulesData} in order ÔÇö the first matching rule wins. Do not continue checking once a match is found:**
   121Ôćĺ- `{ast_grep}` AND `{gh_cli}` AND `{qmd}` all true Ôćĺ **Deep**
   122Ôćĺ- `{ast_grep}` AND `{ccc}` both true (regardless of gh/qmd) Ôćĺ **Forge+**
   123Ôćĺ- `{ast_grep}` true (regardless of ccc/gh/qmd) Ôćĺ **Forge**
   124Ôćĺ- Otherwise Ôćĺ **Quick**
   125Ôćĺ
   126Ôćĺ**If `{tier_override}` is set but invalid:** ignore it, use detected tier, flag for warning in report.
   127Ôćĺ
   128Ôćĺ### 9. Auto-Proceed
   129Ôćĺ
   130Ôćĺ"**Proceeding to CCC index check...**"
   131Ôćĺ
   132Ôćĺ#### Menu Handling Logic:
   133Ôćĺ
   134Ôćĺ- After tier calculation is complete, immediately load, read entire file, then execute {nextStepFile}
   135Ôćĺ
   136Ôćĺ#### EXECUTION RULES:
   137Ôćĺ
   138Ôćĺ- This is an auto-proceed step with no user choices
   139Ôćĺ- Proceed directly to next step after detection and calculation
   140Ôćĺ
   141Ôćĺ## CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE
   142Ôćĺ
   143ÔćĺONLY WHEN all 4 core tools have been verified, optional security scan checked, and the tier calculated will you load and read fully `{nextStepFile}` to execute the CCC index check step.
   144Ôćĺ
   145Ôćĺ---
   146Ôćĺ
   147Ôćĺ## ­čÜĘ SYSTEM SUCCESS/FAILURE METRICS
   148Ôćĺ
   149Ôćĺ### Ôťů SUCCESS:
   150Ôćĺ
   151Ôćĺ- All 4 tools checked via verification commands (not existence checks)
   152Ôćĺ- Existing forge-tier.yaml read for re-run comparison (if present)
   153Ôćĺ- Existing preferences.yaml read for tier_override (if present)
   154Ôćĺ- Tier correctly calculated from tool results or override applied
   155Ôćĺ- All results stored in context for step-01b and step-02
   156Ôćĺ- Auto-proceeded to step-01b
   157Ôćĺ
   158Ôćĺ### ÔŁî SYSTEM FAILURE:
   159Ôćĺ
   160Ôćĺ- Skipping any tool verification (ast-grep, gh, qmd, ccc ÔÇö all 4 must be checked)
   161Ôćĺ- Using `which` or `command -v` instead of verification commands
   162Ôćĺ- Assuming tool availability without running the command
   163Ôćĺ- Writing files in this step (that is step-02)
   164Ôćĺ- Not checking for existing configuration on re-runs
   165Ôćĺ- Not checking ccc availability (both binary and daemon health)
   166Ôćĺ
   167Ôćĺ**Master Rule:** Every tool MUST be verified empirically. No assumptions, no shortcuts.
   168Ôćĺ

1Ôćĺ---
     2Ôćĺname: 'step-01b-ccc-index'
     3Ôćĺdescription: 'Ensure ccc project index exists ÔÇö Forge+ and Deep tiers when ccc is available'
     4Ôćĺ
     5ÔćĺnextStepFile: './step-02-write-config.md'
     6Ôćĺ---
     7Ôćĺ
     8Ôćĺ# Step 1b: CCC Index Verification
     9Ôćĺ
    10Ôćĺ## STEP GOAL:
    11Ôćĺ
    12ÔćĺIf ccc is available (`{ccc: true}` from step-01), verify that the ccc index exists for the project root. If no index exists, create it. Store index state in context for step-02 to write into forge-tier.yaml.
    13Ôćĺ
    14ÔćĺFor Quick and Forge tiers, or when ccc is unavailable, skip silently and proceed.
    15Ôćĺ
    16Ôćĺ## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES (READ FIRST):
    17Ôćĺ
    18Ôćĺ### Universal Rules:
    19Ôćĺ
    20Ôćĺ- ­čôľ CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action
    21Ôćĺ- ­čöä CRITICAL: When loading next step, ensure entire file is read
    22Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Execute all operations autonomously ÔÇö no user interaction
    23Ôćĺ
    24Ôćĺ### Role Reinforcement:
    25Ôćĺ
    26Ôćĺ- Ôťů You are a system executor verifying the ccc index state
    27Ôćĺ- Ôťů ccc indexing failure is never a workflow error ÔÇö degrade gracefully
    28Ôćĺ- Ôťů No negative messaging for tiers that skip this step
    29Ôćĺ
    30Ôćĺ### Step-Specific Rules:
    31Ôćĺ
    32Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Focus only on ccc index verification and creation
    33Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to display skip messages for Quick/Forge tiers
    34Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to fail the workflow if ccc indexing fails
    35Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to re-index if ccc index already exists and is fresh (< staleness threshold)
    36Ôćĺ
    37Ôćĺ## EXECUTION PROTOCOLS:
    38Ôćĺ
    39Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Follow the MANDATORY SEQUENCE exactly
    40Ôćĺ- ­čĺż Store ccc index state in context for step-02
    41Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to write forge-tier.yaml ÔÇö that is step-02's job
    42Ôćĺ
    43Ôćĺ## CONTEXT BOUNDARIES:
    44Ôćĺ
    45Ôćĺ- Available: {ccc} boolean and {ccc_daemon} status from step-01
    46Ôćĺ- Available: {calculated_tier} from step-01
    47Ôćĺ- Available: existing forge-tier.yaml may contain prior `ccc_index` state
    48Ôćĺ- Focus: ccc index state verification and creation only
    49Ôćĺ- Dependencies: step-01 must have completed with tool detection results
    50Ôćĺ
    51Ôćĺ## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
    52Ôćĺ
    53Ôćĺ**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise.
    54Ôćĺ
    55Ôćĺ### 1. Check Eligibility
    56Ôćĺ
    57ÔćĺRead `{ccc}` from step-01 context.
    58Ôćĺ
    59Ôćĺ**If `{ccc}` is false:** Set `{ccc_index_result: "none", ccc_indexed_path: null, ccc_last_indexed: null}`. Proceed directly to section 4 (Auto-Proceed) ÔÇö no output, no messaging.
    60Ôćĺ
    61Ôćĺ**If `{ccc}` is true:** Continue to section 2.
    62Ôćĺ
    63Ôćĺ### 2. Check Existing Index State
    64Ôćĺ
    65ÔćĺRead existing forge-tier.yaml at `{project-root}/_bmad/_memory/forger-sidecar/forge-tier.yaml` (if it exists from a previous run).
    66Ôćĺ
    67ÔćĺCheck the `ccc_index` section:
    68Ôćĺ- If `ccc_index.indexed_path` matches `{project-root}` AND `ccc_index.status` is `"fresh"`:
    69Ôćĺ  - Check freshness: if `ccc_index.last_indexed` is within `staleness_threshold_hours` (default 24h) of now Ôćĺ index is fresh
    70Ôćĺ  - Store `{ccc_index_result: "fresh", ccc_indexed_path: {project-root}, ccc_last_indexed: {existing timestamp}}`
    71Ôćĺ  - Proceed to section 4
    72Ôćĺ
    73Ôćĺ- If `ccc_index.indexed_path` matches `{project-root}` but timestamp is older than threshold:
    74Ôćĺ  - Note index is stale ÔÇö proceed to section 3 for re-index (section 3 will overwrite `ccc_index_result` to `"created"` or `"failed"`)
    75Ôćĺ
    76Ôćĺ- If `ccc_index` is missing, has null values, or path doesn't match:
    77Ôćĺ  - Proceed to section 3 for initial index
    78Ôćĺ
    79Ôćĺ### 3. Create or Refresh CCC Index
    80Ôćĺ
    81Ôćĺ**If `{ccc_daemon}` is `"stopped"` or undefined (healthy daemon where no explicit state was recorded):**
    82Ôćĺ
    83ÔćĺThe `ccc index` command auto-starts the daemon when needed. Proceed with indexing below.
    84Ôćĺ
    85Ôćĺ**If `{ccc_daemon}` is `"error"`:**
    86Ôćĺ
    87ÔćĺAttempt indexing anyway ÔÇö errors will be caught below.
    88Ôćĺ
    89ÔćĺRun:
    90Ôćĺ```bash
    91Ôćĺccc init {project-root}
    92Ôćĺ```
    93Ôćĺ
    94Ôćĺ**If init fails** (project may already be initialized): continue ÔÇö this is not an error.
    95Ôćĺ
    96ÔćĺThen run:
    97Ôćĺ```bash
    98Ôćĺccc index
    99Ôćĺ```
   100Ôćĺ
   101Ôćĺ**If succeeds:**
   102Ôćĺ- Run `ccc status` to get file count
   103Ôćĺ- Store `{ccc_index_result: "created", ccc_indexed_path: {project-root}, ccc_last_indexed: {current ISO timestamp}, ccc_file_count: {count from status}}`
   104Ôćĺ- Display: "**CCC index created.** {file_count} files indexed for semantic discovery."
   105Ôćĺ
   106Ôćĺ**If fails:**
   107Ôćĺ- Store `{ccc_index_result: "failed", ccc_indexed_path: null, ccc_last_indexed: null}`
   108Ôćĺ- Display: "CCC indexing failed: {error}. Extraction will use direct AST scanning ÔÇö semantic pre-ranking unavailable this session."
   109Ôćĺ- Continue ÔÇö this is NOT a workflow error
   110Ôćĺ
   111Ôćĺ### 4. Auto-Proceed
   112Ôćĺ
   113Ôćĺ"**Proceeding to write configuration...**"
   114Ôćĺ
   115Ôćĺ#### Menu Handling Logic:
   116Ôćĺ
   117Ôćĺ- After ccc index check completes (or is skipped), immediately load, read entire file, then execute {nextStepFile}
   118Ôćĺ
   119Ôćĺ#### EXECUTION RULES:
   120Ôćĺ
   121Ôćĺ- This is an auto-proceed step with no user choices
   122Ôćĺ- Proceed directly to next step after ccc index verification
   123Ôćĺ
   124Ôćĺ## CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE
   125Ôćĺ
   126ÔćĺONLY WHEN ccc index verification is complete (or step is skipped for ccc unavailable) will you load and read fully `{nextStepFile}` to execute the configuration write step.
   127Ôćĺ
   128Ôćĺ---
   129Ôćĺ
   130Ôćĺ## ­čÜĘ SYSTEM SUCCESS/FAILURE METRICS
   131Ôćĺ
   132Ôćĺ### Ôťů SUCCESS:
   133Ôćĺ
   134Ôćĺ- ccc unavailable: skipped silently with no output, context variables set to null/none
   135Ôćĺ- ccc available with fresh index: verified freshness, skipped re-index, context variables set
   136Ôćĺ- ccc available with stale/missing index: index created, context variables set with fresh timestamp
   137Ôćĺ- ccc indexing fails: logged gracefully, workflow continues, context variables set to failed/null
   138Ôćĺ- Auto-proceeded to step-02
   139Ôćĺ
   140Ôćĺ### ÔŁî SYSTEM FAILURE:
   141Ôćĺ
   142Ôćĺ- Displaying skip messages when ccc is unavailable
   143Ôćĺ- Halting the workflow on ccc index failure
   144Ôćĺ- Re-indexing when index is already fresh and path matches
   145Ôćĺ- Writing forge-tier.yaml (that is step-02's responsibility)
   146Ôćĺ- Not storing ccc index context variables for step-02
   147Ôćĺ
   148Ôćĺ**Master Rule:** CCC indexing is always best-effort. Failures degrade gracefully. The workflow never halts over ccc issues.
   149Ôćĺ

     7Ôćĺ
     8Ôćĺ# Step 2: Write Configuration
     9Ôćĺ
    10Ôćĺ## STEP GOAL:
    11Ôćĺ
    12ÔćĺWrite the detected tool availability and calculated tier to forge-tier.yaml, create preferences.yaml with defaults if it does not exist, and ensure the forge-data/ directory is present.
    13Ôćĺ
    14Ôćĺ## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES (READ FIRST):
    15Ôćĺ
    16Ôćĺ### Universal Rules:
    17Ôćĺ
    18Ôćĺ- ­čôľ CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action
    19Ôćĺ- ­čöä CRITICAL: When loading next step, ensure entire file is read
    20Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Execute all operations autonomously ÔÇö no user interaction
    21Ôćĺ
    22Ôćĺ### Role Reinforcement:
    23Ôćĺ
    24Ôćĺ- Ôťů You are a system executor writing configuration files
    25Ôćĺ- Ôťů File operations must be precise ÔÇö correct paths, correct YAML structure
    26Ôćĺ- Ôťů Preserve user data (preferences.yaml) ÔÇö never overwrite existing
    27Ôćĺ
    28Ôćĺ### Step-Specific Rules:
    29Ôćĺ
    30Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Focus only on writing configuration files and creating directories
    31Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to re-detect tools ÔÇö use results from step-01
    32Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to overwrite existing preferences.yaml
    33Ôćĺ- ­čĺČ File write failures ARE errors ÔÇö report clearly if they occur
    34Ôćĺ
    35Ôćĺ## EXECUTION PROTOCOLS:
    36Ôćĺ
    37Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Follow the MANDATORY SEQUENCE exactly
    38Ôćĺ- ­čĺż Write files to the forger-sidecar directory
    39Ôćĺ- ­čôľ Use context from step-01 (detected_tools, calculated_tier)
    40Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to proceed if forge-tier.yaml write fails
    41Ôćĺ
    42Ôćĺ## CONTEXT BOUNDARIES:
    43Ôćĺ
    44Ôćĺ- Available: {detected_tools}, {calculated_tier}, {previous_tier} from step-01; {ccc_index_result}, {ccc_indexed_path}, {ccc_last_indexed} from step-01b
    45Ôćĺ- Focus: file I/O operations only
    46Ôćĺ- Limits: do not modify preferences.yaml if it exists
    47Ôćĺ- Dependencies: step-01 and step-01b must have completed with tool detection and CCC index results
    48Ôćĺ
    49Ôćĺ## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
    50Ôćĺ
    51Ôćĺ**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise.
    52Ôćĺ
    53Ôćĺ### 1. Write forge-tier.yaml
    54Ôćĺ
    55ÔćĺWrite to `{project-root}/_bmad/_memory/forger-sidecar/forge-tier.yaml`:
    56Ôćĺ
    57Ôćĺ```yaml
    58Ôćĺ# Ferris Sidecar: Forge Tier State
    59Ôćĺ# Written by setup-forge workflow
    60Ôćĺ
    61Ôćĺ# Tool availability (detected during [SF] Setup Forge)
    62Ôćĺtools:
    63Ôćĺ  ast_grep: {true/false from detection}
    64Ôćĺ  gh_cli: {true/false from detection}
    65Ôćĺ  qmd: {true/false from detection}
    66Ôćĺ  ccc: {true/false from detection}
    67Ôćĺ  ccc_daemon: {ccc_daemon from step-01 if available: "healthy"|"stopped"|"error", or ~}
    68Ôćĺ  security_scan: {true/false ÔÇö true when SNYK_TOKEN is set}
    69Ôćĺ
    70Ôćĺ# Capability tier (derived from tool availability)
    71Ôćĺ# Quick = no tools | Forge = + ast-grep | Forge+ = + ast-grep + ccc | Deep = + ast-grep + gh + QMD
    72Ôćĺtier: {calculated_tier}
    73Ôćĺtier_detected_at: {current ISO timestamp}
    74Ôćĺ
    75Ôćĺ# CCC semantic index state (managed by setup-forge step-01b and extraction workflows)
    76Ôćĺccc_index:
    77Ôćĺ  indexed_path: {ccc_indexed_path from step-01b, or ~}
    78Ôćĺ  last_indexed: {ccc_last_indexed from step-01b, or ~}
    79Ôćĺ  status: {ccc_index_result from step-01b: "fresh"|"created"|"none"|"failed"}
    80Ôćĺ  staleness_threshold_hours: 24
    81Ôćĺ
    82Ôćĺ# CCC index registry (tracks which source paths have been indexed for skill workflows)
    83Ôćĺ# PRESERVE existing entries on re-runs ÔÇö see Note below
    84Ôćĺccc_index_registry: {preserved from existing forge-tier.yaml, or [] if first run}
    85Ôćĺ
    86Ôćĺ# QMD collection registry (populated by create-skill, consumed by audit/update-skill)
    87Ôćĺ# PRESERVE existing entries on re-runs ÔÇö see Note below
    88Ôćĺqmd_collections: {preserved from existing forge-tier.yaml, or [] if first run}
    89Ôćĺ```
    90Ôćĺ
    91Ôćĺ**Note on re-runs:** The `qmd_collections`, `ccc_index_registry` arrays, and `staleness_threshold_hours` value must be preserved across re-runs. Before overwriting forge-tier.yaml, read these existing values and re-inject them into the new write. These values are populated by create-skill workflows or customized by users and must not be reset.
    92Ôćĺ
    93Ôćĺ**This file is ALWAYS overwritten** on every run ÔÇö it reflects current tool state.
    94Ôćĺ
    95ÔćĺIf the write fails, report the error and halt the workflow.
    96Ôćĺ
    97Ôćĺ### 2. Handle preferences.yaml
    98Ôćĺ
    99ÔćĺCheck if `{project-root}/_bmad/_memory/forger-sidecar/preferences.yaml` exists:
   100Ôćĺ
   101Ôćĺ**If it does NOT exist (first run):** Create with defaults:
   102Ôćĺ
   103Ôćĺ```yaml
   104Ôćĺ# Ferris Sidecar: User Preferences
   105Ôćĺ# Created by setup-forge workflow on first run
   106Ôćĺ# Edit this file to customize Ferris behavior
   107Ôćĺ
   108Ôćĺ# Override detected tier (set to Quick, Forge, Forge+, or Deep to force a tier)
   109Ôćĺtier_override: ~
   110Ôćĺ
   111Ôćĺ# Passive context injection (set to false to skip snippet generation and CLAUDE.md updates during export)
   112Ôćĺpassive_context: true
   113Ôćĺ
   114Ôćĺ# Reserved for future use ÔÇö these fields are not yet consumed by any workflow step
   115Ôćĺ# output_language: ~
   116Ôćĺ# skill_format_version: ~
   117Ôćĺ# citation_style: ~
   118Ôćĺ# confidence_display: ~
   119Ôćĺ```
   120Ôćĺ
   121Ôćĺ**If it DOES exist:** Do not modify. Preserve entirely.
   122Ôćĺ
   123Ôćĺ### 3. Ensure forge-data/ Directory
   124Ôćĺ
   125ÔćĺCheck if `{forge_data_folder}` directory exists:
   126Ôćĺ
   127Ôćĺ- If missing: create it
   128Ôćĺ- If exists: skip silently
   129Ôćĺ
   130Ôćĺ### 4. Auto-Proceed
   131Ôćĺ
   132Ôćĺ"**Proceeding to QMD collection hygiene...**"
   133Ôćĺ
   134Ôćĺ#### Menu Handling Logic:
   135Ôćĺ
   136Ôćĺ- After all file operations complete successfully, immediately load, read entire file, then execute {nextStepFile}
   137Ôćĺ
   138Ôćĺ#### EXECUTION RULES:
   139Ôćĺ
   140Ôćĺ- This is an auto-proceed step with no user choices
   141Ôćĺ- Proceed directly to next step after configuration is written
   142Ôćĺ
   143Ôćĺ## CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE
   144Ôćĺ
   145ÔćĺONLY WHEN forge-tier.yaml has been written successfully and preferences.yaml exists (created or pre-existing) will you load and read fully `{nextStepFile}` to execute the auto-index step.
   146Ôćĺ
   147Ôćĺ---
   148Ôćĺ
   149Ôćĺ## ­čÜĘ SYSTEM SUCCESS/FAILURE METRICS
   150Ôćĺ
   151Ôćĺ### Ôťů SUCCESS:
   152Ôćĺ
   153Ôćĺ- forge-tier.yaml written with accurate tool booleans (including ccc), tier, timestamp, ccc_index state, and preserved qmd_collections/ccc_index_registry arrays
   154Ôćĺ- preferences.yaml exists (created with defaults on first run, preserved on re-run)
   155Ôćĺ- forge-data/ directory exists (created or pre-existing)
   156Ôćĺ- Auto-proceeded to step-03
   157Ôćĺ
   158Ôćĺ### ÔŁî SYSTEM FAILURE:
   159Ôćĺ
   160Ôćĺ- forge-tier.yaml write fails and workflow continues
   161Ôćĺ- preferences.yaml overwritten when it already existed
   162Ôćĺ- Incorrect tool values written (not matching step-01 detection)
   163Ôćĺ- Missing tier_detected_at timestamp
   164Ôćĺ- Re-detecting tools instead of using step-01 results
   165Ôćĺ
   166Ôćĺ**Master Rule:** forge-tier.yaml must be written accurately. preferences.yaml must never be overwritten. These are the foundation for all downstream workflows.
   167Ôćĺ

1Ôćĺ---
     2Ôćĺname: 'step-03-auto-index'
     3Ôćĺdescription: 'Verify and clean QMD collections against the forge-tier.yaml registry (Deep tier only) and CCC index registry hygiene (Forge+/Deep)'
     4Ôćĺ
     5ÔćĺnextStepFile: './step-04-report.md'
     6Ôćĺ---
     7Ôćĺ
     8Ôćĺ# Step 3: QMD Collection Hygiene
     9Ôćĺ
    10Ôćĺ## STEP GOAL:
    11Ôćĺ
    12ÔćĺIf the detected tier is Deep, verify the health of existing QMD collections by cross-referencing them against the `qmd_collections` registry in `forge-tier.yaml`. Identify orphaned collections (in QMD but not in registry) and stale registry entries (in registry but collection missing from QMD). Prompt the user before removing orphaned collections.
    13Ôćĺ
    14ÔćĺFor Quick and Forge tiers (without ccc), skip silently and proceed. For Forge+ tier, skip QMD hygiene but the step routes correctly to the next step.
    15Ôćĺ
    16Ôćĺ## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES (READ FIRST):
    17Ôćĺ
    18Ôćĺ### Universal Rules:
    19Ôćĺ
    20Ôćĺ- ­čôľ CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action
    21Ôćĺ- ­čöä CRITICAL: When loading next step, ensure entire file is read
    22Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Execute all operations autonomously ÔÇö minimal user interaction (only prompt before deletion)
    23Ôćĺ
    24Ôćĺ### Role Reinforcement:
    25Ôćĺ
    26Ôćĺ- Ôťů You are a system executor performing QMD collection maintenance
    27Ôćĺ- Ôťů Graceful degradation is paramount ÔÇö never fail the workflow over hygiene checks
    28Ôćĺ- Ôťů No negative messaging ÔÇö do not mention what non-Deep tiers are missing
    29Ôćĺ
    30Ôćĺ### Step-Specific Rules:
    31Ôćĺ
    32Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Focus only on verifying and cleaning QMD collections (Deep tier) or graceful skip (other tiers)
    33Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to display "missing" or "skipped" messages for non-Deep tiers
    34Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to fail the workflow if QMD hygiene encounters errors
    35Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to create new QMD collections ÔÇö that responsibility belongs to create-skill
    36Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to silently delete collections ÔÇö always prompt user before removal
    37Ôćĺ- ­čĺČ If hygiene fails: log the issue, note that it can be retried, continue
    38Ôćĺ
    39Ôćĺ## EXECUTION PROTOCOLS:
    40Ôćĺ
    41Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Follow the MANDATORY SEQUENCE exactly
    42Ôćĺ- ­čĺż QMD hygiene only verifies and cleans ÔÇö it does NOT index new content
    43Ôćĺ- ­čôľ Use {calculated_tier} from step-01 context
    44Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to attempt QMD hygiene for Quick, Forge, or Forge+ tiers (Forge+ has no qmd)
    45Ôćĺ
    46Ôćĺ## CONTEXT BOUNDARIES:
    47Ôćĺ
    48Ôćĺ- Available: {calculated_tier} from step-01, forge-tier.yaml written in step-02
    49Ôćĺ- Available: {project_name} from workflow config
    50Ôćĺ- Focus: QMD collection verification and cleanup only
    51Ôćĺ- Limits: only run if Deep tier
    52Ôćĺ- Dependencies: step-02 must have completed (forge-tier.yaml exists with qmd_collections registry)
    53Ôćĺ
    54Ôćĺ## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
    55Ôćĺ
    56Ôćĺ**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise.
    57Ôćĺ
    58Ôćĺ### 1. Check Tier
    59Ôćĺ
    60ÔćĺRead `{calculated_tier}` from context.
    61Ôćĺ
    62Ôćĺ**If tier is Quick or Forge:** Set `{ccc_registry_stale_cleaned: 0}`. Proceed directly to section 6 (Auto-Proceed) ÔÇö no output, no messaging.
    63Ôćĺ
    64Ôćĺ**If tier is Forge+:** Skip QMD hygiene (qmd is not available at Forge+). Proceed directly to section 5b (CCC Index Registry Hygiene) ÔÇö no QMD output, no messaging.
    65Ôćĺ
    66Ôćĺ**If tier IS Deep:** Continue to section 2.
    67Ôćĺ
    68Ôćĺ### 2. Load Registry and QMD State
    69Ôćĺ
    70ÔćĺRead the `qmd_collections` array from `{project-root}/_bmad/_memory/forger-sidecar/forge-tier.yaml`.
    71Ôćĺ
    72ÔćĺList live QMD collections:
    73Ôćĺ```bash
    74Ôćĺqmd collection list
    75Ôćĺ```
    76Ôćĺ
    77Ôćĺ**Store both lists for cross-reference:**
    78Ôćĺ- `{registry_collections}` ÔÇö entries from forge-tier.yaml qmd_collections
    79Ôćĺ- `{live_collections}` ÔÇö collections currently in QMD
    80Ôćĺ
    81Ôćĺ**Error handling:** If `qmd collection list` fails, log the error, store `{hygiene_result: "qmd_unavailable"}`, and proceed to section 6.
    82Ôćĺ
    83Ôćĺ### 3. Cross-Reference and Classify
    84Ôćĺ
    85ÔćĺCompare the two lists:
    86Ôćĺ
    87Ôćĺ**Healthy** ÔÇö collection exists in both registry AND QMD:
    88Ôćĺ- Mark as verified
    89Ôćĺ- No action needed
    90Ôćĺ
    91Ôćĺ**Orphaned** ÔÇö collection exists in QMD but NOT in registry:
    92Ôćĺ- These may be leftover from prior auto-indexing or manual indexing
    93Ôćĺ- Flag for user-prompted removal
    94Ôćĺ
    95Ôćĺ**Stale** ÔÇö entry exists in registry but collection is missing from QMD:
    96Ôćĺ- The QMD collection was deleted or lost
    97Ôćĺ- Remove the stale entry from the registry
    98Ôćĺ
    99Ôćĺ### 4. Handle Orphaned Collections
   100Ôćĺ
   101Ôćĺ**If orphaned collections found:**
   102Ôćĺ
   103ÔćĺDisplay to user:
   104Ôćĺ"**QMD Hygiene: Found {count} orphaned collection(s) not tracked in the forge registry:**
   105Ôćĺ
   106Ôćĺ{list orphaned collection names}
   107Ôćĺ
   108ÔćĺThese collections exist in QMD but are not managed by any skill workflow. They may be from a previous auto-index run or manual creation.
   109Ôćĺ
   110Ôćĺ**[R]emove** orphaned collections ÔÇö clean up QMD
   111Ôćĺ**[K]eep** orphaned collections ÔÇö leave them as-is"
   112Ôćĺ
   113Ôćĺ**If user selects R (Remove):**
   114ÔćĺFor each orphaned collection:
   115Ôćĺ```bash
   116Ôćĺqmd collection remove {collection_name}
   117Ôćĺ```
   118ÔćĺLog each removal.
   119Ôćĺ
   120Ôćĺ**If user selects K (Keep):**
   121ÔćĺSkip removal. Log that orphaned collections were kept.
   122Ôćĺ
   123Ôćĺ**If no orphaned collections:** Skip this section silently.
   124Ôćĺ
   125Ôćĺ### 5. Handle Stale Registry Entries
   126Ôćĺ
   127Ôćĺ**If stale registry entries found:**
   128Ôćĺ
   129ÔćĺRemove stale entries from the `qmd_collections` array in forge-tier.yaml.
   130Ôćĺ
   131ÔćĺDisplay: "**Cleaned {count} stale registry entry/entries** (collection no longer exists in QMD)."
   132Ôćĺ
   133ÔćĺUpdate forge-tier.yaml with the cleaned registry.
   134Ôćĺ
   135Ôćĺ**If no stale entries:** Skip this section silently.
   136Ôćĺ
   137Ôćĺ### 5b. CCC Index Registry Hygiene (Forge+ and Deep with ccc)
   138Ôćĺ
   139Ôćĺ**IF `tools.ccc` is true in forge-tier.yaml (regardless of whether QMD hygiene ran):**
   140Ôćĺ
   141ÔćĺRead the `ccc_index_registry` array from forge-tier.yaml.
   142Ôćĺ
   143ÔćĺFor each entry, verify the indexed path still exists on disk:
   144Ôćĺ
   145Ôćĺ**Healthy** ÔÇö path exists: no action needed.
   146Ôćĺ
   147Ôćĺ**Stale** ÔÇö path does not exist (source directory removed or moved):
   148Ôćĺ- Remove the stale entry from `ccc_index_registry`
   149Ôćĺ- Log: "Removed stale CCC index registry entry: {path} (path no longer exists)"
   150Ôćĺ
   151ÔćĺUpdate forge-tier.yaml with the cleaned registry.
   152Ôćĺ
   153ÔćĺStore `{ccc_registry_stale_cleaned: count}` in context for step-04 reporting.
   154Ôćĺ
   155Ôćĺ**IF `tools.ccc` is false:** Skip this section silently.
   156Ôćĺ
   157Ôćĺ### 6. Store Hygiene Results and Auto-Proceed
   158Ôćĺ
   159ÔćĺStore in context for step-04 reporting:
   160Ôćĺ```
   161Ôćĺ{hygiene_result: "completed"|"skipped"|"qmd_unavailable"}
   162Ôćĺ{hygiene_healthy: count}
   163Ôćĺ{hygiene_orphaned_removed: count}
   164Ôćĺ{hygiene_orphaned_kept: count}
   165Ôćĺ{hygiene_stale_cleaned: count}
   166Ôćĺ{ccc_registry_stale_cleaned: count}
   167Ôćĺ```
   168Ôćĺ
   169Ôćĺ"**Proceeding to forge status report...**"
   170Ôćĺ
   171Ôćĺ#### Menu Handling Logic:
   172Ôćĺ
   173Ôćĺ- After hygiene completes (or is skipped), immediately load, read entire file, then execute {nextStepFile}
   174Ôćĺ
   175Ôćĺ#### EXECUTION RULES:
   176Ôćĺ
   177Ôćĺ- This step has one optional user interaction (orphan removal prompt)
   178Ôćĺ- If no orphans found, this is an auto-proceed step
   179Ôćĺ- Proceed directly to next step after hygiene or skip
   180Ôćĺ
   181Ôćĺ## CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE
   182Ôćĺ
   183ÔćĺONLY WHEN the hygiene check has been performed (or skipped for non-Deep tiers) will you load and read fully `{nextStepFile}` to execute the report step.
   184Ôćĺ
   185Ôćĺ---
   186Ôćĺ
   187Ôćĺ## ­čÜĘ SYSTEM SUCCESS/FAILURE METRICS
   188Ôćĺ
   189Ôćĺ### Ôťů SUCCESS:
   190Ôćĺ
   191Ôćĺ- Deep tier: forge-tier.yaml registry cross-referenced with live QMD collections
   192Ôćĺ- Healthy collections identified and verified
   193Ôćĺ- Orphaned collections flagged and user prompted before removal
   194Ôćĺ- Stale registry entries cleaned from forge-tier.yaml
   195Ôćĺ- Hygiene results stored for step-04 reporting
   196Ôćĺ- Quick/Forge/Forge+ tier: skipped silently with no negative messaging
   197Ôćĺ- Workflow continues regardless of hygiene outcome
   198Ôćĺ- Auto-proceeded to step-04
   199Ôćĺ
   200Ôćĺ### ÔŁî SYSTEM FAILURE:
   201Ôćĺ
   202Ôćĺ- Creating new QMD collections (that's create-skill's responsibility)
   203Ôćĺ- Silently deleting collections without user prompt
   204Ôćĺ- Attempting QMD hygiene for Quick, Forge, or Forge+ tiers
   205Ôćĺ- Displaying "skipped" or "missing" messages for non-Deep tiers
   206Ôćĺ- Halting the workflow due to QMD hygiene failure
   207Ôćĺ- Indexing project directories (old auto-index behavior ÔÇö removed)
   208Ôćĺ- Not proceeding to step-04 after this step
   209Ôćĺ
   210Ôćĺ**Master Rule:** This step ONLY verifies and cleans. It never indexes new content. QMD collection creation is the responsibility of create-skill. Non-Deep tiers skip silently. Always prompt before removing orphaned collections.
   211Ôćĺ

1Ôćĺ---
     2Ôćĺname: 'step-04-report'
     3Ôćĺdescription: 'Display forge status report with positive capability framing'
     4Ôćĺ
     5ÔćĺtierRulesData: '../data/tier-rules.md'
     6Ôćĺ---
     7Ôćĺ
     8Ôćĺ# Step 4: Forge Status Report
     9Ôćĺ
    10Ôćĺ## STEP GOAL:
    11Ôćĺ
    12ÔćĺDisplay the forge status report with positive capability framing, report tier changes on re-run, and optionally fetch the latest agentskills.io spec if flagged.
    13Ôćĺ
    14Ôćĺ## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES (READ FIRST):
    15Ôćĺ
    16Ôćĺ### Universal Rules:
    17Ôćĺ
    18Ôćĺ- ­čôľ CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action
    19Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Execute all operations autonomously ÔÇö display report and complete
    20Ôćĺ
    21Ôćĺ### Role Reinforcement:
    22Ôćĺ
    23Ôćĺ- Ôťů You are Ferris reporting forge readiness
    24Ôćĺ- Ôťů Frame ALL capabilities positively ÔÇö describe what the tier GIVES
    25Ôćĺ- Ôťů On re-run: explicitly report what changed (upgrade/downgrade/same)
    26Ôćĺ- Ôťů Brief forge metaphor appropriate here ÔÇö this is the completion moment
    27Ôćĺ
    28Ôćĺ### Step-Specific Rules:
    29Ôćĺ
    30Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Focus only on displaying the status report
    31Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to use negative framing ("missing", "lacking", "unavailable")
    32Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to list tools that are NOT available
    33Ôćĺ- ­čĺČ Use tier capability descriptions from tier-rules.md
    34Ôćĺ
    35Ôćĺ## EXECUTION PROTOCOLS:
    36Ôćĺ

   148Ôćĺ### ÔŁî SYSTEM FAILURE:
   149Ôćĺ
   150Ôćĺ- Negative framing in report ("missing", "unavailable", "lacking")
   151Ôćĺ- Listing tools that are NOT available
   152Ôćĺ- Not using tier capability descriptions from tier-rules.md

1Ôćĺ# SKILL.md Section Structure
     2Ôćĺ
     3Ôćĺ## agentskills.io Compliant Format
     4Ôćĺ
     5Ôćĺ### Frontmatter (Required ÔÇö agentskills.io Compliance)
     6Ôćĺ
     7Ôćĺ```yaml
     8Ôćĺ---
     9Ôćĺname: {skill-name}
    10Ôćĺdescription: >
    11Ôćĺ  {Trigger-optimized description of what the skill does and when to use it.
    12Ôćĺ  Include specific keywords for agent discovery.
    13Ôćĺ  Mention what NOT to use it for if applicable.
    14Ôćĺ  1-1024 characters.}
    15Ôćĺ---
    16Ôćĺ```
    17Ôćĺ
    18Ôćĺ**Frontmatter rules (agentskills.io specification):**
    19Ôćĺ
    20Ôćĺ- `name`: 1-64 characters, lowercase alphanumeric + hyphens only, must match parent directory name. Prefer gerund form (`processing-pdfs`, `analyzing-spreadsheets`) for clarity; noun phrases and action-oriented forms are acceptable alternatives.
    21Ôćĺ- `description`: 1-1024 characters, trigger-optimized for agent matching. MUST use third-person voice ("Processes..." not "I can..." or "You can...").
    22Ôćĺ- Only 6 fields permitted: `name`, `description`, `license`, `compatibility`, `metadata`, `allowed-tools`
    23Ôćĺ- `version` and `author` belong in metadata.json, NOT in frontmatter
    24Ôćĺ
    25Ôćĺ### Two-Tier Section Structure
    26Ôćĺ
    27ÔćĺSKILL.md uses a two-tier structure to ensure actionable content survives `split-body` extraction.
    28Ôćĺ
    29Ôćĺ#### Tier 1 ÔÇö Always Inline (survives split-body, target <300 lines)
    30Ôćĺ
    31Ôćĺ| # | Section | Budget | Purpose |
    32Ôćĺ|---|---------|--------|---------|
    33Ôćĺ| 1 | **Overview** | ~10 lines | What the library does, source repo, version, tier, export count |
    34Ôćĺ| 2 | **Quick Start** | ~30 lines | 3-5 core functions with one runnable end-to-end example |
    35Ôćĺ| 3 | **Common Workflows** | ~30 lines | 4-5 typical function call sequences for common tasks |
    36Ôćĺ| 4 | **Key API Summary** | ~20 lines | Table of top 10-15 functions (name, purpose, key params) |
    37Ôćĺ| 4b | **Migration & Deprecation Warnings** | ~10 lines | T2-future warnings inline (Deep tier only, skip if none) |
    38Ôćĺ| 5 | **Key Types** | ~20 lines | Most important enum/type values inline |
    39Ôćĺ| 6 | **Architecture at a Glance** | ~10 lines | Bullet list of subsystem categories |
    40Ôćĺ| 7 | **CLI** | ~10 lines | Basic CLI commands (skip if no CLI) |
    41Ôćĺ| 7b | **Scripts & Assets** | ~10 lines | Manifest of included scripts and assets (skip if none detected) |
    42Ôćĺ| 8 | **Manual Sections** | ~5 lines | `<!-- [MANUAL] -->` markers for update-skill |
    43Ôćĺ
    44Ôćĺ#### Tier 2 ÔÇö Reference-Eligible (extracted by split-body into references/)
    45Ôćĺ
    46Ôćĺ| # | Section | Purpose |
    47Ôćĺ|---|---------|---------|
    48Ôćĺ| 9 | **Full API Reference** | Complete signatures, parameter tables, return types, examples, citations |
    49Ôćĺ| 10 | **Full Type Definitions** | All types, interfaces, enums with full field details |
    50Ôćĺ| 11 | **Full Integration Patterns** | Co-import patterns, adapter details, pipeline internals (Forge/Deep only) |
    51Ôćĺ
    52Ôćĺ#### Assembly Rules
    53Ôćĺ
    54Ôćĺ- Tier 1 sections are assembled first ÔÇö they form the standalone body
    55Ôćĺ- Tier 2 sections are assembled after ÔÇö they are progressive disclosure detail
    56Ôćĺ- Tier 1 sections are kept short enough that `split-body` targets the larger Tier 2 sections (`## Full` headings) into `references/`
    57Ôćĺ- After split-body, SKILL.md retains all Tier 1 content ÔÇö actionable without loading references
    58Ôćĺ- An agent loading only SKILL.md (no references) must get enough to act
    59Ôćĺ- **Section 4b (Migration & Deprecation Warnings)** is conditional: only emitted for Deep tier when T2-future annotations exist. Quick/Forge/Forge+ tiers and Deep tiers without T2-future annotations omit it entirely (no empty section). Parsers and validators must treat this section as optional.
    60Ôćĺ- **Section 7b (Scripts & Assets)** is conditional: only emitted when `scripts_inventory` or `assets_inventory` is non-empty. Omitted entirely when no scripts or assets are detected. Parsers and validators must treat this section as optional.
    61Ôćĺ
    62Ôćĺ### Provenance Citation Format
    63Ôćĺ
    64Ôćĺ| Tier | Format | Example |
    65Ôćĺ|------|--------|---------|
    66Ôćĺ| T1 (AST) | `[AST:{file}:L{line}]` | `[AST:src/auth/index.ts:L42]` |
    67Ôćĺ| T1-low (Source) | `[SRC:{file}:L{line}]` | `[SRC:src/auth/index.ts:L42]` |
    68Ôćĺ| T2 (QMD) | `[QMD:{collection}:{doc}]` | `[QMD:project:CHANGELOG.md]` |
    69Ôćĺ| T3 (External) | `[EXT:{url}]` | `[EXT:docs.example.com/api]` |
    70Ôćĺ
    71Ôćĺ### [MANUAL] Section Markers
    72Ôćĺ
    73ÔćĺSeed empty manual sections for future update-skill compatibility:
    74Ôćĺ
    75Ôćĺ```markdown
    76Ôćĺ<!-- [MANUAL:additional-notes] -->
    77Ôćĺ<!-- Add custom notes here. This section is preserved during skill updates. -->
    78Ôćĺ<!-- [/MANUAL:additional-notes] -->
    79Ôćĺ```
    80Ôćĺ
    81ÔćĺPlace after Quick Start and after API Reference sections.
    82Ôćĺ
    83Ôćĺ---
    84Ôćĺ
    85Ôćĺ## context-snippet.md Format (Vercel-aligned indexed format)
    86Ôćĺ
    87ÔćĺIndexed pipe-delimited format for CLAUDE.md managed section (~80-120 tokens per skill). Aligned with Vercel's research finding that retrieval instructions + indexed file maps + inline gotchas dramatically improve agent performance.
    88Ôćĺ
    89Ôćĺ```markdown
    90Ôćĺ[{skill-name} v{version}]|root: skills/{skill-name}/
    91Ôćĺ|IMPORTANT: {skill-name} v{version} ÔÇö read SKILL.md before writing {skill-name} code. Do NOT rely on training data.
    92Ôćĺ|quick-start:{SKILL.md#quick-start}
    93Ôćĺ|api: {top exports with () for functions, comma-separated}
    94Ôćĺ|key-types:{SKILL.md#key-types} ÔÇö {inline summary of most important type values}
    95Ôćĺ|gotchas: {2-3 most critical pitfalls or breaking changes, inline}
    96Ôćĺ```
    97Ôćĺ
    98Ôćĺ### Format rules
    99Ôćĺ
   100Ôćĺ- **Line 1:** Skill name + version + root path ÔÇö version signals training data staleness
   101Ôćĺ- **Line 2 (IMPORTANT):** Retrieval instruction ÔÇö always present, tells agent to read SKILL.md before acting
   102Ôćĺ- **Lines 3+:** Pipe-delimited index mapping topics to SKILL.md sections (with `#anchor` pointers)
   103Ôćĺ- **Inline content:** Each index line includes a brief inline summary (~10 words) so the agent can decide whether to read the full section
   104Ôćĺ- **gotchas line:** 2-3 most critical pitfalls inline ÔÇö prevents mistakes without requiring a file read
   105Ôćĺ- **Token budget:** ~80-120 tokens per skill (justified by Vercel's finding that indexed format maintains performance at 80% compression)
   106Ôćĺ- **T1-now content only** ÔÇö no T2 annotations in the snippet
   107Ôćĺ- **Section anchors** (`#quick-start`, `#key-types`) must match actual SKILL.md heading slugs
   108Ôćĺ- **Version** comes from source detection (per issue #32), not brief default
   109Ôćĺ
   110Ôćĺ---
