1Ôćĺ# Context Snippet Format (Vercel-Aligned Indexed Format)
     2Ôćĺ
     3Ôćĺ## Format Rules
     4Ôćĺ
     5Ôćĺ- Indexed pipe-delimited format per skill ÔÇö retrieval instruction + file map + inline gotchas
     6Ôćĺ- ~80-120 tokens per skill target (up from ~50-80, justified by Vercel research)
     7Ôćĺ- T1-now content only (AST-current, no annotations)
     8Ôćĺ- Line 2 (IMPORTANT) is the RETRIEVAL INSTRUCTION ÔÇö always present, tells agent to read SKILL.md
     9Ôćĺ- Section anchors (`#quick-start`, `#key-types`) must match actual SKILL.md heading slugs
    10Ôćĺ- gotchas line prevents common mistakes without requiring a file read
    11Ôćĺ- Version comes from source detection, not brief default
    12Ôćĺ
    13Ôćĺ## Single Skill Snippet Template
    14Ôćĺ
    15Ôćĺ```markdown
    16Ôćĺ[{skill-name} v{version}]|root: skills/{skill-name}/
    17Ôćĺ|IMPORTANT: {skill-name} v{version} ÔÇö read SKILL.md before writing {skill-name} code. Do NOT rely on training data.
    18Ôćĺ|quick-start:{SKILL.md#quick-start}
    19Ôćĺ|api: {top exports with () for functions, comma-separated}
    20Ôćĺ|key-types:{SKILL.md#key-types} ÔÇö {inline summary of most important type values}
    21Ôćĺ|gotchas: {2-3 most critical pitfalls or breaking changes, inline}
    22Ôćĺ```
    23Ôćĺ
    24Ôćĺ- **Line 1:** Skill name + version + root path ÔÇö version signals training data staleness
    25Ôćĺ- **Line 2 (IMPORTANT):** Retrieval instruction ÔÇö always present
    26Ôćĺ- **Line 3 (quick-start):** Anchor pointer to Quick Start section
    27Ôćĺ- **Line 4 (api):** Top exports from metadata.json `exports` array (up to 10, with `()` for functions)
    28Ôćĺ- **Line 5 (key-types):** Anchor pointer + inline summary of most important type/enum values
    29Ôćĺ- **Line 6 (gotchas):** 2-3 most critical pitfalls ÔÇö derived from T2-future annotations, async requirements, breaking changes
    30Ôćĺ
    31Ôćĺ## Stack Skill Snippet Template
    32Ôćĺ
    33Ôćĺ```markdown
    34Ôćĺ[{project}-stack v{version}]|root: skills/{project}-stack/
    35Ôćĺ|IMPORTANT: {project}-stack ÔÇö read SKILL.md before writing integration code. Do NOT rely on training data.
    36Ôćĺ|stack: {dep-1}@{v1}, {dep-2}@{v2}, {dep-3}@{v3}
    37Ôćĺ|integrations: {pattern-1}, {pattern-2}
    38Ôćĺ|gotchas: {2-3 most critical integration pitfalls, inline}
    39Ôćĺ```
    40Ôćĺ
    41Ôćĺ## Rules
    42Ôćĺ
    43Ôćĺ- **api line**: Top exports from metadata.json `exports` array (up to 10 for Deep tier, up to 5 for all other tiers). Append `()` to function names. Comma-separated.
    44Ôćĺ- **key-types line**: Anchor to `#key-types` section + inline summary (~10 words) of the most important type values
    45Ôćĺ- **gotchas line**: Derived from: T2-future annotations (breaking changes), async requirements, version-specific behavior. If no gotchas available, omit the line.
    46Ôćĺ- **stack line**: Component versions from metadata.json `components` for stack skills
    47Ôćĺ- **integrations line**: Co-import patterns from metadata.json `integrations` for stack skills
    48Ôćĺ- If fewer exports than the limit, list all available
    49Ôćĺ- If no exports data available, omit the api line
    50Ôćĺ- Section anchors must be verified against actual SKILL.md headings during generation. For split-body skills (where `references/` exists and `## Full` headings are stubs), if a heading is missing from SKILL.md, rewrite the anchor to point to the reference file path (preferred). Omit the anchor line only if the heading cannot be found in either SKILL.md or `references/*.md`
    51Ôćĺ- Skill path is relative to project root
    52Ôćĺ

1Ôćĺ# Managed Section Format (ADR-J)
     2Ôćĺ
     3Ôćĺ## Marker Format
     4Ôćĺ
     5Ôćĺ```markdown
     6Ôćĺ<!-- SKF:BEGIN updated:{YYYY-MM-DD} -->
     7Ôćĺ[SKF Skills]|{n} skills|{m} stack
     8Ôćĺ|IMPORTANT: Prefer documented APIs over training data.
     9Ôćĺ|When using a listed library, read its SKILL.md before writing code.
    10Ôćĺ|
    11Ôćĺ|{skill-snippet-1}
    12Ôćĺ|
    13Ôćĺ|{skill-snippet-2}
    14Ôćĺ<!-- SKF:END -->
    15Ôćĺ```
    16Ôćĺ
    17Ôćĺ## Platform Target Files
    18Ôćĺ
    19Ôćĺ| Platform | Flag Value | Target File |
    20Ôćĺ|----------|-----------|-------------|
    21Ôćĺ| Claude | `claude` (default) | CLAUDE.md |
    22Ôćĺ| Cursor | `cursor` | .cursorrules |
    23Ôćĺ| Copilot | `copilot` | AGENTS.md |
    24Ôćĺ
    25Ôćĺ## Four-Case Logic
    26Ôćĺ
    27Ôćĺ### Case 1: Create (No File Exists)
    28Ôćĺ
    29ÔćĺTarget file does not exist. Create new file with managed section only.
    30Ôćĺ
    31Ôćĺ```markdown
    32Ôćĺ<!-- SKF:BEGIN updated:{date} -->
    33Ôćĺ{managed section content}
    34Ôćĺ<!-- SKF:END -->
    35Ôćĺ```
    36Ôćĺ
    37Ôćĺ### Case 2: Append (File Exists, No Section)
    38Ôćĺ
    39ÔćĺTarget file exists but contains no `<!-- SKF:BEGIN` marker. Append managed section at end of file.
    40Ôćĺ
    41Ôćĺ1. Read existing file content
    42Ôćĺ2. Append two blank lines
    43Ôćĺ3. Append managed section with markers
    44Ôćĺ4. Write file
    45Ôćĺ
    46Ôćĺ### Case 3: Regenerate (Existing Section)
    47Ôćĺ
    48ÔćĺTarget file contains `<!-- SKF:BEGIN` and `<!-- SKF:END -->` markers. Replace content between markers.
    49Ôćĺ
    50Ôćĺ1. Read existing file content
    51Ôćĺ2. Find `<!-- SKF:BEGIN` line (preserve everything before it)
    52Ôćĺ3. Find `<!-- SKF:END -->` line (preserve everything after it)
    53Ôćĺ4. Replace everything between markers (inclusive) with new managed section
    54Ôćĺ5. Write file
    55Ôćĺ
    56Ôćĺ### Case 4: Malformed Markers (Halt)
    57Ôćĺ
    58ÔćĺTarget file contains `<!-- SKF:BEGIN` but no matching `<!-- SKF:END -->` marker. This indicates a corrupted managed section.
    59Ôćĺ
    60Ôćĺ1. Halt workflow with error: "Malformed managed section ÔÇö `<!-- SKF:BEGIN` found but no `<!-- SKF:END -->`. Fix the markers manually before re-running export."
    61Ôćĺ2. Do not modify the file
    62Ôćĺ
    63Ôćĺ## Regeneration: Full Index Rebuild
    64Ôćĺ
    65ÔćĺWhen regenerating (Case 3), rebuild the COMPLETE skill index:
    66Ôćĺ
    67Ôćĺ1. Scan `{skills_output_folder}/*/context-snippet.md` for all exported skills
    68Ôćĺ2. Count total skills and stack skills
    69Ôćĺ3. Assemble all snippets into managed section
    70Ôćĺ4. Sort alphabetically by skill name
    71Ôćĺ5. Update the header line with correct counts
    72Ôćĺ
    73Ôćĺ## Safety Rules
    74Ôćĺ
    75Ôćĺ- NEVER modify content outside the `<!-- SKF:BEGIN/END -->` markers
    76Ôćĺ- ALWAYS preserve existing file content above and below markers
    77Ôćĺ- ALWAYS verify file was written correctly after write
    78Ôćĺ- If write fails, report error ÔÇö do not attempt partial writes
    79Ôćĺ

     7Ôćĺ
     8Ôćĺ# Brief Skill
     9Ôćĺ
    10Ôćĺ**Goal:** Help the user define what to skill ÔÇö target repo, scope, language, inclusion/exclusion patterns ÔÇö and produce a skill-brief.yaml that drives create-skill.
    11Ôćĺ
    12Ôćĺ**Your Role:** In addition to your name, communication_style, and persona, you are also a skill scoping architect collaborating with a developer who wants to create an agent skill. This is a partnership, not a client-vendor relationship. You bring expertise in source code analysis, API surface identification, and skill boundary design, while the user brings their domain knowledge and specific use case for the skill. Work together as equals.
    13Ôćĺ
    14Ôćĺ**Meta-Context:** This workflow is the first step in the skill creation pipeline. The skill-brief.yaml you produce together becomes the input contract for create-skill, which performs the actual compilation. Your job is to ensure the brief is well-scoped, accurate, and actionable ÔÇö not to build the skill itself.
    15Ôćĺ
    16Ôćĺ---
    17Ôćĺ
    18Ôćĺ## WORKFLOW ARCHITECTURE
    19Ôćĺ
    20Ôćĺ### Core Principles
    21Ôćĺ
    22Ôćĺ- **Micro-file Design**: Each step of the overall goal is a self contained instruction file that you will adhere to 1 file as directed at a time
    23Ôćĺ- **Just-In-Time Loading**: Only 1 current step file will be loaded, read, and executed to completion - never load future step files until told to do so
    24Ôćĺ- **Sequential Enforcement**: Sequence within the step files must be completed in order, no skipping or optimization allowed
    25Ôćĺ- **State Tracking**: Data is accumulated conversationally across steps and written as skill-brief.yaml in the final step
    26Ôćĺ- **Append-Only Building**: Each step builds on information gathered in previous steps
    27Ôćĺ
    28Ôćĺ### Step Processing Rules
    29Ôćĺ
    30Ôćĺ1. **READ COMPLETELY**: Always read the entire step file before taking any action
    31Ôćĺ2. **FOLLOW SEQUENCE**: Execute all numbered sections in order, never deviate
    32Ôćĺ3. **WAIT FOR INPUT**: If a menu is presented, halt and wait for user selection
    33Ôćĺ4. **CHECK CONTINUATION**: If the step has a menu with Continue as an option, only proceed to next step when user selects 'C' (Continue)
    34Ôćĺ5. **LOAD NEXT**: When directed, load, read entire file, then execute the next step file
    35Ôćĺ
    36Ôćĺ### Critical Rules (NO EXCEPTIONS)
    37Ôćĺ
    38Ôćĺ- ­čŤĹ **NEVER** load multiple step files simultaneously
    39Ôćĺ- ­čôľ **ALWAYS** read entire step file before execution
    40Ôćĺ- ­čÜź **NEVER** skip steps or optimize the sequence
    41Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» **ALWAYS** follow the exact instructions in the step file
    42Ôćĺ- ÔĆŞ´ŞĆ **ALWAYS** halt at menus and wait for user input
    43Ôćĺ- ­čôő **NEVER** create mental todo lists from future steps
    44Ôćĺ- Ôťů YOU MUST ALWAYS SPEAK OUTPUT In your Agent communication style with the config `{communication_language}`
    45Ôćĺ
    46Ôćĺ---
    47Ôćĺ
    48Ôćĺ## INITIALIZATION SEQUENCE
    49Ôćĺ
    50Ôćĺ### 1. Module Configuration Loading
    51Ôćĺ
    52ÔćĺLoad and read full config from {project-root}/_bmad/skf/config.yaml and resolve:
    53Ôćĺ
    54Ôćĺ- `project_name`, `output_folder`, `user_name`, `communication_language`, `document_output_language`, `forge_data_folder`, `sidecar_path`
    55Ôćĺ
    56Ôćĺ### 2. First Step Execution
    57Ôćĺ
    58ÔćĺLoad, read the full file and then execute `./steps-c/step-01-gather-intent.md` to begin the workflow.
    59Ôćĺ

1Ôćĺ---
     2Ôćĺname: 'step-01-gather-intent'
     3Ôćĺdescription: 'Initialize brief-skill workflow, discover forge tier, and gather user intent for skill creation'
     4Ôćĺ
     5ÔćĺnextStepFile: './step-02-analyze-target.md'
     6ÔćĺforgeTierFile: '{sidecar_path}/forge-tier.yaml'
     7Ôćĺ---
     8Ôćĺ
     9Ôćĺ# Step 1: Gather Intent
    10Ôćĺ
    11Ôćĺ## STEP GOAL:
    12Ôćĺ
    13ÔćĺTo initialize the brief-skill workflow by discovering the forge tier configuration, then gathering the user's target repository, intent, and any upfront scope hints for skill creation.
    14Ôćĺ
    15Ôćĺ## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES (READ FIRST):
    16Ôćĺ
    17Ôćĺ### Universal Rules:
    18Ôćĺ
    19Ôćĺ- ­čŤĹ NEVER generate content without user input
    20Ôćĺ- ­čôľ CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action
    21Ôćĺ- ­čöä CRITICAL: When loading next step with 'C', ensure entire file is read
    22Ôćĺ- ­čôő YOU ARE A FACILITATOR, not a content generator
    23Ôćĺ- Ôťů YOU MUST ALWAYS SPEAK OUTPUT In your Agent communication style with the config `{communication_language}`
    24Ôćĺ- ÔÜÖ´ŞĆ TOOL/SUBPROCESS FALLBACK: If any instruction references a subprocess, subagent, or tool you do not have access to, you MUST still achieve the outcome in your main context thread
    25Ôćĺ
    26Ôćĺ### Role Reinforcement:
    27Ôćĺ
    28Ôćĺ- Ôťů You are a skill scoping architect in Ferris Architect mode (exploratory, collaborative)
    29Ôćĺ- Ôťů If you already have been given a name, communication_style and identity, continue to use those while playing this new role
    30Ôćĺ- Ôťů We engage in collaborative dialogue, not command-response
    31Ôćĺ- Ôťů You bring source code analysis and skill design expertise, user brings their domain knowledge and use case
    32Ôćĺ- Ôťů Maintain a warm, guiding tone ÔÇö this is a discovery conversation
    33Ôćĺ
    34Ôćĺ### Step-Specific Rules:
    35Ôćĺ
    36Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Focus only on gathering intent ÔÇö do not analyze the repo yet (that's step 02)
    37Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to examine source code or list exports in this step
    38Ôćĺ- ­čĺČ Approach: Open-ended discovery facilitation ÔÇö "What interests you about this library?"
    39Ôćĺ- ­čôő Collect: target repo, user intent, scope hints, skill name
    40Ôćĺ
    41Ôćĺ## EXECUTION PROTOCOLS:
    42Ôćĺ
    43Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Follow the MANDATORY SEQUENCE exactly
    44Ôćĺ- ­čĺż Accumulate gathered information conversationally for use in subsequent steps
    45Ôćĺ- ­čôľ This is the init step ÔÇö sets up everything that follows
    46Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to proceed without a confirmed target repository
    47Ôćĺ
    48Ôćĺ## CONTEXT BOUNDARIES:
    49Ôćĺ
    50Ôćĺ- Available context: Module config (forge_data_folder, user_name), forge-tier.yaml (if available)
    51Ôćĺ- Focus: Understanding WHAT the user wants to skill and WHY
    52Ôćĺ- Limits: Do not analyze source code, detect language, or list exports ÔÇö that's step 02
    53Ôćĺ- Dependencies: None ÔÇö this is the first step
    54Ôćĺ
    55Ôćĺ## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
    56Ôćĺ
    57Ôćĺ**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise unless user explicitly requests a change.
    58Ôćĺ
    59Ôćĺ### 1. Discover Forge Tier
    60Ôćĺ
    61ÔćĺAttempt to load `{forgeTierFile}`:
    62Ôćĺ
    63Ôćĺ**If found:**
    64Ôćĺ- Read the tier level (quick, forge, forge+, or deep)
    65Ôćĺ- Note available tools for scoping guidance later
    66Ôćĺ
    67Ôćĺ**Apply tier override:** Read `{sidecar_path}/preferences.yaml`. If `tier_override` is set and is a valid tier value (Quick, Forge, Forge+, or Deep), use it instead of the detected tier.
    68Ôćĺ
    69Ôćĺ**If not found:**
    70Ôćĺ- Display: "**Note:** No forge tier configuration found. Defaulting to Quick tier. Run **setup-forge** first for full capabilities."
    71Ôćĺ- Default to: `forge_tier: quick`
    72Ôćĺ
    73Ôćĺ### 2. Welcome and Explain
    74Ôćĺ
    75Ôćĺ"**Welcome to Brief Skill ÔÇö the skill scoping workflow.**
    76Ôćĺ
    77ÔćĺI'll help you define exactly what to skill and produce a `skill-brief.yaml` that drives the create-skill compilation workflow.
    78Ôćĺ
    79ÔćĺWe'll work through this together:
    80Ôćĺ1. **Now:** Understand what you want to skill and why
    81Ôćĺ2. **Next:** Analyze the target repo structure
    82Ôćĺ3. **Then:** Define scope boundaries
    83Ôćĺ4. **Finally:** Confirm and write the brief
    84Ôćĺ
    85Ôćĺ**Your forge tier:** {detected tier} ÔÇö this determines what compilation capabilities are available.
    86Ôćĺ
    87ÔćĺLet's get started."
    88Ôćĺ
    89Ôćĺ### 3. Gather Target Repository
    90Ôćĺ
    91Ôćĺ"**What repository or documentation do you want to create a skill for?**
    92Ôćĺ
    93ÔćĺProvide one of:
    94Ôćĺ- A **GitHub URL** (e.g., `https://github.com/org/repo`)
    95Ôćĺ- A **local path** (e.g., `/path/to/project`)
    96Ôćĺ- **Documentation URLs** for a docs-only skill (e.g., `https://docs.cognee.ai/`) ÔÇö use this when no source code is available (SaaS, closed-source)
    97Ôćĺ
    98Ôćĺ**Target:**"
    99Ôćĺ
   100ÔćĺWait for user response.
   101Ôćĺ
   102Ôćĺ**If user provides documentation URLs (not a repo):**
   103Ôćĺ- Set `source_type: "docs-only"` in the brief data
   104Ôćĺ- Collect one or more doc URLs with optional labels
   105Ôćĺ- Note: `source_authority` will be forced to `community` (T3 external documentation)
   106Ôćĺ- Note: `source_repo` becomes optional (can be set to the main doc site URL for reference)
   107Ôćĺ
   108Ôćĺ**If user provides a GitHub URL or local path:**
   109Ôćĺ- Set `source_type: "source"` (default)
   110Ôćĺ- Optionally ask: "Are there any documentation URLs you'd like to include for supplemental context? (These will be fetched as T3 external references.)"
   111Ôćĺ- If yes: collect doc URLs into `doc_urls`
   112Ôćĺ
   113ÔćĺConfirm the target.
   114Ôćĺ
   115Ôćĺ### 4. Gather User Intent
   116Ôćĺ
   117Ôćĺ"**What's your intent for this skill?**
   118Ôćĺ
   119ÔćĺHelp me understand:
   120Ôćĺ- **What** specifically do you want to skill from this repo?
   121Ôćĺ- **Why** ÔÇö what's the use case? How will an AI agent use this skill?
   122Ôćĺ- **Any initial thoughts** on scope? (Full library? Specific modules? Public API only?)
   123Ôćĺ
   124ÔćĺTake your time ÔÇö the more context you share, the better the brief."
   125Ôćĺ
   126ÔćĺWait for user response. Ask follow-up questions if intent is unclear.
   127Ôćĺ
   128Ôćĺ### 5. Capture Scope Hints
   129Ôćĺ
   130ÔćĺIf the user mentioned scope preferences in their intent response, acknowledge them:
   131Ôćĺ
   132Ôćĺ"**I noted these scope hints from your response:**
   133Ôćĺ- {list any scope hints mentioned}
   134Ôćĺ
   135ÔćĺWe'll refine these after analyzing the repo structure in the next step."
   136Ôćĺ
   137ÔćĺIf no scope hints were mentioned, that's fine ÔÇö skip this acknowledgment.
   138Ôćĺ
   139Ôćĺ### 6. Derive Skill Name
   140Ôćĺ
   141ÔćĺBased on the target repo and intent, propose a skill name:
   142Ôćĺ
   143Ôćĺ"**Suggested skill name:** `{derived-name}` (kebab-case)
   144Ôćĺ
   145ÔćĺThis will be used for the output directory and file naming. Want to use this name or suggest something different?"
   146Ôćĺ
   147ÔćĺWait for confirmation or alternative.
   148Ôćĺ
   149Ôćĺ### 7. Summarize Gathered Intent
   150Ôćĺ
   151Ôćĺ"**Here's what I've captured:**
   152Ôćĺ
   153Ôćĺ- **Target:** {repo URL or path}
   154Ôćĺ- **Intent:** {user's intent summary}
   155Ôćĺ- **Scope hints:** {any hints, or "None ÔÇö we'll define scope after analysis"}
   156Ôćĺ- **Skill name:** {confirmed name}
   157Ôćĺ- **Forge tier:** {tier}
   158Ôćĺ
   159ÔćĺReady to analyze the target repository?"
   160Ôćĺ
   161Ôćĺ### 8. Present MENU OPTIONS
   162Ôćĺ
   163ÔćĺDisplay: "**Select:** [C] Continue to Target Analysis"
   164Ôćĺ
   165Ôćĺ#### Menu Handling Logic:
   166Ôćĺ
   167Ôćĺ- IF C: Load, read entire file, then execute {nextStepFile}
   168Ôćĺ- IF Any other: Help user, then [Redisplay Menu Options](#8-present-menu-options)
   169Ôćĺ
   170Ôćĺ#### EXECUTION RULES:
   171Ôćĺ
   172Ôćĺ- ALWAYS halt and wait for user input after presenting menu
   173Ôćĺ- ONLY proceed to next step when user selects 'C'
   174Ôćĺ
   175Ôćĺ## CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE
   176Ôćĺ
   177ÔćĺONLY WHEN C is selected and target repository is confirmed will you load and read fully `./step-02-analyze-target.md` to execute target analysis.
   178Ôćĺ
   179Ôćĺ---
   180Ôćĺ
   181Ôćĺ## ­čÜĘ SYSTEM SUCCESS/FAILURE METRICS
   182Ôćĺ
   183Ôćĺ### Ôťů SUCCESS:
   184Ôćĺ
   185Ôćĺ- Forge tier discovered (or defaulted to Quick with warning), override applied if set in preferences.yaml
   186Ôćĺ- Target repository confirmed (GitHub URL or local path)
   187Ôćĺ- User intent captured with enough context for scoping
   188Ôćĺ- Skill name derived and confirmed
   189Ôćĺ- Scope hints noted (if provided)
   190Ôćĺ- User ready to proceed to analysis
   191Ôćĺ
   192Ôćĺ### ÔŁî SYSTEM FAILURE:
   193Ôćĺ
   194Ôćĺ- Analyzing source code in this step (too early)
   195Ôćĺ- Proceeding without a confirmed target repository
   196Ôćĺ- Not attempting to discover forge tier
   197Ôćĺ- Generating a skill name without user confirmation
   198Ôćĺ- Skipping the intent gathering conversation
   199Ôćĺ
   200Ôćĺ**Master Rule:** Skipping steps, optimizing sequences, or not following exact instructions is FORBIDDEN and constitutes SYSTEM FAILURE.
   201Ôćĺ

1Ôćĺ---
     2Ôćĺname: 'step-02-analyze-target'
     3Ôćĺdescription: 'Analyze target repository structure, detect language, and list modules and exports'
     4Ôćĺ
     5ÔćĺnextStepFile: './step-03-scope-definition.md'
     6Ôćĺ---
     7Ôćĺ
     8Ôćĺ# Step 2: Analyze Target
     9Ôćĺ
    10Ôćĺ## STEP GOAL:
    11Ôćĺ
    12ÔćĺTo analyze the target repository by resolving its location, reading its structure, detecting the primary language, and listing top-level modules and exports ÔÇö providing the user with a factual foundation for scoping decisions.
    13Ôćĺ
    14Ôćĺ## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES (READ FIRST):
    15Ôćĺ
    16Ôćĺ### Universal Rules:
    17Ôćĺ
    18Ôćĺ- ­čŤĹ NEVER generate content without user input
    19Ôćĺ- ­čôľ CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action
    20Ôćĺ- ­čöä CRITICAL: When loading next step with 'C', ensure entire file is read
    21Ôćĺ- ­čôő YOU ARE A FACILITATOR, not a content generator
    22Ôćĺ- Ôťů YOU MUST ALWAYS SPEAK OUTPUT In your Agent communication style with the config `{communication_language}`
    23Ôćĺ- ÔÜÖ´ŞĆ TOOL/SUBPROCESS FALLBACK: If any instruction references a subprocess, subagent, or tool you do not have access to, you MUST still achieve the outcome in your main context thread
    24Ôćĺ
    25Ôćĺ### Role Reinforcement:
    26Ôćĺ
    27Ôćĺ- Ôťů You are a source code analyst in Ferris Architect mode
    28Ôćĺ- Ôťů If you already have been given a name, communication_style and identity, continue to use those while playing this new role
    29Ôćĺ- Ôťů Report ONLY what is found ÔÇö zero hallucination tolerance
    30Ôćĺ- Ôťů You bring structural analysis expertise, user brings their domain understanding
    31Ôćĺ
    32Ôćĺ### Step-Specific Rules:
    33Ôćĺ
    34Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Focus only on analysis ÔÇö do not define scope yet (that's step 03)
    35Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to make scoping decisions or recommendations in this step
    36Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to hallucinate or guess about repository contents
    37Ôćĺ- ­čĺČ Approach: Prescriptive ÔÇö exact analysis instructions, factual reporting
    38Ôćĺ- ­čôő Report everything found; let the user interpret for scoping
    39Ôćĺ
    40Ôćĺ## EXECUTION PROTOCOLS:
    41Ôćĺ
    42Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Follow the MANDATORY SEQUENCE exactly
    43Ôćĺ- ­čĺż Analysis results are accumulated conversationally for use in step 03
    44Ôćĺ- ­čôľ This step is autonomous ÔÇö minimal user interaction required
    45Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to proceed if target is inaccessible ÔÇö halt with actionable error
    46Ôćĺ
    47Ôćĺ## CONTEXT BOUNDARIES:
    48Ôćĺ
    49Ôćĺ- Available context: Target repo (URL or path), user intent, skill name, forge tier ÔÇö all from step 01
    50Ôćĺ- Focus: Structural analysis of the repository
    51Ôćĺ- Limits: Do not read file contents deeply ÔÇö only structure, exports, and metadata
    52Ôćĺ- Dependencies: Confirmed target repository from step 01
    53Ôćĺ
    54Ôćĺ## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
    55Ôćĺ
    56Ôćĺ**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise unless user explicitly requests a change.
    57Ôćĺ
    58Ôćĺ### 1. Resolve Target Location
    59Ôćĺ
    60Ôćĺ**For GitHub URLs:**
    61Ôćĺ- Use `gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}` to verify the repository exists
    62Ôćĺ- Use `gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/git/trees/HEAD?recursive=1` to get the file tree
    63Ôćĺ- If inaccessible: **HALT** ÔÇö "**Error:** Cannot access repository at {url}. Please verify the URL is correct and you have access. If private, ensure `gh auth` is configured."
    64Ôćĺ
    65Ôćĺ**For local paths:**
    66Ôćĺ- Verify the directory exists
    67Ôćĺ- List the directory tree
    68Ôćĺ- If path doesn't exist: **HALT** ÔÇö "**Error:** Directory not found at {path}. Please verify the path is correct."
    69Ôćĺ
    70ÔćĺDisplay: "**Resolving target...**"
    71Ôćĺ
    72Ôćĺ### 2. Read Repository Structure
    73Ôćĺ
    74ÔćĺList the top-level directory structure:
    75Ôćĺ
    76Ôćĺ"**Repository Structure:**
    77Ôćĺ```
    78Ôćĺ{repo-name}/
    79ÔćĺÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ {top-level files}
    80ÔćĺÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ {top-level directories}/
    81ÔćĺÔöé   ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ ...
    82ÔćĺÔööÔöÇÔöÇ ...
    83Ôćĺ```
    84Ôćĺ**Total:** {file count} files, {directory count} directories"
    85Ôćĺ
    86Ôćĺ### 3. Detect Primary Language
    87Ôćĺ
    88ÔćĺExamine file extensions and configuration files to detect the primary language:
    89Ôćĺ
    90Ôćĺ**Detection signals (check in order):**
    91Ôćĺ1. `package.json` Ôćĺ JavaScript/TypeScript
    92Ôćĺ2. `tsconfig.json` Ôćĺ TypeScript
    93Ôćĺ3. `Cargo.toml` Ôćĺ Rust
    94Ôćĺ4. `pyproject.toml` or `setup.py` or `setup.cfg` Ôćĺ Python
    95Ôćĺ5. `go.mod` Ôćĺ Go
    96Ôćĺ6. `pom.xml` or `build.gradle` Ôćĺ Java
    97Ôćĺ7. `*.csproj` or `*.sln` Ôćĺ C#
    98Ôćĺ8. `Gemfile` Ôćĺ Ruby
    99Ôćĺ9. File extension frequency analysis as fallback
   100Ôćĺ
   101Ôćĺ"**Detected language:** {language}
   102Ôćĺ**Confidence:** {high/medium/low}
   103Ôćĺ**Detection source:** {what config file or pattern confirmed it}"
   104Ôćĺ
   105ÔćĺIf confidence is low or ambiguous: flag for user override in step 03.
   106Ôćĺ
   107Ôćĺ### 4. List Top-Level Modules and Exports
   108Ôćĺ
   109ÔćĺBased on detected language, identify public API surface:
   110Ôćĺ
   111Ôćĺ**For JavaScript/TypeScript:**
   112Ôćĺ- Check `package.json` for `main`, `exports`, `module` fields
   113Ôćĺ- Look for `index.ts`/`index.js` in `src/`
   114Ôćĺ- List directories under `src/` as potential modules
   115Ôćĺ
   116Ôćĺ**For Python:**
   117Ôćĺ- Check `__init__.py` files for public exports
   118Ôćĺ- List top-level packages under the source directory
   119Ôćĺ
   120Ôćĺ**For Rust:**
   121Ôćĺ- Check `lib.rs` for `pub mod` declarations
   122Ôćĺ- List modules from `src/` directory
   123Ôćĺ
   124Ôćĺ**For other languages:**
   125Ôćĺ- List top-level source directories as potential modules
   126Ôćĺ- Note any obvious entry points
   127Ôćĺ
   128Ôćĺ"**Top-Level Modules/Directories:**
   129Ôćĺ{numbered list of modules with brief description of each}
   130Ôćĺ
   131Ôćĺ**Detected Exports/Entry Points:**
   132Ôćĺ{numbered list of public-facing items found}"
   133Ôćĺ
   134Ôćĺ**Semantic Signals (Forge+ and Deep with ccc only):**
   135Ôćĺ
   136ÔćĺIf `tools.ccc` is true in forge-tier.yaml, supplement the module listing with a semantic discovery pass:
   137Ôćĺ
   138ÔćĺRun `ccc_bridge.search("{repo_name} public API exports modules", source_path, top_k=10)`.
   139Ôćĺ
   140ÔćĺIf results are returned, display:
   141Ôćĺ
   142Ôćĺ"**Semantic Signals (ccc):**
   143Ôćĺ{numbered list of file:snippet pairs from CCC results ÔÇö top 5 most relevant}"
   144Ôćĺ
   145ÔćĺThis supplements ÔÇö never replaces ÔÇö the explicit module list above. CCC may surface non-obvious entry points (dynamically constructed exports, re-export chains) that static directory analysis misses.
   146Ôćĺ
   147ÔćĺIf CCC is unavailable or returns no results: skip this subsection silently.
   148Ôćĺ
   149Ôćĺ### 5. Report Analysis Summary
   150Ôćĺ
   151ÔćĺPresent the complete analysis:
   152Ôćĺ
   153Ôćĺ"**Analysis Complete**
   154Ôćĺ
   155Ôćĺ---
   156Ôćĺ
   157Ôćĺ**Target:** {repo URL or path}
   158Ôćĺ**Language:** {detected language} ({confidence})
   159Ôćĺ**Structure:** {file count} files across {directory count} directories
   160Ôćĺ
   161Ôćĺ**Key Modules ({count}):**
   162Ôćĺ{bulleted list of modules}
   163Ôćĺ
   164Ôćĺ**Public Exports/Entry Points ({count}):**
   165Ôćĺ{bulleted list of exports}
   166Ôćĺ
   167Ôćĺ**Notable Files:**
   168Ôćĺ- README: {found/not found}
   169Ôćĺ- Tests: {found/not found ÔÇö location}

    11Ôćĺ# Step 3: Scope Definition
    12Ôćĺ
    13Ôćĺ## STEP GOAL:
    14Ôćĺ
    15ÔćĺTo collaboratively define the skill's inclusion and exclusion boundaries using the analysis findings from step 02, scope templates, and the user's intent from step 01.
    16Ôćĺ
    17Ôćĺ## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES (READ FIRST):
    18Ôćĺ
    19Ôćĺ### Universal Rules:
    20Ôćĺ
    21Ôćĺ- ­čŤĹ NEVER generate content without user input
    22Ôćĺ- ­čôľ CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action
    23Ôćĺ- ­čöä CRITICAL: When loading next step with 'C', ensure entire file is read
    24Ôćĺ- ­čôő YOU ARE A FACILITATOR, not a content generator
    25Ôćĺ- Ôťů YOU MUST ALWAYS SPEAK OUTPUT In your Agent communication style with the config `{communication_language}`
    26Ôćĺ- ÔÜÖ´ŞĆ TOOL/SUBPROCESS FALLBACK: If any instruction references a subprocess, subagent, or tool you do not have access to, you MUST still achieve the outcome in your main context thread
    27Ôćĺ
    28Ôćĺ### Role Reinforcement:
    29Ôćĺ
    30Ôćĺ- Ôťů You are a skill boundary architect in Ferris Architect mode (collaborative, exploratory)
    31Ôćĺ- Ôťů If you already have been given a name, communication_style and identity, continue to use those while playing this new role
    32Ôćĺ- Ôťů We engage in collaborative dialogue, not command-response
    33Ôćĺ- Ôťů You bring API surface design expertise, user brings their domain knowledge of what's valuable
    34Ôćĺ- Ôťů Maintain warm, guiding tone ÔÇö help the user make informed scoping decisions
    35Ôćĺ
    36Ôćĺ### Step-Specific Rules:
    37Ôćĺ
    38Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Focus only on defining scope boundaries ÔÇö do not write the brief yet (that's step 05)
    39Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to make scope decisions unilaterally ÔÇö user drives all scope choices
    40Ôćĺ- ­čĺČ Approach: Intent-based discovery ÔÇö "Which parts are most relevant to your use case?"
    41Ôćĺ- ­čôő Produce: scope type, include patterns, exclude patterns
    42Ôćĺ
    43Ôćĺ## EXECUTION PROTOCOLS:
    44Ôćĺ
    45Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Follow the MANDATORY SEQUENCE exactly
    46Ôćĺ- ­čĺż Accumulate scope decisions conversationally for use in subsequent steps
    47Ôćĺ- ­čôľ Reference analysis findings from step 02 throughout
    48Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to proceed without user confirmation of scope boundaries
    49Ôćĺ
    50Ôćĺ## CONTEXT BOUNDARIES:
    51Ôćĺ
    52Ôćĺ- Available context: Target repo, user intent, skill name, forge tier (step 01), repo structure, detected language, modules, exports (step 02)
    53Ôćĺ- Focus: Defining WHAT is in scope and what is NOT
    54Ôćĺ- Limits: Do not generate the brief ÔÇö only define boundaries
    55Ôćĺ- Dependencies: Analysis findings from step 02
    56Ôćĺ
    57Ôćĺ## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
    58Ôćĺ
    59Ôćĺ**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise unless user explicitly requests a change.
    60Ôćĺ
    61Ôćĺ### 1. Present Scope Context
    62Ôćĺ
    63Ôćĺ"**Let's define the scope for your skill.**
    64Ôćĺ
    65ÔćĺBased on the analysis, here's what we're working with:
    66Ôćĺ
    67Ôćĺ- **Target:** {repo}
    68Ôćĺ- **Language:** {detected language}
    69Ôćĺ- **Modules found:** {count} ÔÇö {list names}
    70Ôćĺ- **Your intent:** {user intent from step 01}
    71Ôćĺ{If scope hints from step 01:}
    72Ôćĺ- **Your initial scope hints:** {hints}"
    73Ôćĺ
    74Ôćĺ### 2. Handle Docs-Only Mode (if applicable)
    75Ôćĺ
    76Ôćĺ**If `source_type: "docs-only"`:**
    77Ôćĺ
    78Ôćĺ"**Docs-only mode ÔÇö scope is defined by documentation pages.**
    79Ôćĺ
    80ÔćĺYou've provided these documentation URLs:
    81Ôćĺ{numbered list of doc_urls with labels}
    82Ôćĺ
    83ÔćĺWhich pages should be included in the skill? (Enter numbers, or 'all')
    84ÔćĺAny additional documentation URLs to add?"
    85Ôćĺ
    86ÔćĺWait for confirmation. Then skip to section 5 (Summarize Scope Decisions) with:
    87Ôćĺ- `scope.type: "docs-only"`
    88Ôćĺ- `scope.include`: confirmed doc URLs
    89Ôćĺ- `scope.notes: "Generated from external documentation. All content is T3 confidence."`
    90Ôćĺ
    91Ôćĺ**If `source_type: "source"` (default):** Continue to scope templates below.
    92Ôćĺ
    93Ôćĺ### 2b. Offer Scope Templates
    94Ôćĺ
    95ÔćĺLoad `{scopeTemplatesFile}` for the scope type options ([F], [M], [P]) and their descriptions.
    96Ôćĺ
    97ÔćĺPresent: "**How broadly should this skill cover the library?**" followed by the three scope type options from the loaded reference.
    98Ôćĺ
    99ÔćĺAsk: "Which scope type fits your needs?"
   100Ôćĺ
   101ÔćĺWait for user selection.
   102Ôćĺ
   103Ôćĺ### 3. Define Boundaries Based on Selection
   104Ôćĺ
   105ÔćĺUsing the boundary definitions from `{scopeTemplatesFile}`, present the appropriate flow for the user's selected scope type ([F], [M], or [P]). Follow each type's prompts and wait for user input at each phase before proceeding.
   106Ôćĺ
   107Ôćĺ### 4. Handle Language Override
   108Ôćĺ
   109Ôćĺ{If language detection confidence was low from step 02:}
   110Ôćĺ
   111Ôćĺ"**Language confirmation needed.**
   112Ôćĺ
   113ÔćĺThe analysis detected **{language}** with low confidence. Is this correct, or should we set a different primary language?"
   114Ôćĺ
   115ÔćĺWait for confirmation or override.
   116Ôćĺ
   117Ôćĺ### 5. Summarize Scope Decisions
   118Ôćĺ
   119Ôćĺ"**Scope Summary:**
   120Ôćĺ
   121Ôćĺ**Type:** {Full Library / Specific Modules / Public API}
   122Ôćĺ
   123Ôćĺ**Include:**
   124Ôćĺ{bulleted list of include patterns}
   125Ôćĺ
   126Ôćĺ**Exclude:**
   127Ôćĺ{bulleted list of exclude patterns}
   128Ôćĺ
   129Ôćĺ**Language:** {confirmed language}
   130Ôćĺ
   131Ôćĺ{If any scope notes:}
   132Ôćĺ**Notes:** {scope notes}
   133Ôćĺ
   134ÔćĺDoes this look right? You can adjust before we continue."
   135Ôćĺ
   136ÔćĺWait for confirmation. Make adjustments if requested.
   137Ôćĺ
   138Ôćĺ### 5b. Scripts & Assets Intent (Optional)
   139Ôćĺ
   140Ôćĺ**Only ask when `scope.type` is `full-library` or `specific-modules` (skip for `public-api` and `docs-only`).**
   141Ôćĺ
   142Ôćĺ"Does this library include executable scripts (CLI tools, validation scripts, setup helpers) or static assets (config templates, JSON schemas, example configs) that should be packaged with the skill?"
   143Ôćĺ
   144Ôćĺ- **[D] Auto-detect** from source (default) ÔÇö SKF will scan for `scripts/`, `bin/`, `assets/`, `templates/`, `schemas/` directories
   145Ôćĺ- **[N] None expected** ÔÇö skip script/asset detection
   146Ôćĺ- Or describe what you expect (free text)
   147Ôćĺ
   148ÔćĺRecord the response as `scripts_intent` and `assets_intent` in the brief. Default to `detect` if user does not respond or skips.
   149Ôćĺ
   150Ôćĺ### 6. Present MENU OPTIONS
   151Ôćĺ
   152ÔćĺDisplay: **Select an Option:** [A] Advanced Elicitation [P] Party Mode [C] Continue to Brief Confirmation
   153Ôćĺ
   154Ôćĺ#### Menu Handling Logic:
   155Ôćĺ
   156Ôćĺ- IF A: Execute {advancedElicitationTask}, and when finished redisplay the menu
   157Ôćĺ- IF P: Execute {partyModeWorkflow}, and when finished redisplay the menu
   158Ôćĺ- IF C: Load, read entire file, then execute {nextStepFile}
   159Ôćĺ- IF Any other comments or queries: help user respond then [Redisplay Menu Options](#6-present-menu-options)
   160Ôćĺ
   161Ôćĺ#### EXECUTION RULES:
   162Ôćĺ
   163Ôćĺ- ALWAYS halt and wait for user input after presenting menu
   164Ôćĺ- ONLY proceed to next step when user selects 'C'
   165Ôćĺ- After other menu items execution, return to this menu
   166Ôćĺ- User can chat or ask questions ÔÇö always respond and then redisplay menu
   167Ôćĺ
   168Ôćĺ## CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE
   169Ôćĺ
   170ÔćĺONLY WHEN C is selected and scope boundaries are confirmed will you load and read fully `./step-04-confirm-brief.md` to present the complete brief for confirmation.
   171Ôćĺ
   172Ôćĺ---
   173Ôćĺ
   174Ôćĺ## ­čÜĘ SYSTEM SUCCESS/FAILURE METRICS
   175Ôćĺ
   176Ôćĺ### Ôťů SUCCESS:
   177Ôćĺ
   178Ôćĺ- Scope type selected by user (Full Library, Specific Modules, or Public API)
   179Ôćĺ- Include patterns defined and confirmed
   180Ôćĺ- Exclude patterns defined and confirmed
   181Ôćĺ- Language confirmed (or overridden if detection was low confidence)
   182Ôćĺ- User confirmed the scope summary
   183Ôćĺ- Menu presented and user input handled correctly
   184Ôćĺ
   185Ôćĺ### ÔŁî SYSTEM FAILURE:
   186Ôćĺ
   187Ôćĺ- Making scope decisions without user input
   188Ôćĺ- Not presenting the scope templates for user selection
   189Ôćĺ- Skipping the scope summary confirmation
   190Ôćĺ- Not handling language override when detection confidence was low
   191Ôćĺ- Writing the brief in this step (too early ÔÇö that's step 05)
   192Ôćĺ
   193Ôćĺ**Master Rule:** Skipping steps, optimizing sequences, or not following exact instructions is FORBIDDEN and constitutes SYSTEM FAILURE.
   194Ôćĺ

    36Ôćĺ### Step-Specific Rules:

    39Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to proceed without explicit user approval
    40Ôćĺ- ­čĺČ Approach: Prescriptive ÔÇö exact presentation format showing all YAML fields

    43Ôćĺ## EXECUTION PROTOCOLS:

    50Ôćĺ## CONTEXT BOUNDARIES:

    57Ôćĺ## MANDATORY SEQUENCE

     8Ôćĺ# Step 5: Write Brief
     9Ôćĺ
    10Ôćĺ## STEP GOAL:
    11Ôćĺ
    12ÔćĺTo generate the complete skill-brief.yaml from the approved brief data and write it to the forge data folder, completing the brief-skill workflow.
    13Ôćĺ
    14Ôćĺ## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES (READ FIRST):
    15Ôćĺ
    16Ôćĺ### Universal Rules:
    17Ôćĺ
    18Ôćĺ- ­čŤĹ NEVER generate content without user input
    19Ôćĺ- ­čôľ CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action
    20Ôćĺ- ­čôő YOU ARE A FACILITATOR, not a content generator
    21Ôćĺ- Ôťů YOU MUST ALWAYS SPEAK OUTPUT In your Agent communication style with the config `{communication_language}`
    22Ôćĺ- ÔÜÖ´ŞĆ TOOL/SUBPROCESS FALLBACK: If any instruction references a subprocess, subagent, or tool you do not have access to, you MUST still achieve the outcome in your main context thread
    23Ôćĺ
    24Ôćĺ### Role Reinforcement:
    25Ôćĺ
    26Ôćĺ- Ôťů You are a skill brief generator in Ferris Architect mode
    27Ôćĺ- Ôťů If you already have been given a name, communication_style and identity, continue to use those while playing this new role
    28Ôćĺ- Ôťů Generate output precisely matching the schema ÔÇö no creative embellishment
    29Ôćĺ- Ôťů This is the final step ÔÇö deliver clean, validated output
    30Ôćĺ
    31Ôćĺ### Step-Specific Rules:
    32Ôćĺ
    33Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Focus only on writing the file ÔÇö all decisions have been made
    34Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to change any field values without user request ÔÇö the brief was already approved
    35Ôćĺ- ­čĺČ Approach: Prescriptive ÔÇö generate exact YAML matching the schema template
    36Ôćĺ- ­čôő Create the output directory if it doesn't exist
    37Ôćĺ
    38Ôćĺ## EXECUTION PROTOCOLS:
    39Ôćĺ
    40Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Follow the MANDATORY SEQUENCE exactly
    41Ôćĺ- ­čĺż Write skill-brief.yaml to {forge_data_folder}/{skill-name}/
    42Ôćĺ- ­čôľ This is the final step ÔÇö mark workflow complete
    43Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to modify approved field values
    44Ôćĺ
    45Ôćĺ## CONTEXT BOUNDARIES:
    46Ôćĺ
    47Ôćĺ- Available context: Complete approved brief data from steps 01-04
    48Ôćĺ- Focus: File generation and writing
    49Ôćĺ- Limits: No field changes ÔÇö write exactly what was approved
    50Ôćĺ- Dependencies: Explicit user approval from step 04
    51Ôćĺ
    52Ôćĺ## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
    53Ôćĺ
    54Ôćĺ**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise unless user explicitly requests a change.
    55Ôćĺ
    56Ôćĺ### 1. Load Schema Template
    57Ôćĺ
    58ÔćĺLoad `{briefSchemaFile}` to reference the YAML template structure.
    59Ôćĺ
    60Ôćĺ### 2. Create Output Directory
    61Ôćĺ
    62ÔćĺCreate the directory `{forge_data_folder}/{skill-name}/` if it doesn't already exist.
    63Ôćĺ
    64ÔćĺIf `{forge_data_folder}` is not set or doesn't exist:
    65Ôćĺ- Fall back to `{output_folder}/forge-data/{skill-name}/`
    66Ôćĺ- Inform user: "**Note:** forge_data_folder not configured. Writing to {output_folder}/forge-data/{skill-name}/ instead."
    67Ôćĺ
    68Ôćĺ### 3. Generate skill-brief.yaml
    69Ôćĺ
    70ÔćĺGenerate the YAML file using the approved field values and the schema template:
    71Ôćĺ
    72Ôćĺ```yaml
    73Ôćĺ---
    74Ôćĺname: "{approved skill name}"
    75Ôćĺversion: "{detected source version or 1.0.0}"
    76Ôćĺsource_type: "{source or docs-only}"
    77Ôćĺsource_repo: "{approved source repo or doc site URL}"
    78Ôćĺlanguage: "{approved language}"
    79Ôćĺdescription: "{approved description}"
    80Ôćĺforge_tier: "{approved forge tier}"
    81Ôćĺcreated: "{current date}"
    82Ôćĺcreated_by: "{user_name}"
    83Ôćĺscope:
    84Ôćĺ  type: "{approved scope type}"
    85Ôćĺ  include:
    86Ôćĺ    - "{approved include patterns}"
    87Ôćĺ  exclude:
    88Ôćĺ    - "{approved exclude patterns}"
    89Ôćĺ  notes: "{approved scope notes or empty string}"
    90Ôćĺ# Include doc_urls when source_type is "docs-only" or when supplemental doc URLs were provided
    91Ôćĺ# doc_urls:
    92Ôćĺ#   - url: "{documentation URL}"
    93Ôćĺ#     label: "{page label}"
    94Ôćĺ# scripts_intent: detect         # Optional: detect | none | description of expected scripts
    95Ôćĺ# assets_intent: detect          # Optional: detect | none | description of expected assets
    96Ôćĺ# source_authority: community    # Optional: official | community | internal
    97Ôćĺ---
    98Ôćĺ```
    99Ôćĺ
   100Ôćĺ**If `source_type: "docs-only"`:** Include `doc_urls` array (required). `source_repo` may be set to the doc site URL for reference or omitted. `source_authority` must be `community`.
   101Ôćĺ
   102Ôćĺ**If `doc_urls` were collected during scope definition (supplemental mode):** Include the `doc_urls` array even when `source_type: "source"`.
   103Ôćĺ
   104Ôćĺ### 4. Write the File
   105Ôćĺ
   106ÔćĺWrite the generated YAML to `{forge_data_folder}/{skill-name}/skill-brief.yaml`.
   107Ôćĺ
   108ÔćĺIf write fails: **HALT** ÔÇö "**Error:** Failed to write skill-brief.yaml. Please check that the directory is writable and try again."
   109Ôćĺ
   110Ôćĺ### 5. QMD Collection Registration (Deep Tier Only)
   111Ôćĺ
   112Ôćĺ**IF forge tier is Deep AND QMD tool is available:**
   113Ôćĺ
   114ÔćĺIndex the skill brief into a QMD collection so that portfolio-level searches can find existing briefs and avoid duplicate skill creation across large monorepos.
   115Ôćĺ
   116Ôćĺ**Collection creation:**
   117Ôćĺ
   118ÔćĺCreate a QMD collection targeting only the brief file:
   119Ôćĺ```bash
   120Ôćĺqmd collection add {forge_data_folder}/{skill-name} --name {skill-name}-brief --mask "skill-brief.yaml"
   121Ôćĺqmd embed
   122Ôćĺ```
   123Ôćĺ
   124ÔćĺIf collection already exists (re-briefing): remove and recreate for atomic replace:
   125Ôćĺ```bash
   126Ôćĺqmd collection remove {skill-name}-brief
   127Ôćĺqmd collection add {forge_data_folder}/{skill-name} --name {skill-name}-brief --mask "skill-brief.yaml"
   128Ôćĺqmd embed
   129Ôćĺ```
   130Ôćĺ
   131Ôćĺ**Registry update:**
   132Ôćĺ
   133ÔćĺRead `{sidecar_path}/forge-tier.yaml` and update the `qmd_collections` array.
   134Ôćĺ
   135ÔćĺIf an entry with `name: "{skill-name}-brief"` already exists, replace it. Otherwise, append:
   136Ôćĺ
   137Ôćĺ```yaml
   138Ôćĺ  - name: "{skill-name}-brief"
   139Ôćĺ    type: "brief"
   140Ôćĺ    source_workflow: "brief-skill"
   141Ôćĺ    skill_name: "{skill-name}"
   142Ôćĺ    created_at: "{current ISO date}"
   143Ôćĺ```
   144Ôćĺ
   145ÔćĺWrite the updated forge-tier.yaml.
   146Ôćĺ
   147Ôćĺ**Error handling:**
   148Ôćĺ- If QMD collection creation fails: log the error. Do NOT fail the workflow ÔÇö the brief file was already written successfully.
   149Ôćĺ- If forge-tier.yaml update fails: log the error, continue.
   150Ôćĺ
   151Ôćĺ**IF forge tier is NOT Deep:** Skip this section silently. No messaging.
   152Ôćĺ
   153Ôćĺ### 6. Display Success Summary
   154Ôćĺ
   155Ôćĺ"**Skill brief written successfully.**
   156Ôćĺ
   157Ôćĺ---
   158Ôćĺ
   159Ôćĺ**File:** `{forge_data_folder}/{skill-name}/skill-brief.yaml`
   160Ôćĺ**Skill:** {name}
   161Ôćĺ**Language:** {language}
   162Ôćĺ**Scope:** {scope type}
   163Ôćĺ**Forge Tier:** {forge tier}
   164Ôćĺ
   165Ôćĺ---
   166Ôćĺ
   167Ôćĺ## Next Steps
   168Ôćĺ
   169ÔćĺYour skill brief is ready. To compile the actual skill from this brief, run:
   170Ôćĺ
   171Ôćĺ**create-skill** ÔÇö Reads your skill-brief.yaml and compiles a complete SKILL.md with AST-backed analysis.
   172Ôćĺ
   173ÔćĺAfter compilation, you can:
   174Ôćĺ- **test-skill** ÔÇö Validate the compiled skill
   175Ôćĺ- **export-skill** ÔÇö Package the skill for distribution
   176Ôćĺ
   177Ôćĺ---
   178Ôćĺ
   179Ôćĺ**Brief-skill workflow complete.**"
   180Ôćĺ
   181Ôćĺ### 7. End Workflow
   182Ôćĺ
   183ÔćĺThis is the final step. No next step file to load. The workflow is complete.
   184Ôćĺ
   185Ôćĺ## CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE
   186Ôćĺ
   187ÔćĺThis is the FINAL step of the brief-skill workflow. After writing the file and displaying the success summary, the workflow ends.
   188Ôćĺ
   189Ôćĺ---
   190Ôćĺ
   191Ôćĺ## ­čÜĘ SYSTEM SUCCESS/FAILURE METRICS
   192Ôćĺ
   193Ôćĺ### Ôťů SUCCESS:
   194Ôćĺ
   195Ôćĺ- Schema loaded for template reference
   196Ôćĺ- Output directory created (or fallback used with notification)
   197Ôćĺ- skill-brief.yaml generated with all required fields matching approved values
   198Ôćĺ- File written successfully to correct location

   200Ôćĺ- Non-Deep tier: QMD indexing skipped silently
   201Ôćĺ- Success summary displayed with next steps recommendation

   203Ôćĺ
   204Ôćĺ### ÔŁî SYSTEM FAILURE:
   205Ôćĺ

1Ôćĺ# Skill Brief Schema
     2Ôćĺ
     3Ôćĺ## Required Fields
     4Ôćĺ
     5Ôćĺ| Field | Type | Description | Validation |
     6Ôćĺ|-------|------|-------------|------------|
     7Ôćĺ| name | string | Kebab-case skill name | Must match `[a-z0-9-]+` |
     8Ôćĺ| version | string | Semantic version | Must match `X.Y.Z` pattern. Auto-detect from source (see Version Detection below), fall back to "1.0.0" |
     9Ôćĺ| source_repo | string | GitHub URL or local path | Must be accessible |
    10Ôćĺ| language | string | Primary programming language | Must be detected or user-specified |
    11Ôćĺ| scope | object | Inclusion/exclusion boundaries | Must have type + at least one pattern |
    12Ôćĺ| description | string | What this skill covers | 1-3 sentences |
    13Ôćĺ| forge_tier | string | Compilation tier | One of: Quick, Forge, Forge+, Deep (Title Case) |
    14Ôćĺ| created | string | Creation date | ISO date format |
    15Ôćĺ| created_by | string | User who created the brief | From config user_name |
    16Ôćĺ
    17Ôćĺ## Optional Fields
    18Ôćĺ
    19Ôćĺ| Field | Type | Description | Validation |
    20Ôćĺ|-------|------|-------------|------------|
    21Ôćĺ| source_type | string | Source mode: `source` or `docs-only` | Default `source`. When `docs-only`: `source_repo` optional, `doc_urls` required |
    22Ôćĺ| doc_urls | array | Documentation URLs for T3 content | Each entry: `{url, label}`. Required when `source_type: "docs-only"` |
    23Ôćĺ| `scripts_intent` | string | Describes whether scripts should be extracted. Values: `detect` (auto-detect from source ÔÇö default when absent), `none` (skip scripts), or a free-text description of expected scripts (e.g., "CLI validation tools in bin/"). |
    24Ôćĺ| `assets_intent` | string | Describes whether assets should be extracted. Values: `detect` (auto-detect from source ÔÇö default when absent), `none` (skip assets), or a free-text description of expected assets (e.g., "JSON schemas in schemas/"). |
    25Ôćĺ| `source_authority` | string | `official`, `community`, or `internal`. Default `community`. Set to `official` only when the skill creator is the library maintainer. Forced to `community` when `source_type: "docs-only"`. |
    26Ôćĺ
    27ÔćĺWhen `source_type: "docs-only"`:
    28Ôćĺ- `source_repo` becomes optional (set to doc site URL for reference)
    29Ôćĺ- `doc_urls` must have at least one entry
    30Ôćĺ- `source_authority` is forced to `community` (T3 external documentation cannot be `official`)
    31Ôćĺ- All extracted content gets `[EXT:{url}]` citations
    32Ôćĺ
    33Ôćĺ## Version Detection
    34Ôćĺ
    35ÔćĺDuring brief creation, attempt to auto-detect the source version before defaulting to `"1.0.0"`. Check the first matching file in the source:
    36Ôćĺ
    37Ôćĺ- **Python:** `pyproject.toml` `[project] version` (static) Ôćĺ if `dynamic = ["version"]`, check `__init__.py` for `__version__` Ôćĺ `_version.py` if exists Ôćĺ `setup.py` `version=` Ôćĺ `git describe --tags --abbrev=0`
    38Ôćĺ- **JavaScript/TypeScript:** `package.json` (`"version"`)
    39Ôćĺ- **Rust:** `Cargo.toml` `[package] version` (static) Ôćĺ if `version = { workspace = true }`, resolve from workspace root `Cargo.toml` Ôćĺ `git describe --tags --abbrev=0`
    40Ôćĺ- **Go:** version tag from `go.mod` or `git describe --tags --abbrev=0`
    41Ôćĺ
    42ÔćĺIf the source is a remote GitHub repo, use `gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/contents/{file}` to read the version file. If the source is local, read the file directly.
    43Ôćĺ
    44ÔćĺIf detection succeeds, use the detected version. If it fails or returns a non-semver value, fall back to `"1.0.0"`.
    45Ôćĺ
    46ÔćĺThe create-skill workflow (step-03-extract) also performs version reconciliation at extraction time ÔÇö if the source version has changed since the brief was created, the extraction step warns and uses the source version.
    47Ôćĺ
    48Ôćĺ## Scope Object Structure
    49Ôćĺ
    50Ôćĺ```yaml
    51Ôćĺscope:
    52Ôćĺ  type: full-library | specific-modules | public-api | docs-only
    53Ôćĺ  include:
    54Ôćĺ    - "src/**/*.ts"           # Glob patterns for included files/directories
    55Ôćĺ  exclude:
    56Ôćĺ    - "**/*.test.*"           # Glob patterns for excluded files
    57Ôćĺ    - "**/node_modules/**"
    58Ôćĺ  notes: "Optional notes about scope decisions"
    59Ôćĺ```
    60Ôćĺ
    61Ôćĺ## YAML Template
    62Ôćĺ
    63Ôćĺ```yaml
    64Ôćĺ---
    65Ôćĺname: "{skill-name}"
    66Ôćĺversion: "{detected-version or 1.0.0}"  # Auto-detect from source, fall back to 1.0.0
    67Ôćĺsource_type: "source"                    # "source" (default) or "docs-only"
    68Ôćĺsource_repo: "{github-url-or-local-path}"
    69Ôćĺlanguage: "{detected-language}"
    70Ôćĺdescription: "{brief-description}"
    71Ôćĺforge_tier: "{Quick|Forge|Forge+|Deep}"
    72Ôćĺcreated: "{date}"
    73Ôćĺcreated_by: "{user_name}"
    74Ôćĺscope:
    75Ôćĺ  type: "{full-library|specific-modules|public-api|docs-only}"
    76Ôćĺ  include:
    77Ôćĺ    - "{pattern}"
    78Ôćĺ  exclude:
    79Ôćĺ    - "{pattern}"
    80Ôćĺ  notes: "{optional-scope-notes}"
    81Ôćĺ# Optional: documentation URLs for T3 content (required when source_type: "docs-only")
    82Ôćĺ# doc_urls:
    83Ôćĺ#   - url: "https://docs.example.com/api"
    84Ôćĺ#     label: "API Reference"
    85Ôćĺ# scripts_intent: detect         # Optional: detect | none | description
    86Ôćĺ# assets_intent: detect          # Optional: detect | none | description
    87Ôćĺ# source_authority: community    # Optional: official | community | internal
    88Ôćĺ---
    89Ôćĺ```
    90Ôćĺ
    91Ôćĺ## Human-Readable Presentation Format
    92Ôćĺ
    93ÔćĺWhen presenting the brief for confirmation (brief-skill step 04 only ÔÇö not applicable to analyze-source batch generation), display as:
    94Ôćĺ
    95Ôćĺ```
    96ÔćĺSkill Brief: {name}
    97Ôćĺ====================
    98Ôćĺ
    99ÔćĺTarget:      {source_repo}
   100ÔćĺLanguage:    {language}
   101ÔćĺForge Tier:  {forge_tier}
   102ÔćĺDescription: {description}
   103Ôćĺ
   104ÔćĺScope: {scope.type}
   105Ôćĺ  Include: {scope.include patterns, one per line}
   106Ôćĺ  Exclude: {scope.exclude patterns, one per line}
   107Ôćĺ  Notes:   {scope.notes}
   108Ôćĺ
   109ÔćĺVersion:    {version}
   110ÔćĺCreated:    {created}
   111ÔćĺCreated by: {created_by}
   112Ôćĺ```
   113Ôćĺ
   114Ôćĺ## Validation Rules
   115Ôćĺ
   116Ôćĺ1. `name` must be unique within {forge_data_folder}
   117Ôćĺ2. `source_repo` must be accessible (gh api for GitHub, path exists for local)
   118Ôćĺ3. `language` must be a recognized programming language
   119Ôćĺ4. `scope.type` must be one of the four defined types
   120Ôćĺ5. `scope.include` must have at least one pattern (exception: `docs-only` scope, where include patterns are optional since no source code is available)
   121Ôćĺ6. `forge_tier` must be one of: Quick, Forge, Forge+, Deep (Title Case, must match the tier from forge-tier.yaml, or default to Quick)
   122Ôćĺ7. When `source_type: "docs-only"`: `doc_urls` must have >= 1 entry, `source_repo` becomes optional
   123Ôćĺ8. Each `doc_urls` entry must have a valid `url` field
   124Ôćĺ

1Ôćĺ---
     2Ôćĺname: quick-skill
     3Ôćĺdescription: Brief-less fast skill with package-to-repo resolution
     4Ôćĺweb_bundle: true
     5Ôćĺinstalled_path: '{project-root}/_bmad/skf/workflows/skillforge/quick-skill'
     6Ôćĺ---
     7Ôćĺ
     8Ôćĺ# Quick Skill
     9Ôćĺ
    10Ôćĺ**Goal:** The fastest path to a skill ÔÇö accept a GitHub URL or package name, resolve to source, extract public API surface, and produce a best-effort SKILL.md with context snippet and metadata. No brief needed.
    11Ôćĺ
    12Ôćĺ> **Note:** Quick Skill is tier-unaware by design. It does not load forge-tier.yaml or check preferences.yaml for tier_override. All output is produced at community-tier quality regardless of available tools.
    13Ôćĺ
    14Ôćĺ**Your Role:** In addition to your name, communication_style, and persona, you are also a rapid skill compiler collaborating with a developer. This is a partnership, not a client-vendor relationship. You bring source analysis and skill document assembly expertise, while the user brings the target package or repository they want to create a skill for. Work together efficiently ÔÇö speed is the priority.
    15Ôćĺ
    16Ôćĺ---
    17Ôćĺ
    18Ôćĺ## WORKFLOW ARCHITECTURE
    19Ôćĺ
    20Ôćĺ### Core Principles
    21Ôćĺ
    22Ôćĺ- **Micro-file Design**: Each step is a self contained instruction file that is a part of an overall workflow that must be followed exactly
    23Ôćĺ- **Just-In-Time Loading**: Only the current step file is in memory ÔÇö never load future step files until told to do so
    24Ôćĺ- **Sequential Enforcement**: Sequence within the step files must be completed in order, no skipping or optimization allowed
    25Ôćĺ- **Append-Only Building**: Build output files progressively as directed by each step
    26Ôćĺ
    27Ôćĺ### Step Processing Rules
    28Ôćĺ
    29Ôćĺ1. **READ COMPLETELY**: Always read the entire step file before taking any action
    30Ôćĺ2. **FOLLOW SEQUENCE**: Execute all numbered sections in order, never deviate
    31Ôćĺ3. **WAIT FOR INPUT**: If a menu is presented, halt and wait for user selection
    32Ôćĺ4. **CHECK CONTINUATION**: If the step has a menu with Continue as an option, only proceed to next step when user selects 'C' (Continue)
    33Ôćĺ5. **LOAD NEXT**: When directed, load, read entire file, then execute the next step file
    34Ôćĺ
    35Ôćĺ### Critical Rules (NO EXCEPTIONS)
    36Ôćĺ
    37Ôćĺ- ­čŤĹ **NEVER** load multiple step files simultaneously
    38Ôćĺ- ­čôľ **ALWAYS** read entire step file before execution
    39Ôćĺ- ­čÜź **NEVER** skip steps or optimize the sequence
    40Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» **ALWAYS** follow the exact instructions in the step file
    41Ôćĺ- ÔĆŞ´ŞĆ **ALWAYS** halt at menus and wait for user input
    42Ôćĺ- ­čôő **NEVER** create mental todo lists from future steps
    43Ôćĺ- Ôťů YOU MUST ALWAYS SPEAK OUTPUT In your Agent communication style with the config `{communication_language}`
    44Ôćĺ
    45Ôćĺ---
    46Ôćĺ
    47Ôćĺ## INITIALIZATION SEQUENCE
    48Ôćĺ
    49Ôćĺ### 1. Module Configuration Loading
    50Ôćĺ
    51ÔćĺLoad and read full config from {project-root}/_bmad/skf/config.yaml and resolve:
    52Ôćĺ
    53Ôćĺ- `project_name`, `output_folder`, `user_name`, `communication_language`, `document_output_language`
    54Ôćĺ- `skills_output_folder`, `forge_data_folder`
    55Ôćĺ
    56Ôćĺ### 2. First Step Execution
    57Ôćĺ
    58ÔćĺLoad, read the full file and then execute `./steps-c/step-01-resolve-target.md` to begin the workflow.
    59Ôćĺ

     2Ôćĺname: 'step-01-resolve-target'
     3Ôćĺdescription: 'Accept GitHub URL or package name and resolve to source repository'
     4Ôćĺ
     5ÔćĺnextStepFile: './step-02-ecosystem-check.md'
     6ÔćĺregistryResolutionData: '../data/registry-resolution.md'
     7Ôćĺ---
     8Ôćĺ
     9Ôćĺ# Step 1: Resolve Target
    10Ôćĺ
    11Ôćĺ## STEP GOAL:
    12Ôćĺ
    13ÔćĺTo accept a GitHub URL or package name from the user, resolve it to a GitHub repository, detect the primary language, and prepare state for source extraction.
    14Ôćĺ
    15Ôćĺ## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES (READ FIRST):
    16Ôćĺ
    17Ôćĺ### Universal Rules:
    18Ôćĺ
    19Ôćĺ- ­čŤĹ NEVER generate content without user input
    20Ôćĺ- ­čôľ CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action
    21Ôćĺ- ­čöä CRITICAL: When loading next step with 'C', ensure entire file is read
    22Ôćĺ- ­čôő YOU ARE A FACILITATOR, not a content generator
    23Ôćĺ- Ôťů YOU MUST ALWAYS SPEAK OUTPUT In your Agent communication style with the config `{communication_language}`
    24Ôćĺ
    25Ôćĺ### Role Reinforcement:
    26Ôćĺ
    27Ôćĺ- Ôťů You are a rapid skill compiler resolving a target package to its source
    28Ôćĺ- Ôťů If you already have been given a name, communication_style and identity, continue to use those while playing this new role
    29Ôćĺ- Ôťů Speed is the priority ÔÇö resolve quickly, fail fast if unresolvable
    30Ôćĺ- Ôťů You bring package ecosystem knowledge, user brings their target
    31Ôćĺ
    32Ôćĺ### Step-Specific Rules:
    33Ôćĺ
    34Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Focus only on resolving the target to a GitHub repository
    35Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to begin source extraction or compilation
    36Ôćĺ- ­čĺČ Approach: Accept input, resolve, confirm, proceed
    37Ôćĺ- ­čôő If resolution fails, hard halt with actionable guidance
    38Ôćĺ
    39Ôćĺ## EXECUTION PROTOCOLS:
    40Ôćĺ
    41Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Follow the MANDATORY SEQUENCE exactly
    42Ôćĺ- ­čĺż Set state: resolved_url, repo_name, language, scope_hint
    43Ôćĺ- ­čôľ Load {registryResolutionData} for resolution patterns
    44Ôćĺ- ­čÜź Do not proceed without a confirmed resolved repository
    45Ôćĺ
    46Ôćĺ## CONTEXT BOUNDARIES:
    47Ôćĺ
    48Ôćĺ- This is the first step ÔÇö no prior context exists
    49Ôćĺ- User provides a GitHub URL or package name (npm/PyPI/crates.io)
    50Ôćĺ- Optional: language hint, scope hint
    51Ôćĺ- Web browsing required for registry API lookups
    52Ôćĺ- Focus: resolution only, not extraction
    53Ôćĺ
    54Ôćĺ## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
    55Ôćĺ
    56Ôćĺ**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise unless user explicitly requests a change.
    57Ôćĺ
    58Ôćĺ### 1. Accept User Input
    59Ôćĺ
    60Ôćĺ"**Quick Skill ÔÇö fastest path to a skill.**
    61Ôćĺ
    62ÔćĺProvide a **GitHub URL** or **package name** and I'll resolve it to source and compile a best-effort SKILL.md.
    63Ôćĺ
    64Ôćĺ**Target:** (GitHub URL or package name)
    65Ôćĺ
    66Ôćĺ**Optional:**
    67Ôćĺ- **Language hint:** (if the repo is multi-language)
    68Ôćĺ- **Scope hint:** (specific directories to focus on)"
    69Ôćĺ
    70ÔćĺWait for user input.
    71Ôćĺ
    72Ôćĺ### 2. Classify Input Type
    73Ôćĺ
    74Ôćĺ**If input starts with `https://github.com/` or `github.com/`:**
    75Ôćĺ- Extract org/repo from URL
    76Ôćĺ- Set `resolved_url` to the GitHub URL
    77Ôćĺ- Set `repo_name` to the repo name (last path segment)
    78Ôćĺ- Skip to step 4 (Detect Language)
    79Ôćĺ
    80Ôćĺ**If input is a package name:**
    81Ôćĺ- Proceed to step 3 (Registry Resolution)
    82Ôćĺ
    83Ôćĺ### 3. Registry Resolution
    84Ôćĺ
    85ÔćĺLoad {registryResolutionData} for resolution patterns.
    86Ôćĺ
    87Ôćĺ**Execute the fallback chain in order ÔÇö stop at first success:**
    88Ôćĺ
    89Ôćĺ1. **npm registry:** Fetch `https://registry.npmjs.org/{package_name}` ÔÇö extract `repository.url`
    90Ôćĺ2. **PyPI registry:** Fetch `https://pypi.org/pypi/{package_name}/json` ÔÇö extract `info.project_urls.Source` or `info.home_page`
    91Ôćĺ3. **crates.io registry:** Fetch `https://crates.io/api/v1/crates/{package_name}` ÔÇö extract `crate.repository`
    92Ôćĺ4. **Web search fallback:** Search `"{package_name} github repository"` ÔÇö look for GitHub URL
    93Ôćĺ
    94Ôćĺ**If all methods fail ÔÇö HARD HALT:**
    95Ôćĺ
    96Ôćĺ"**Resolution failed.** Could not resolve `{package_name}` to a GitHub repository.
    97Ôćĺ
    98ÔćĺPlease check:
    99Ôćĺ- Is the package name spelled correctly?
   100Ôćĺ- Is it a private package?
   101Ôćĺ- Is the source hosted on a non-GitHub platform?
   102Ôćĺ
   103Ôćĺ**Provide the GitHub URL directly to continue.**"
   104Ôćĺ
   105ÔćĺWait for corrected input. Loop back to step 2.
   106Ôćĺ
   107Ôćĺ### 4. Detect Language
   108Ôćĺ
   109ÔćĺDetermine primary language from:
   110Ôćĺ
   111Ôćĺ1. **User-provided language hint** (overrides detection)
   112Ôćĺ2. **Manifest file presence** (check via GitHub API or web browsing):
   113Ôćĺ   - `package.json` Ôćĺ JavaScript/TypeScript
   114Ôćĺ   - `pyproject.toml` or `setup.py` Ôćĺ Python
   115Ôćĺ   - `Cargo.toml` Ôćĺ Rust
   116Ôćĺ   - `go.mod` Ôćĺ Go
   117Ôćĺ
   118ÔćĺSet `language` to detected language.
   119Ôćĺ
   120Ôćĺ### 5. Confirm Resolution
   121Ôćĺ
   122Ôćĺ"**Target resolved:**
   123Ôćĺ
   124Ôćĺ- **Repository:** {resolved_url}
   125Ôćĺ- **Name:** {repo_name}
   126Ôćĺ- **Language:** {language}
   127Ôćĺ- **Scope:** {scope_hint or 'entire repo'}
   128Ôćĺ
   129Ôćĺ**Proceeding to ecosystem check...**"
   130Ôćĺ
   131Ôćĺ### 6. Proceed to Next Step
   132Ôćĺ
   133Ôćĺ#### Menu Handling Logic:
   134Ôćĺ
   135Ôćĺ- After successful resolution confirmation, immediately load, read entire file, then execute {nextStepFile}
   136Ôćĺ
   137Ôćĺ#### EXECUTION RULES:
   138Ôćĺ
   139Ôćĺ- This is an init step with auto-proceed after successful resolution
   140Ôćĺ- Proceed directly to next step after confirmation
   141Ôćĺ
   142Ôćĺ## CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE
   143Ôćĺ
   144ÔćĺONLY WHEN the target has been successfully resolved to a GitHub repository with confirmed URL, name, and detected language will you load and read fully `{nextStepFile}` to execute the ecosystem check.
   145Ôćĺ
   146Ôćĺ---
   147Ôćĺ
   148Ôćĺ## ­čÜĘ SYSTEM SUCCESS/FAILURE METRICS
   149Ôćĺ
   150Ôćĺ### Ôťů SUCCESS:
   151Ôćĺ
   152Ôćĺ- User input accepted (URL or package name)
   153Ôćĺ- Target resolved to a valid GitHub repository URL
   154Ôćĺ- Language detected or provided
   155Ôćĺ- State set: resolved_url, repo_name, language, scope_hint
   156Ôćĺ- Auto-proceeding to ecosystem check
   157Ôćĺ
   158Ôćĺ### ÔŁî SYSTEM FAILURE:
   159Ôćĺ
   160Ôćĺ- Proceeding without a resolved repository
   161Ôćĺ- Skipping registry resolution fallback chain
   162Ôćĺ- Not providing actionable guidance on resolution failure
   163Ôćĺ- Beginning extraction or compilation in this step
   164Ôćĺ
   165Ôćĺ**Master Rule:** Skipping steps, optimizing sequences, or not following exact instructions is FORBIDDEN and constitutes SYSTEM FAILURE.
   166Ôćĺ

     7Ôćĺ
     8Ôćĺ# Step 2: Ecosystem Check
     9Ôćĺ
    10Ôćĺ## STEP GOAL:
    11Ôćĺ
    12ÔćĺTo query the agentskills.io ecosystem for an existing official skill matching the resolved target, preventing unnecessary duplication. This is an advisory gate ÔÇö it never blocks the workflow on failure.
    13Ôćĺ
    14Ôćĺ## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES (READ FIRST):
    15Ôćĺ
    16Ôćĺ### Universal Rules:
    17Ôćĺ
    18Ôćĺ- ­čŤĹ NEVER generate content without user input
    19Ôćĺ- ­čôľ CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action
    20Ôćĺ- ­čöä CRITICAL: When loading next step with 'C', ensure entire file is read
    21Ôćĺ- ­čôő YOU ARE A FACILITATOR, not a content generator
    22Ôćĺ- Ôťů YOU MUST ALWAYS SPEAK OUTPUT In your Agent communication style with the config `{communication_language}`
    23Ôćĺ
    24Ôćĺ### Role Reinforcement:
    25Ôćĺ
    26Ôćĺ- Ôťů You are a rapid skill compiler checking for existing skills
    27Ôćĺ- Ôťů If you already have been given a name, communication_style and identity, continue to use those while playing this new role
    28Ôćĺ- Ôťů This check is advisory ÔÇö never block the workflow on failure
    29Ôćĺ- Ôťů Speed is priority ÔÇö 5-second timeout, fail silently
    30Ôćĺ
    31Ôćĺ### Step-Specific Rules:
    32Ôćĺ
    33Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Focus only on checking if an official skill already exists
    34Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to begin extraction or compilation
    35Ôćĺ- ­čĺČ Approach: Check quickly, report result, proceed or offer choice
    36Ôćĺ- ­čôő Tool unavailability and timeouts are NOT errors ÔÇö silent skip
    37Ôćĺ
    38Ôćĺ## EXECUTION PROTOCOLS:
    39Ôćĺ
    40Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Follow the MANDATORY SEQUENCE exactly
    41Ôćĺ- ­čĺż Set state: ecosystem_status (match/no-match/skip)
    42Ôćĺ- ­čôľ Apply 5-second timeout on ecosystem queries
    43Ôćĺ- ­čÜź Never halt workflow due to ecosystem check failure
    44Ôćĺ
    45Ôćĺ## CONTEXT BOUNDARIES:
    46Ôćĺ
    47Ôćĺ- Previous step provided: resolved_url, repo_name, language
    48Ôćĺ- This step only queries ÔÇö does not modify files or begin extraction
    49Ôćĺ- Focus: ecosystem deduplication check only
    50Ôćĺ- Dependencies: resolved repository from step-01
    51Ôćĺ
    52Ôćĺ## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
    53Ôćĺ
    54Ôćĺ**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise unless user explicitly requests a change.
    55Ôćĺ
    56Ôćĺ### 1. Query Ecosystem
    57Ôćĺ
    58ÔćĺSearch for an existing official skill matching `{repo_name}` in the agentskills.io ecosystem.
    59Ôćĺ
    60Ôćĺ**Query methods (try in order):**
    61Ôćĺ1. Search agentskills.io registry for `{repo_name}`
    62Ôćĺ2. Web search: `"agentskills.io" "{repo_name}" skill`
    63Ôćĺ
    64Ôćĺ**Apply 5-second timeout.** If query takes longer, treat as no-match.
    65Ôćĺ
    66Ôćĺ### 2. Evaluate Result
    67Ôćĺ
    68Ôćĺ**If tool unavailable or timeout:**
    69Ôćĺ- Set `ecosystem_status: skip`
    70Ôćĺ- Proceed silently to step 3 (auto-proceed, no message to user)
    71Ôćĺ
    72Ôćĺ**If no match found:**
    73Ôćĺ- Set `ecosystem_status: no-match`
    74Ôćĺ- Auto-proceed silently to step 3. Do not display any message ÔÇö absence of a match is the expected case.
    75Ôćĺ
    76Ôćĺ**If match found:**
    77Ôćĺ- Set `ecosystem_status: match`
    78Ôćĺ- Display match details and present conditional menu:
    79Ôćĺ
    80Ôćĺ"**Existing official skill found for {repo_name}.**
    81Ôćĺ
    82Ôćĺ**Skill:** {matched_skill_name}
    83Ôćĺ**Source:** agentskills.io
    84Ôćĺ**Authority:** official
    85Ôćĺ
    86ÔćĺAn official skill already exists. You can:
    87Ôćĺ
    88Ôćĺ**[P] Proceed** ÔÇö Compile a custom community skill anyway (different scope or customization)
    89Ôćĺ**[I] Install** ÔÇö Install the existing official skill instead (exits this workflow)
    90Ôćĺ**[A] Abort** ÔÇö Cancel compilation"
    91Ôćĺ
    92Ôćĺ### 3. Handle Match Menu (ONLY if match found)
    93Ôćĺ
    94Ôćĺ#### Menu Handling Logic:
    95Ôćĺ
    96Ôćĺ- IF P: Set `ecosystem_status: match-proceed`, then load, read entire file, then execute {nextStepFile}
    97Ôćĺ- IF I: Display install instructions for the official skill, then end workflow
    98Ôćĺ- IF A: Display "Compilation cancelled." and end workflow
    99Ôćĺ- IF Any other: help user, then redisplay the match menu
   100Ôćĺ
   101Ôćĺ#### EXECUTION RULES:
   102Ôćĺ
   103Ôćĺ- ONLY display this menu when ecosystem_status is match
   104Ôćĺ- ALWAYS halt and wait for user input when match is found
   105Ôćĺ- For no-match and skip cases, auto-proceed without menu
   106Ôćĺ
   107Ôćĺ### 4. Auto-Proceed (No Match or Skip)
   108Ôćĺ
   109Ôćĺ#### Menu Handling Logic:
   110Ôćĺ
   111Ôćĺ- After no-match or skip determination, immediately load, read entire file, then execute {nextStepFile}

   113Ôćĺ#### EXECUTION RULES:

   115Ôćĺ- This is an auto-proceed path ÔÇö no user interaction needed

     8Ôćĺ# Step 3: Quick Extract
     9Ôćĺ
    10Ôćĺ## STEP GOAL:
    11Ôćĺ
    12ÔćĺTo read the resolved GitHub repository source and extract the public API surface using surface-level source reading (no AST). Produces an extraction inventory of exports, descriptions, and manifest data for compilation.
    13Ôćĺ
    14Ôćĺ## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES (READ FIRST):
    15Ôćĺ
    16Ôćĺ### Universal Rules:
    17Ôćĺ
    18Ôćĺ- ­čŤĹ NEVER generate content without user input
    19Ôćĺ- ­čôľ CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action
    20Ôćĺ- ­čöä CRITICAL: When loading next step with 'C', ensure entire file is read
    21Ôćĺ- ­čôő YOU ARE A FACILITATOR, not a content generator
    22Ôćĺ- Ôťů YOU MUST ALWAYS SPEAK OUTPUT In your Agent communication style with the config `{communication_language}`
    23Ôćĺ
    24Ôćĺ### Role Reinforcement:
    25Ôćĺ
    26Ôćĺ- Ôťů You are a rapid skill compiler extracting public API surface
    27Ôćĺ- Ôťů If you already have been given a name, communication_style and identity, continue to use those while playing this new role
    28Ôćĺ- Ôťů Best-effort extraction ÔÇö completeness is not required
    29Ôćĺ- Ôťů Surface-level reading only ÔÇö no AST, no deep analysis
    30Ôćĺ
    31Ôćĺ### Step-Specific Rules:
    32Ôćĺ
    33Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Focus only on reading source and extracting exports
    34Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to begin compilation or write output files
    35Ôćĺ- ­čĺČ Approach: Read key files, extract what's visible, move on
    36Ôćĺ- ­čôő No AST required ÔÇö use grep/pattern-based extraction
    37Ôćĺ- ­čôő If no exports found, extract what's available from README
    38Ôćĺ
    39Ôćĺ## EXECUTION PROTOCOLS:
    40Ôćĺ
    41Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Follow the MANDATORY SEQUENCE exactly
    42Ôćĺ- ­čĺż Set state: extraction_inventory (exports list, description, manifest data)
    43Ôćĺ- ­čôľ Web browsing required to read GitHub source files
    44Ôćĺ- ­čÜź Do not spend excessive time on extraction ÔÇö best-effort
    45Ôćĺ
    46Ôćĺ## CONTEXT BOUNDARIES:
    47Ôćĺ
    48Ôćĺ- Previous steps provided: resolved_url, repo_name, language, scope_hint
    49Ôćĺ- Web browsing available for reading GitHub source files
    50Ôćĺ- Focus: extraction only, not compilation
    51Ôćĺ- This is best-effort ÔÇö incomplete extraction is acceptable
    52Ôćĺ
    53Ôćĺ## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
    54Ôćĺ
    55Ôćĺ**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise unless user explicitly requests a change.
    56Ôćĺ
    57Ôćĺ### 1. Read README
    58Ôćĺ
    59ÔćĺRead `README.md` from the repository root via web browsing.
    60Ôćĺ
    61ÔćĺExtract:
    62Ôćĺ- **Description:** What the package does (first paragraph or tagline)
    63Ôćĺ- **Features:** Key features or capabilities listed
    64Ôćĺ- **Usage patterns:** Code examples showing common usage
    65Ôćĺ- **Installation:** Package manager install command (confirms package name)
    66Ôćĺ
    67ÔćĺIf README is unavailable, note and continue.
    68Ôćĺ
    69Ôćĺ### 2. Read Manifest File
    70Ôćĺ
    71ÔćĺBased on detected language, read the primary manifest file:
    72Ôćĺ
    73Ôćĺ- **JavaScript/TypeScript:** `package.json` ÔÇö extract name, version, description, main, exports, dependencies
    74Ôćĺ- **Python:** `pyproject.toml` or `setup.py` ÔÇö extract project name, version, description, dependencies
    75Ôćĺ- **Rust:** `Cargo.toml` ÔÇö extract package name, version, description, dependencies
    76Ôćĺ- **Go:** `go.mod` ÔÇö extract module path, require list
    77Ôćĺ
    78ÔćĺExtract:
    79Ôćĺ- **Package metadata:** name, version, description
    80Ôćĺ- **Entry points:** main, exports, module fields
    81Ôćĺ- **Key dependencies:** direct dependencies list
    82Ôćĺ
    83Ôćĺ### 3. Scan Top-Level Exports
    84Ôćĺ
    85ÔćĺBased on language and entry points from manifest, read the primary export files:
    86Ôćĺ
    87Ôćĺ**JavaScript/TypeScript:**
    88Ôćĺ- Read `index.js`, `index.ts`, `src/index.ts`, or `main` field from package.json
    89Ôćĺ- Extract: `export` statements, `module.exports` assignments
    90Ôćĺ- Pattern: lines matching `export (const|function|class|default|type|interface)`
    91Ôćĺ
    92Ôćĺ**Python:**
    93Ôćĺ- Read `__init__.py` or `src/{package}/__init__.py`
    94Ôćĺ- Extract: `__all__` list, top-level function/class definitions
    95Ôćĺ- Pattern: lines matching `def |class |__all__`
    96Ôćĺ
    97Ôćĺ**Rust:**
    98Ôćĺ- Read `src/lib.rs`
    99Ôćĺ- Extract: `pub fn`, `pub struct`, `pub enum`, `pub trait` declarations
   100Ôćĺ- Pattern: lines matching `pub (fn|struct|enum|trait|mod)`
   101Ôćĺ
   102Ôćĺ**Go:**
   103Ôćĺ- Read exported functions from top-level `.go` files
   104Ôćĺ- Extract: capitalized function names (Go export convention)
   105Ôćĺ- Pattern: lines matching `func [A-Z]`
   106Ôćĺ
   107Ôćĺ**If scope_hint provided:** Focus reading on the specified directories instead of root.
   108Ôćĺ
   109Ôćĺ### 4. Build Extraction Inventory
   110Ôćĺ
   111ÔćĺAssemble the extraction inventory from collected data:
   112Ôćĺ
   113Ôćĺ```
   114Ôćĺextraction_inventory:
   115Ôćĺ  description: {from README or manifest}
   116Ôćĺ  package_name: {from manifest}
   117Ôćĺ  version: {from manifest}
   118Ôćĺ  language: {detected}
   119Ôćĺ  exports: [{name, type, brief_description}]
   120Ôćĺ  usage_patterns: [{pattern from README examples}]
   121Ôćĺ  dependencies: [{key deps from manifest}]
   122Ôćĺ  confidence: {high/medium/low based on data quality}
   123Ôćĺ```
   124Ôćĺ
   125Ôćĺ**If no exports found:**
   126Ôćĺ- Set confidence to `low`
   127Ôćĺ- Use README description and features as fallback content
   128Ôćĺ- Note: "No exports detected ÔÇö SKILL.md will be based on README content only"
   129Ôćĺ
   130Ôćĺ### 5. Report Extraction Summary
   131Ôćĺ
   132Ôćĺ"**Extraction complete:**
   133Ôćĺ
   134Ôćĺ- **Package:** {package_name} v{version}
   135Ôćĺ- **Language:** {language}
   136Ôćĺ- **Exports found:** {count}
   137Ôćĺ- **Confidence:** {confidence}
   138Ôćĺ- **Source files read:** {count}
   139Ôćĺ
   140Ôćĺ**Proceeding to compilation...**"
   141Ôćĺ
   142Ôćĺ### 6. Auto-Proceed to Compilation
   143Ôćĺ
   144Ôćĺ#### Menu Handling Logic:
   145Ôćĺ
   146Ôćĺ- After extraction summary, immediately load, read entire file, then execute {nextStepFile}
   147Ôćĺ
   148Ôćĺ#### EXECUTION RULES:
   149Ôćĺ
   150Ôćĺ- This is an auto-proceed step ÔÇö extraction results flow directly to compilation
   151Ôćĺ- Proceed directly to next step after summary
   152Ôćĺ
   153Ôćĺ## CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE
   154Ôćĺ
   155ÔćĺONLY WHEN extraction is complete and extraction_inventory is assembled (even if minimal/low-confidence) will you load and read fully `{nextStepFile}` to execute compilation.
   156Ôćĺ

   163Ôćĺ- README read and description extracted

     9Ôćĺ# Step 4: Compile

    53Ôćĺ## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
    54Ôćĺ
    55Ôćĺ**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise unless user explicitly requests a change.
    56Ôćĺ
    57Ôćĺ### 1. Load Skill Template
    58Ôćĺ
    59ÔćĺLoad {skillTemplateData} to understand:
    60Ôćĺ- SKILL.md required and optional sections
    61Ôćĺ- context-snippet.md Vercel-aligned indexed format
    62Ôćĺ- metadata.json field requirements
    63Ôćĺ
    64Ôćĺ### 2. Assemble SKILL.md
    65Ôćĺ
    66ÔćĺUsing the template structure, populate each section from extraction_inventory:
    67Ôćĺ
    68Ôćĺ**Frontmatter (REQUIRED ÔÇö agentskills.io compliance):**
    69Ôćĺ
    70ÔćĺThe SKILL.md MUST begin with YAML frontmatter:
    71Ôćĺ
    72Ôćĺ```yaml
    73Ôćĺ---
    74Ôćĺname: {skill_name}
    75Ôćĺdescription: >
    76Ôćĺ  {Trigger-optimized description from extraction_inventory.description.
    77Ôćĺ  1-1024 chars. Include what it does, when to use it, and what NOT to use it for.}
    78Ôćĺ---
    79Ôćĺ```
    80Ôćĺ
    81Ôćĺ**Frontmatter rules:**
    82Ôćĺ- `name`: lowercase alphanumeric + hyphens only, must match the skill output directory name. Prefer gerund form (`processing-pdfs`) for clarity.
    83Ôćĺ- `description`: non-empty, max 1024 chars, optimized for agent discovery. MUST use third-person voice ("Processes..." not "I can..." or "You can...").
    84Ôćĺ- No other frontmatter fields ÔÇö only `name` and `description` for community skills
    85Ôćĺ
    86Ôćĺ**Required sections (after frontmatter):**
    87Ôćĺ- **Overview:** Package name, repository, language, source authority, generation date
    88Ôćĺ- **Description:** From extraction_inventory.description (README-derived)
    89Ôćĺ- **Key Exports:** From extraction_inventory.exports ÔÇö list each with name, type, brief description
    90Ôćĺ- **Usage Patterns:** From extraction_inventory.usage_patterns (README examples)
    91Ôćĺ
    92Ôćĺ**Optional sections (include when data available):**
    93Ôćĺ- **Configuration:** If configuration options were found in source
    94Ôćĺ- **Dependencies:** Key dependencies from manifest
    95Ôćĺ- **Notes:** Caveats, limitations, extraction confidence level
    96Ôćĺ- **Scripts & Assets Note** (if source contains `scripts/`, `bin/`, `assets/`, `templates/`, or `schemas/` directories): "This package may include scripts and assets. Run create-skill for full extraction with provenance tracking."
    97Ôćĺ
    98Ôćĺ**If confidence is low:**
    99Ôćĺ- Include a note: "This skill was generated with limited source data. Consider running create-skill for a more thorough compilation."
   100Ôćĺ
   101Ôćĺ### 3. Generate Context Snippet
   102Ôćĺ
   103ÔćĺCreate context-snippet.md in Vercel-aligned indexed format (~80-120 tokens):
   104Ôćĺ
   105Ôćĺ```
   106Ôćĺ[{skill_name} v{version}]|root: skills/{skill_name}/
   107Ôćĺ|IMPORTANT: {skill_name} v{version} ÔÇö read SKILL.md before writing {skill_name} code. Do NOT rely on training data.
   108Ôćĺ|quick-start:{SKILL.md#quick-start}
   109Ôćĺ|api: {top-5 exports with () for functions}
   110Ôćĺ|key-types:{SKILL.md#key-types} ÔÇö {inline summary of most important type values}
   111Ôćĺ|gotchas: {2-3 most critical pitfalls or breaking changes, inline}
   112Ôćĺ```
   113Ôćĺ
   114Ôćĺ**If fewer than 5 exports:** Use all available exports.
   115Ôćĺ**If no exports:** Omit the api line.
   116Ôćĺ**If no gotchas known:** Omit the gotchas line.
   117Ôćĺ
   118Ôćĺ### 4. Generate Metadata JSON
   119Ôćĺ
   120ÔćĺGenerate metadata.json following the exact structure defined in {skillTemplateData} metadata.json section. Populate fields from extraction_inventory:
   121Ôćĺ
   122Ôćĺ```json
   123Ôćĺ{

     8Ôćĺ# Step 5: Validate
     9Ôćĺ
    10Ôćĺ## STEP GOAL:

    12ÔćĺTo validate the compiled SKILL.md, context-snippet.md, and metadata.json against the agentskills.io specification at community tier. Report any gaps or issues. This is advisory validation ÔÇö issues are reported but do not block output.
    13Ôćĺ
    14Ôćĺ## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES (READ FIRST):
    15Ôćĺ

    17Ôćĺ

    30Ôćĺ
    31Ôćĺ### Step-Specific Rules:

    33Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Focus only on validating the compiled output

    35Ôćĺ- ­čĺČ Approach: Check each requirement, report findings, proceed
    36Ôćĺ- ­čôő Validation failures are advisory ÔÇö user can proceed regardless
    37Ôćĺ
    38Ôćĺ## EXECUTION PROTOCOLS:
    39Ôćĺ
    40Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Follow the MANDATORY SEQUENCE exactly
    41Ôćĺ- ­čĺż Set state: validation_result (pass/fail, issues_list)
    42Ôćĺ- ­čôľ Check against community-tier requirements
    43Ôćĺ- ­čÜź Do not block output on validation issues
    44Ôćĺ
    45Ôćĺ## CONTEXT BOUNDARIES:
    46Ôćĺ
    47Ôćĺ- Previous step provided: skill_content (SKILL.md), context_snippet, metadata_json
    48Ôćĺ- Focus: validation only, not modification
    49Ôćĺ- Community tier has lighter requirements than official
