    51Ôćĺ
    52Ôćĺ## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
    53Ôćĺ
    54Ôćĺ**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise unless user explicitly requests a change.
    55Ôćĺ
    56Ôćĺ### 1. Check Tool Availability
    57Ôćĺ
    58ÔćĺRun: `npx skill-check -h`
    59Ôćĺ
    60Ôćĺ- If succeeds (returns usage information): Continue to automated validation (section 2)
    61Ôćĺ- If fails (command not found or error): Skip to manual fallback in section 2
    62Ôćĺ
    63Ôćĺ**Important:** Use the verification command. Do not assume availability ÔÇö empirical check required.
    64Ôćĺ
    65Ôćĺ### 2. Validate SKILL.md via skill-check (if available)
    66Ôćĺ
    67Ôćĺ**If `npx skill-check` is available**, run automated validation with auto-fix:
    68Ôćĺ
    69Ôćĺ```bash
    70Ôćĺnpx skill-check check <skill-dir> --fix --format json --no-security-scan
    71Ôćĺ```
    72Ôćĺ
    73ÔćĺThis validates frontmatter, description, body limits, links, and formatting ÔÇö and auto-fixes deterministic issues (field ordering, slug format, required fields, trailing newlines).
    74Ôćĺ
    75Ôćĺ**Parse JSON output** to extract:
    76Ôćĺ- `qualityScore` ÔÇö overall score (0-100)
    77Ôćĺ- `diagnostics[]` ÔÇö remaining issues after auto-fix
    78Ôćĺ- `fixed[]` ÔÇö issues automatically corrected
    79Ôćĺ
    80ÔćĺRecord quality score and any remaining diagnostics as validation issues.
    81Ôćĺ
    82Ôćĺ**If skill-check is NOT available**, perform manual frontmatter check:
    83Ôćĺ
    84Ôćĺ- [ ] **Frontmatter present** ÔÇö file starts with `---` delimiter and has closing `---`
    85Ôćĺ- [ ] **`name` field** ÔÇö present, non-empty, lowercase alphanumeric + hyphens only, 1-64 chars
    86Ôćĺ- [ ] **`name` matches directory** ÔÇö frontmatter `name` matches the skill output directory name
    87Ôćĺ- [ ] **`description` field** ÔÇö present, non-empty, 1-1024 characters
    88Ôćĺ- [ ] **No unknown fields** ÔÇö only `name`, `description`, `license`, `compatibility`, `metadata`, `allowed-tools` are permitted
    89Ôćĺ
    90Ôćĺ**For each violation, log an issue.** Missing frontmatter or missing required fields are high-severity issues ÔÇö skills without valid frontmatter will fail `npx skills add` and `npx skill-check check`.
    91Ôćĺ
    92Ôćĺ### 3. Validate SKILL.md Body Structure
    93Ôćĺ
    94ÔćĺCheck that SKILL.md has these required sections populated:
    95Ôćĺ
    96Ôćĺ- [ ] **Overview section** present with package name, repo, language, authority
    97Ôćĺ- [ ] **Description section** present with non-empty content
    98Ôćĺ- [ ] **Key Exports section** present (may be empty if confidence is low)
    99Ôćĺ- [ ] **Usage Patterns section** present (may have README fallback)
   100Ôćĺ
   101Ôćĺ**For each missing or empty required section, log an issue.**
   102Ôćĺ
   103Ôćĺ### 4. Validate Context Snippet Format
   104Ôćĺ
   105ÔćĺCheck context-snippet.md format compliance:
   106Ôćĺ
   107Ôćĺ- [ ] **Vercel-aligned indexed format** ÔÇö pipe-delimited with version, retrieval instruction, section anchors
   108Ôćĺ- [ ] **First line** matches pattern: `[{name} v{version}]|root: skills/{name}/`
   109Ôćĺ- [ ] **Second line** starts with: `|IMPORTANT:`
   110Ôćĺ- [ ] **Approximate token count** is ~80-120 tokens
   111Ôćĺ
   112Ôćĺ**If format is wrong, log an issue.**
   113Ôćĺ
   114Ôćĺ### 5. Validate Metadata JSON
   115Ôćĺ
   116ÔćĺCheck metadata.json has required fields:
   117Ôćĺ
   118Ôćĺ- [ ] `name` ÔÇö present, non-empty
   119Ôćĺ- [ ] `version` ÔÇö present (auto-detected or "1.0.0")
   120Ôćĺ- [ ] `source_authority` ÔÇö must be "community"
   121Ôćĺ- [ ] `source_repo` ÔÇö present, valid GitHub URL
   122Ôćĺ- [ ] `language` ÔÇö present, non-empty
   123Ôćĺ- [ ] `generated_by` ÔÇö must be "quick-skill"
   124Ôćĺ- [ ] `generation_date` ÔÇö present
   125Ôćĺ- [ ] `stats.exports_documented` ÔÇö present, number
   126Ôćĺ- [ ] `stats.exports_public_api` ÔÇö present, number
   127Ôćĺ- [ ] `stats.exports_total` ÔÇö present, number
   128Ôćĺ- [ ] `stats.public_api_coverage` ÔÇö present, number
   129Ôćĺ- [ ] `stats.total_coverage` ÔÇö present, number
   130Ôćĺ- [ ] `confidence_tier` ÔÇö present
   131Ôćĺ
   132Ôćĺ**For each missing or invalid field, log an issue.**
   133Ôćĺ
   134Ôćĺ### 6. Security Scan (if skill-check available)
   135Ôćĺ
   136ÔćĺRun security scan on the compiled skill:
   137Ôćĺ
   138Ôćĺ```bash
   139Ôćĺnpx skill-check check <skill-dir> --format json
   140Ôćĺ```
   141Ôćĺ
   142Ôćĺ(Security scan is enabled by default when `--no-security-scan` is omitted.)
   143Ôćĺ
   144ÔćĺRecord any security findings as advisory warnings. Security issues do not block output.
   145Ôćĺ
   146Ôćĺ**If skill-check unavailable:** Skip with note in validation results.
   147Ôćĺ
   148Ôćĺ### 7. Report Validation Results
   149Ôćĺ
   150Ôćĺ"**Validation complete:**
   151Ôćĺ
   152Ôćĺ**SKILL.md:** {pass/issues found} (quality score: {score}/100 if skill-check was available)
   153Ôćĺ{list any issues}
   154Ôćĺ{list any auto-fixed issues}
   155Ôćĺ
   156Ôćĺ**context-snippet.md:** {pass/issues found}
   157Ôćĺ{list any issues}
   158Ôćĺ
   159Ôćĺ**metadata.json:** {pass/issues found}
   160Ôćĺ{list any issues}
   161Ôćĺ
   162Ôćĺ**Security:** {pass/warn/skipped}
   163Ôćĺ{list any security findings}
   164Ôćĺ
   165Ôćĺ**Overall:** {pass / N issues found}
   166Ôćĺ
   167Ôćĺ{If issues found:}
   168ÔćĺThese issues are advisory for community-tier skills. You can proceed to write output or go back to adjust.
   169Ôćĺ
   170Ôćĺ**Proceeding to write output...**"
   171Ôćĺ
   172ÔćĺSet `validation_result` with pass/fail status, quality score, and issues list.
   173Ôćĺ
   174Ôćĺ### 8. Auto-Proceed to Write
   175Ôćĺ
   176Ôćĺ#### Menu Handling Logic:
   177Ôćĺ
   178Ôćĺ- After validation report, immediately load, read entire file, then execute {nextStepFile}
   179Ôćĺ
   180Ôćĺ#### EXECUTION RULES:
   181Ôćĺ
   182Ôćĺ- This is an auto-proceed step ÔÇö validation is advisory
   183Ôćĺ- Proceed directly to write step after reporting results
   184Ôćĺ
   185Ôćĺ## CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE
   186Ôćĺ
   187ÔćĺONLY WHEN validation checks are complete and results reported will you load and read fully `{nextStepFile}` to execute file writing.
   188Ôćĺ
   189Ôćĺ---
   190Ôćĺ
   191Ôćĺ## ­čÜĘ SYSTEM SUCCESS/FAILURE METRICS
   192Ôćĺ
   193Ôćĺ### Ôťů SUCCESS:

   103Ôćĺ

   136Ôćĺ**Note:** This is a best-effort community skill. For deeper analysis with AST-verified exports and provenance tracking, use the full **create-skill** workflow with a skill brief."

   138Ôćĺ### 7. End Workflow

     3Ôćĺ## SKILL.md Section Structure
     4Ôćĺ
     5ÔćĺThe following sections should be populated in the generated SKILL.md. Best-effort ÔÇö not all sections will have data for every skill.
     6Ôćĺ
     7Ôćĺ### Required Sections

    17Ôćĺ
    18Ôćĺ# {skill_name}

    24Ôćĺ- **Source Authority:** community

    36Ôćĺ
    37Ôćĺ### Optional Sections (include when data available)

    50Ôćĺ## context-snippet.md Format (Vercel-Aligned)

    62Ôćĺ

1Ôćĺ---
     2Ôćĺname: analyze-source
     3Ôćĺdescription: Decomposition engine ÔÇö discover what to skill, recommend stack skill
     4Ôćĺweb_bundle: true
     5Ôćĺinstalled_path: '{project-root}/_bmad/skf/workflows/skillforge/analyze-source'
     6Ôćĺ---
     7Ôćĺ
     8Ôćĺ# Analyze Source
     9Ôćĺ
    10Ôćĺ**Goal:** Analyze a large repo or multi-service project to identify discrete skillable units, map exports and integration points, and produce recommended skill-brief.yaml files as the primary entry point for brownfield onboarding.
    11Ôćĺ
    12Ôćĺ**Your Role:** In addition to your name, communication_style, and persona, you are also a source code analyst and decomposition architect collaborating with a developer onboarding an existing project. This is a partnership, not a client-vendor relationship. You bring expertise in codebase analysis, service boundary detection, and skill scoping, while the user brings their domain knowledge of the project being analyzed. Work together as equals.
    13Ôćĺ
    14Ôćĺ**Meta-Context:** This workflow is the entry point of the SKF skill creation pipeline. Its output (skill-brief.yaml files) feeds directly into brief-skill and create-skill workflows. The analysis must be thorough enough to produce actionable briefs, but scoped enough to avoid overwhelming the user with false positives.
    15Ôćĺ
    16Ôćĺ---
    17Ôćĺ
    18Ôćĺ## WORKFLOW ARCHITECTURE
    19Ôćĺ
    20Ôćĺ### Core Principles
    21Ôćĺ
    22Ôćĺ- **Micro-file Design**: Each step of the overall goal is a self contained instruction file that you will adhere to 1 file as directed at a time
    23Ôćĺ- **Just-In-Time Loading**: Only 1 current step file will be loaded, read, and executed to completion - never load future step files until told to do so
    24Ôćĺ- **Sequential Enforcement**: Sequence within the step files must be completed in order, no skipping or optimization allowed
    25Ôćĺ- **State Tracking**: Document progress in output file frontmatter using `stepsCompleted` array
    26Ôćĺ- **Append-Only Building**: Build the analysis report by appending content as directed to the output file
    27Ôćĺ- **Tier-Aware Analysis**: Scanning depth adapts to forge tier ÔÇö Quick (file structure), Forge (AST), Forge+ (AST + CCC semantic pre-ranking), Deep (AST+QMD)
    28Ôćĺ
    29Ôćĺ### Step Processing Rules
    30Ôćĺ
    31Ôćĺ1. **READ COMPLETELY**: Always read the entire step file before taking any action
    32Ôćĺ2. **FOLLOW SEQUENCE**: Execute all numbered sections in order, never deviate
    33Ôćĺ3. **WAIT FOR INPUT**: If a menu is presented, halt and wait for user selection
    34Ôćĺ4. **CHECK CONTINUATION**: If the step has a menu with Continue as an option, only proceed to next step when user selects 'C' (Continue)
    35Ôćĺ5. **SAVE STATE**: Update `stepsCompleted` in frontmatter before loading next step
    36Ôćĺ6. **LOAD NEXT**: When directed, load, read entire file, then execute the next step file
    37Ôćĺ
    38Ôćĺ### Critical Rules (NO EXCEPTIONS)
    39Ôćĺ
    40Ôćĺ- ­čŤĹ **NEVER** load multiple step files simultaneously
    41Ôćĺ- ­čôľ **ALWAYS** read entire step file before execution
    42Ôćĺ- ­čÜź **NEVER** skip steps or optimize the sequence
    43Ôćĺ- ­čĺż **ALWAYS** update frontmatter of output files when writing the final output for a specific step
    44Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» **ALWAYS** follow the exact instructions in the step file
    45Ôćĺ- ÔĆŞ´ŞĆ **ALWAYS** halt at menus and wait for user input
    46Ôćĺ- ­čôő **NEVER** create mental todo lists from future steps
    47Ôćĺ- ÔÜÖ´ŞĆ **TOOL/SUBPROCESS FALLBACK**: If any instruction references a subprocess, subagent, or tool you do not have access to, you MUST still achieve the outcome in your main context thread
    48Ôćĺ
    49Ôćĺ---
    50Ôćĺ
    51Ôćĺ## INITIALIZATION SEQUENCE
    52Ôćĺ
    53Ôćĺ### 1. Module Configuration Loading
    54Ôćĺ
    55ÔćĺLoad and read full config from {project-root}/_bmad/skf/config.yaml and resolve:
    56Ôćĺ
    57Ôćĺ- `project_name`, `output_folder`, `user_name`, `communication_language`, `document_output_language`, `forge_data_folder`, `skills_output_folder`, `sidecar_path`
    58Ôćĺ
    59Ôćĺ### 2. First Step Execution
    60Ôćĺ
    61ÔćĺLoad, read the full file and then execute `./steps-c/step-01-init.md` to begin the workflow.
    62Ôćĺ

1Ôćĺ---
     2Ôćĺname: 'step-01-init'

    59Ôćĺ
    60Ôćĺ### 1. Check for Existing Report (Continuation Detection)

    72Ôćĺ### 2. Verify Prerequisites

    53Ôćĺ- Focus: Project structure mapping only ÔÇö directory tree, manifests, entry points, service configs
    54Ôćĺ- Limits: Do not analyze exports, imports, or integration points (that's steps 03-04)

    56Ôćĺ
    57Ôćĺ## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
    58Ôćĺ
    59Ôćĺ**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise unless user explicitly requests a change.
    60Ôćĺ
    61Ôćĺ### 1. Load Context
    62Ôćĺ
    63ÔćĺRead {outputFile} frontmatter to obtain:

    70Ôćĺ### 2. Scan Directory Structure
    71Ôćĺ
    72Ôćĺ**For each path in `project_paths[]`**, launch a subprocess that scans the project directory structure (aggregate results across all repos with clear repo-level grouping):
    73Ôćĺ
    74Ôćĺ1. Map the top-level directory tree (2-3 levels deep)
    75Ôćĺ2. Identify workspace configuration files (pnpm-workspace.yaml, lerna.json, Cargo.toml [workspace], go.work, etc.)
    76Ôćĺ3. Find all package manifests (package.json, Cargo.toml, go.mod, requirements.txt, pyproject.toml, build.gradle, pom.xml)
    77Ôćĺ4. Locate entry point files (index.ts, main.ts, app.ts, main.go, main.rs, __init__.py, etc.)
    78Ôćĺ5. Detect service configuration (Dockerfile, docker-compose.yml, kubernetes manifests, serverless.yml)
    79Ôćĺ6. Return structured findings ÔÇö file paths and types only, not contents
    80Ôćĺ
    81Ôćĺ**If subprocess unavailable:** Perform directory scanning in main thread using file I/O tools.

    86Ôćĺ
    87Ôćĺ**Deep tier additional scanning (IF Deep tier):**
    88Ôćĺ- Use ast-grep to detect structural patterns across the codebase: `ast-grep -p 'class $NAME' --lang python` (or equivalent per language) to build a class/type inventory
    89Ôćĺ- Use ast-grep to identify exported function patterns: `ast-grep -p 'def $FUNC($$$PARAMS)' --lang python` at entry points
    90Ôćĺ- If QMD is available, query for temporal context on the project: recent changes, active development areas, refactoring patterns
    91Ôćĺ- Record Deep-tier findings separately ÔÇö they supplement (not replace) the Quick/Forge scan results
    92Ôćĺ
    93Ôćĺ### 3. Detect Service Boundaries
    94Ôćĺ
    95ÔćĺBased on scan results, identify potential service boundaries:
    96Ôćĺ
    97Ôćĺ**Strong boundary signals:**
    98Ôćĺ- Independent package manifest (own package.json, Cargo.toml, etc.)
    99Ôćĺ- Docker/container configuration
   100Ôćĺ- Separate entry point file

   128Ôćĺ### 5. Present Scan Results

   155Ôćĺ
   156Ôćĺ### 6. Append to Report

    10Ôćĺ# Step 3: Identify Units

    55Ôćĺ## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
    56Ôćĺ
    57Ôćĺ**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise unless user explicitly requests a change.
    58Ôćĺ
    59Ôćĺ### 1. Load Context

    64Ôćĺ- `existing_skills` from frontmatter

    67Ôćĺ
    68Ôćĺ### 2. Apply Detection Heuristics
    69Ôćĺ
    70ÔćĺFor EACH detected boundary from the scan:
    71Ôćĺ
    72Ôćĺ**Step A ÔÇö Count detection signals:**
    73Ôćĺ- Check strong signals (independent manifest, separate entry point, Docker config, distinct export surface, workspace member)
    74Ôćĺ- Check moderate signals (directory depth, naming convention, separate tests, README, CI/CD reference)
    75Ôćĺ- Check weak signals (large directory, comment boundaries, import clustering)
    76Ôćĺ
    77Ôćĺ**Step B ÔÇö Classify boundary type:**
    78Ôćĺ- Service Boundary ÔÇö independent deployable unit
    79Ôćĺ- Package Boundary ÔÇö workspace member or independently versioned
    80Ôćĺ- Module Boundary ÔÇö logical grouping within a package
    81Ôćĺ- Library Boundary ÔÇö third-party with significant project-specific usage
    82Ôćĺ
    83Ôćĺ**Step C ÔÇö Assign scope type:**
    84Ôćĺ- `full-library` ÔÇö entire codebase of the unit
    85Ôćĺ- `specific-modules` ÔÇö selected components or packages
    86Ôćĺ- `public-api` ÔÇö only exported interfaces
    87Ôćĺ
    88Ôćĺ**Step D ÔÇö Check disqualification rules:**
    89Ôćĺ- Too small (fewer than 3 source files or 100 lines)
    90Ôćĺ- Generated code
    91Ôćĺ- Pure configuration
    92Ôćĺ- Test-only
    93Ôćĺ- Vendor/dependency copy
    94Ôćĺ- Already skilled (exists in existing_skills list)
    95Ôćĺ

   106Ôćĺ**Disqualified:**
   107Ôćĺ| Path | Reason |

   110Ôćĺ
   111Ôćĺ### 4. Detect Primary Language Per Unit
   112Ôćĺ
   113ÔćĺFor each qualifying unit, determine the primary programming language based on:
   114Ôćĺ- File extensions in the unit directory
   115Ôćĺ- Manifest file type (package.json Ôćĺ JS/TS, Cargo.toml Ôćĺ Rust, go.mod Ôćĺ Go, etc.)
   116Ôćĺ- Entry point file extension

   135Ôćĺ
   136ÔćĺDo these classifications look correct? Should any units be added, removed, or reclassified?"
   137Ôćĺ

   140Ôćĺ### 6. Append to Report
   141Ôćĺ
   142ÔćĺAppend the complete "## Identified Units" section to {outputFile}:
   143Ôćĺ

     7ÔćĺheuristicsFile: '../data/unit-detection-heuristics.md'
     8Ôćĺ---
     9Ôćĺ
    10Ôćĺ# Step 4: Map Exports and Detect Integrations
    11Ôćĺ
    12Ôćĺ## STEP GOAL:
    13Ôćĺ
    14ÔćĺTo analyze each qualifying unit's export surface and import graph, detect cross-unit integration points, and flag potential stack skill candidates ÔÇö completing the analysis foundation needed for recommendations.
    15Ôćĺ
    16Ôćĺ## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES (READ FIRST):
    17Ôćĺ
    18Ôćĺ### Universal Rules:
    19Ôćĺ
    20Ôćĺ- ­čŤĹ NEVER generate content without user input
    21Ôćĺ- ­čôľ CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action
    22Ôćĺ- ­čöä CRITICAL: When loading next step with 'C', ensure entire file is read
    23Ôćĺ- ­čôő YOU ARE A FACILITATOR, not a content generator
    24Ôćĺ- Ôťů YOU MUST ALWAYS SPEAK OUTPUT In your Agent communication style with the config `{communication_language}`
    25Ôćĺ- ÔÜÖ´ŞĆ TOOL/SUBPROCESS FALLBACK: If any instruction references a subprocess, subagent, or tool you do not have access to, you MUST still achieve the outcome in your main context thread
    26Ôćĺ
    27Ôćĺ### Role Reinforcement:
    28Ôćĺ
    29Ôćĺ- Ôťů You are a source code analyst and decomposition architect (Ferris Architect mode)
    30Ôćĺ- Ôťů If you already have been given a name, communication_style and identity, continue to use those while playing this new role
    31Ôćĺ- Ôťů Prescriptive precision ÔÇö zero-hallucination, only report what is found
    32Ôćĺ- Ôťů File:line citation tracing ÔÇö cite specific files and line numbers for all claims
    33Ôćĺ
    34Ôćĺ### Step-Specific Rules:
    35Ôćĺ
    36Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Use subprocess optimization (Pattern 2) ÔÇö per-unit analysis for export mapping
    37Ôćĺ- ­čĺČ DO NOT BE LAZY ÔÇö For EACH qualifying unit, perform thorough export surface analysis
    38Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to make recommendations in this step (that's step 05)
    39Ôćĺ- ­čôő Tier-aware depth: Quick (file-level exports), Forge (AST export analysis), Deep (AST + semantic relationships)
    40Ôćĺ
    41Ôćĺ**AST Degradation Fallback:** If the AST tool (ast-grep) is unavailable at Forge or Deep tier, degrade to Quick-tier behavior for this step and emit a warning: "AST tool unavailable ÔÇö falling back to file-level export mapping for this step."
    42Ôćĺ
    43Ôćĺ## EXECUTION PROTOCOLS:
    44Ôćĺ
    45Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Follow the MANDATORY SEQUENCE exactly
    46Ôćĺ- ­čĺż Append "## Export Map" and "## Integration Points" sections to {outputFile}
    47Ôćĺ- ­čôľ Update stepsCompleted in {outputFile} frontmatter
    48Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to proceed without presenting findings to user
    49Ôćĺ
    50Ôćĺ## CONTEXT BOUNDARIES:
    51Ôćĺ
    52Ôćĺ- Available: Identified Units section from report (qualifying units with paths, types, scope types, languages)
    53Ôćĺ- Focus: Export surfaces, import graphs, and cross-unit integration patterns
    54Ôćĺ- Limits: Do not recommend or filter units ÔÇö present all findings objectively
    55Ôćĺ- Dependencies: step-03-identify-units must have populated the Identified Units section
    56Ôćĺ
    57Ôćĺ## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
    58Ôćĺ
    59Ôćĺ**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise unless user explicitly requests a change.
    60Ôćĺ
    61Ôćĺ### 1. Load Context
    62Ôćĺ
    63ÔćĺRead {outputFile} to obtain:
    64Ôćĺ- Qualifying units from Identified Units section (names, paths, scope types, languages)
    65Ôćĺ- `forge_tier` from frontmatter
    66Ôćĺ
    67ÔćĺLoad {heuristicsFile} for stack skill candidate detection rules.
    68Ôćĺ
    69Ôćĺ### 2. Map Export Surfaces Per Unit
    70Ôćĺ
    71ÔćĺDO NOT BE LAZY ÔÇö For EACH qualifying unit, launch a subprocess (or analyze in main thread) that:
    72Ôćĺ
    73Ôćĺ**Size-aware strategy selection:**
    74Ôćĺ- **< 50 files:** Full export scan ÔÇö analyze every file for exports
    75Ôćĺ- **50-200 files:** Targeted scan ÔÇö entry points (`__init__.py`, `index.ts`, `lib.rs`) + public modules + barrel exports only
    76Ôćĺ- **200+ files:** Entry-point strategy ÔÇö analyze top-level entry point for public API surface, list submodule entry points, analyze each submodule entry point only. Report coverage confidence based on percentage of files analyzed.
    77Ôćĺ
    78Ôćĺ**Per-unit analysis (scaled by strategy above):**
    79Ôćĺ
    80Ôćĺ1. Scans the unit's directory for export/public interface files
    81Ôćĺ2. Counts and categorizes exports based on forge tier:
    82Ôćĺ   - **Quick tier:** Count files by type, identify index/barrel files, list directory structure
    83Ôćĺ   - **Forge tier:** Parse export statements, identify public API surface, count exported functions/classes/types
    84Ôćĺ   - **Forge+ tier:** All Forge analysis plus:
    85Ôćĺ     - If `tools.ccc` is true: run `ccc_bridge.search("{unit_name} exports public API", top_k=15)` to discover semantically relevant files beyond directory scan
    86Ôćĺ     - Merge CCC-discovered files with scoped file list ÔÇö files from CCC that are within the unit's directory are added to the analysis queue
    87Ôćĺ     - Record CCC signals in per-unit findings: top 3 CCC-ranked file names (or "ÔÇö" if no ccc results)
    88Ôćĺ   - **Deep tier:** All Forge analysis plus:
    89Ôćĺ     - ast-grep structural export extraction: `ast-grep -p 'export $$$' --lang typescript` or equivalent per language to build a verified export inventory
    90Ôćĺ     - ast-grep type/interface mapping: `ast-grep -p 'interface $NAME' --lang typescript` or `ast-grep -p 'class $NAME($$$)' --lang python`
    91Ôćĺ     - If QMD available: query for temporal evolution of identified exports (deprecation signals, recent additions, refactoring patterns)
    92Ôćĺ     - Record semantic relationships between exports (which exports reference/depend on each other)
    93Ôćĺ3. Returns structured findings to parent:
    94Ôćĺ   - Export count (files, functions, types, classes)
    95Ôćĺ   - Primary export patterns (barrel exports, direct exports, re-exports)
    96Ôćĺ   - Public API surface size estimate
    97Ôćĺ   - Key entry points
    98Ôćĺ   - Script/asset presence: check for `scripts/`, `bin/`, `assets/`, `templates/` directories and files matching detection signals in `{heuristicsFile}`. Record counts in per-unit findings.
    99Ôćĺ   - Analysis strategy used and coverage confidence
   100Ôćĺ
   101Ôćĺ**Per-unit export summary:**
   102Ôćĺ
   103Ôćĺ| Unit | Files | Exports | Export Pattern | API Surface | Scripts/Assets | CCC Signals |
   104Ôćĺ|------|-------|---------|----------------|-------------|----------------|-------------|
   105Ôćĺ| {name} | {count} | {count} | {pattern} | {small/medium/large} | {N scripts, M assets or --} | {CCC signals or --} |
   106Ôćĺ
   107Ôćĺ### 3. Map Import Graph
   108Ôćĺ
   109ÔćĺFor each qualifying unit, analyze inbound and outbound imports:
   110Ôćĺ
   111Ôćĺ**Outbound imports (this unit imports from):**
   112Ôćĺ- Which other qualifying units does it depend on?
   113Ôćĺ- Which external dependencies does it use?
   114Ôćĺ
   115Ôćĺ**Inbound imports (other units import from this):**
   116Ôćĺ- Which other qualifying units consume this unit's exports?
   117Ôćĺ- How many import sites exist?
   118Ôćĺ
   119Ôćĺ**Build cross-reference matrix:**
   120Ôćĺ
   121Ôćĺ| Unit | Imports From | Imported By | External Deps |
   122Ôćĺ|------|-------------|-------------|---------------|
   123Ôćĺ| {name} | {list units} | {list units} | {count} |
   124Ôćĺ
   125Ôćĺ### 4. Detect Integration Points
   126Ôćĺ
   127ÔćĺIdentify cross-unit integration patterns:
   128Ôćĺ
   129Ôćĺ**Direct integrations:**
   130Ôćĺ- Unit A imports from Unit B Ôćĺ document the interface boundary
   131Ôćĺ- Shared type definitions across units
   132Ôćĺ- Cross-unit function calls
   133Ôćĺ
   134Ôćĺ**For each integration point document:**
   135Ôćĺ- Source unit Ôćĺ Target unit
   136Ôćĺ- Integration type (import, shared types, API call, message passing, shared state)
   137Ôćĺ- Files involved (with paths)
   138Ôćĺ- Coupling strength (tight / loose / indirect)
   139Ôćĺ
   140Ôćĺ### 5. Flag Stack Skill Candidates
   141Ôćĺ
   142ÔćĺUsing rules from {heuristicsFile}, check for stack skill indicators:
   143Ôćĺ
   144Ôćĺ1. **Co-import frequency:** Two or more units imported together in 3+ files
   145Ôćĺ2. **Integration adapter:** A unit exists primarily to bridge two other units
   146Ôćĺ3. **Shared state:** Multiple units read/write to the same data store
   147Ôćĺ4. **Orchestration layer:** A unit coordinates calls across multiple other units
   148Ôćĺ
   149Ôćĺ**For each candidate, document:**
   150Ôćĺ- Units involved
   151Ôćĺ- Detection signal
   152Ôćĺ- Recommended stack skill grouping
   153Ôćĺ- Evidence (specific files/lines)
   154Ôćĺ
   155Ôćĺ### 6. Present Findings
   156Ôćĺ
   157Ôćĺ"**Export Mapping and Integration Detection Complete**
   158Ôćĺ
   159Ôćĺ**Export Map Summary:**
   160Ôćĺ{Per-unit export summary table}
   161Ôćĺ
   162Ôćĺ**Cross-Reference Matrix:**
   163Ôćĺ{Import graph matrix}
   164Ôćĺ
   165Ôćĺ**Integration Points:** {count}
   166Ôćĺ{List each integration with source Ôćĺ target, type, coupling}
   167Ôćĺ
   168Ôćĺ**Stack Skill Candidates:** {count}
   169Ôćĺ{List each candidate with units involved and detection signal}
   170Ôćĺ
   171Ôćĺ**Observations:**
   172Ôćĺ- {Key architectural patterns observed}
   173Ôćĺ- {Tightly coupled areas}
   174Ôćĺ- {Loosely coupled areas ideal for independent skills}
   175Ôćĺ
   176ÔćĺDoes this analysis look complete? Any integration patterns I should investigate further?"
   177Ôćĺ
   178ÔćĺWait for user feedback. Adjust analysis based on user input.
   179Ôćĺ
   180Ôćĺ### 7. Append to Report
   181Ôćĺ
   182ÔćĺAppend the complete "## Export Map" section to {outputFile}:
   183ÔćĺReplace `[Appended by step-04-map-and-detect]` under Export Map with:
   184Ôćĺ- Per-unit export summary table
   185Ôćĺ- Export pattern analysis
   186Ôćĺ
   187ÔćĺAppend the complete "## Integration Points" section to {outputFile}:
   188ÔćĺReplace `[Appended by step-04-map-and-detect]` under Integration Points with:
   189Ôćĺ- Cross-reference matrix
   190Ôćĺ- Integration point details
   191Ôćĺ- Stack skill candidate flags
   192Ôćĺ
   193ÔćĺUpdate {outputFile} frontmatter:
   194Ôćĺ```yaml
   195ÔćĺstepsCompleted: [append 'step-04-map-and-detect' to existing array]
   196ÔćĺlastStep: 'step-04-map-and-detect'
   197Ôćĺstack_skill_candidates: [{list flagged candidate groupings}]
   198Ôćĺ```
   199Ôćĺ
   200Ôćĺ### 8. Present MENU OPTIONS
   201Ôćĺ
   202ÔćĺDisplay: "**Select:** [C] Continue to Recommendations | [D] Discover Additional Source"
   203Ôćĺ
   204Ôćĺ#### Menu Handling Logic:
   205Ôćĺ
   206Ôćĺ- IF C: Save findings to {outputFile}, update frontmatter, then load, read entire file, then execute {nextStepFile}
   207Ôćĺ- IF D: Accept a new repo path/URL from the user. Run a lightweight scan (directory structure + manifest detection from step 02) and classify (unit identification from step 03) for the new source only. Merge results into the existing report ÔÇö append new units to the unit list, update `project_paths[]` in frontmatter. Then redisplay this step's export mapping for the new units before returning to the menu.
   208Ôćĺ- IF Any other: help user, then [Redisplay Menu Options](#8-present-menu-options)
   209Ôćĺ
   210Ôćĺ#### EXECUTION RULES:
   211Ôćĺ
   212Ôćĺ- ALWAYS halt and wait for user input after presenting menu
   213Ôćĺ- ONLY proceed to next step when user selects 'C'
   214Ôćĺ
   215Ôćĺ## CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE
   216Ôćĺ
   217ÔćĺONLY WHEN both the Export Map and Integration Points sections have been appended to {outputFile} with complete findings, and frontmatter stepsCompleted and stack_skill_candidates have been updated, will you load and read fully {nextStepFile} to begin recommendations.
   218Ôćĺ
   219Ôćĺ---
   220Ôćĺ
   221Ôćĺ## ­čÜĘ SYSTEM SUCCESS/FAILURE METRICS
   222Ôćĺ
   223Ôćĺ### Ôťů SUCCESS:
   224Ôćĺ
   225Ôćĺ- Every qualifying unit's export surface mapped with tier-appropriate depth
   226Ôćĺ- Import graph built showing cross-unit dependencies
   227Ôćĺ- Integration points documented with coupling strength
   228Ôćĺ- Stack skill candidates flagged with evidence
   229Ôćĺ- Findings presented to user for confirmation
   230Ôćĺ- Report updated with both Export Map and Integration Points sections
   231Ôćĺ- stepsCompleted and stack_skill_candidates updated in frontmatter
   232Ôćĺ
   233Ôćĺ### ÔŁî SYSTEM FAILURE:
   234Ôćĺ
   235Ôćĺ- Skipping units during export analysis (DO NOT BE LAZY)
   236Ôćĺ- Not building the cross-reference matrix
   237Ôćĺ- Missing stack skill candidate detection
   238Ôćĺ- Making recommendations in this step (that's step 05)
   239Ôćĺ- Not citing specific files for integration points
   240Ôćĺ- Not presenting findings for user confirmation
   241Ôćĺ
   242Ôćĺ**Master Rule:** Skipping steps, optimizing sequences, or not following exact instructions is FORBIDDEN and constitutes SYSTEM FAILURE.

    12Ôćĺ# Step 5: Recommend
    13Ôćĺ
    14Ôćĺ## STEP GOAL:
    15Ôćĺ
    16ÔćĺTo present each qualifying unit as a recommendation with evidence-based rationale, allow the user to confirm, reject, or modify each recommendation, and build the confirmed units list that drives brief generation.
    17Ôćĺ
    18Ôćĺ## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES (READ FIRST):
    19Ôćĺ
    20Ôćĺ### Universal Rules:
    21Ôćĺ
    22Ôćĺ- ­čŤĹ NEVER generate content without user input
    23Ôćĺ- ­čôľ CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action
    24Ôćĺ- ­čöä CRITICAL: When loading next step with 'C', ensure entire file is read
    25Ôćĺ- ­čôő YOU ARE A FACILITATOR, not a content generator
    26Ôćĺ- Ôťů YOU MUST ALWAYS SPEAK OUTPUT In your Agent communication style with the config `{communication_language}`
    27Ôćĺ- ÔÜÖ´ŞĆ TOOL/SUBPROCESS FALLBACK: If any instruction references a subprocess, subagent, or tool you do not have access to, you MUST still achieve the outcome in your main context thread
    28Ôćĺ
    29Ôćĺ### Role Reinforcement:
    30Ôćĺ
    31Ôćĺ- Ôťů You are a source code analyst and decomposition architect (Ferris Architect mode)
    32Ôćĺ- Ôťů If you already have been given a name, communication_style and identity, continue to use those while playing this new role
    33Ôćĺ- Ôťů Prescriptive precision ÔÇö zero-hallucination, every recommendation backed by evidence
    34Ôćĺ- Ôťů This is the COLLABORATIVE GATE ÔÇö user must explicitly confirm each unit
    35Ôćĺ
    36Ôćĺ### Step-Specific Rules:
    37Ôćĺ
    38Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» This is the primary user decision point ÔÇö do NOT rush through it
    39Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to proceed without explicit confirmation for each unit
    40Ôćĺ- ­čĺČ Present evidence for each recommendation, invite questions and pushback
    41Ôćĺ- ­čôő User can ask "why?" for any recommendation ÔÇö explain with specific file citations
    42Ôćĺ

    57Ôćĺ## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
    58Ôćĺ
    59Ôćĺ**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise unless user explicitly requests a change.
    60Ôćĺ
    61Ôćĺ### 1. Load Context
    62Ôćĺ
    63ÔćĺRead {outputFile} completely to obtain:

    72Ôćĺ### 2. Build Recommendation Cards

    98Ôćĺ### 3. Present Recommendations
    99Ôćĺ
   100Ôćĺ"**Recommendations for {project_name}**

   120ÔćĺYou can respond with a list (e.g., '1:Y, 2:Y, 3:N, 4:M') or address each individually.
   121Ôćĺ

   124Ôćĺ### 4. Process User Decisions

   131Ôćĺ**IF N (Reject):**

   169Ôćĺ
   170ÔćĺWait for explicit final confirmation.
   171Ôćĺ
   172Ôćĺ### 7. Append to Report
   173Ôćĺ
   174ÔćĺAppend the complete "## Recommendations" section to {outputFile}:

   190Ôćĺ### 8. Present MENU OPTIONS
   191Ôćĺ
   192ÔćĺDisplay: "**Select an Option:** [A] Advanced Elicitation [P] Party Mode [D] Discover Additional Source [C] Continue to Brief Generation"

Merge new units into the existing report and update `project_paths[]` in frontmatter. Run export mapping for the new units (same logic as step 04 section 2). Generate recommendation cards for the new units and present them for confirmation. Then redisplay this menu.
   199Ôćĺ- IF C: Save recommendations to {outputFile}, update frontmatter, then load, read entire file, then execute {nextStepFile}

   201Ôćĺ

1Ôćĺ# Skill Brief Schema
     2Ôćĺ
     3Ôćĺ## Purpose
     4Ôćĺ
     5ÔćĺDefines the output contract for skill-brief.yaml files generated by analyze-source. Each generated brief must conform to this schema to be consumable by brief-skill and create-skill downstream workflows.
     6Ôćĺ
     7Ôćĺ## Required Fields
     8Ôćĺ
     9Ôćĺ| Field | Type | Constraint | Description |
    10Ôćĺ|-------|------|------------|-------------|
    11Ôćĺ| name | string | kebab-case `[a-z0-9-]+` | Unique skill identifier |

    23Ôćĺ| Field | Type | Constraint | Description |

    27Ôćĺ| `scripts_intent` | string | `detect` / `none` / free-text | Describes whether scripts should be extracted. Values: `detect` (auto-detect from source ÔÇö default when absent), `none` (skip scripts), or a free-text description of expected scripts (e.g., "CLI validation tools in bin/"). |

    31ÔćĺWhen `source_type: "docs-only"`:

    36Ôćĺ
    37Ôćĺ## Version Detection
    38Ôćĺ
    39ÔćĺDuring brief generation, attempt to auto-detect the source version before defaulting to `"1.0.0"`. Check the first matching file in the source:
    40Ôćĺ
    41Ôćĺ- **Python:** `pyproject.toml` `[project] version` (static) Ôćĺ if `dynamic = ["version"]`, check `__init__.py` for `__version__` Ôćĺ `_version.py` if exists Ôćĺ `setup.py` `version=` Ôćĺ `git describe --tags --abbrev=0`
    42Ôćĺ- **JavaScript/TypeScript:** `package.json` (`"version"`)
    43Ôćĺ- **Rust:** `Cargo.toml` `[package] version` (static) Ôćĺ if `version = { workspace = true }`, resolve from workspace root `Cargo.toml` Ôćĺ `git describe --tags --abbrev=0`
    44Ôćĺ- **Go:** version tag from `go.mod` or `git describe --tags --abbrev=0`
    45Ôćĺ
    46ÔćĺIf the source is a remote GitHub repo, use `gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/contents/{file}` to read the version file. If the source is local, read the file directly.
    47Ôćĺ
    48ÔćĺIf detection succeeds, use the detected version. If it fails or returns a non-semver value, fall back to `"1.0.0"`.
    49Ôćĺ
    50ÔćĺThe create-skill workflow (step-03-extract) also performs version reconciliation at extraction time ÔÇö if the source version has changed since the brief was created, the extraction step warns and uses the source version.
    51Ôćĺ
    52Ôćĺ## Scope Object
    53Ôćĺ
    54Ôćĺ```yaml
    55Ôćĺscope:
    56Ôćĺ  type: full-library | specific-modules | public-api | docs-only
    57Ôćĺ  include:
    58Ôćĺ    - 'src/**/*.ts'          # At least one required
    59Ôćĺ  exclude:
    60Ôćĺ    - 'src/**/*.test.ts'     # Optional
    61Ôćĺ  notes: 'Optional rationale for scope decision'
    62Ôćĺ```
    63Ôćĺ
    64Ôćĺ### Scope Types
    65Ôćĺ
    66Ôćĺ| Type | Use When |
    67Ôćĺ|------|----------|
    68Ôćĺ| full-library | Entire codebase of a unit |
    69Ôćĺ| specific-modules | Selected components or packages |
    70Ôćĺ| public-api | Only exported interfaces |
    71Ôćĺ| docs-only | When source_type is docs-only ÔÇö no source code available, all content from doc_urls |
    72Ôćĺ
    73Ôćĺ## YAML Template
    74Ôćĺ
    75Ôćĺ```yaml
    76Ôćĺname: '{unit-name}'
    77Ôćĺversion: '{detected-version or 1.0.0}'
    78Ôćĺsource_type: 'source'
    79Ôćĺsource_repo: '{project_path}'
    80Ôćĺlanguage: '{detected_language}'
    81Ôćĺscope:
    82Ôćĺ  type: '{detected_scope_type}'
    83Ôćĺ  include:

    85Ôćĺ  exclude:
    86Ôćĺ    - '{exclude_pattern}'
    87Ôćĺ  notes: '{scope_rationale}'
    88Ôćĺdescription: '{1-3 sentence description}'
    89Ôćĺforge_tier: '{Quick|Forge|Forge+|Deep}'
    90Ôćĺcreated: '{current_date}'
    91Ôćĺcreated_by: '{user_name}'
    92Ôćĺ```
    93Ôćĺ
    94Ôćĺ## Validation Rules
    95Ôćĺ
    96Ôćĺ1. **Name uniqueness**: No duplicate names within forge_data_folder
    97Ôćĺ2. **Source accessibility**: source_repo path must exist or be reachable
    98Ôćĺ3. **Language recognized**: Must be a known programming language
    99Ôćĺ4. **Scope type valid**: Must match one of the four defined types (full-library, specific-modules, public-api, docs-only)
   100Ôćĺ5. **Include patterns**: At least one include glob pattern required (exception: `docs-only` scope, where include patterns are optional since no source code is available)
   101Ôćĺ6. **Forge tier match**: Must match value in forge-tier.yaml
   102Ôćĺ7. **Docs-only mode**: When `source_type: "docs-only"`, `doc_urls` required (>= 1), `source_repo` optional
   103Ôćĺ8. Each `doc_urls` entry must have a valid `url` field
   104Ôćĺ
   105Ôćĺ## Output Location
   106Ôćĺ
   107ÔćĺEach skill-brief.yaml is written to:
   108Ôćĺ```
   109Ôćĺ{forge_data_folder}/{unit-name}/skill-brief.yaml
   110Ôćĺ```

     8Ôćĺ# Create Stack Skill
     9Ôćĺ
    10Ôćĺ**Goal:** Produce a consolidated stack skill documenting how libraries connect. **Code-mode:** analyzes dependency manifests and co-import patterns from actual source code. **Compose-mode:** synthesizes from pre-generated individual skills and architecture documents when no codebase exists yet.
    11Ôćĺ
    12Ôćĺ**Your Role:** In addition to your name, communication_style, and persona, you are also a dependency analyst and integration architect operating in Ferris Architect mode. You bring expertise in dependency analysis, cross-library integration patterns, and compositional architecture, while the user brings their project knowledge and scope preferences. Execute with prescriptive precision ÔÇö every finding must trace to actual code with file:line citations.
    13Ôćĺ
    14Ôćĺ---
    15Ôćĺ
    16Ôćĺ## WORKFLOW ARCHITECTURE
    17Ôćĺ
    18ÔćĺThis uses **step-file architecture** for disciplined execution:
    19Ôćĺ
    20Ôćĺ### Core Principles
    21Ôćĺ
    22Ôćĺ- **Micro-file Design**: Each step is a self-contained instruction file that must be followed exactly
    23Ôćĺ- **Just-In-Time Loading**: Only the current step file is in memory ÔÇö never load future step files until told to do so
    24Ôćĺ- **Sequential Enforcement**: Sequence within the step files must be completed in order, no skipping or optimization allowed
    25Ôćĺ- **Append-Only Building**: Build outputs by accumulating state across steps
    26Ôćĺ- **Zero Hallucination**: All extracted content must trace to actual source code ÔÇö no inferred or assumed patterns. In compose-mode, inferred integrations are permitted but must be labeled `[inferred from shared domain]` per compose-mode-rules.md
    27Ôćĺ
    28Ôćĺ### Step Processing Rules
    29Ôćĺ
    30Ôćĺ1. **READ COMPLETELY**: Always read the entire step file before taking any action
    31Ôćĺ2. **FOLLOW SEQUENCE**: Execute all numbered sections in order, never deviate
    32Ôćĺ3. **WAIT FOR INPUT**: If a menu is presented, halt and wait for user selection
    33Ôćĺ4. **CHECK CONTINUATION**: If the step has a menu with Continue as an option, only proceed to next step when user selects 'C' (Continue)
    34Ôćĺ5. **LOAD NEXT**: When directed, load, read entire file, then execute the next step file
    35Ôćĺ
    36Ôćĺ### Critical Rules (NO EXCEPTIONS)
    37Ôćĺ
    38Ôćĺ- ­čŤĹ **NEVER** load multiple step files simultaneously
    39Ôćĺ- ­čôľ **ALWAYS** read entire step file before execution
    40Ôćĺ- ­čÜź **NEVER** skip steps or optimize the sequence
    41Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» **ALWAYS** follow the exact instructions in the step file
    42Ôćĺ- ÔĆŞ´ŞĆ **ALWAYS** halt at menus and wait for user input
    43Ôćĺ- ­čôő **NEVER** create mental todo lists from future steps
    44Ôćĺ- ÔÜÖ´ŞĆ **TOOL/SUBPROCESS FALLBACK**: If any instruction references a subprocess, subagent, or tool you do not have access to, you MUST still achieve the outcome in your main context thread
    45Ôćĺ- Ôťů YOU MUST ALWAYS SPEAK OUTPUT in your Agent communication style with the config `{communication_language}`
    46Ôćĺ
    47Ôćĺ---
    48Ôćĺ
    49Ôćĺ## INITIALIZATION SEQUENCE
    50Ôćĺ
    51Ôćĺ### 1. Module Configuration Loading
    52Ôćĺ
    53ÔćĺLoad and read full config from {project-root}/_bmad/skf/config.yaml and resolve:
    54Ôćĺ

    57Ôćĺ### 2. First Step Execution
    58Ôćĺ
    59ÔćĺLoad, read the full file and then execute `./steps-c/step-01-init.md` to begin the workflow.

    47Ôćĺ- Does NOT rank, filter, or analyze ÔÇö just detect and parse
    48Ôćĺ
    49Ôćĺ## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
    50Ôćĺ
    51Ôćĺ**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise.
    52Ôćĺ
    53Ôćĺ### 0. Check Compose Mode
    54Ôćĺ
    55Ôćĺ**If `compose_mode` is true:**
    56Ôćĺ
    57ÔćĺScan `{skills_output_folder}` for subdirectories containing SKILL.md and metadata.json.
    58Ôćĺ
    59Ôćĺ**Filter:** Skip any subdirectory named `{project_name}-stack` or any skill where `metadata.json` has `"skill_type": "stack"` ÔÇö stack skills must not be loaded as source dependencies to avoid self-referencing loops.
    60Ôćĺ
    61Ôćĺ**If zero skills remain after filtering:** HALT with: "**Cannot proceed in compose-mode.** No individual skills found in `{skills_output_folder}` (after filtering stack skills). Run [CS] Create Skill or [QS] Quick Skill to generate individual skills first, then re-run [SS]."
    62Ôćĺ
    63ÔćĺFor each skill found:
    64Ôćĺ1. Read metadata.json
    65Ôćĺ2. Extract: name, language, confidence_tier, source_repo, exports count, version
    66Ôćĺ3. Store as `raw_dependencies` with source: "existing_skill"
    67Ôćĺ
    68ÔćĺDisplay:
    69Ôćĺ"**Loaded {N} existing skills as dependencies.**
    70Ôćĺ
    71Ôćĺ| Skill | Language | Tier | Exports | Source |
    72Ôćĺ|-------|----------|------|---------|--------|
    73Ôćĺ| {name} | {lang} | {tier} | {count} | {repo} |
    74Ôćĺ
    75Ôćĺ**Proceeding to scope confirmation...**"
    76Ôćĺ
    77ÔćĺSkip to section 5 (Auto-Proceed to Next Step) ÔÇö the skills table above serves as the detection summary.
    78Ôćĺ
    79Ôćĺ**If not compose_mode:** Continue with section 1 (existing flow).
    80Ôćĺ
    81Ôćĺ### 1. Check for Explicit Dependency List
    82Ôćĺ
    83Ôćĺ**If `explicit_deps` was provided in step 01:**
    84Ôćĺ
    85Ôćĺ"**Using provided dependency list.** Skipping manifest auto-detection.
    86Ôćĺ
    87Ôćĺ**Dependencies:** {explicit_deps_count} libraries provided"
    88Ôćĺ
    89ÔćĺStore the explicit list as `raw_dependencies` and skip to section 4.
    90Ôćĺ
    91Ôćĺ**If no explicit list:** Continue to section 2.
    92Ôćĺ
    93Ôćĺ### 2. Scan for Manifest Files
    94Ôćĺ
    95ÔćĺLoad `{manifestPatterns}` for supported ecosystem detection patterns.
    96Ôćĺ
    97ÔćĺScan the project root (depth 0-1) for manifest files:
    98Ôćĺ
    99Ôćĺ- Search for each supported manifest filename
   100Ôćĺ- Record: file path, ecosystem type, file size
   101Ôćĺ- Note any unusual structures (monorepo with multiple manifests, workspace configurations)
   102Ôćĺ
   103Ôćĺ**If no manifest files found:**
   104Ôćĺ
   105Ôćĺ"**No dependency manifests detected** in the project root.
   106Ôćĺ
   107ÔćĺSearched for: package.json, requirements.txt, Cargo.toml, go.mod, pom.xml, build.gradle, Gemfile, composer.json, *.csproj
   108Ôćĺ
   109Ôćĺ**Options:**
   110Ôćĺ1. Provide an explicit dependency list
   111Ôćĺ2. Specify a different project root path
   112Ôćĺ3. Cancel workflow
   113Ôćĺ
   114Ôćĺ**Halting ÔÇö please provide input.**"
   115Ôćĺ
   116ÔćĺSTOP ÔÇö wait for user response.
   117Ôćĺ
   118Ôćĺ### 3. Parse Each Manifest
   119Ôćĺ
   120ÔćĺFor each discovered manifest file:
   121Ôćĺ
   122Ôćĺ1. Read the file contents
   123Ôćĺ2. Extract dependency names and version constraints using ecosystem-specific parsing:
   124Ôćĺ   - JSON manifests: parse dependencies/devDependencies keys
   125Ôćĺ   - TOML manifests: parse [dependencies] sections
   126Ôćĺ   - Text manifests: parse line-by-line (name==version or name>=version)
   127Ôćĺ   - XML manifests: parse dependency elements
   128Ôćĺ   - Gradle: parse implementation/api/compile declarations
   129Ôćĺ3. Categorize: runtime vs dev-only
   130Ôćĺ4. Normalize dependency names across ecosystems
   131Ôćĺ
   132ÔćĺDeduplicate dependencies found across multiple manifests.
   133Ôćĺ
   134Ôćĺ### 4. Display Detection Summary
   135Ôćĺ
   136Ôćĺ"**Manifest detection complete.**
   137Ôćĺ
   138Ôćĺ**Manifests found:** {count}
   139Ôćĺ{For each manifest:}
   140Ôćĺ- `{file_path}` ({ecosystem}) ÔÇö {dep_count} dependencies
   141Ôćĺ
   142Ôćĺ**Total unique dependencies:** {total_count}
   143Ôćĺ- Runtime: {runtime_count}
   144Ôćĺ- Dev-only: {dev_count}
   145Ôćĺ
   146Ôćĺ**Proceeding to dependency ranking...**"
   147Ôćĺ
   148Ôćĺ### 5. Auto-Proceed to Next Step
   149Ôćĺ
   150ÔćĺLoad, read the full file and then execute `{nextStepFile}`.
   151Ôćĺ
   152Ôćĺ---
   153Ôćĺ
   154Ôćĺ## ­čÜĘ SYSTEM SUCCESS/FAILURE METRICS
   155Ôćĺ
   156Ôćĺ### Ôťů SUCCESS:
   157Ôćĺ
   158Ôćĺ- All manifest files in project root detected
   159Ôćĺ- Each manifest parsed with dependency names extracted
   160Ôćĺ- Runtime vs dev-only categorization applied
   161Ôćĺ- Deduplication across multiple manifests completed
   162Ôćĺ- Detection summary displayed with accurate counts
   163Ôćĺ- Auto-proceeded to step 03
   164Ôćĺ
   165Ôćĺ### ÔŁî SYSTEM FAILURE:
   166Ôćĺ
   167Ôćĺ- Missing manifest files that exist in the project
   168Ôćĺ- Inventing dependencies not in manifest files
   169Ôćĺ- Starting to rank or analyze dependencies (step 03's job)
   170Ôćĺ- Not halting when no manifests found
   171Ôćĺ- Displaying A/P menu (this is auto-proceed)
   172Ôćĺ
   173Ôćĺ**Master Rule:** Detect and parse only. No ranking, no filtering, no analysis.
   174Ôćĺ

9Ôćĺ| Field | Type | Constraint | Description |

    18Ôćĺ## Naive Mode Weight Redistribution

    20ÔćĺThe following weights replace the default table for naive mode. The 18% coherence weight from the default table has been proportionally redistributed into these values. Do not apply a second redistribution.
    21Ôćĺ

    23Ôćĺ- Export Coverage: 45%

    32Ôćĺ## tessl and Split-Body Interaction
    33Ôćĺ
    34Ôćĺtessl evaluates SKILL.md body content only ÔÇö it does not read `references/*.md` files. After split-body extraction, the tessl content score will drop significantly (e.g., 65% to 38%) because Tier 2 content is no longer inline. This is expected behavior and does not reflect actual content quality. When reporting scores for a split-body skill, note: "tessl content score reflects post-split inline content only. Use the pre-split tessl score as the content quality baseline."

     7Ôćĺ
     8Ôćĺ# Step 3: Rank and Confirm Scope
     9Ôćĺ
    10Ôćĺ## STEP GOAL:
    11Ôćĺ
    12ÔćĺCount import frequency for each dependency across the codebase, rank by usage, and present for user confirmation of which libraries to include in the stack skill.
    13Ôćĺ
    14Ôćĺ## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES (READ FIRST):
    15Ôćĺ
    16Ôćĺ### Universal Rules:
    17Ôćĺ
    18Ôćĺ- ­čôľ CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action
    19Ôćĺ- ÔÜÖ´ŞĆ TOOL/SUBPROCESS FALLBACK: If any instruction references a subprocess, subagent, or tool you do not have access to, you MUST still achieve the outcome in your main context thread
    20Ôćĺ- Ôťů YOU MUST ALWAYS SPEAK OUTPUT in your Agent communication style with the config `{communication_language}`
    21Ôćĺ
    22Ôćĺ### Role Reinforcement:
    23Ôćĺ
    24Ôćĺ- Ôťů You are a dependency analyst operating in Ferris Architect mode
    25Ôćĺ- Ôťů Prescriptive precision ÔÇö import counts must reflect actual code
    26Ôćĺ- Ôťů User has final authority on scope ÔÇö present data, let them decide
    27Ôćĺ
    28Ôćĺ### Step-Specific Rules:
    29Ôćĺ
    30Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Focus on counting imports, ranking, and getting user confirmation
    31Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to extract library documentation ÔÇö that is step 04
    32Ôćĺ- ­čĺČ Present data clearly so user can make informed scope decisions
    33Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Use subprocess (Pattern 1 grep) for import counting when available
    34Ôćĺ
    35Ôćĺ## EXECUTION PROTOCOLS:
    36Ôćĺ
    37Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Count imports per library across the codebase
    38Ôćĺ- ­čĺż Store confirmed_dependencies as workflow state
    39Ôćĺ- ­čôľ Wait for user confirmation before proceeding
    40Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to proceed without explicit user scope confirmation
    41Ôćĺ
    42Ôćĺ## CONTEXT BOUNDARIES:
    43Ôćĺ
    44Ôćĺ- From step 02: raw_dependencies[] with ecosystem info
    45Ôćĺ- This step produces: confirmed_dependencies[] (user-approved scope)
    46Ôćĺ- User interaction: Gate 1 ÔÇö scope confirmation required
    47Ôćĺ- Apply scope_overrides from step 01 if provided
    48Ôćĺ
    49Ôćĺ## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
    50Ôćĺ
    51Ôćĺ**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise.
    52Ôćĺ
    53Ôćĺ### 1. Count Import Frequency
    54Ôćĺ
    55Ôćĺ**If `compose_mode` is true:**
    56Ôćĺ
    57ÔćĺSkip import counting entirely. All skills are included by default.
    58Ôćĺ
    59ÔćĺSet `confirmed_dependencies` = all `raw_dependencies` (the list already stored as workflow state from Step 02).
    60Ôćĺ
    61Ôćĺ**Apply scope_overrides:** If `scope_overrides` were provided in step 01, apply them now ÔÇö force-include or force-exclude skills as specified. Log any overrides applied.
    62Ôćĺ
    63ÔćĺPresent skills sorted by architectural layer (from architecture doc if available):
    64Ôćĺ- If `architecture_doc_path` is not null: parse architecture doc for section headers to determine layer grouping
    65Ôćĺ- If `architecture_doc_path` is null or layers not detectable: present alphabetically
    66Ôćĺ
    67ÔćĺDisplay skills as a table:
    68Ôćĺ
    69Ôćĺ| # | Skill | Language | Tier | Architecture Layer |
    70Ôćĺ|---|-------|----------|------|--------------------|
    71Ôćĺ| 1 | {skill_name} | {language} | {confidence_tier} | {layer or 'Unclassified'} |
    72Ôćĺ
    73ÔćĺUser confirms inclusion/exclusion at the gate (same [C] menu as code-mode).
    74Ôćĺ
    75ÔćĺSkip to section 5 (Present MENU OPTIONS).
    76Ôćĺ
    77Ôćĺ**If not compose_mode:**
    78Ôćĺ
    79ÔćĺFor each dependency in `raw_dependencies`:
    80Ôćĺ
    81Ôćĺ**Launch a subprocess** that runs grep across all source files in the project to count import statements for each library. Return only the counts, not file contents.
    82Ôćĺ
    83ÔćĺUse ecosystem-appropriate import patterns:
    84Ôćĺ- JavaScript/TypeScript: `import .* from ['"]library`, `require\(['"]library`
    85Ôćĺ- Python: `import library`, `from library import`
    86Ôćĺ- Rust: `use library::`, `extern crate library`
    87Ôćĺ- Go: `"library"` in import blocks
    88Ôćĺ- (Match patterns from manifest-patterns.md)
    89Ôćĺ
    90Ôćĺ**Subprocess returns:** `{library_name: import_count, files: [file_paths]}` for each dependency.
    91Ôćĺ
    92Ôćĺ**If subprocess unavailable:** Perform grep operations in main thread sequentially.
    93Ôćĺ
    94ÔćĺExclude from counting:
    95Ôćĺ- Test files (*/test/*, *_test.*, *.spec.*, *.test.*)
    96Ôćĺ- Config files (*.config.*, .eslintrc, etc.)
    97Ôćĺ- Build artifacts (dist/, build/, node_modules/, target/, __pycache__/)
    98Ôćĺ
    99Ôćĺ### 2. Rank and Filter
   100Ôćĺ
   101ÔćĺSort dependencies by import count (descending).
   102Ôćĺ
   103ÔćĺApply filtering:
   104Ôćĺ- **Include by default:** Libraries with 2+ import files
   105Ôćĺ- **Flag as trivial:** Libraries with 0-1 import files
   106Ôćĺ- **Apply scope_overrides** from step 01 if provided (force include/exclude)
   107Ôćĺ
   108Ôćĺ### 3. Present Ranked List
   109Ôćĺ
   110Ôćĺ"**Dependency ranking complete.** Here are your project's libraries ranked by usage:
   111Ôćĺ
   112Ôćĺ| # | Library | Imports | Files | Category |
   113Ôćĺ|---|---------|---------|-------|----------|
   114Ôćĺ| 1 | {name} | {count} | {file_count} | runtime |
   115Ôćĺ| 2 | {name} | {count} | {file_count} | runtime |
   116Ôćĺ| ... | ... | ... | ... | ... |
   117Ôćĺ
   118Ôćĺ**Below threshold** (0-1 imports ÔÇö excluded by default):
   119Ôćĺ| Library | Imports | Category |
   120Ôćĺ|---------|---------|----------|
   121Ôćĺ| {name} | {count} | {category} |
   122Ôćĺ
   123Ôćĺ**Total:** {total} dependencies detected, {above_threshold} recommended for inclusion
   124Ôćĺ
   125Ôćĺ---
   126Ôćĺ
   127Ôćĺ**Please confirm your scope:**
   128Ôćĺ- Type **C** to accept the recommended scope (all above-threshold libraries)
   129Ôćĺ- Type library names to **add** from the below-threshold list
   130Ôćĺ- Type **-library_name** to **exclude** a recommended library
   131Ôćĺ- Type a custom list to override entirely"
   132Ôćĺ
   133Ôćĺ### 4. Process User Response
   134Ôćĺ
   135Ôćĺ**If C (accept recommended):**
   136ÔćĺStore all above-threshold libraries as `confirmed_dependencies`.
   137Ôćĺ
   138Ôćĺ**If modifications requested:**
   139ÔćĺApply additions/exclusions, display updated list, and ask for final confirmation.
   140Ôćĺ
   141Ôćĺ**If custom list provided:**
   142ÔćĺUse the custom list as `confirmed_dependencies`.
   143Ôćĺ
   144ÔćĺDisplay final confirmation:
   145Ôćĺ
   146Ôćĺ"**Scope confirmed:** {count} libraries selected for stack skill extraction.
   147Ôćĺ
   148Ôćĺ{List confirmed libraries}
   149Ôćĺ
   150Ôćĺ**Proceeding to parallel extraction...**"
   151Ôćĺ
   152Ôćĺ### 5. Present MENU OPTIONS
   153Ôćĺ
   154ÔćĺDisplay: **Select:** [C] Continue to Extraction
   155Ôćĺ
   156Ôćĺ#### EXECUTION RULES:
   157Ôćĺ
   158Ôćĺ- ALWAYS halt and wait for user input after presenting scope
   159Ôćĺ- ONLY proceed to next step when user confirms scope and selects 'C'
   160Ôćĺ
   161Ôćĺ#### Menu Handling Logic:
   162Ôćĺ
   163Ôćĺ- IF C: Store current `confirmed_dependencies` (including any modifications made since initial presentation), then load, read entire file, then execute {nextStepFile}
   164Ôćĺ- IF Any other: Process as scope modification (add/remove skills from `confirmed_dependencies`), update the in-memory `confirmed_dependencies` list accordingly, redisplay the updated skills table, then [Redisplay Menu Options](#5-present-menu-options)
   165Ôćĺ
   166Ôćĺ---
   167Ôćĺ
   168Ôćĺ## ­čÜĘ SYSTEM SUCCESS/FAILURE METRICS
   169Ôćĺ
   170Ôćĺ### Ôťů SUCCESS:
   171Ôćĺ
   172Ôćĺ- Import counts reflect actual codebase usage (not guesses)
   173Ôćĺ- Dependencies ranked by import frequency
   174Ôćĺ- Trivial dependencies filtered below threshold
   175Ôćĺ- User explicitly confirmed scope
   176Ôćĺ- confirmed_dependencies stored for step 04
   177Ôćĺ
   178Ôćĺ### ÔŁî SYSTEM FAILURE:
   179Ôćĺ
   180Ôćĺ- Fabricating import counts without grepping
   181Ôćĺ- Proceeding without user scope confirmation
   182Ôćĺ- Including test/config files in import counts
   183Ôćĺ- Starting library extraction (step 04's job)
   184Ôćĺ
   185Ôćĺ**Master Rule:** Present data, let user decide. No extraction until scope is confirmed.
   186Ôćĺ

     8Ôćĺ# Step 4: Parallel Library Extraction
     9Ôćĺ
    10Ôćĺ## STEP GOAL:
    11Ôćĺ
    12ÔćĺFor each confirmed dependency, extract key exports, usage patterns, and API surface documentation using tier-dependent tools in parallel.
    13Ôćĺ
    14Ôćĺ## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES (READ FIRST):
    15Ôćĺ
    16Ôćĺ### Universal Rules:
    17Ôćĺ
    18Ôćĺ- ­čôľ CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action
    19Ôćĺ- ÔÜÖ´ŞĆ TOOL/SUBPROCESS FALLBACK: If any instruction references a subprocess, subagent, or tool you do not have access to, you MUST still achieve the outcome in your main context thread
    20Ôćĺ- Ôťů YOU MUST ALWAYS SPEAK OUTPUT in your Agent communication style with the config `{communication_language}`
    21Ôćĺ
    22Ôćĺ### Role Reinforcement:
    23Ôćĺ
    24Ôćĺ- Ôťů You are a dependency analyst operating in Ferris Architect mode
    25Ôćĺ- Ôťů Zero hallucination ÔÇö only extract what exists in actual source code
    26Ôćĺ- Ôťů Every export and pattern must cite actual file:line references
    27Ôćĺ
    28Ôćĺ### Step-Specific Rules:
    29Ôćĺ
    30Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Extract per-library using Pattern 4 (parallel subprocess) when available
    31Ôćĺ- ­čĺČ Each subprocess returns structured extraction, not raw file contents
    32Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to analyze cross-library integrations ÔÇö that is step 05
    33Ôćĺ- ÔÜÖ´ŞĆ If parallel subprocess unavailable, extract libraries sequentially in main thread
    34Ôćĺ
    35Ôćĺ## EXECUTION PROTOCOLS:
    36Ôćĺ
    37Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Launch parallel extraction per confirmed library
    38Ôćĺ- ­čĺż Store per_library_extractions with confidence tier labels
    39Ôćĺ- ­čôľ Report extraction summary before auto-proceeding
    40Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to proceed if zero libraries successfully extracted
    41Ôćĺ
    42Ôćĺ## CONTEXT BOUNDARIES:
    43Ôćĺ
    44Ôćĺ- From step 03: confirmed_dependencies[] (user-approved scope)
    45Ôćĺ- From step 01: forge_tier, available_tools
    46Ôćĺ- This step produces: per_library_extractions[] per library
    47Ôćĺ- Each extraction includes: exports, patterns, confidence tier
    48Ôćĺ
    49Ôćĺ## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
    50Ôćĺ
    51Ôćĺ**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise.
    52Ôćĺ
    53Ôćĺ### 0. Check Compose Mode
    54Ôćĺ
    55Ôćĺ**If `compose_mode` is true:**
    56Ôćĺ
    57Ôćĺ"**Extraction data already available from individual skills. Skipping extraction phase.**"
    58Ôćĺ
    59ÔćĺFor each confirmed skill, load the full SKILL.md content into context. Build a `per_library_extractions[]` entry for each skill with the following fields:
    60Ôćĺ- `library`: skill name from metadata.json
    61Ôćĺ- `exports`: exports list extracted from the SKILL.md exports section
    62Ôćĺ- `usage_patterns`: usage patterns from the SKILL.md usage section
    63Ôćĺ- `confidence`: the skill's existing confidence tier (T1, T1-low, T2) ÔÇö inherited directly
    64Ôćĺ
    65ÔćĺDisplay an extraction summary:
    66Ôćĺ
    67Ôćĺ"**Loaded {N} skill extractions from existing skills.**
    68Ôćĺ
    69Ôćĺ| Skill | Exports | Confidence | Status |
    70Ôćĺ|-------|---------|------------|--------|
    71Ôćĺ| {name} | {count} | {tier} | Loaded |"
    72Ôćĺ
    73ÔćĺAuto-proceed to next step.
    74Ôćĺ
    75Ôćĺ**If not compose_mode:** Continue with section 1 (existing flow).
    76Ôćĺ
    77Ôćĺ### 1. Prepare Extraction Plan
    78Ôćĺ
    79Ôćĺ**AST Tool Availability Check (Forge/Deep only):**
    80Ôćĺ
    81ÔćĺThis workflow operates on local project files and installed library packages. Remote source resolution does not apply ÔÇö libraries are analyzed as they exist within the project's dependency tree.
    82Ôćĺ
    83Ôćĺ**If AST tool is unavailable at Forge/Deep tier:**
    84Ôćĺ
    85ÔćĺÔÜá´ŞĆ **Warn the user explicitly:** "AST tools are unavailable ÔÇö extraction will use source reading (T1-low). Run [SF] Setup Forge to detect and configure AST tools for T1 confidence."
    86Ôćĺ
    87ÔćĺDegrade to Quick tier extraction. Note the degradation reason in context for the evidence report.
    88Ôćĺ
    89Ôćĺ**Per-file AST failure handling:**
    90Ôćĺ

    92Ôćĺ
    93ÔćĺFor each library in `confirmed_dependencies`, determine extraction strategy based on forge tier:
    94Ôćĺ

   110Ôćĺ- Track API changes across project history
   111Ôćĺ- Confidence: T2 (QMD-enriched temporal context)
   112Ôćĺ
   113Ôćĺ### 2. Launch Parallel Extraction
   114Ôćĺ
   115Ôćĺ**Launch subprocesses in parallel** (max_parallel_generation) ÔÇö one per confirmed library:
   116Ôćĺ
   117ÔćĺEach subprocess:
   118Ôćĺ1. Reads all files importing the library (from step 03 file lists)
   119Ôćĺ2. Extracts key exports used in this project (functions, classes, types, constants)

   137Ôćĺ

   154Ôćĺ"**Library extraction complete.**

   159Ôćĺ| {name} | {count} | {count} | {tier} | Ôťô |

   162Ôćĺ**Results:** {success_count}/{total_count} libraries extracted

    10Ôćĺ# Step 5: Detect Integrations
    11Ôćĺ

    15Ôćĺ
    16Ôćĺ## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES (READ FIRST):
    17Ôćĺ
    18Ôćĺ### Universal Rules:

    29Ôćĺ

    33Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to compile SKILL.md ÔÇö that is step 06

    46Ôćĺ- From step 03: confirmed_dependencies[] with file lists per library
    47Ôćĺ- From step 04: per_library_extractions[] with exports and patterns
    48Ôćĺ- This step produces: integration_graph {pairs[], types[], files[]}

    51Ôćĺ## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
    52Ôćĺ
    53Ôćĺ**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise.
    54Ôćĺ
    55Ôćĺ### 1. Generate Library Pairs
    56Ôćĺ
    57ÔćĺFrom `confirmed_dependencies`, generate all unique pairs:
    58Ôćĺ- N libraries Ôćĺ N*(N-1)/2 pairs

    61ÔćĺReport: "**Analyzing {pair_count} library pairs for integration patterns...**"
    62Ôćĺ
    63Ôćĺ### 2. Detect Co-Import Files
    64Ôćĺ
    65Ôćĺ**If `compose_mode` is true:**
    66Ôćĺ
    67ÔćĺInstead of co-import grep, detect integrations from architecture document:
    68Ôćĺ
    69Ôćĺ1. Load `{composeModeRules}` for integration evidence format rules
    70Ôćĺ2. **If `{architecture_doc_path}` is null or not available:** Skip directly to the "If no architecture document available" fallback below
    71Ôćĺ3. Load the architecture document from `{architecture_doc_path}`
    72Ôćĺ4. Use prose-based co-mention analysis: find paragraphs mentioning 2+ confirmed skill names
    73Ôćĺ5. For each detected integration pair:
    74Ôćĺ   - Load both skills' export lists and API signatures
    75Ôćĺ   - Compose an integration section following the format from `{composeModeRules}`
    76Ôćĺ   - Include VS feasibility verdict if a feasibility report (`feasibility-report-{project_name}.md`) exists in `{forge_data_folder}/`
    77Ôćĺ   - Cite evidence from both skills: `[from skill: {skill_name}]`
    78Ôćĺ
    79ÔćĺAll integration evidence inherits confidence tiers from the source skills. Apply the `[composed]` suffix to all confidence labels per `{composeModeRules}` ÔÇö e.g., `T1 [composed]`, `T1-low [composed, +T2 annotations]`.
    80Ôćĺ
    81Ôćĺ**VS verdict parsing (if feasibility report exists):** Read the `overall_verdict` from the report's YAML frontmatter. Parse the `## Integration Verdicts` markdown table for per-pair verdicts. For all pairs, include `VS overall: {verdict}` and `VS pair: {verdict}` in the integration evidence per the format in `{composeModeRules}`. Additionally, flag any pairs where VS reported `Risky` or `Blocked` by appending a `[VS: Risky]` or `[VS: Blocked]` warning annotation to the integration entry.
    82Ôćĺ
    83ÔćĺIf no architecture document available:
    84Ôćĺ- Infer potential integrations from skills sharing the same `language` field or sharing domain keywords in their SKILL.md descriptions (use the SKILL.md content loaded in step-04)
    85Ôćĺ- Mark inferred integrations: `[inferred from shared domain]` ÔÇö use this suffix instead of `[composed]` for inferred integrations
    86Ôćĺ- Inferred integrations qualify automatically ÔÇö no file-count threshold applies
    87Ôćĺ
    88ÔćĺSkip to section 3 (Classify Integration Types) with the compose-mode pairs.
    89Ôćĺ
    90Ôćĺ**If not compose_mode:**
    91Ôćĺ
    92ÔćĺFor each library pair (A, B):
    93Ôćĺ
    94Ôćĺ**Launch a subprocess** that greps across all source files to find files importing BOTH library A and library B. Return only file paths and import line numbers.
    95Ôćĺ
    96Ôćĺ**Subprocess returns:** `{pair: [A, B], co_import_files: [{path, line_A, line_B}], count: N}`
    97Ôćĺ
    98Ôćĺ**If subprocess unavailable:** Intersect the file lists from step 03 import counts for each pair.
    99Ôćĺ
   100Ôćĺ**Threshold:** A pair must have 2+ co-import files to qualify as an integration pattern (single file co-imports may be incidental).
   101Ôćĺ
   102Ôćĺ**CCC Semantic Augmentation (Forge+ and Deep with ccc):**
   103Ôćĺ
   104ÔćĺIf `tools.ccc` is true AND `ccc_index.status` is `"fresh"` or `"stale"` in forge-tier.yaml, augment co-import detection with semantic search (max 1 query per library pair):
   105Ôćĺ
   106ÔćĺFor each library pair that has exactly 1 co-import file (below the 2-file threshold), run `ccc_bridge.search("{libA} {libB}", source_root, top_k=10)` to find files where the two libraries interact semantically ÔÇö even without explicit import co-location. If CCC returns additional files where both libraries appear, add them to the pair's co-import candidate list and re-evaluate against the 2-file threshold.
   107Ôćĺ
   108ÔćĺFor pairs that already qualify (2+ files), CCC is not needed for detection ÔÇö but the CCC results may surface additional integration files for richer classification in section 3.
   109Ôćĺ
   110ÔćĺCCC failures: skip augmentation silently, proceed with grep-only results.
   111Ôćĺ
   112Ôćĺ### 3. Classify Integration Types
   113Ôćĺ
   114ÔćĺLoad `{integrationPatterns}` for classification rules.
   115Ôćĺ
   116ÔćĺFor each qualifying pair, analyze to classify the integration type (**in compose-mode**: all architecture-document-detected pairs qualify automatically ÔÇö the 2+ co-import file threshold applies only in code-mode; **in code-mode**: pair must have 2+ co-import files):
   117Ôćĺ
   118Ôćĺ- **Type 1: Middleware Chain** ÔÇö Sequential function calls piping output between libraries
   119Ôćĺ- **Type 2: Shared Types** ÔÇö Type definitions exchanged between libraries
   120Ôćĺ- **Type 3: Configuration Bridge** ÔÇö One library configuring or initializing another
   121Ôćĺ- **Type 4: Event Handler** ÔÇö Event patterns crossing library boundaries
   122Ôćĺ- **Type 5: Adapter/Wrapper** ÔÇö Thin wrapper connecting library interfaces
   123Ôćĺ- **Type 6: State Sharing** ÔÇö Shared state stores or context providers
   124Ôćĺ
   125ÔćĺFor each detected integration:
   126Ôćĺ- Identify the top 3 files demonstrating the pattern
   127Ôćĺ- Extract a brief description of how the libraries connect
   128Ôćĺ- Assign confidence: T1 if AST-verified, T1-low if source reading
   129Ôćĺ
   130Ôćĺ### 4. Build Integration Graph
   131Ôćĺ
   132ÔćĺAssemble the integration graph:
   133Ôćĺ- **Nodes:** Confirmed libraries (with extraction data from step 04)
   134Ôćĺ- **Edges:** Detected integration pairs with type, file count, and description
   135Ôćĺ- Identify **hub libraries** (connected to 3+ other libraries)
   136Ôćĺ- Identify **cross-cutting patterns** (patterns spanning 3+ libraries)
   137Ôćĺ
   138Ôćĺ### 5. Display Integration Summary
   139Ôćĺ
   140Ôćĺ**If integrations detected:**
   141Ôćĺ
   142Ôćĺ"**Integration detection complete.**
   143Ôćĺ
   144Ôćĺ**Integration graph:** {lib_count} libraries, {pair_count} integration pairs
   145Ôćĺ
   146Ôćĺ**Hub libraries** (connected to 3+ others):
   147Ôćĺ{For each hub:} - **{library}** ÔÇö integrates with {partner_list}
   148Ôćĺ
   149Ôćĺ**Detected integrations:**
   150Ôćĺ| Library A | Library B | Type | Co-import Files | Confidence |
   151Ôćĺ|-----------|-----------|------|-----------------|------------|
   152Ôćĺ| {name} | {name} | {type} | {count} | {tier} |
   153Ôćĺ
   154Ôćĺ{If cross-cutting patterns:}

   156Ôćĺ- {description spanning 3+ libraries}
   157Ôćĺ
   158Ôćĺ**Proceeding to stack compilation...**"
   159Ôćĺ
   160Ôćĺ**If no integrations detected:**
   161Ôćĺ
   162Ôćĺ"**No co-import integration patterns detected** between confirmed libraries.
   163Ôćĺ

   165Ôćĺ
   166Ôćĺ**Proceeding to stack compilation...**"
   167Ôćĺ
   168Ôćĺ### 6. Auto-Proceed to Next Step
   169Ôćĺ
   170ÔćĺLoad, read the full file and then execute `{nextStepFile}`.
   171Ôćĺ
   172Ôćĺ---
   173Ôćĺ
   174Ôćĺ## ­čÜĘ SYSTEM SUCCESS/FAILURE METRICS
   175Ôćĺ
   176Ôćĺ### Ôťů SUCCESS:
   177Ôćĺ
   178Ôćĺ- All library pairs analyzed for co-imports
   179Ôćĺ- Integration types classified using pattern rules
   180Ôćĺ- Hub libraries and cross-cutting patterns identified
   181Ôćĺ- Integration graph built with confidence labels
   182Ôćĺ- Graceful handling of zero integrations (not a failure)

   184Ôćĺ
   185Ôćĺ### ÔŁî SYSTEM FAILURE:

   113Ôćĺ- Error handling approaches across libraries
   114Ôćĺ- Configuration conventions
   115Ôćĺ- Import organization patterns
   116Ôćĺ
   117Ôćĺ### 6. Compile Library Reference Index
   118Ôćĺ
   119ÔćĺCreate the reference index table:
   120Ôćĺ
   121Ôćĺ| Library | Imports | Key Exports | Confidence | Reference |
   122Ôćĺ|---------|---------|-------------|------------|-----------|
   123Ôćĺ| ... | ... | ... | ... | ... |
   124Ôćĺ
   125Ôćĺ(**in compose-mode**: replace the Imports column with Export Count from source skill metadata, since import counts are not available)
   126Ôćĺ
   127Ôćĺ### 7. Present Compiled SKILL.md Preview
   128Ôćĺ
   129Ôćĺ"**Stack skill compilation complete. Please review:**
   130Ôćĺ
   131Ôćĺ---
   132Ôćĺ
   133Ôćĺ{Display full compiled SKILL.md content}

   135Ôćĺ---

   148Ôćĺ### 8. Present MENU OPTIONS
   149Ôćĺ
   150ÔćĺDisplay: **Select:** [C] Continue to Output Generation

   152Ôćĺ#### EXECUTION RULES:

   154Ôćĺ- ALWAYS halt and wait for user input after presenting compilation

   157Ôćĺ#### Menu Handling Logic:

   160Ôćĺ- IF Any other: Process as feedback, adjust compilation, redisplay preview, then [Redisplay Menu Options](#8-present-menu-options)

     9Ôćĺ# Step 7: Generate Output Files
    10Ôćĺ
    11Ôćĺ## STEP GOAL:
    12Ôćĺ
    13ÔćĺWrite all deliverable and workspace artifact files to their target directories.
    14Ôćĺ
    15Ôćĺ## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES (READ FIRST):
    16Ôćĺ
    17Ôćĺ### Universal Rules:
    18Ôćĺ
    19Ôćĺ- ­čôľ CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action
    20Ôćĺ- ÔÜÖ´ŞĆ TOOL/SUBPROCESS FALLBACK: If any instruction references a subprocess, subagent, or tool you do not have access to, you MUST still achieve the outcome in your main context thread
    21Ôćĺ- Ôťů YOU MUST ALWAYS SPEAK OUTPUT in your Agent communication style with the config `{communication_language}`
    22Ôćĺ
    23Ôćĺ### Role Reinforcement:
    24Ôćĺ
    25Ôćĺ- Ôťů You are a skill packager operating in Ferris Architect mode
    26Ôćĺ- Ôťů Precise file writing ÔÇö every file matches its template structure
    27Ôćĺ- Ôťů Hard halt on any write failure ÔÇö partial output is worse than no output
    28Ôćĺ
    29Ôćĺ### Step-Specific Rules:
    30Ôćĺ
    31Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Write ALL output files in correct directory structure
    32Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to modify compiled content ÔÇö step 06 produced the approved version
    33Ôćĺ- ­čĺČ Report each file written with path and size
    34Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Create directory structure before writing files
    35Ôćĺ
    36Ôćĺ## EXECUTION PROTOCOLS:
    37Ôćĺ
    38Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Create output directories, write all files
    39Ôćĺ- ­čĺż Store written_files list as workflow state
    40Ôćĺ- ­čôľ Auto-proceed to validation after all files written
    41Ôćĺ- ­čÜź HARD HALT on any individual file write failure
    42Ôćĺ
    43Ôćĺ## CONTEXT BOUNDARIES:
    44Ôćĺ
    45Ôćĺ- From step 06: skill_content (approved compiled SKILL.md)
    46Ôćĺ- From step 04: per_library_extractions[] for reference files
    47Ôćĺ- From step 05: integration_graph for integration pair references
    48Ôćĺ- From step 01: forge_tier, project info for metadata
    49Ôćĺ- This step produces: written_files[] (all output artifacts)
    50Ôćĺ
    51Ôćĺ## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
    52Ôćĺ
    53Ôćĺ**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise.
    54Ôćĺ
    55Ôćĺ### 1. Create Output Directories
    56Ôćĺ
    57ÔćĺCreate the directory structure:
    58Ôćĺ
    59Ôćĺ```
    60Ôćĺ{skills_output_folder}/{project_name}-stack/
    61ÔćĺÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ references/
    62ÔćĺÔöé   ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ integrations/
    63Ôćĺ```
    64Ôćĺ
    65Ôćĺ```
    66Ôćĺ{forge_data_folder}/{project_name}-stack/
    67Ôćĺ```
    68Ôćĺ
    69ÔćĺIf directories already exist, proceed (overwrite previous output).
    70Ôćĺ
    71Ôćĺ### 2. Write SKILL.md
    72Ôćĺ
    73ÔćĺWrite `{skills_output_folder}/{project_name}-stack/SKILL.md` with the approved skill_content from step 06.
    74Ôćĺ
    75Ôćĺ### 3. Write Per-Library Reference Files
    76Ôćĺ
    77ÔćĺFor each confirmed library, write `{skills_output_folder}/{project_name}-stack/references/{library_name}.md`:
    78Ôćĺ
    79ÔćĺLoad structure from `{stackSkillTemplate}` references section:
    80Ôćĺ- Library name, version from manifest (**in compose-mode**: version from source skill `metadata.json`)
    81Ôćĺ- Import count and file count (**in compose-mode**: export count from source skill metadata)
    82Ôćĺ- Key exports with signatures
    83Ôćĺ- Usage patterns with file:line citations (**in compose-mode**: usage patterns from source skill SKILL.md)
    84Ôćĺ- Confidence tier label
    85Ôćĺ
    86Ôćĺ### 4. Write Integration Pair Reference Files
    87Ôćĺ
    88ÔćĺFor each detected integration pair, write `{skills_output_folder}/{project_name}-stack/references/integrations/{libraryA}-{libraryB}.md`:
    89Ôćĺ
    90ÔćĺLoad structure from `{stackSkillTemplate}` integrations section:
    91Ôćĺ- Library pair and integration type
    92Ôćĺ- Co-import file count
    93Ôćĺ- Integration pattern description with file:line citations
    94Ôćĺ- Usage convention
    95Ôćĺ- Confidence tier label
    96Ôćĺ
    97Ôćĺ**If no integrations detected:** Skip this section (no files to write).
    98Ôćĺ
    99Ôćĺ### 5. Write context-snippet.md
   100Ôćĺ
   101ÔćĺWrite `{skills_output_folder}/{project_name}-stack/context-snippet.md`:
   102Ôćĺ
   103ÔćĺUse the Vercel-aligned indexed format targeting ~80-120 tokens:
   104Ôćĺ```
   105Ôćĺ[{project_name}-stack v{version}]|root: {skills_output_folder}/{project_name}-stack/
   106Ôćĺ|IMPORTANT: {project_name}-stack ÔÇö read SKILL.md before writing integration code. Do NOT rely on training data.
   107Ôćĺ|stack: {dep-1}@{v1}, {dep-2}@{v2}, {dep-3}@{v3}
   108Ôćĺ|integrations: {pattern-1}, {pattern-2}
   109Ôćĺ|gotchas: {1-2 most critical integration pitfalls}
   110Ôćĺ```
   111Ôćĺ
   112Ôćĺ### 6. Write metadata.json
   113Ôćĺ
   114ÔćĺWrite `{skills_output_folder}/{project_name}-stack/metadata.json`:
   115Ôćĺ
   116Ôćĺ```json
   117Ôćĺ{
   118Ôćĺ  "skill_type": "stack",
   119Ôćĺ  "name": "{project_name}-stack",
   120Ôćĺ  "version": "{primary_library_version or 1.0.0}",
   121Ôćĺ  "generation_date": "{current_date}",
   122Ôćĺ  "confidence_tier": "{tier}",
   123Ôćĺ  "library_count": N,
   124Ôćĺ  "integration_count": N,
   125Ôćĺ  "libraries": ["lib1", "lib2"],
   126Ôćĺ  "integration_pairs": [["lib1", "lib2"]],
   127Ôćĺ  "confidence_distribution": {"t1": N, "t1_low": N, "t2": N, "t3": N},
   128Ôćĺ  "spec_version": "1.3",
   129Ôćĺ  "source_authority": "{lowest authority among constituent skills: community if any source is community, internal if all are internal}",
   130Ôćĺ  "exports": []
   131Ôćĺ}
   132Ôćĺ```
   133Ôćĺ
   134Ôćĺ### 7. Write Forge Data Artifacts
   135Ôćĺ
   136ÔćĺWrite workspace artifacts to `{forge_data_folder}/{project_name}-stack/`:
   137Ôćĺ
   138Ôćĺ**provenance-map.json:**
   139Ôćĺ
   140Ôćĺ**In code-mode:**
   141Ôćĺ```json
   142Ôćĺ{
   143Ôćĺ  "libraries": {
   144Ôćĺ    "lib_name": {
   145Ôćĺ      "manifest_source": "package.json",
   146Ôćĺ      "extraction_method": "ast_bridge|source_reading|qmd_bridge",
   147Ôćĺ      "confidence": "T1|T1-low|T2",
   148Ôćĺ      "files_analyzed": N,
   149Ôćĺ      "exports_found": N
   150Ôćĺ    }
   151Ôćĺ  },
   152Ôćĺ  "integrations": {
   153Ôćĺ    "libA+libB": {
   154Ôćĺ      "detection_method": "co-import grep",
   155Ôćĺ      "co_import_files": N,
   156Ôćĺ      "type": "pattern_type"
   157Ôćĺ    }
   158Ôćĺ  }
   159Ôćĺ}
   160Ôćĺ```
   161Ôćĺ
   162Ôćĺ**In compose-mode:**
   163Ôćĺ```json
   164Ôćĺ{
   165Ôćĺ  "libraries": {
   166Ôćĺ    "lib_name": {
   167Ôćĺ      "source_skill_path": "skills/{skill_name}/",
   168Ôćĺ      "compose_source": "architecture_co_mention|inferred_from_shared_domain",
   169Ôćĺ      "confidence": "T1|T1-low|T2",
   170Ôćĺ      "source_skill_tier": "{original skill confidence tier}",
   171Ôćĺ      "exports_found": N
   172Ôćĺ    }
   173Ôćĺ  },
   174Ôćĺ  "integrations": {
   175Ôćĺ    "libA+libB": {
   176Ôćĺ      "detection_method": "architecture_co_mention|inferred_from_shared_domain",
   177Ôćĺ      "type": "pattern_type"
   178Ôćĺ    }
   179Ôćĺ  }
   180Ôćĺ}
   181Ôćĺ```
   182Ôćĺ
   183Ôćĺ**evidence-report.md:**
   184Ôćĺ- Extraction summary per library
   185Ôćĺ- Integration detection results per pair
   186Ôćĺ- Warnings and failures encountered
   187Ôćĺ- Confidence tier distribution
   188Ôćĺ
   189Ôćĺ### 8. Display Write Summary
   190Ôćĺ
   191Ôćĺ"**Output files written.**
   192Ôćĺ
   193Ôćĺ**Deliverables** ({skills_output_folder}/{project_name}-stack/):
   194Ôćĺ- Ôťô SKILL.md ({line_count} lines)
   195Ôćĺ- Ôťô context-snippet.md ({token_estimate} tokens)
   196Ôćĺ- Ôťô metadata.json
   197Ôćĺ- Ôťô references/ ÔÇö {lib_count} library files
   198Ôćĺ- Ôťô references/integrations/ ÔÇö {pair_count} integration files
   199Ôćĺ
   200Ôćĺ**Workspace** ({forge_data_folder}/{project_name}-stack/):
   201Ôćĺ- Ôťô provenance-map.json
   202Ôćĺ- Ôťô evidence-report.md
   203Ôćĺ
   204Ôćĺ**Total files written:** {total_count}
   205Ôćĺ
   206Ôćĺ**Proceeding to validation...**"
   207Ôćĺ
   208Ôćĺ### 9. Auto-Proceed to Next Step
   209Ôćĺ
   210ÔćĺLoad, read the full file and then execute `{nextStepFile}`.

   214Ôćĺ## ­čÜĘ SYSTEM SUCCESS/FAILURE METRICS

   219Ôćĺ- All workspace artifacts written to forge_data_folder
