     8Ôćĺ
     9Ôćĺ# Step 8: Validate Output
    10Ôćĺ
    11Ôćĺ## STEP GOAL:
    12Ôćĺ
    13ÔćĺValidate all written output files against their expected structure and verify confidence tier label completeness.
    14Ôćĺ
    15Ôćĺ## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES (READ FIRST):
    16Ôćĺ
    17Ôćĺ### Universal Rules:
    18Ôćĺ
    19Ôćĺ- ­čôľ CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action
    20Ôćĺ- ÔÜÖ´ŞĆ TOOL/SUBPROCESS FALLBACK: If any instruction references a subprocess, subagent, or tool you do not have access to, you MUST still achieve the outcome in your main context thread
    21Ôćĺ- Ôťů YOU MUST ALWAYS SPEAK OUTPUT in your Agent communication style with the config `{communication_language}`
    22Ôćĺ
    23Ôćĺ### Role Reinforcement:
    24Ôćĺ
    25Ôćĺ- Ôťů You are a quality gate operator in Ferris Architect mode
    26Ôćĺ- Ôťů Validation is advisory ÔÇö findings are warnings, not blockers
    27Ôćĺ- Ôťů Report findings accurately ÔÇö do not downplay or exaggerate
    28Ôćĺ
    29Ôćĺ### Step-Specific Rules:
    30Ôćĺ
    31Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Validate structure and completeness, not content quality
    32Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to modify any output files ÔÇö validation is read-only
    33Ôćĺ- ­čĺČ Report findings with specific file paths and issue descriptions
    34Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Advisory mode: always proceed to report regardless of findings
    35Ôćĺ
    36Ôćĺ## EXECUTION PROTOCOLS:
    37Ôćĺ
    38Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Check each deliverable file against its expected structure
    39Ôćĺ- ­čĺż Store validation_result as workflow state
    40Ôćĺ- ­čôľ Auto-proceed to report after validation complete
    41Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to halt on validation warnings (advisory only)
    42Ôćĺ
    43Ôćĺ## CONTEXT BOUNDARIES:
    44Ôćĺ
    45Ôćĺ- From step 07: written_files[] (all output artifacts with paths)
    46Ôćĺ- From step 01: forge_tier, project_name, skills_output_folder, forge_data_folder
    47Ôćĺ- This step produces: validation_result {status, findings[], warnings[]}
    48Ôćĺ- This is a quality gate ÔÇö findings inform the report but do not block it
    49Ôćĺ
    50Ôćĺ## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
    51Ôćĺ
    52Ôćĺ**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise.
    53Ôćĺ
    54Ôćĺ### 1. Verify File Existence
    55Ôćĺ
    56ÔćĺCheck that all expected files exist from written_files[]:
    57Ôćĺ
    58Ôćĺ**Deliverables** (`{skills_output_folder}/{project_name}-stack/`):
    59Ôćĺ- [ ] SKILL.md
    60Ôćĺ- [ ] context-snippet.md
    61Ôćĺ- [ ] metadata.json
    62Ôćĺ- [ ] references/ directory with per-library files
    63Ôćĺ- [ ] references/integrations/ directory with pair files (if integrations detected)
    64Ôćĺ
    65Ôćĺ**Workspace** (`{forge_data_folder}/{project_name}-stack/`):
    66Ôćĺ- [ ] provenance-map.json
    67Ôćĺ- [ ] evidence-report.md
    68Ôćĺ
    69ÔćĺRecord any missing files as **ERROR** findings.
    70Ôćĺ
    71Ôćĺ### 2. Check Tool Availability
    72Ôćĺ
    73ÔćĺRun: `npx skill-check -h`
    74Ôćĺ
    75Ôćĺ- If succeeds: Use skill-check for automated validation in sections 3, 9
    76Ôćĺ- If fails: Use manual fallback paths in those sections
    77Ôćĺ
    78Ôćĺ**Important:** Do not assume availability ÔÇö empirical check required.
    79Ôćĺ
    80Ôćĺ### 3. Validate SKILL.md via skill-check (if available)
    81Ôćĺ
    82Ôćĺ**If available**, run: `npx skill-check check <skill-dir> --fix --format json --no-security-scan`
    83Ôćĺ
    84ÔćĺThis validates frontmatter, description, body limits, links, formatting ÔÇö and auto-fixes deterministic issues. Parse JSON for `qualityScore`, `diagnostics[]`, `fixed[]`.
    85Ôćĺ
    86Ôćĺ**If `body.max_lines` reported**, prefer selective split: extract only the largest Tier 2 section(s) to `references/`, keeping Tier 1 content inline (inline passive context achieves 100% task accuracy vs 79% for on-demand retrieval). Fall back to `npx skill-check split-body <skill-dir> --write` if not feasible. Verify `#quick-start` and `#key-types` anchors still resolve after split. Then re-validate.
    87Ôćĺ
    88Ôćĺ**If unavailable**, perform manual frontmatter check:
    89Ôćĺ- [ ] Frontmatter present with `---` delimiters
    90Ôćĺ- [ ] `name` ÔÇö present, non-empty, lowercase alphanumeric + hyphens, 1-64 chars, matches `{project_name}-stack`
    91Ôćĺ- [ ] `description` ÔÇö present, non-empty, 1-1024 characters
    92Ôćĺ- [ ] No unknown fields ÔÇö only `name`, `description`, `license`, `compatibility`, `metadata`, `allowed-tools` permitted
    93Ôćĺ
    94ÔćĺRecord frontmatter violations as **WARNING** findings. Invalid frontmatter will fail `npx skills add` and `npx skill-check check`.
    95Ôćĺ
    96Ôćĺ### 4. Validate SKILL.md Body Structure
    97Ôćĺ
    98ÔćĺLoad `{stackSkillTemplate}` and verify SKILL.md contains expected sections:
    99Ôćĺ
   100Ôćĺ- [ ] Header with project name, library count, integration count, forge tier
   101Ôćĺ- [ ] Integration Patterns section (before per-library summaries)
   102Ôćĺ- [ ] Library Reference Index table
   103Ôćĺ- [ ] Per-Library Summaries section
   104Ôćĺ- [ ] Conventions section
   105Ôćĺ
   106ÔćĺRecord missing sections as **WARNING** findings.
   107Ôćĺ
   108Ôćĺ### 5. Validate metadata.json Fields
   109Ôćĺ
   110ÔćĺParse metadata.json and verify required fields:
   111Ôćĺ
   112Ôćĺ- [ ] `skill_type` equals "stack"
   113Ôćĺ- [ ] `name` matches `{project_name}-stack`
   114Ôćĺ- [ ] `version` and `generation_date` present
   115Ôćĺ- [ ] `confidence_tier` matches forge tier from step 01 (Quick/Forge/Forge+/Deep) ÔÇö in both code-mode and compose-mode, this is the forge tier; per-library inherited confidence is tracked separately in `confidence_distribution`
   116Ôćĺ- [ ] `library_count` matches actual reference files; `integration_count` matches pair files
   117Ôćĺ- [ ] `libraries` array present and non-empty
   118Ôćĺ- [ ] `confidence_distribution` object present with `t1`, `t1_low`, `t2`, `t3` keys (lowercase, matching template definition)
   119Ôćĺ
   120ÔćĺRecord mismatches as **WARNING** findings.
   121Ôćĺ
   122Ôćĺ### 6. Validate Reference File Completeness
   123Ôćĺ
   124ÔćĺFor each confirmed library, verify `references/{library}.md` contains: library name header, version from manifest (**in compose-mode**: version from source skill metadata), Key Exports section, Usage Patterns section.
   125Ôćĺ
   126ÔćĺFor each integration pair, verify `references/integrations/{libraryA}-{libraryB}.md` contains: integration pair header, type classification, Integration Pattern section, Key Files section.
   127Ôćĺ
   128ÔćĺRecord missing or incomplete files as **WARNING** findings.
   129Ôćĺ
   130Ôćĺ### 7. Validate Confidence Tier Labels
   131Ôćĺ
   132ÔćĺScan all output files for confidence tier coverage:
   133Ôćĺ
   134Ôćĺ- [ ] SKILL.md: each per-library summary and integration pair entry has a confidence label
   135Ôćĺ- [ ] Reference files: each has a confidence label in its header
   136Ôćĺ- [ ] metadata.json: confidence_distribution sums match library_count
   137Ôćĺ
   138ÔćĺRecord missing tier labels as **WARNING** findings.
   139Ôćĺ
   140Ôćĺ### 8. Validate context-snippet.md
   141Ôćĺ
   142ÔćĺVerify context-snippet.md follows Vercel-aligned indexed format:

    41Ôćĺ- ­čôľ Auto-proceed to next step after successful initialization
    42Ôćĺ- ­čÜź HALT with actionable error if minimum requirements not met
    43Ôćĺ
    44Ôćĺ## CONTEXT BOUNDARIES:
    45Ôćĺ
    46Ôćĺ- This is the first step ÔÇö no prior workflow state exists
    47Ôćĺ- Requires skills to have been generated by create-skill or quick-skill workflows
    48Ôćĺ- User provides architecture document path (required) and optional PRD/vision document path
    49Ôćĺ- No dependency on setup-forge or forge-tier ÔÇö this workflow operates on skill content only
    50Ôćĺ
    51Ôćĺ## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
    52Ôćĺ
    53Ôćĺ**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise.
    54Ôćĺ
    55Ôćĺ### 1. Accept Input Documents
    56Ôćĺ
    57Ôćĺ"**Verify Stack ÔÇö Feasibility Analysis**
    58Ôćĺ
    59ÔćĺPlease provide the following:
    60Ôćĺ1. **Architecture document path** (REQUIRED) ÔÇö your project's architecture doc
    61Ôćĺ2. **PRD or vision document path** (OPTIONAL) ÔÇö for requirements coverage analysis
    62Ôćĺ3. **Previous feasibility report path** (OPTIONAL) ÔÇö for delta comparison with a prior run (provide a backup copy)"
    63Ôćĺ
    64ÔćĺWait for user input.
    65Ôćĺ
    66Ôćĺ**Validate architecture document:**
    67Ôćĺ- Confirm the file exists and is readable
    68Ôćĺ- If missing or unreadable Ôćĺ "Architecture document not found at `{path}`. Provide a valid path."
    69Ôćĺ- HALT until a valid architecture document is provided
    70Ôćĺ
    71Ôćĺ**Validate PRD document (if provided):**
    72Ôćĺ- Confirm the file exists and is readable
    73Ôćĺ- If missing Ôćĺ "PRD document not found at `{path}`. Proceeding without PRD ÔÇö requirements pass will be skipped."
    74Ôćĺ- Store PRD availability as `prd_available: true|false`
    75Ôćĺ
    76Ôćĺ**Validate previous report (if provided):**
    77Ôćĺ- Confirm the file exists and is readable
    78Ôćĺ- **Collision check:** Resolve both the provided path and `{outputFile}` to their absolute form before comparing (do not rely on string equality alone). If they resolve to the same location, warn: "The previous report path points to the same location as the new report. This file will be overwritten during this run. Provide a path to a backup copy, or leave empty to skip delta comparison." HALT until resolved.
    79Ôćĺ- If missing Ôćĺ "Previous report not found at `{path}`. Proceeding without delta comparison."
    80Ôćĺ- Store as `previous_report: {path}` (or empty string if not provided)
    81Ôćĺ
    82Ôćĺ### 2. Scan Skills Folder
    83Ôćĺ
    84ÔćĺRead the `{skills_output_folder}` directory.
    85Ôćĺ
    86ÔćĺFor each subdirectory, check for the presence of `SKILL.md` and `metadata.json`.
    87Ôćĺ
    88Ôćĺ**For each valid skill directory, extract from metadata.json:**
    89Ôćĺ- `name` ÔÇö skill name
    90Ôćĺ- `language` ÔÇö primary language
    91Ôćĺ- `confidence_tier` ÔÇö Quick, Forge, Forge+, or Deep
    92Ôćĺ- `exports_documented` ÔÇö read from `stats.exports_documented` in metadata.json (count of documented exports)
    93Ôćĺ- `source_repo` or `source_root` ÔÇö original source repository
    94Ôćĺ
    95Ôćĺ**Build a skill inventory** as an internal list of all loaded skills with the fields above.
    96Ôćĺ
    97Ôćĺ**If a subdirectory lacks SKILL.md or metadata.json:**
    98Ôćĺ- Log: "Skipping `{dir_name}` ÔÇö missing SKILL.md or metadata.json"
    99Ôćĺ- Do not include in inventory
   100Ôćĺ
   101Ôćĺ### 3. Validate Minimum Requirements
   102Ôćĺ
   103Ôćĺ**Check skill count:**
   104Ôćĺ- At least 2 valid skills must exist (a stack requires multiple libraries)
   105Ôćĺ- If fewer than 2 Ôćĺ "**Cannot proceed.** Only {count} skill(s) found in `{skills_output_folder}`. A stack requires at least 2 skills. Generate more skills with [CS] Create Skill, [QS] Quick Skill, or [SS] Stack Skill, then re-run [VS]."
   106Ôćĺ- HALT workflow

   108Ôćĺ**Check forge_data_folder:**

   110Ôćĺ- If undefined or empty Ôćĺ "**Cannot proceed.** `forge_data_folder` is not configured in config.yaml. Re-run [SF] Setup Forge to initialize."
   111Ôćĺ- HALT workflow
   112Ôćĺ
   113Ôćĺ**Check architecture document:**

   115Ôćĺ- If not Ôćĺ HALT with error (should not reach here if section 1 validation passed)
   116Ôćĺ
   117Ôćĺ### 4. Create Feasibility Report
   118Ôćĺ
   119ÔćĺLoad `{reportTemplate}` and create the output file at `{outputFile}`.
   120Ôćĺ
   121Ôćĺ**Populate frontmatter:**

   128Ôćĺ
   129Ôćĺ### 5. Display Initialization Summary
   130Ôćĺ
   131Ôćĺ"**Stack Verification Initialized**
   132Ôćĺ
   133Ôćĺ| Field | Value |
   134Ôćĺ|-------|-------|

   139Ôćĺ
   140Ôćĺ**Skill Inventory:**

   142Ôćĺ| Skill | Language | Tier | Exports |

   145Ôćĺ
   146Ôćĺ**Proceeding to coverage analysis...**"
   147Ôćĺ
   148Ôćĺ### 6. Auto-Proceed to Next Step
   149Ôćĺ
   150ÔćĺLoad, read the full file and then execute `{nextStepFile}`.
   151Ôćĺ
   152Ôćĺ---
   153Ôćĺ
   154Ôćĺ## ­čÜĘ SYSTEM SUCCESS/FAILURE METRICS

     9Ôćĺ
    10Ôćĺ# Step 2: Technology Coverage Analysis
    11Ôćĺ
    12Ôćĺ## STEP GOAL:
    13Ôćĺ
    14ÔćĺVerify that a generated skill exists for every technology, library, or framework referenced in the architecture document. Produce a coverage matrix showing which technologies are covered and which are missing. Detect extra skills not referenced in the architecture.
    15Ôćĺ
    16Ôćĺ## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES (READ FIRST):
    17Ôćĺ
    18Ôćĺ### Universal Rules:
    19Ôćĺ
    20Ôćĺ- ­čôľ CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action
    21Ôćĺ- ­čöä CRITICAL: When loading next step with 'C', ensure entire file is read
    22Ôćĺ- ÔÜÖ´ŞĆ TOOL/SUBPROCESS FALLBACK: If any instruction references a subprocess, subagent, or tool you do not have access to, you MUST still achieve the outcome in your main context thread
    23Ôćĺ- Ôťů YOU MUST ALWAYS SPEAK OUTPUT in your Agent communication style with the config `{communication_language}`
    24Ôćĺ
    25Ôćĺ### Role Reinforcement:
    26Ôćĺ
    27Ôćĺ- Ôťů You are a stack verification analyst performing coverage analysis
    28Ôćĺ- Ôťů Every technology reference must trace to actual text in the architecture document
    29Ôćĺ- Ôťů Matching must be precise ÔÇö do not guess or infer technologies not explicitly mentioned
    30Ôćĺ
    31Ôćĺ### Step-Specific Rules:
    32Ôćĺ
    33Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Focus ONLY on technology-to-skill coverage mapping
    34Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to analyze API surfaces or integration compatibility ÔÇö that is Step 03
    35Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to evaluate requirements ÔÇö that is Step 04
    36Ôćĺ- ­čĺČ Coverage verdicts must be binary: Covered or Missing ÔÇö no ambiguity
    37Ôćĺ
    38Ôćĺ## EXECUTION PROTOCOLS:
    39Ôćĺ
    40Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Extract technology references from architecture document using {coveragePatternsData}
    41Ôćĺ- ­čĺż Build and display coverage matrix with Covered/Missing verdicts
    42Ôćĺ- ­čôľ Append Coverage Matrix section to {outputFile}
    43Ôćĺ- ­čÜź Only coverage mapping ÔÇö no integration analysis, no requirements checking
    44Ôćĺ
    45Ôćĺ## CONTEXT BOUNDARIES:
    46Ôćĺ
    47Ôćĺ- Available: Architecture document content, skill inventory from Step 01, coverage patterns data
    48Ôćĺ- Focus: Technology-to-skill matching only
    49Ôćĺ- Limits: Do not read SKILL.md in detail ÔÇö use metadata.json for name and source_repo matching only
    50Ôćĺ- Dependencies: Step 01 must have loaded skill inventory and validated architecture document
    51Ôćĺ
    52Ôćĺ## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
    53Ôćĺ
    54Ôćĺ**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise.
    55Ôćĺ
    56Ôćĺ### 1. Load Coverage Patterns
    57Ôćĺ
    58ÔćĺLoad `{coveragePatternsData}` for detection rules.
    59Ôćĺ
    60ÔćĺExtract: technology name patterns, section heading indicators, common aliases, and framework-to-library mappings.
    61Ôćĺ
    62Ôćĺ### 2. Extract Technology References
    63Ôćĺ
    64ÔćĺParse the architecture document for technology, library, and framework names.
    65Ôćĺ
    66Ôćĺ**Detection methods (apply in order):**
    67Ôćĺ
    68Ôćĺ**Section-based detection:**
    69Ôćĺ- Identify section headings that indicate technology listings (e.g., "Tech Stack", "Dependencies", "Technologies", "Libraries", layer-specific headings)
    70Ôćĺ- Extract technology names listed under these headings
    71Ôćĺ
    72Ôćĺ**Direct name matching:**
    73Ôćĺ- Scan the full document for names that match loaded skill names (case-insensitive)
    74Ôćĺ- Apply alias resolution from {coveragePatternsData} (e.g., "React" matches "react", "PostgreSQL" matches "postgres")
    75Ôćĺ
    76Ôćĺ**Contextual detection:**
    77Ôćĺ- Identify technology names mentioned in prose alongside architectural descriptions
    78Ôćĺ- Look for version-pinned references (e.g., "Express v4", "Tailwind CSS 3.x")
    79Ôćĺ
    80Ôćĺ**Build a deduplicated list** of all referenced technologies with the document section where each was found.
    81Ôćĺ
    82Ôćĺ### 3. Cross-Reference Against Skills
    83Ôćĺ
    84ÔćĺFor each referenced technology in the list:
    85Ôćĺ
    86Ôćĺ**Check if a matching skill exists** in the skill inventory from Step 01.
    87Ôćĺ- Match by skill name (case-insensitive)
    88Ôćĺ- Match by alias from {coveragePatternsData}
    89Ôćĺ- Match by `source_repo` or `source_root` field in metadata.json if skill name differs from technology name
    90Ôćĺ
    91Ôćĺ**Assign verdict:**
    92Ôćĺ- **Covered** ÔÇö a matching skill exists in the inventory
    93Ôćĺ- **Missing** ÔÇö no matching skill found
    94Ôćĺ
    95ÔćĺBuild the coverage matrix as a structured table.
    96Ôćĺ
    97Ôćĺ### 4. Detect Extra Skills
    98Ôćĺ
    99ÔćĺCheck if any skills in the inventory are NOT referenced in the architecture document.
   100Ôćĺ
   101Ôćĺ**For each extra skill:**
   102Ôćĺ- Mark as **Extra** (informational ÔÇö not an error)
   103Ôćĺ- Note: "Skill `{skill_name}` exists but is not referenced in the architecture document"
   104Ôćĺ
   105ÔćĺExtra skills are informational only. They do not affect the coverage verdict.
   106Ôćĺ
   107Ôćĺ### 5. Display Coverage Results
   108Ôćĺ
   109Ôćĺ"**Pass 1: Technology Coverage**
   110Ôćĺ
   111Ôćĺ| Technology | Source Section | Skill Match | Verdict |
   112Ôćĺ|------------|---------------|-------------|---------|
   113Ôćĺ| {tech_name} | {section_heading} | {skill_name or 'ÔÇö'} | {Covered / Missing} |
   114Ôćĺ
   115Ôćĺ**Coverage: {covered_count}/{total_count} ({percentage}%)**
   116Ôćĺ
   117Ôćĺ{IF 100% coverage AND no Extra skills:}
   118Ôćĺ**All referenced technologies have a matching skill. No extra skills detected.**
   119Ôćĺ
   120Ôćĺ{IF any Missing:}
   121Ôćĺ**Missing Skills ÔÇö Action Required:**
   122Ôćĺ{For each missing technology:}
   123Ôćĺ- `{tech_name}` Ôćĺ Run **[CS] Create Skill** or **[QS] Quick Skill** for `{tech_name}`, then re-run **[VS]**
   124Ôćĺ
   125Ôćĺ{IF any Extra:}
   126Ôćĺ**Extra Skills (informational):**
   127Ôćĺ{For each extra skill:}
   128Ôćĺ- `{skill_name}` ÔÇö not referenced in architecture document"
   129Ôćĺ
   130Ôćĺ### 6. Append to Report
   131Ôćĺ
   132ÔćĺWrite the **Coverage Matrix** section to `{outputFile}`:
   133Ôćĺ- Include the full coverage table
   134Ôćĺ- Include coverage percentage
   135Ôćĺ- Include missing skill recommendations
   136Ôćĺ- Include extra skills list
   137Ôćĺ- Update frontmatter: append `'step-02-coverage'` to `stepsCompleted`, set `coverage_percentage`
   138Ôćĺ
   139Ôćĺ### 7. Auto-Proceed to Next Step
   140Ôćĺ
   141Ôćĺ{IF coverage_percentage is 0%:}
   142Ôćĺ"**ÔÜá´ŞĆ 0% coverage ÔÇö no matching skills found for any referenced technology.** All subsequent analysis (integration, requirements) will be vacuous and produce empty tables.
   143Ôćĺ
   144Ôćĺ**Recommended:** Generate skills with [CS] or [QS] for your architecture technologies, then re-run [VS].
   145Ôćĺ
   146Ôćĺ**Select:** [X] Halt workflow (recommended) | [C] Continue anyway"
   147Ôćĺ
   148Ôćĺ- IF X: "**Workflow halted.** Generate skills and re-run [VS] when ready." ÔÇö END workflow
   149Ôćĺ- IF C: "**Continuing with 0% coverage ÔÇö results will be limited.**"
   150Ôćĺ
   151Ôćĺ  Load, read the full file and then execute `{nextStepFile}`.
   152Ôćĺ
   153Ôćĺ{IF coverage_percentage is NOT 0%:}
   154Ôćĺ"**Proceeding to integration analysis...**"
   155Ôćĺ
   156ÔćĺLoad, read the full file and then execute `{nextStepFile}`.
   157Ôćĺ
   158Ôćĺ---
   159Ôćĺ
   160Ôćĺ## ­čÜĘ SYSTEM SUCCESS/FAILURE METRICS
   161Ôćĺ
   162Ôćĺ### Ôťů SUCCESS:
   163Ôćĺ
   164Ôćĺ- Coverage patterns loaded from {coveragePatternsData}
   165Ôćĺ- All technology references extracted from architecture document with section citations
   166Ôćĺ- Every referenced technology cross-referenced against skill inventory
   167Ôćĺ- Coverage matrix displayed with binary Covered/Missing verdicts
   168Ôćĺ- Extra skills detected and reported as informational
   169Ôćĺ- Missing skills have actionable recommendations (run [CS] or [QS])
   170Ôćĺ- Coverage Matrix section appended to {outputFile}
   171Ôćĺ- Auto-proceeded to step 03
   172Ôćĺ
   173Ôćĺ### ÔŁî SYSTEM FAILURE:
   174Ôćĺ
   175Ôćĺ- Inventing technologies not mentioned in the architecture document
   176Ôćĺ- Ambiguous verdicts (anything other than Covered or Missing)
   177Ôćĺ- Analyzing API surfaces or skill content in detail (that is Step 03)
   178Ôćĺ- Not providing actionable recommendations for missing skills

   180Ôćĺ
   181Ôćĺ**Master Rule:** Skipping steps, optimizing sequences, or not following exact instructions is FORBIDDEN and constitutes SYSTEM FAILURE.

     9Ôćĺ
    10Ôćĺ# Step 3: Integration Verification
    11Ôćĺ
    12Ôćĺ## STEP GOAL:
    13Ôćĺ
    14ÔćĺCross-reference API surfaces between library pairs that the architecture document claims work together. For each integration pair, verify language compatibility, protocol alignment, type compatibility, and documentation cross-references. Produce an evidence-backed verdict for each integration.
    15Ôćĺ
    16Ôćĺ## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES (READ FIRST):
    17Ôćĺ
    18Ôćĺ### Universal Rules:
    19Ôćĺ
    20Ôćĺ- ­čôľ CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action
    21Ôćĺ- ­čöä CRITICAL: When loading next step with 'C', ensure entire file is read
    22Ôćĺ- ÔÜÖ´ŞĆ TOOL/SUBPROCESS FALLBACK: If any instruction references a subprocess, subagent, or tool you do not have access to, you MUST still achieve the outcome in your main context thread
    23Ôćĺ- Ôťů YOU MUST ALWAYS SPEAK OUTPUT in your Agent communication style with the config `{communication_language}`
    24Ôćĺ
    25Ôćĺ### Role Reinforcement:
    26Ôćĺ
    27Ôćĺ- Ôťů You are a stack verification analyst performing integration feasibility analysis
    28Ôćĺ- Ôťů Every verdict must cite evidence from the actual skill content ÔÇö no speculation
    29Ôćĺ- Ôťů Apply the verification protocol strictly ÔÇö do not skip compatibility checks
    30Ôćĺ
    31Ôćĺ### Step-Specific Rules:
    32Ôćĺ
    33Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Focus ONLY on integration pair verification using skill API surfaces
    34Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to evaluate requirements coverage ÔÇö that is Step 04
    35Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to parse Mermaid diagrams ÔÇö use prose-based co-mention analysis only
    36Ôćĺ- ­čĺČ Every verdict MUST include evidence citations from the skills
    37Ôćĺ
    38Ôćĺ## EXECUTION PROTOCOLS:
    39Ôćĺ
    40Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Extract integration claims from architecture document prose
    41Ôćĺ- ­čĺż Load skill API surfaces and cross-reference each integration pair
    42Ôćĺ- ­čôľ Append Integration Verdicts section to {outputFile}
    43Ôćĺ- ­čÜź Only integration analysis ÔÇö no requirements checking, no synthesis
    44Ôćĺ
    45Ôćĺ## CONTEXT BOUNDARIES:
    46Ôćĺ
    47Ôćĺ- Available: Architecture document content, skill SKILL.md and metadata.json files, integration verification rules
    48Ôćĺ- Focus: Pairwise integration compatibility between skills
    49Ôćĺ- Limits: Only verify pairs explicitly claimed in the architecture document ÔÇö do not invent integration relationships
    50Ôćĺ- Dependencies: Step 01 (skill inventory), Step 02 (coverage matrix ÔÇö only verify skills that exist)
    51Ôćĺ
    52Ôćĺ## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
    53Ôćĺ
    54Ôćĺ**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise.
    55Ôćĺ
    56Ôćĺ### 1. Load Integration Verification Rules
    57Ôćĺ
    58ÔćĺLoad `{integrationRulesData}` for the cross-reference verification protocol.
    59Ôćĺ
    60ÔćĺExtract: verification checks (language boundary, protocol compatibility, type compatibility, documentation cross-reference), verdict criteria, and evidence requirements.
    61Ôćĺ
    62Ôćĺ### 2. Extract Integration Claims
    63Ôćĺ
    64ÔćĺParse the architecture document for statements describing two or more technologies working together.
    65Ôćĺ
    66Ôćĺ**Detection method ÔÇö prose-based co-mention analysis:**
    67Ôćĺ- Identify sentences or paragraphs where two or more technology names appear together
    68Ôćĺ- Look for integration verbs: "connects to", "communicates with", "wraps", "extends", "consumes", "produces", "bridges", "integrates with", "sits between"
    69Ôćĺ- Look for data flow descriptions: "{A} sends data to {B}", "{A} results are consumed by {B}"
    70Ôćĺ- Look for layer boundary descriptions: "{A} at the API layer connects to {B} at the data layer"
    71Ôćĺ
    72Ôćĺ**CRITICAL:** Do NOT parse Mermaid diagram syntax. Use only prose text for co-mention detection.
    73Ôćĺ
    74Ôćĺ**Build integration pairs list:**
    75Ôćĺ- Each pair: `{library_a, library_b, architectural_context}`
    76Ôćĺ- `architectural_context`: the quoted text or paraphrased description of their relationship
    77Ôćĺ
    78Ôćĺ**Filter:** Only include pairs where BOTH libraries have a corresponding skill (Covered in Step 02). Skip pairs involving Missing skills ÔÇö they cannot be verified.
    79Ôćĺ
    80Ôćĺ### 3. Load Skill API Surfaces
    81Ôćĺ
    82ÔćĺFor each library in an integration pair, load the skill artifacts:
    83Ôćĺ
    84Ôćĺ**From SKILL.md, extract:**
    85Ôćĺ- Exported functions and their signatures
    86Ôćĺ- Exported types, interfaces, and classes
    87Ôćĺ- Protocol indicators (HTTP, gRPC, WebSocket, message queue, file I/O, IPC)
    88Ôćĺ- Data format indicators (JSON, protobuf, CSV, binary, streaming)
    89Ôćĺ
    90Ôćĺ**From metadata.json, extract:**
    91Ôćĺ- `language` ÔÇö primary programming language
    92Ôćĺ- `exports` ÔÇö export count and names
    93Ôćĺ- `confidence_tier` ÔÇö extraction confidence level
    94Ôćĺ
    95ÔćĺStore loaded API surfaces for cross-referencing.
    96Ôćĺ
    97Ôćĺ### 4. Cross-Reference Each Integration Pair
    98Ôćĺ
    99ÔćĺFor each integration pair `{library_a, library_b}`, apply the verification protocol from `{integrationRulesData}`:
   100Ôćĺ
   101Ôćĺ**Check 1 ÔÇö Language Boundary:**
   102Ôćĺ- Same language Ôćĺ compatible
   103Ôćĺ- Different languages Ôćĺ check for FFI, IPC, or network protocol bridge
   104Ôćĺ- If no bridge mechanism documented Ôćĺ flag as risk
   105Ôćĺ
   106Ôćĺ**Check 2 ÔÇö Protocol Compatibility:**
   107Ôćĺ- Both use same protocol (e.g., both HTTP) Ôćĺ compatible
   108Ôćĺ- Complementary protocols (e.g., HTTP client + HTTP server) Ôćĺ compatible
   109Ôćĺ- Incompatible protocols with no adapter Ôćĺ flag as risk
   110Ôćĺ
   111Ôćĺ**Check 3 ÔÇö Type Compatibility:**
   112Ôćĺ- Shared types or compatible serialization formats Ôćĺ compatible
   113Ôćĺ- Incompatible type systems with no conversion layer Ôćĺ flag as risk
   114Ôćĺ
   115Ôćĺ**Check 4 ÔÇö Documentation Cross-Reference:**
   116Ôćĺ- Skill A mentions skill B's library (or vice versa) Ôćĺ strong evidence
   117Ôćĺ- Neither skill mentions the other Ôćĺ weak evidence (plausible but unverified)
   118Ôćĺ
   119Ôćĺ**Assign verdict per pair:**
   120Ôćĺ- **Verified** ÔÇö all checks pass with evidence from both skills
   121Ôćĺ- **Plausible** ÔÇö checks pass but evidence is indirect or incomplete
   122Ôćĺ- **Risky** ÔÇö one or more checks flag compatibility concerns
   123Ôćĺ- **Blocked** ÔÇö fundamental incompatibility detected (language barrier with no bridge, incompatible protocols)
   124Ôćĺ
   125Ôćĺ**Each verdict MUST include:**
   126Ôćĺ- Which checks passed and which flagged
   127Ôćĺ- Evidence citations: specific exports, types, or protocol references from the skills
   128Ôćĺ
   129Ôćĺ### 5. Display Integration Results
   130Ôćĺ
   131Ôćĺ"**Pass 2: Integration Verification**
   132Ôćĺ
   133Ôćĺ| Library A | Library B | Context | Verdict | Evidence |
   134Ôćĺ|-----------|-----------|---------|---------|----------|
   135Ôćĺ| {lib_a} | {lib_b} | {brief context} | {Verified/Plausible/Risky/Blocked} | {key evidence} |
   136Ôćĺ
   137Ôćĺ**Summary:** {verified_count} Verified, {plausible_count} Plausible, {risky_count} Risky, {blocked_count} Blocked
   138Ôćĺ
   139Ôćĺ{IF zero integration pairs found:}
   140Ôćĺ**No integration claims detected in the architecture document prose.** Ensure your architecture document describes relationships between technologies in text form (not exclusively in Mermaid diagrams). Coverage-only analysis was performed.
   141Ôćĺ
   142Ôćĺ{IF any Risky:}
   143Ôćĺ**Risky Integrations ÔÇö Recommendations:**
   144Ôćĺ{For each risky pair:}
   145Ôćĺ- `{lib_a}` Ôćö `{lib_b}`: {specific concern}. **Recommendation:** {prescriptive action}
   146Ôćĺ
   147Ôćĺ{IF any Blocked:}
   148Ôćĺ**Blocked Integrations ÔÇö Action Required:**
   149Ôćĺ{For each blocked pair:}
   150Ôćĺ- `{lib_a}` Ôćö `{lib_b}`: {fundamental incompatibility}. **Recommendation:** {prescriptive action}"
   151Ôćĺ
   152Ôćĺ### 6. Append to Report
   153Ôćĺ
   154ÔćĺWrite the **Integration Verdicts** section to `{outputFile}`:
   155Ôćĺ- Include the full integration verdicts table with evidence
   156Ôćĺ- Include recommendations for Risky and Blocked pairs
   157Ôćĺ- Update frontmatter: append `'step-03-integrations'` to `stepsCompleted`
   158Ôćĺ- Set `integrations_verified`, `integrations_plausible`, `integrations_risky`, `integrations_blocked` counts
   159Ôćĺ
   160Ôćĺ### 7. Auto-Proceed to Next Step
   161Ôćĺ
   162Ôćĺ**Early halt guard:** If ALL integration pairs are Blocked, present: "**All integrations are Blocked** ÔÇö fundamental incompatibilities detected across all library pairs. Remaining analysis will produce limited value. **[X] Halt workflow (recommended)** | **[C] Continue anyway**" ÔÇö wait for user input. If X: halt with: "**Workflow halted ÔÇö all integrations blocked.** Integration Verdicts saved to `{outputFile}`. Run **[VS]** after applying architectural changes. **Blocked integrations:** {list each blocked pair with reason}." If C: continue.
   163Ôćĺ
   164Ôćĺ"**Proceeding to requirements verification...**"
   165Ôćĺ
   166ÔćĺLoad, read the full file and then execute `{nextStepFile}`.
   167Ôćĺ
   168Ôćĺ---
   169Ôćĺ
   170Ôćĺ## ­čÜĘ SYSTEM SUCCESS/FAILURE METRICS
   171Ôćĺ
   172Ôćĺ### Ôťů SUCCESS:
   173Ôćĺ
   174Ôćĺ- Integration rules loaded from {integrationRulesData}
   175Ôćĺ- Integration claims extracted from architecture document prose (not Mermaid diagrams)
   176Ôćĺ- Skill API surfaces loaded for each library in integration pairs

   180Ôćĺ- Integration Verdicts section appended to {outputFile}

   183Ôćĺ### ÔŁî SYSTEM FAILURE:

    14Ôćĺ## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES (READ FIRST):
    15Ôćĺ
    16Ôćĺ### Universal Rules:

    18Ôćĺ- ­čôľ CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action
    19Ôćĺ- ÔÜÖ´ŞĆ TOOL/SUBPROCESS FALLBACK: If any instruction references a subprocess, subagent, or tool you do not have access to, you MUST still achieve the outcome in your main context thread

    59Ôćĺ**Extract metrics from frontmatter:** Read `skills_analyzed`, `coverage_percentage`, `integrations_verified`, `integrations_plausible`, `integrations_risky`, `integrations_blocked`, `requirements_fulfilled`, `requirements_partial`, `requirements_not_addressed`, `requirements_pass`, `overall_verdict`, and `recommendation_count`. Use these values for the summary table and next steps below.

    61Ôćĺ### 2. Present Summary

   122Ôćĺ### 4. Present Next Steps

   136Ôćĺ
   137Ôćĺ**Required actions:**
   138Ôćĺ{List the specific recommendations from Step 05 synthesis}
   139Ôćĺ
   140Ôćĺ**After addressing these items:** Re-run **[VS] Verify Stack** to confirm resolution, then proceed to **[RA]**."
   141Ôćĺ
   142Ôćĺ**IF NOT FEASIBLE:**
   143Ôćĺ"**Critical blockers must be resolved.** The stack cannot support the architecture as described.
   144Ôćĺ
   145Ôćĺ**Critical actions:**
   146Ôćĺ{List the blocked integration recommendations and missing skill actions from Step 05}
   147Ôćĺ
   148Ôćĺ**After resolving blockers:** Re-run **[VS] Verify Stack**. Repeat until verdict improves to FEASIBLE or CONDITIONALLY FEASIBLE."
   149Ôćĺ
   150Ôćĺ### 5. Present Menu
   151Ôćĺ
   152ÔćĺDisplay: "**[R] Review full report** | **[X] Exit verification**"
   153Ôćĺ
   154Ôćĺ#### Menu Handling Logic:
   155Ôćĺ
   156Ôćĺ- **IF R:** Walk through the report section by section, presenting each section's content from {outputFile} in a readable format. After completing the walkthrough, redisplay the menu. (Note: the R walkthrough loop terminates only when the user selects X.)
   157Ôćĺ- **IF X:** "**Feasibility report saved to:** `{outputFile}`
   158Ôćĺ
   159ÔćĺRe-run **[VS] Verify Stack** anytime after making changes to your skills or architecture document.
   160Ôćĺ
   161Ôćĺ**Verification workflow complete.**"
   162Ôćĺ
   163Ôćĺ#### EXECUTION RULES:
   164Ôćĺ
   165Ôćĺ- ALWAYS halt and wait for user input after presenting the menu
   166Ôćĺ- R may be selected multiple times ÔÇö always walk through the full report
   167Ôćĺ- X ends the workflow
   168Ôćĺ
   169Ôćĺ## CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE
   170Ôćĺ
   171ÔćĺThis is the final step of the verify-stack workflow. When the user selects X, the workflow is complete. The feasibility report at `{outputFile}` contains the full analysis: Coverage Matrix, Integration Verdicts, Requirements Coverage, and Synthesis & Recommendations.
   172Ôćĺ
   173Ôćĺ---
   174Ôćĺ
   175Ôćĺ## ­čÜĘ SYSTEM SUCCESS/FAILURE METRICS
   176Ôćĺ
   177Ôćĺ### Ôťů SUCCESS:
   178Ôćĺ
   179Ôćĺ- Complete report loaded and verified for section completeness
   180Ôćĺ- Overall verdict displayed prominently with key metrics
   181Ôćĺ- Delta from previous run shown if applicable

   183Ôćĺ- Next steps match the verdict exactly (FEASIBLE/CONDITIONALLY FEASIBLE/NOT FEASIBLE)
   184Ôćĺ- Menu presented with R and X options
   185Ôćĺ- Report walkthrough available on R selection
   186Ôćĺ- Workflow exits cleanly on X with saved file path
   187Ôćĺ
   188Ôćĺ### ÔŁî SYSTEM FAILURE:
   189Ôćĺ
   190Ôćĺ- Discovering new findings or changing verdicts in this step
   191Ôćĺ- Modifying the report content
   192Ôćĺ- Next step recommendations that do not match the verdict
   193Ôćĺ- Not presenting the menu
   194Ôćĺ- Not displaying the report save path on exit

1Ôćĺ---
     2Ôćĺname: 'step-05-synthesize'
     3Ôćĺdescription: 'Produce overall feasibility verdict, prescriptive recommendations, and diff from previous run'
     4Ôćĺ
     5ÔćĺnextStepFile: './step-06-report.md'
     6ÔćĺoutputFile: '{forge_data_folder}/feasibility-report-{project_name}.md'
     7Ôćĺ---
     8Ôćĺ
     9Ôćĺ# Step 5: Synthesize Verdict
    10Ôćĺ
    11Ôćĺ## STEP GOAL:
    12Ôćĺ
    13ÔćĺCalculate the overall feasibility verdict based on all three analysis passes, generate prescriptive recommendations for every non-verified finding, check for a previous feasibility report to produce a delta, and compile the synthesis section of the report.
    14Ôćĺ
    15Ôćĺ## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES (READ FIRST):
    16Ôćĺ
    17Ôćĺ### Universal Rules:
    18Ôćĺ
    19Ôćĺ- ­čôľ CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action
    20Ôćĺ- ­čöä CRITICAL: When loading next step with 'C', ensure entire file is read
    21Ôćĺ- ÔÜÖ´ŞĆ TOOL/SUBPROCESS FALLBACK: If any instruction references a subprocess, subagent, or tool you do not have access to, you MUST still achieve the outcome in your main context thread
    22Ôćĺ- Ôťů YOU MUST ALWAYS SPEAK OUTPUT in your Agent communication style with the config `{communication_language}`
    23Ôćĺ
    24Ôćĺ### Role Reinforcement:
    25Ôćĺ
    26Ôćĺ- Ôťů You are a stack verification analyst delivering the final synthesis
    27Ôćĺ- Ôťů The overall verdict must follow the decision logic exactly ÔÇö no subjectivity
    28Ôćĺ- Ôťů Every recommendation must be specific and actionable ÔÇö not vague advice
    29Ôćĺ
    30Ôćĺ### Step-Specific Rules:
    31Ôćĺ
    32Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Focus ONLY on synthesizing findings from Steps 02-04 into a verdict
    33Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to discover new findings or re-analyze skills
    34Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to present the final report to the user ÔÇö that is Step 06
    35Ôćĺ- ­čĺČ Recommendations must name specific tools, libraries, or actions
    36Ôćĺ
    37Ôćĺ## EXECUTION PROTOCOLS:
    38Ôćĺ
    39Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Apply verdict decision logic to produce FEASIBLE / CONDITIONALLY FEASIBLE / NOT FEASIBLE
    40Ôćĺ- ­čĺż Append Synthesis & Recommendations section to {outputFile}
    41Ôćĺ- ­čôľ Check for previous report and generate delta if found
    42Ôćĺ- ­čÜź Only synthesis ÔÇö no new analysis, no final presentation
    43Ôćĺ
    44Ôćĺ## CONTEXT BOUNDARIES:
    45Ôćĺ
    46Ôćĺ- Available: Coverage matrix (Step 02), integration verdicts (Step 03), requirements coverage (Step 04 if applicable)
    47Ôćĺ- Focus: Verdict calculation, recommendation generation, delta comparison
    48Ôćĺ- Limits: Do not present the full report ÔÇö that is Step 06
    49Ôćĺ- Dependencies: Steps 02-03 must be complete; Step 04 completed or skipped
    50Ôćĺ
    51Ôćĺ## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
    52Ôćĺ
    53Ôćĺ**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise.
    54Ôćĺ
    55Ôćĺ### 1. Calculate Overall Verdict
    56Ôćĺ
    57ÔćĺApply the following decision logic using findings from all completed passes:
    58Ôćĺ
    59Ôćĺ**Evaluate in order ÔÇö the first matching condition wins. Do not continue once a verdict is determined.**
    60Ôćĺ
    61Ôćĺ**NOT FEASIBLE (evaluate first):**
    62Ôćĺ- Any integration is **Blocked** Ôćĺ overall verdict is NOT FEASIBLE
    63Ôćĺ- Rationale: a blocked integration represents a fundamental architectural incompatibility. Also note in the rationale any co-occurring Missing skills or Risky integrations so the user understands the full set of problems
    64Ôćĺ
    65Ôćĺ**CONDITIONALLY FEASIBLE (evaluate second):**
    66ÔćĺIf ANY of the following apply, the verdict is CONDITIONALLY FEASIBLE. Include ALL matching conditions in the rationale:
    67Ôćĺ- Any technology is **Missing** from coverage (no skill exists)
    68Ôćĺ- Any integration is **Risky** (but none Blocked)
    69Ôćĺ- Requirements have any **Not Addressed** items
    70Ôćĺ- Requirements have any **Partially Fulfilled** items (but none Not Addressed)
    71Ôćĺ- Rationale: the stack can work but has gaps, risks, or unverified assumptions that must be addressed
    72Ôćĺ
    73Ôćĺ**FEASIBLE (evaluate last):**
    74Ôćĺ- Coverage is 100% (no Missing skills) AND all integrations are Verified or Plausible AND requirements are all Fulfilled (or requirements pass was skipped) AND no Blocked or Risky integrations Ôćĺ overall verdict is FEASIBLE
    75Ôćĺ- Rationale: the stack can support the architecture as described ÔÇö all requirements fully fulfilled
    76Ôćĺ
    77Ôćĺ**Post-verdict: Zero integration pairs guard (apply after ANY verdict):**
    78ÔćĺIf zero integration pairs were extracted (all four integration counts are 0) AND the architecture document references 2+ technologies:
    79Ôćĺ- If the verdict was FEASIBLE, override to CONDITIONALLY FEASIBLE
    80Ôćĺ- Regardless of verdict, append this note to the rationale: "No integration claims were found in the architecture document prose. Manual review recommended to confirm that technology relationships are not documented exclusively in diagrams or implied without explicit co-mention."
    81Ôćĺ
    82ÔćĺStore the verdict for use in the report.
    83Ôćĺ
    84Ôćĺ### 2. Generate Prescriptive Recommendations
    85Ôćĺ
    86ÔćĺFor each non-verified finding across all passes, generate an actionable next step:
    87Ôćĺ
    88Ôćĺ**Missing skill (from Step 02):**
    89Ôćĺ- "Run **[CS] Create Skill** or **[QS] Quick Skill** for `{library_name}`, then re-run **[VS]** to verify coverage."
    90Ôćĺ
    91Ôćĺ**Risky integration (from Step 03):**
    92Ôćĺ- If protocol mismatch Ôćĺ "Consider adding a bridge layer between `{lib_a}` and `{lib_b}` (e.g., HTTP adapter, message queue). Document the bridge in the architecture."
    93Ôćĺ- If type incompatibility Ôćĺ "Add a serialization/conversion layer between `{lib_a}` and `{lib_b}` to resolve the type mismatch identified in their API surfaces."
    94Ôćĺ- If weak evidence Ôćĺ "Add cross-references between `{lib_a}` and `{lib_b}` skills by re-running **[CS]** with integration context."
    95Ôćĺ
    96Ôćĺ**Blocked integration (from Step 03):**
    97Ôćĺ- If language barrier Ôćĺ "Replace `{lib_a}` with a `{lib_b_language}`-compatible alternative, or introduce an IPC/FFI bridge. Redesign the integration path in the architecture document."
    98Ôćĺ- If fundamental incompatibility Ôćĺ "Replace `{blocked_lib}` with an alternative that is compatible with `{other_lib}` in the same domain, or redesign the integration path in the architecture document."
    99Ôćĺ
   100Ôćĺ**Not Addressed requirement (from Step 04):**
   101Ôćĺ- "No library in the stack covers `{requirement}`. Evaluate `{category}` libraries that provide this capability, generate a skill, then re-run **[VS]**."
   102Ôćĺ
   103Ôćĺ**Partially Fulfilled requirement (from Step 04):**
   104Ôćĺ- "Gap in `{requirement}`: {what_is_missing}. Consider extending `{contributing_skill}` or adding a dedicated library."
   105Ôćĺ
   106Ôćĺ**Zero integration pairs (from Step 03):**
   107Ôćĺ- If zero integration pairs were found AND the architecture references 2+ technologies: "No integration claims were found in the architecture document prose. Add explicit prose descriptions of how your technologies interact (not only in diagrams), then re-run **[VS]** to verify integrations."
   108Ôćĺ
   109Ôćĺ### 3. Check for Previous Report
   110Ôćĺ
   111ÔćĺCheck if a `previous_report` path was recorded in the current report's frontmatter. Since the current workflow run overwrites the report starting in Step 01, the delta feature requires the user to have saved a copy before re-running [VS].
   112Ôćĺ
   113Ôćĺ**Note:** The delta feature is only available when the user has manually backed up a prior report and provided the path. To enable delta comparisons, instruct the user to copy their feasibility report (e.g., `feasibility-report-{project_name}-v1.md`) before re-running [VS], then provide the backup path when prompted in Step 01.
   114Ôćĺ
   115Ôćĺ**If a previous report is found:**
   116Ôćĺ- Load its verdict, coverage percentage, and integration verdicts
   117Ôćĺ- Generate a delta comparison:
   118Ôćĺ  - **Improved items:** findings that were Risky/Blocked/Missing and are now Verified/Covered
   119Ôćĺ  - **Regressed items:** findings that were Verified/Covered and are now Risky/Blocked/Missing
   120Ôćĺ  - **New items:** findings not present in the previous report
   121Ôćĺ  - **Unchanged items:** count of findings with the same verdict
   122Ôćĺ
   123Ôćĺ**If no previous report found:**
   124Ôćĺ- Note: "First verification run ÔÇö no delta available."
   125Ôćĺ
   126Ôćĺ### 4. Compile Synthesis Section
   127Ôćĺ
   128ÔćĺAssemble the following for the report:
   129Ôćĺ
   130Ôćĺ**Overall verdict** with rationale citing the decision logic.
   131Ôćĺ
   132Ôćĺ**Recommendation list** ordered by priority (count total recommendations as `recommendation_count` ÔÇö persist this count to `{outputFile}` frontmatter for use in step-06):
   133Ôćĺ1. Blocked integrations (if any)
   134Ôćĺ2. Missing skills
   135Ôćĺ3. Risky integrations
   136Ôćĺ4. Not Addressed requirements
   137Ôćĺ5. Partially Fulfilled requirements
   138Ôćĺ
   139Ôćĺ**Delta from previous run** (if applicable):
   140Ôćĺ- Improved, regressed, new, unchanged counts
   141Ôćĺ- Specific items that changed
   142Ôćĺ
   143Ôćĺ**Suggested next workflow:**
   144Ôćĺ- FEASIBLE Ôćĺ "Proceed to **[RA] Refine Architecture** to produce an implementation-ready architecture, then **[SS]** to compose your stack skill, then **[TS]** to test and **[EX]** to export."
   145Ôćĺ- CONDITIONALLY FEASIBLE Ôćĺ "Address the {recommendation_count} recommendations above, then re-run **[VS]**. Once all clear, proceed to **[RA]**."
   146Ôćĺ- NOT FEASIBLE Ôćĺ "Critical blockers must be resolved before proceeding. Apply the recommendations above and re-run **[VS]**."
   147Ôćĺ
   148Ôćĺ### 5. Append to Report
   149Ôćĺ
   150ÔćĺWrite the **Synthesis & Recommendations** section to `{outputFile}`:
   151Ôćĺ- Include overall verdict with rationale
   152Ôćĺ- Include prioritized recommendation list
   153Ôćĺ- Include delta from previous run (if applicable)
   154Ôćĺ- Include suggested next workflow
   155Ôćĺ- **Replace the body placeholder** in the `## Overall Verdict` section: replace `{FEASIBLE / CONDITIONALLY FEASIBLE / NOT FEASIBLE}` and `{1-2 sentence summary}` with the actual calculated verdict and rationale text
   156Ôćĺ- Update frontmatter: append `'step-05-synthesize'` to `stepsCompleted`
   157Ôćĺ- Set `overall_verdict` to the calculated verdict
   158Ôćĺ- Set `recommendation_count` to the total number of recommendations
   159Ôćĺ- Verify that `integrations_verified`, `integrations_plausible`, `integrations_risky`, `integrations_blocked` in frontmatter match the counts from Step 03 (these were set in Step 03 ÔÇö correct any discrepancies)
   160Ôćĺ
   161Ôćĺ### 6. Auto-Proceed to Next Step
   162Ôćĺ
   163Ôćĺ"**Proceeding to final report presentation...**"
   164Ôćĺ
   165ÔćĺLoad, read the full file and then execute `{nextStepFile}`.
   166Ôćĺ
   167Ôćĺ---
   168Ôćĺ
   169Ôćĺ## ­čÜĘ SYSTEM SUCCESS/FAILURE METRICS
   170Ôćĺ
   171Ôćĺ### Ôťů SUCCESS:
   172Ôćĺ
   173Ôćĺ- Overall verdict calculated using the exact decision logic (not subjective judgment)
   174Ôćĺ- Every non-verified finding has a specific, actionable recommendation
   175Ôćĺ- Recommendations name concrete tools ([CS], [QS], [VS], [RA]) and specific libraries
   176Ôćĺ- Previous report delta generated if applicable
   177Ôćĺ- Synthesis section appended to {outputFile} with verdict, recommendations, and next workflow
   178Ôćĺ- Frontmatter updated with overall_verdict and metric counts
   179Ôćĺ- Auto-proceeded to step 06
   180Ôćĺ
   181Ôćĺ### ÔŁî SYSTEM FAILURE:
   182Ôćĺ
   183Ôćĺ- Subjective or vague overall verdict not following the decision logic
   184Ôćĺ- Recommendations without specific actions ("improve the integration")
   185Ôćĺ- Not checking for previous report
   186Ôćĺ- Discovering new findings or re-analyzing skills
   187Ôćĺ- Presenting the full report to the user (that is Step 06)
   188Ôćĺ- Hardcoded paths instead of frontmatter variables
   189Ôćĺ
   190Ôćĺ**Master Rule:** Skipping steps, optimizing sequences, or not following exact instructions is FORBIDDEN and constitutes SYSTEM FAILURE.
   191Ôćĺ

     8Ôćĺ
     9Ôćĺ# Step 1: Initialize Refinement
    10Ôćĺ
    11Ôćĺ## STEP GOAL:
    12Ôćĺ
    13ÔćĺLoad the architecture document (required), scan the skills folder to build a skill inventory with metadata, load the optional VS feasibility report for context, validate that all inputs exist and meet minimum requirements, and present an initialization summary before auto-proceeding.
    14Ôćĺ
    15Ôćĺ## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES (READ FIRST):
    16Ôćĺ
    17Ôćĺ### Universal Rules:
    18Ôćĺ
    19Ôćĺ- ­čôľ CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action
    20Ôćĺ- ÔÜÖ´ŞĆ TOOL/SUBPROCESS FALLBACK: If any instruction references a subprocess, subagent, or tool you do not have access to, you MUST still achieve the outcome in your main context thread
    21Ôćĺ- Ôťů YOU MUST ALWAYS SPEAK OUTPUT in your Agent communication style with the config `{communication_language}`
    22Ôćĺ
    23Ôćĺ### Role Reinforcement:
    24Ôćĺ
    25Ôćĺ- Ôťů You are an architecture refinement analyst operating in Ferris Architect mode
    26Ôćĺ- Ôťů Execute with analytical precision ÔÇö every loaded skill must be validated against actual files
    27Ôćĺ- Ôťů You enforce the zero-hallucination principle: only report skills that physically exist on disk
    28Ôćĺ
    29Ôćĺ### Step-Specific Rules:
    30Ôćĺ
    31Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Focus ONLY on loading inputs, scanning skills, and validating prerequisites
    32Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to perform any gap analysis, issue detection, or improvement suggestions
    33Ôćĺ- ­čÜź No A/P menu ÔÇö this is an auto-proceed init step
    34Ôćĺ- ­čĺČ Present a clear initialization summary so downstream steps have validated inputs
    35Ôćĺ
    36Ôćĺ## EXECUTION PROTOCOLS:
    37Ôćĺ
    38Ôćĺ- Collect and validate all input documents and skill artifacts
    39Ôćĺ- Load refinement rules from {refinementRulesData} for downstream step reference
    40Ôćĺ- Auto-proceed to next step after successful initialization
    41Ôćĺ- HALT with actionable error if minimum requirements not met
    42Ôćĺ
    43Ôćĺ## CONTEXT BOUNDARIES:
    44Ôćĺ
    45Ôćĺ- This is the first step ÔÇö no prior workflow state exists
    46Ôćĺ- Requires skills to have been generated by create-skill or quick-skill workflows
    47Ôćĺ- User provides architecture document path (required) and optional VS report path
    48Ôćĺ- No dependency on setup-forge or forge-tier ÔÇö this workflow operates on skill content only
    49Ôćĺ
    50Ôćĺ## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
    51Ôćĺ
    52Ôćĺ**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise.
    53Ôćĺ
    54Ôćĺ### 1. Accept Input Documents
    55Ôćĺ
    56Ôćĺ"**Refine Architecture ÔÇö Evidence-Backed Refinement**
    57Ôćĺ
    58ÔćĺPlease provide the following:
    59Ôćĺ1. **Architecture document path** (REQUIRED) ÔÇö your project's architecture doc to refine
    60Ôćĺ2. **VS feasibility report path** (OPTIONAL) ÔÇö from a previous [VS] Verify Stack run, for additional context"
    61Ôćĺ
    62ÔćĺWait for user input. Store the validated architecture document path as `architecture_doc`.
    63Ôćĺ
    64Ôćĺ**Validate architecture document:**
    65Ôćĺ- Confirm the file exists and is readable
    66Ôćĺ- If missing or unreadable: "Architecture document not found at `{path}`. Provide a valid path."
    67Ôćĺ- HALT until a valid architecture document is provided
    68Ôćĺ
    69Ôćĺ**Validate VS report (if provided):**
    70Ôćĺ- Confirm the file exists and is readable
    71Ôćĺ- If missing at user-provided path: attempt auto-probe (below) before giving up
    72Ôćĺ- Store VS report availability as `vs_report_available: true|false` and `vs_report_path`
    73Ôćĺ
    74Ôćĺ**Auto-probe VS report (if not provided by user, OR if user-provided path was invalid):**
    75Ôćĺ- Check for `{forge_data_folder}/feasibility-report-{project_name}.md`
    76Ôćĺ- If found: "Auto-discovered VS report at `{path}`. Loading for additional context."
    77Ôćĺ- Store `vs_report_available: true` and `vs_report_path`
    78Ôćĺ- If not found: `vs_report_available: false` ÔÇö "Proceeding without VS report ÔÇö issue detection will rely on skill data only."
    79Ôćĺ
    80Ôćĺ### 2. Scan Skills Folder
    81Ôćĺ
    82ÔćĺRead the `{skills_output_folder}` directory.
    83Ôćĺ
    84ÔćĺFor each subdirectory, check for the presence of `SKILL.md` and `metadata.json`.
    85Ôćĺ
    86Ôćĺ**For each valid skill directory, extract from metadata.json:**
    87Ôćĺ- `name` ÔÇö skill name
    88Ôćĺ- `language` ÔÇö primary language
    89Ôćĺ- `confidence_tier` ÔÇö Quick, Forge, Forge+, or Deep
    90Ôćĺ- `exports_documented` ÔÇö number of documented exports
    91Ôćĺ- `source_repo` or `source_root` ÔÇö original source repository
    92Ôćĺ
    93Ôćĺ**Build a skill inventory** as an internal list of all loaded skills with the fields above.
    94Ôćĺ
    95Ôćĺ**If a subdirectory lacks SKILL.md or metadata.json:**
    96Ôćĺ- Log: "Skipping `{dir_name}` ÔÇö missing SKILL.md or metadata.json"
    97Ôćĺ- Do not include in inventory
    98Ôćĺ
    99Ôćĺ### 3. Validate Minimum Requirements
   100Ôćĺ
   101Ôćĺ**Check skill count:**
   102Ôćĺ- At least 1 valid skill must exist
   103Ôćĺ- If no skills found: "**Cannot proceed.** No skills found in `{skills_output_folder}`. Generate skills with [CS] Create Skill or [QS] Quick Skill, then re-run [RA]."
   104Ôćĺ- HALT workflow
   105Ôćĺ- If exactly 1 valid skill found: "ÔÜá´ŞĆ Proceeding with 1 skill. Note: gap analysis will find no gaps ÔÇö pairwise analysis requires at least 2 skills. Step 02 will still execute and issue an appropriate notice. Issue detection and improvement detection will proceed normally."
   106Ôćĺ
   107Ôćĺ**Check output_folder:**
   108Ôćĺ- Verify `output_folder` was resolved from config.yaml and is non-empty
   109Ôćĺ- If undefined or empty: "**Cannot proceed.** `output_folder` is not configured in config.yaml. Add an `output_folder` path and re-run [RA]."
   110Ôćĺ- HALT workflow
   111Ôćĺ- Verify the `output_folder` directory exists. If it does not exist, create it. HALT with error if creation fails.
   112Ôćĺ
   113Ôćĺ**Check forge_data_folder:**
   114Ôćĺ- Verify `forge_data_folder` was resolved from config.yaml and is non-empty
   115Ôćĺ- If undefined or empty: "**Cannot proceed.** `forge_data_folder` is not configured in config.yaml. Re-run [SF] Setup Forge to initialize."
   116Ôćĺ- HALT workflow
   117Ôćĺ- Verify the `forge_data_folder` directory exists. If it does not exist: "**Cannot proceed.** `forge_data_folder` at `{forge_data_folder}` does not exist. Re-run [SF] Setup Forge to initialize."
   118Ôćĺ- HALT workflow
   119Ôćĺ
   120Ôćĺ**Check architecture document:**
   121Ôćĺ- Confirm it was loaded successfully in section 1
   122Ôćĺ- If not: HALT with error (should not reach here if section 1 validation passed)
   123Ôćĺ
   124Ôćĺ### 3b. Reset RA State File
   125Ôćĺ
   126ÔćĺCreate (or overwrite) `{forge_data_folder}/ra-state-{project_name}.md` with a fresh header:
   127Ôćĺ
   128Ôćĺ```markdown
   129Ôćĺ<!-- RA state for {project_name} ÔÇö generated {date} -->
   130Ôćĺ```
   131Ôćĺ
   132ÔćĺThis ensures steps 02-04 append to a clean slate and context recovery in step-05 never loads stale findings from a prior run.
   133Ôćĺ
   134Ôćĺ### 4. Load Refinement Rules
   135Ôćĺ
   136ÔćĺLoad `{refinementRulesData}` for reference by downstream steps.
   137Ôćĺ
   138ÔćĺExtract: gap detection rules, issue detection rules, improvement detection rules, citation format, and preservation rules.
   139Ôćĺ
   140Ôćĺ### 5. Display Initialization Summary
   141Ôćĺ
   142Ôćĺ"**Architecture Refinement Initialized**
   143Ôćĺ
   144Ôćĺ| Field | Value |
   145Ôćĺ|-------|-------|
   146Ôćĺ| **Architecture Doc** | {architecture_doc} |
   147Ôćĺ| **VS Report** | {vs_report_path or 'Not provided ÔÇö issue detection will use skill data only'} |
   148Ôćĺ| **Skills Loaded** | {skill_count} |
   149Ôćĺ
   150Ôćĺ**Skill Inventory:**
   151Ôćĺ
   152Ôćĺ| Skill | Language | Tier | Exports |
   153Ôćĺ|-------|----------|------|---------|
   154Ôćĺ| {skill_name} | {language} | {confidence_tier} | {exports_documented} |
   155Ôćĺ
   156Ôćĺ**Proceeding to gap analysis...**"
   157Ôćĺ
   158Ôćĺ### 6. Auto-Proceed to Next Step
   159Ôćĺ
   160ÔćĺLoad, read the full file and then execute `{nextStepFile}`.
   161Ôćĺ
   162Ôćĺ---
   163Ôćĺ
   164Ôćĺ## ­čÜĘ SYSTEM SUCCESS/FAILURE METRICS
   165Ôćĺ
   166Ôćĺ### Ôťů SUCCESS:
   167Ôćĺ
   168Ôćĺ- Architecture document loaded and validated
   169Ôćĺ- VS report loaded or absence recorded
   170Ôćĺ- Skills folder scanned with SKILL.md and metadata.json validation per skill
   171Ôćĺ- At least 1 valid skill in inventory
   172Ôćĺ- Refinement rules loaded from {refinementRulesData}
   173Ôćĺ- Initialization summary displayed with skill inventory table
   174Ôćĺ- Auto-proceeded to step 02
   175Ôćĺ
   176Ôćĺ### ÔŁî SYSTEM FAILURE:
   177Ôćĺ
   178Ôćĺ- Proceeding with no valid skills
   179Ôćĺ- Proceeding without a valid architecture document
   180Ôćĺ- Not validating SKILL.md and metadata.json existence per skill directory
   181Ôćĺ- Performing gap analysis, issue detection, or improvement suggestions in this step
   182Ôćĺ- Hardcoded paths instead of frontmatter variables
   183Ôćĺ
   184Ôćĺ**Master Rule:** This step validates inputs and initializes state. No analysis, no comparison, no refinement suggestions.

     8Ôćĺ
     9Ôćĺ# Step 5: Compile Refined Architecture
    10Ôćĺ
    11Ôćĺ## STEP GOAL:
    12Ôćĺ
    13ÔćĺProduce the refined architecture document by starting with the original as a base, adding gap-fill subsections, issue callout blocks, and improvement suggestions. Append a Refinement Summary. Present for user review before finalizing.
    14Ôćĺ
    15Ôćĺ## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES (READ FIRST):
    16Ôćĺ
    17Ôćĺ### Universal Rules:
    18Ôćĺ
    19Ôćĺ- ­čôľ CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action
    20Ôćĺ- ÔÜÖ´ŞĆ TOOL/SUBPROCESS FALLBACK: If any instruction references a subprocess, subagent, or tool you do not have access to, you MUST still achieve the outcome in your main context thread
    21Ôćĺ- Ôťů YOU MUST ALWAYS SPEAK OUTPUT in your Agent communication style with the config `{communication_language}`
    22Ôćĺ
    23Ôćĺ### Role Reinforcement:
    24Ôćĺ
    25Ôćĺ- Ôťů You are an architecture refinement analyst compiling the final refined document
    26Ôćĺ- Ôťů Additive, not destructive ÔÇö preserve every word of the original, only add refinements
    27Ôćĺ- Ôťů The document must be in `{document_output_language}`
    28Ôćĺ
    29Ôćĺ### Step-Specific Rules:
    30Ôćĺ
    31Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to discover new gaps, issues, or improvements ÔÇö use only what Steps 02-04 produced
    32Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to delete, reword, or rearrange original architecture content
    33Ôćĺ- ­čĺČ Present compiled document for user review (Gate checkpoint)
    34Ôćĺ
    35Ôćĺ## EXECUTION PROTOCOLS:
    36Ôćĺ
    37Ôćĺ- Compile refined architecture from original + findings from Steps 02-04
    38Ôćĺ- Write output to {outputFile}, wait for user approval before proceeding
    39Ôćĺ
    40Ôćĺ## CONTEXT BOUNDARIES:
    41Ôćĺ
    42Ôćĺ- From Steps 01-04: Architecture document, skill inventory, gap/issue/improvement findings
    43Ôćĺ- This step produces: The refined architecture document at {outputFile}
    44Ôćĺ
    45Ôćĺ## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
    46Ôćĺ
    47Ôćĺ**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise.
    48Ôćĺ
    49Ôćĺ### 1. Prepare the Original as Base
    50Ôćĺ
    51ÔćĺLoad the complete original architecture document.
    52Ôćĺ
    53ÔćĺThis is the base. Every line of the original MUST appear in the refined document, unmodified.
    54Ôćĺ
    55Ôćĺ**Context recovery check:** If gap, issue, or improvement findings from Steps 02-04 are not available in context (e.g., due to context degradation in long runs), attempt to read the durability state from `{forge_data_folder}/ra-state-{project_name}.md`. Parse the `<!-- [RA-GAPS] -->`, `<!-- [RA-ISSUES] -->`, and `<!-- [RA-IMPROVEMENTS] -->` comment blocks to recover the complete formatted findings (each block contains full citation text with evidence, not just counts). If a section is still missing or contains only summary counts after recovery, HALT: "ÔÜá´ŞĆ Context for {pass} was lost and the durability state is insufficient to reconstruct findings. Re-run [RA] from step 01 to rebuild."
    56Ôćĺ
    57Ôćĺ### 2. Insert Gap-Fill Subsections
    58Ôćĺ
    59ÔćĺFor each gap finding from Step 02:
    60Ôćĺ
    61Ôćĺ**Locate the most relevant section** in the original architecture where this integration would logically belong.
    62Ôćĺ
    63Ôćĺ**Insert a gap-fill subsection** one heading level deeper than the parent section:
    64Ôćĺ
    65Ôćĺ```markdown
    66Ôćĺ#### RA: {Library A} <-> {Library B} Integration Path
    67Ôćĺ
    68Ôćĺ> [!NOTE] **Gap Identified by Refine Architecture**
    69Ôćĺ> This integration path was not documented in the original architecture but is supported by skill API evidence.
    70Ôćĺ
    71Ôćĺ{Gap description with full evidence citation from Step 02}
    72Ôćĺ
    73Ôćĺ**Proposed Integration:**
    74Ôćĺ{Suggested architecture content describing how the libraries connect}
    75Ôćĺ```
    76Ôćĺ
    77ÔćĺIf no clear parent section exists, collect orphan gaps into a new section: "## RA: Additional Integration Paths"
    78Ôćĺ
    79Ôćĺ### 3. Insert Issue Annotations
    80Ôćĺ
    81ÔćĺFor each issue finding from Step 03:
    82Ôćĺ
    83Ôćĺ**Locate the section** containing the contradicted claim.
    84Ôćĺ
    85Ôćĺ**Insert an issue callout block** immediately after the contradicted text:
    86Ôćĺ
    87Ôćĺ```markdown
    88Ôćĺ> [!WARNING] **Issue Detected by Refine Architecture** ({severity})
    89Ôćĺ> Architecture states: "{quoted claim}"
    90Ôćĺ> Skill reality: {contradicting evidence from skill}
    91Ôćĺ> {IF VS report}: VS verdict: {verdict} for {pair}
    92Ôćĺ>
    93Ôćĺ> **Suggested Correction:** {specific correction with API evidence}
    94Ôćĺ```
    95Ôćĺ
    96Ôćĺ**Placement priority:** Place each issue callout immediately after the contradicted text in its original location. If the contradicted claim is inside a Markdown list item, code block, or table row where inserting a callout block would break syntax, place the callout immediately after the enclosing block instead. If the contradicted claim cannot be precisely located, collect into "## RA: Additional Issues Detected" at the end of the document, ordered by severity (Critical first, then Major, then Minor).
    97Ôćĺ
    98Ôćĺ### 4. Insert Improvement Suggestions
    99Ôćĺ
   100ÔćĺFor each improvement finding from Step 04:
   101Ôćĺ
   102Ôćĺ**Locate the section** where the library is discussed.
   103Ôćĺ
   104Ôćĺ**Insert an improvement subsection** one heading level deeper:
   105Ôćĺ
   106Ôćĺ```markdown
   107Ôćĺ#### RA: Enhancement ÔÇö {Improvement Title}
   108Ôćĺ
   109Ôćĺ> [!TIP] **Improvement Suggested by Refine Architecture** ({value} value)
   110Ôćĺ> {skill_name} provides `{api}` which is not currently leveraged.
   111Ôćĺ
   112Ôćĺ{Full improvement description with evidence citation from Step 04}
   113Ôćĺ
   114Ôćĺ**How to Incorporate:**
   115Ôćĺ{Specific suggestion for updating the architecture}
   116Ôćĺ```
   117Ôćĺ
   118Ôćĺ**Order by value:** High value improvements first, then Medium, then Low.
   119Ôćĺ
   120Ôćĺ**Placement fallback:** If the library's only architecture mention is inside a table, code block, or Mermaid diagram where inserting a subsection would break syntax, collect into `## RA: Additional Improvements Suggested` at the end of the document.
   121Ôćĺ
   122Ôćĺ### 5. Add Refinement Summary Section
   123Ôćĺ
   124ÔćĺAppend a `## Refinement Summary` section containing:
   125Ôćĺ
   126Ôćĺ- **Header:** "Produced by: Refine Architecture workflow using {skill_count} skills" and date
   127Ôćĺ- **Changes Made table:** Gaps Filled ({gap_count}), Issues Flagged ({issue_count} with severity breakdown), Improvements Suggested ({improvement_count} with value breakdown)
   128Ôćĺ- **Evidence Sources table:** Each skill name and how many refinements cite it
   129Ôćĺ- **Next Steps:** Review `[!WARNING]` issues, `[!NOTE]` gaps, `[!TIP]` improvements; then run **[SS] Create Stack Skill** ÔÇö compose-mode activates automatically when SS detects existing individual skills without a codebase; provide this refined architecture doc as the architecture document when prompted
   130Ôćĺ
   131Ôćĺ### 6. Write the Refined Document
   132Ôćĺ
   133ÔćĺWrite the complete refined architecture to `{outputFile}`.
   134Ôćĺ
   135Ôćĺ### 7. Present Compiled Document for Review
   136Ôćĺ
   137Ôćĺ"**Refined architecture compiled. Please review:**
   138Ôćĺ
   139Ôćĺ---
   140Ôćĺ
   141Ôćĺ{Display the Refinement Summary section only ÔÇö not the full document}
   142Ôćĺ
   143Ôćĺ---
   144Ôćĺ
   145Ôćĺ**The full refined document has been written to:** `{outputFile}`
   146Ôćĺ
   147ÔćĺPlease review the refinements:
   148Ôćĺ- {gap_count} gap-fill subsections added
   149Ôćĺ- {issue_count} issue annotations inserted
   150Ôćĺ- {improvement_count} improvement suggestions included
   151Ôćĺ- Original architecture content preserved in full
   152Ôćĺ
   153Ôćĺ**Does the refinement look correct?**"
   154Ôćĺ
   155Ôćĺ### 8. Present MENU OPTIONS
   156Ôćĺ
   157ÔćĺDisplay: **Select:** [C] Continue to Final Report
   158Ôćĺ
   159Ôćĺ#### EXECUTION RULES:
   160Ôćĺ
   161Ôćĺ- ALWAYS halt and wait for user input after presenting compilation
   162Ôćĺ- ONLY proceed to next step when user approves and selects 'C'
   163Ôćĺ
   164Ôćĺ#### Menu Handling Logic:
   165Ôćĺ
   166Ôćĺ- IF C: Load, read entire file, then execute {nextStepFile}
   167Ôćĺ- IF Any other: Process as feedback, adjust specific refinements in the document, rewrite {outputFile}, redisplay preview, then [Redisplay Menu Options](#8-present-menu-options)
   168Ôćĺ
   169Ôćĺ---
   170Ôćĺ
   171Ôćĺ## ­čÜĘ SYSTEM SUCCESS/FAILURE METRICS
   172Ôćĺ
   173Ôćĺ### Ôťů SUCCESS:
   174Ôćĺ
   175Ôćĺ- Original architecture document preserved in full without modifications
   176Ôćĺ- Gap-fill subsections inserted at logical locations with evidence citations
   177Ôćĺ- Issue callout blocks inserted adjacent to contradicted claims with severity
   178Ôćĺ- Improvement suggestions inserted with value ratings and evidence
   179Ôćĺ- Refinement Summary section appended with accurate counts
   180Ôćĺ- Refined document written to {outputFile}
   181Ôćĺ- User reviewed and approved compilation
   182Ôćĺ- Proceeded to step 06 only after user selected C
   183Ôćĺ
   184Ôćĺ### ÔŁî SYSTEM FAILURE:
   185Ôćĺ
   186Ôćĺ- Deleting, rewording, or rearranging original architecture content
   187Ôćĺ- Discovering new findings not from Steps 02-04
   188Ôćĺ- Inserting refinements without evidence citations
   189Ôćĺ- Proceeding without user review (Gate checkpoint)
   190Ôćĺ- Not writing the document to {outputFile} before presenting for review
   191Ôćĺ- Hardcoded paths instead of frontmatter variables
   192Ôćĺ
   193Ôćĺ**Master Rule:** Additive only. Preserve the original. Get user approval before proceeding.
   194Ôćĺ

     8Ôćĺ# Step 6: Present Report
     9Ôćĺ
    10Ôćĺ## STEP GOAL:
    11Ôćĺ
    12ÔćĺPresent the complete refinement summary to the user. Display counts of gaps filled, issues flagged, and improvements suggested. Provide the output file path and recommend next steps. Offer the user options to review changes in detail or exit. This is the FINAL step ÔÇö no nextStepFile.
    13Ôćĺ
    14Ôćĺ## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES (READ FIRST):
    15Ôćĺ
    16Ôćĺ### Universal Rules:
    17Ôćĺ
    18Ôćĺ- ­čôľ CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action

Use these extracted values in the summary table and next-steps sections below.

1Ôćĺ---
     2Ôćĺname: 'step-02-gap-analysis'
     3Ôćĺdescription: 'Find undocumented integration paths that the architecture does not describe but skill APIs suggest'
     4Ôćĺ
     5ÔćĺnextStepFile: './step-03-issue-detection.md'
     6ÔćĺrefinementRulesData: '../data/refinement-rules.md'
     7Ôćĺ---
     8Ôćĺ
     9Ôćĺ# Step 2: Gap Analysis
    10Ôćĺ
    11Ôćĺ## STEP GOAL:
    12Ôćĺ
    13ÔćĺFind undocumented integration paths ÔÇö library pairs that have compatible APIs (from the generated skills) but are not described in the architecture document. For each gap, document what APIs connect and propose an architecture section describing the integration.
    14Ôćĺ
    15Ôćĺ## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES (READ FIRST):
    16Ôćĺ
    17Ôćĺ### Universal Rules:
    18Ôćĺ
    19Ôćĺ- ­čôľ CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action
    20Ôćĺ- ­čöä CRITICAL: When loading next step with 'C', ensure entire file is read
    21Ôćĺ- ÔÜÖ´ŞĆ TOOL/SUBPROCESS FALLBACK: If any instruction references a subprocess, subagent, or tool you do not have access to, you MUST still achieve the outcome in your main context thread
    22Ôćĺ- Ôťů YOU MUST ALWAYS SPEAK OUTPUT in your Agent communication style with the config `{communication_language}`
    23Ôćĺ
    24Ôćĺ### Role Reinforcement:
    25Ôćĺ
    26Ôćĺ- Ôťů You are an architecture refinement analyst performing gap detection
    27Ôćĺ- Ôťů Every gap must cite specific APIs from the generated skills ÔÇö no speculation
    28Ôćĺ- Ôťů Apply the gap detection rules from {refinementRulesData} strictly
    29Ôćĺ
    30Ôćĺ### Step-Specific Rules:
    31Ôćĺ
    32Ôćĺ- ­čÄ» Focus ONLY on undocumented integration paths (gaps)
    33Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to detect contradictions or issues ÔÇö that is Step 03
    34Ôćĺ- ­čÜź FORBIDDEN to suggest capability expansions or improvements ÔÇö that is Step 04
    35Ôćĺ- ­čĺČ Every gap MUST include evidence citations from actual skill content
    36Ôćĺ
    37Ôćĺ## EXECUTION PROTOCOLS:
    38Ôćĺ
    39Ôćĺ- Load refinement rules for gap detection criteria
    40Ôćĺ- Extract integration claims from architecture document using prose co-mention analysis
    41Ôćĺ- Generate all possible library pairs and cross-reference against architecture
    42Ôćĺ- Append gap analysis findings as workflow state for Step 05
    43Ôćĺ- Only gap detection ÔÇö no issue detection, no improvement suggestions
    44Ôćĺ
    45Ôćĺ## CONTEXT BOUNDARIES:
    46Ôćĺ
    47Ôćĺ- Available: Architecture document content, skill inventory from Step 01, SKILL.md and metadata.json files, refinement rules
    48Ôćĺ- Focus: Finding library pairs with compatible APIs that the architecture does not describe
    49Ôćĺ- Limits: Only detect gaps ÔÇö do not flag issues or suggest improvements
    50Ôćĺ- Dependencies: Step 01 must have loaded skill inventory and validated architecture document
    51Ôćĺ
    52Ôćĺ## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
    53Ôćĺ
    54Ôćĺ**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise.
    55Ôćĺ
    56Ôćĺ### 1. Reference Refinement Rules
    57Ôćĺ
    58ÔćĺUse the refinement rules loaded in Step 01 from `{refinementRulesData}`. If not available in context, reload from `{refinementRulesData}`.
    59Ôćĺ
    60ÔćĺExtract: gap classification (Missing Integration Path, Undocumented Data Flow, Absent Bridge Layer), detection method, and citation format.
    61Ôćĺ
    62Ôćĺ### 2. Extract Integration Claims from Architecture
    63Ôćĺ
    64ÔćĺParse the architecture document for statements describing two or more technologies working together.
    65Ôćĺ
    66Ôćĺ**Detection method ÔÇö prose-based co-mention analysis:**
    67Ôćĺ- Identify sentences or paragraphs where two or more technology names appear together
    68Ôćĺ- Look for integration verbs: "connects to", "communicates with", "wraps", "extends", "consumes", "produces", "bridges", "integrates with", "sits between", "feeds into", "receives from"
    69Ôćĺ- Look for data flow descriptions: "{A} sends data to {B}", "{A} results are consumed by {B}"
    70Ôćĺ- Look for layer boundary descriptions: "{A} at the API layer connects to {B} at the data layer"
    71Ôćĺ
    72Ôćĺ**CRITICAL:** Do NOT parse Mermaid diagram syntax. Use only prose text for co-mention detection.
    73Ôćĺ
    74Ôćĺ**Mermaid Limitation Warning:** If `` ```mermaid `` blocks are present in the architecture document, inform the user: "Integration paths documented exclusively in Mermaid diagrams are excluded from co-mention analysis and may appear as false-positive gaps. Consider adding prose descriptions for diagram-only integration paths." Display this warning informatively and immediately continue ÔÇö this does not halt or modify the analysis sequence.
    75Ôćĺ
    76Ôćĺ**Build documented pairs list:**
    77Ôćĺ- Each pair: `{library_a, library_b, architectural_context}`
    78Ôćĺ- `architectural_context`: the quoted text or paraphrased description of their relationship
    79Ôćĺ
    80Ôćĺ### 3. Generate All Possible Library Pairs
    81Ôćĺ
    82ÔćĺFrom the skill inventory, generate all unique combinations of library pairs.
    83Ôćĺ
    84ÔćĺFor N skills, this produces N*(N-1)/2 unique pairs.
    85Ôćĺ
    86Ôćĺ### 4. Load Skill API Surfaces for Cross-Reference
    87Ôćĺ
    88ÔćĺFor each library in the skill inventory, load the skill artifacts:
    89Ôćĺ
    90Ôćĺ**From SKILL.md, extract:**
    91Ôćĺ- Exported functions and their signatures
    92Ôćĺ- Exported types, interfaces, and classes
    93Ôćĺ- Protocol indicators (HTTP, gRPC, WebSocket, message queue, file I/O, IPC)
    94Ôćĺ- Data format indicators (JSON, protobuf, CSV, binary, streaming)
    95Ôćĺ
    96Ôćĺ**From metadata.json, extract:**
    97Ôćĺ- `language` ÔÇö primary programming language
    98Ôćĺ- `exports` ÔÇö export count and names
    99Ôćĺ
   100Ôćĺ### 5. Cross-Reference: Identify Gaps
   101Ôćĺ
   102ÔćĺFor each possible library pair NOT already documented in the architecture:
   103Ôćĺ
   104Ôćĺ**Check API compatibility:**
   105Ôćĺ- Does Library A export types or data that Library B can consume?
   106Ôćĺ- Do both libraries share a compatible protocol or data format?
   107Ôćĺ- Are they in the same language or is there a bridge mechanism available?
   108Ôćĺ
   109Ôćĺ**If compatible APIs exist but NO architecture mention:**
   110Ôćĺ- Classify the gap type (Missing Integration Path, Undocumented Data Flow, or Absent Bridge Layer)
   111Ôćĺ- Document the connecting APIs from both skills
   112Ôćĺ- Propose a brief architecture section describing the integration
   113Ôćĺ
   114Ôćĺ**Apply the citation format from {refinementRulesData}:**
   115Ôćĺ```
   116Ôćĺ**[GAP]**: {description}
   117Ôćĺ
   118ÔćĺEvidence:
   119Ôćĺ- {skill_a} exports: `{function}({params}) -> {return_type}`
   120Ôćĺ- {skill_b} accepts: `{function}({params})`
   121Ôćĺ- Compatibility: {explanation}
   122Ôćĺ
   123ÔćĺSuggestion: {proposed architecture section content}
   124Ôćĺ```
   125Ôćĺ
   126Ôćĺ**If no compatible APIs:** Skip this pair ÔÇö not all pairs need to integrate.
   127Ôćĺ
   128Ôćĺ### 6. Display Gap Analysis Results
   129Ôćĺ
   130Ôćĺ"**Pass 1: Gap Analysis ÔÇö Undocumented Integration Paths**
   131Ôćĺ
   132Ôćĺ**Gaps Found:** {count}
   133Ôćĺ
   134Ôćĺ{IF gaps found:}
   135Ôćĺ| # | Library A | Library B | Gap Type | Connecting APIs |
   136Ôćĺ|---|-----------|-----------|----------|-----------------|
   137Ôćĺ| {n} | {lib_a} | {lib_b} | {gap_type} | {brief API description} |
   138Ôćĺ
   139Ôćĺ{For each gap, display the full citation with evidence and suggestion}
   140Ôćĺ
   141Ôćĺ{IF no gaps found AND N > 1:}
   142Ôćĺ**No undocumented integration paths detected.** The architecture document covers all compatible library pairs.
   143Ôćĺ
   144Ôćĺ{IF no gaps found AND N == 1:}
   145Ôćĺ**ÔÜá´ŞĆ Gap analysis skipped ÔÇö only 1 skill loaded.** Pairwise integration analysis requires at least 2 skills. If the architecture references multiple libraries, those without a matching skill are invisible to gap analysis. **Recommendation:** Generate skills for all architecture libraries with [CS] or [QS] before running [RA] for comprehensive gap detection.
   146Ôćĺ
   147Ôćĺ**Proceeding to issue detection (still produces value with 1 skill)...**"
   148Ôćĺ
   149ÔćĺStore all gap findings as workflow state for Step 05. To ensure durability across long runs, also append a `<!-- [RA-GAPS] ... -->` comment block to `{forge_data_folder}/ra-state-{project_name}.md` containing the **complete formatted gap findings** (full citation blocks with evidence and suggestions, not just counts) ÔÇö Step 05 can read this back if context degrades. **Do NOT write to `{output_folder}/refined-architecture-{project_name}.md` ÔÇö that file is created only in step-05.**
   150Ôćĺ
   151Ôćĺ### 7. Auto-Proceed to Next Step
   152Ôćĺ
   153ÔćĺLoad, read the full file and then execute `{nextStepFile}`.
   154Ôćĺ
   155Ôćĺ---
   156Ôćĺ
   157Ôćĺ## ­čÜĘ SYSTEM SUCCESS/FAILURE METRICS
   158Ôćĺ
   159Ôćĺ### Ôťů SUCCESS:
   160Ôćĺ
   161Ôćĺ- Refinement rules loaded from {refinementRulesData}
   162Ôćĺ- Integration claims extracted from architecture document using prose co-mention analysis
   163Ôćĺ- All possible library pairs generated from skill inventory
   164Ôćĺ- Skill API surfaces loaded for cross-reference
   165Ôćĺ- Each gap includes evidence citations from actual skill content
   166Ôćĺ- Gap analysis results displayed with count and details
   167Ôćĺ- Gap findings stored as workflow state for Step 05
   168Ôćĺ- Auto-proceeded to step 03
   169Ôćĺ
   170Ôćĺ### ÔŁî SYSTEM FAILURE:
   171Ôćĺ
   172Ôćĺ- Inventing APIs not present in the actual skills
   173Ôćĺ- Flagging contradictions or issues (that is Step 03)
   174Ôćĺ- Suggesting capability improvements (that is Step 04)
   175Ôćĺ- Gaps without evidence citations from generated skills
   176Ôćĺ- Parsing Mermaid diagrams instead of using prose-based co-mention analysis
   177Ôćĺ- Hardcoded paths instead of frontmatter variables
   178Ôćĺ
   179Ôćĺ**Master Rule:** Skipping steps, optimizing sequences, or not following exact instructions is FORBIDDEN and constitutes SYSTEM FAILURE.
   180Ôćĺ

    70Ôćĺ## Gap Entry Format

    84Ôćĺ## Remediation Quality Rules
